401(k) plan.definition: A retirement savings plan run by a company for its  employees that allows employees to save or invest part of their  salary tax-free and often includes contributions by the employer.

419 scam.definition: A common scam named for a section of the Nigerian  criminal code in which scammers persuade victims to send them  large sums of money in order to receive promised large payoffs that  never arrive.

abandon.definition: To intentionally give up a right or property without any  plan of reclaiming it in the future; to desert a spouse or child. N.  abandonment.

abatable nuisance.definition: A nuisance that can be reduced.

abate.definition: To decrease, reduce, or diminish; to end, dismiss, or  temporarily suspend a lawsuit. N. abatement. ADJ. abatable.

abatement of a legacy.definition: A reduction in the amount of a legacy  after the payment of debts owed by the person who granted  the legacy.

abatement of taxes.definition: A reduction in the amount owed by the  taxee.

abdicate.definition: To renounce a responsibility or position; generally  used to describe the act of a sovereign giving up a throne or an offi-  cial renouncing the privileges and duties of his or her office; also  used to describe a government or official failing to fulfill responsi-  bilities or duties. N. abdication.

abduct.definition: (1) To take or carry away a person illegally by force or  persuasion. (2) To take away or detain unlawfully a female,  intending to force her into marriage, concubinage, or prostitution.  (3) For a woman to entice a husband to abandon his wife for her.  N. abduction. See also kidnap, alienation of affections.

abet.definition: To help or encourage someone else to commit a crime.

abettor.definition: A person who abets or instigates a crime; an abettor  shares criminal intent with the person who in fact commits the  crime. See also aid and abet, accessory, accomplice.

ab initio.definition: (Latin) From the beginning; used to describe  contracts, marriages, deeds, etc., that are either valid or void from  their inception.

abode.definition: A home or place of residence; a domicile.

abolish.definition: To end or do away with; generally used to describe  formally ending an institution, system, or custom, such as slavery  or a tax. N. abolition.

aboriginal.definition: Indigenous, native to a place from earliest times.

aboriginal title.definition: The exclusive rights of American Indians to  lands and waters they occupied before the United States claimed  them.

abortion.definition: The premature ending of a pregnancy; in legal context,  generally refers to a deliberate termination, though the term can  also apply to spontaneous natural expulsion of a fetus before it  becomes viable. V. abort.

about.definition: Approximately; near in time, degree, or quality.

abridge.definition: To shorten or condense while retaining the sense of the  original document. N. abridgement.

abrogate.definition: To repeal, revoke, or end; particularly applies to laws,  rights, orders, or formal agreements. N. abrogation.

abscond.definition: To depart quickly and secretly in order to avoid arrest  or a lawsuit, particularly after committing some crime such as  theft; to leave the jurisdiction of local courts or to hide from them.

abstain.definition: To refrain from doing something, such as voting. N.  abstention.

abstention doctrine.definition: A policy that allows federal courts to relin-  quish jurisdiction over a matter and allow a state court to decide a  federal constitutional question or a matter of state law.

abstract.definition: Existing only in thought or theory and not in reality  N. (1) A summary, abridgement, or condensation of a longer docu-  ment. (2) That which is abstract or theoretical, often used in the  phrase “in the abstract.” V. (1) To summarize or abridge. (2) To  remove something from something else, as in abstracting money  from a bank. N. abstraction.

abstract of record.definition: A short summary of the history of a case  taken from the trial court record to show an appellate court what  issues were considered at trial.

abstract of title.definition: A short history of a piece of land tracing title  through all conveyances, transfers, and liens or encumbrances;  used to prove an owner’s right to the property.

absurd.definition: Illogical, incongruous, and obviously untrue. N.  absurdity.

abuse.definition: To misuse; to wrong or mistreat a person or animal phys-  ically, mentally, or sexually. N. Improper use; corrupt acts; cruel  treatment of another; violence or sexual assault toward another.

abuse of process.definition: Using the courts and legal process for some  improper purpose, such as initiating a lawsuit for revenge or intim-  idation.

abut.definition: To share a boundary; to touch; to adjoin.

accelerate.definition: To go faster; to increase; to shorten the time in which  a future event will occur, particularly used to discuss the vesting of  property rights. N. acceleration.

accelerated cost recovery system.definition: An accounting method that  writes off the cost of a fixed asset over a period of time. See also  cash method. ABBRV. ACRS.

acceleration clause.definition: A clause in a contract or mortgage that  causes the entire payment to become due if a specific event occurs;  common in installment contracts, in which the entire debt  becomes due if the buyer fails to make payments on time.

accept.definition: To receive willingly; to agree voluntarily; implies the  right to refuse.

acceptance.definition: In contract law, voluntarily consenting to an offer,  which then creates a binding contract.

acceptance, conditional.definition: Agreeing to accept an offer if a certain  condition is fulfilled.

acceptance, implied.definition: An agreement that is implied from a  person’s words and deeds rather than from explicit acceptance of  the offer.

access.definition: An opportunity to do or use something; a means of  approach or communication; an opportunity for sexual inter-  course; a property owner’s right to go to and from his or her land.

accession.definition: (1) An addition; something added to an existing body  of property; the right to ownership of one’s property even after its  form has been altered (e.g., if A cuts down B’s tree and makes it  into a chair, B can still claim ownership of the chair by accession).  (2) The attainment of a rank or title, as in a monarch’s accession to  a throne. (3) Accepting or joining a treaty or association. V. accede.

accessory.definition: (1) Something added to another object as a decora-  tion or to make it more useful. (2) Someone who helps another  person commit a crime.

accessory after the fact.definition: A person who aids a felon, knowing  that he or she has committed a felony and intending to help him or  her escape punishment. See also accomplice, aid and abet,  conspiracy.

accessory before the fact.definition: Someone who encourages or helps  another plan to commit a felony but who is not present during the  commission of the crime.

accident.definition: A chance occurrence or incident; an unforeseen and  unintended event; often used to describe unfortunate occurrences,  such as injury or mishap. ADJ. accidental.

accident, unavoidable.definition: An accident that occurs despite the  exercise of due care and common sense; an accident that could not  have been prevented.

accommodate.definition: To meet someone’s wishes or demands; to  adapt to; to do something as a favor, without consideration. N.  accommodation.

accommodation note.definition: A note signed by an accommodation  maker. Also called accommodation paper.

accommodation party (maker).definition: A person who signs his or her  name to a note or commercial paper to lend credit to another  signer, the accommodated party, without consideration and  thereby assuming liability for the debt; a cosigner or surety.

accomplice.definition: Someone who knowingly and willingly helps  another commit a crime. See also aid and abet, accessory.

accord.definition: To give or grant; to agree. N. An agreement; a treaty; an  agreement between two parties that settles a dispute and provides  satisfaction to the wronged party.

accord and satisfaction.definition: A means of ending a dispute by forming  an agreement (the accord) that one party will pay the other some  consideration (the satisfaction, often less than the amount origi-  nally agreed to) and that this will discharge any remaining obliga-  tion.

account.definition: (1) A statement of financial events, debits, and credits.  (2) A body of funds held by one institution or individual on behalf  of another. (3) A description of an event.

accountability.definition: Responsibility; the state of being answerable for  something.

accountant, certified public.definition: An accountant who has passed an  official examination and fulfilled other requirements and has thus  been licensed as an accountant by his or her state. ABBRV. CPA.

account for.definition: To provide a satisfactory explanation for something.

accounting.definition: (1) A system of keeping financial records. (2) An  action in equity to settle the finances of a partnership.

accounting method.definition: The method used by a business to calculate  its income and expenditures for tax purposes. See also accrual  method, cash method.

accredit.definition: To recognize officially or authorize; to attribute; to send  someone to another place (often internationally) as an official  envoy.

accrete.definition: To grow or increase by accumulation.

accretion.definition: (1) Growing or adding on to something; typically used  to describe natural growth such as the accumulation of sediment at  the mouth of a river. (2) Income to a trust that comes from some  unusual source. (3) The right of heirs and legatees to claim the  property of any other heir or legatee who refuses to accept it or  dies before inheriting.

accrual method.definition: An accounting method that records incurred  expenses and income earned during a period even if all money has  not been paid or received; businesses that maintain inventory must  use this method.

accrue.definition: (1) To accumulate or increase; to receive at regular inter-  vals; to become due. (2) To come into existence as a cause of  action. N. accrual.

accusatory body.definition: A group such as a grand jury that hears  evidence and decides that someone should be accused of a crime.

accusatory instrument.definition: A document that sets forth an  accusation.

accusatory stage.definition: The stage of criminal procedure after the  accused has been arrested and is being interrogated.

accuse.definition: To charge someone with a crime; to institute legal  proceedings against a suspected criminal. N. accusation.

acknowledge.definition: To admit or confirm; to accept responsibility. N.  acknowledgement.

acquiesce.definition: To accept without protest; to give implied consent by  silence. N. acquiescence. See also laches.

acquiescence, estoppel by.definition: Estoppel that arises if a party has  the opportunity to object to something but gives implied consent  by inaction.

acquire.definition: To gain or obtain; to become the owner of something.

acquisition.definition: The process of acquiring; something acquired.

acquit.definition: To set free or release; to absolve of criminal liability. N.  acquittal.

acre.definition: A unit of land with an area of 43,560 square feet (4,840  square yards).

act.definition: To do something, usually voluntarily. N. (1) An action or  deed. (2) A law or written ordinance passed by Congress or  another legislative body; when done by Congress, this is called an  act of Congress.

acting.definition: Temporarily performing the duties of a position or  office without officially holding that position or office.

action.definition: (Latin) (1) A proceeding or an action; the right to pursue  a lawsuit. (2) A court proceeding; a lawsuit; a formal complaint  brought by one party to prosecute another or demand rights within  a court of law. See also case.

actionable.definition: Forming the legal basis of a cause of action.

action at equity.definition: An action brought in a court of equity.

action at law.definition: An action brought in a court of law.

actio non.definition: (Latin) No action; a pleading of nonperformance; a  nonsuit.

active.definition: Doing purposefully; currently in action; the opposite of  passive.

active duty.definition: The state of serving full-time in the military; see  reserve.

active negligence.definition: Negligence that occurs through some posi-  tive act as opposed to passive inaction.

activism, judicial.definition: The practice of making legal decisions based  on beliefs about individual rights and attitudes rather than prece-  dent and statute.

activist judge.definition: A judge who uses his or her position on the bench  to make decisions that reflect personal attitudes and beliefs instead  of applying the letter of the law or following precedent.

act of God.definition: Something that happens as a result of natural forces  that cannot be controlled by humans, such as storms, earthquakes,  or floods. Also called act of providence.

act of sale.definition: An official document certifying that a sale has  occurred, signed by the parties and verified by witnesses and a  notary.

actor.definition: A person who acts; one who performs an action.

actual damages.definition: Damages awarded for real injuries as opposed  to nominal or punitive damages.

actual residence.definition: The place where someone actually lives, as  opposed to a legal residence. See also constructive.

actual value.definition: A value awarded in condemnation proceedings  based on the price that a property would probably fetch from a  willing buyer to a willing seller.

actuarial tables.definition: Statistical tables that predict the likely ages  that people will reach; used for such purposes as calculating the  value of annuities or damages stemming from premature deaths;  also called life tables.

actuary.definition: Someone who uses statistics to calculate insurance  rates. ADJ. actuarial.

actus reus.definition: (Latin) The guilty act; a criminal action. See also  mens rea.

ad damnum.definition: (Latin) To the damage; the clause in a complaint in  which a plaintiff specifies the damages he or she seeks.

addendum.definition: Something that is added on; usually written material  added to the end of a document.

addict.definition: Someone who uses, does, or consumes something habit-  ually and who is dependent on that substance or activity; usually  applies to those who are dependent on drugs or alcohol and cannot  control their consumption thereof.

addition.definition: Something added to something else; a new structure  added to an existing building.

additur.definition: (Latin) A trial court’s increase in the amount of  damages awarded to a plaintiff by a jury; this can be done with the  defendant’s consent if the court rules that the jury’s award is inad-  equate, and on the condition that the plaintiff’s motion for a new  trial will be denied.

adeem.definition: To remove, revoke, or take away; to take away a legacy  or future bequest in advance.

ademption.definition: Revocation of a legacy by a testator before the  testator’s death, sometimes by giving the recipient the property

adequate care.definition: Care appropriate to the risk in question.

adequate compensation.definition: Under eminent domain, the market  value of property when taken.

adequate remedy at law.definition: A remedy that provides complete and  appropriate relief.

adhesion.definition: Joining; clinging to; sticking together; allegiance. V.  adhere.

adhesion contract.definition: A contract offered to one party by another  on a take-it-or-leave-it basis, in which the offering party creates all  the details of the contract and the receiving party has no opportu-  nity to bargain or modify the contract. This is common with stan-  dard form contracts. Often there is doubt as to whether an  adhesion contract is valid because one party has so little bargaining  power. See also unconscionable.

ad hoc.definition: (Latin) For this; arranged for one particular purpose.  ADV. ad hoc.

ad hominem.definition: (Latin) To the person; appealing to the emotions  instead of to logic and reason. ADV. ad hominem.

adjective law.definition: Rules of procedure; the rules that administer  substantive law.

adjoining.definition: Joined with; touching; in contact with.

adjourn.definition: To postpone; to suspend; to stop with the intent of  resuming later.

adjudicate.definition: To judge; to formally issue a final judgment in a  court proceeding. Synonymous with adjudge. N. adjudication.

adjudge.definition: To decide; to pass judgment; to sentence.

adjust.definition: To alter; to make satisfactory; to remove discrepancies;  to settle.

adjusted basis.definition: Basis plus additions to capital minus depre-  ciation.

adjusted gross income.definition: Gross income minus expenses and  capital loss deduction.

adjuster.definition: A person who settles things, especially insurance  matters; one who determines the amount of a claim against an  insurer and then agrees on a settlement with the insured.

ad litem.definition: (Latin) For the lawsuit; for the purposes of the  lawsuit being prosecuted.

ad litem, guardian.definition: Someone appointed to act in a lawsuit on  behalf of a child or incapacitated party.

administer.definition: To manage; to run (a business or other operation); to  make someone take an oath; to enforce a decree.

administration.definition: The process of managing or running something;  the people who manage something.

administrative agency.definition: A governmental organization that imple-  ments a particular piece of legislation, such as workers’ compensa-  tion or tax law.

administrative law judge.definition: The officer who presides over admin-  istrative hearings. This officer is not a judge of law; he or she may  administer oaths, hear testimony, make determinations of fact, and  recommend or make decisions.

Administrative Procedure Act.definition: A federal law that governs the  proceedings of federal administrative agencies.

administrator.definition: A person appointed by a court to handle the  estate of someone who dies intestate, i.e., without a will. FEM.  administratrix. See also executor.

admiralty and maritime jurisdiction.definition: Jurisdiction over events  happening on the seas, oceans, and navigable waters.

admiralty court.definition: A court that hears disputes related to admiralty  and maritime matters.

admissible.definition: Acceptable; valid; able to be admitted.

admissible evidence.definition: Evidence that is proper to admit at trial  because it is relevant to the matter at hand. See also admit,  evidence.

admit.definition: (1) To allow in; to accept as evidence (2) To acknowledge  that something is true; to confess to a crime. N. admission.

adopt.definition: (1) To make one’s own; to accept; to choose. (2) To create  a legal parent-child relationship between people unrelated by  blood. N. adoption.

adult.definition: A fully grown person; one who has reached the age of  majority.

adult entertainment.definition: Entertainment not suitable for children,  typically erotic or sexual acts or movies; pornography.

adulterate.definition: To corrupt; to mix into a pure or good substance  something of poorer quality. N. adulteration.

adultery.definition: Voluntary sexual congress between a married person  and someone who is not his or her spouse. ADJ. adulterous.

ad valorem.definition: (Latin) According to the value; in proportion  to value.

ad valorem tax.definition: A tax assessed on the value of property.

advance.definition: To move forward or make progress; to approach; to  pay money or some other payment before it is due, as when an  author receives an advance before a book is written. N. Money paid  before it is due; money or other objects given on credit, with some  future repayment anticipated.

advance directive.definition: A document such as a living will or durable  power of attorney that describes the kind of medical care a person  wishes to receive if he or she becomes incapacitated.

advancement.definition: A portion of an anticipated inheritance paid  before the death of the testator (usually a parent to a child) that is  then deducted from the recipient’s share of the estate after the  testator’s death.

advance sheets.definition: Recent judicial opinions published in pamphlet  or loose-leaf form that are later compiled into bound volumes of  regional reporters; hardbound collections of court opinions.

adventure.definition: A hazardous and risky enterprise or activity; often  used in marine insurance policies as a synonym for “peril.”

adverse possession.definition: A method of acquiring property without  buying it; if a person uses land not belonging to him or her in a  manner that is open (so that the owner knows about it or should  know), but without permission of the owner, continuously, actu-  ally, and exclusively for a period of time prescribed by statute  (usually a number of years), then a court will find that the person  has earned title to the land.

advertise.definition: To describe publicly in order to promote sales or  solicit applications; to announce; to call to public attention.

advertisement.definition: A public notice promoting a product or service  or soliciting applications.

advice.definition: Guidance; suggestions; recommendations intended to  help someone; the opinions and recommendations given to clients  by lawyers.

advise.definition: To counsel; to recommend a course of action; to offer  helpful suggestions. ADJ. advisory.

advisory opinion.definition: An opinion rendered by a judge or court that  indicates how the court would rule on a question without actually  ruling on some adversary proceeding; an advisory opinion is only  informative and is not binding.

advocacy.definition: Pleading or arguing for a cause.

advocacy, trial.definition: Arguing for a cause in court as part of a trial.

advocate.definition: Someone who supports a cause or argues for some-  thing; someone who defends another; a legal counselor or repre-  sentative. V. To argue in support or defense of; to recommend.

adware.definition: A computer program that automatically displays adver-  tisements on a computer, often bundled with another program that  users buy for actual use. See also bundling.

aesthetic value.definition: The value of something due to its beauty or  artistic worth rather than its practical use.

affect.definition: To have an effect on; to influence; to change.

affiant.definition: Someone who makes and swears to an affidavit.

affidavit.definition: A written statement of facts whose truth is confirmed  by oath of the party making it, used as evidence in court.

affiliate.definition: To associate with; to join officially. N. A person or organ-  ization allied with or officially joined to a larger body.

affinity.definition: (1) A relationship; fondness or liking. (2) The degree of  relationship between people who are related by marriage. See also  consanguinity.

affirm.definition: To confirm; state as fact; ratify; to declare that a previous  judgment is correct.

affirmation.definition: The act of confirming that something is true. It can  be used as a substitute for an oath in the case of religious or ethical  objections to swearing.

affirmative defense.definition: In pleading, a response to a complaint that  constitutes a defense and justification for the defendant’s actions  instead of attacking the truth of the plaintiff’s allegations; affirma-  tive defenses include self-defense, assumption of risk, estoppel,  and insanity.

affirmative misconduct.definition: A deliberate act done by someone who  knows it is wrong; deliberately misrepresenting a fact or  concealing a fact.

affirmative relief.definition: Relief or compensation granted to a defendant  in a lawsuit.

AFL-CIO.definition: American Federation of Labor and Congress of  Industrial Organizations.

a fortiori.definition: (Latin) With a stronger reason; used in argument to  describe a proposition that must be true because it is a subcategory  of something that is true. ADV. a fortiori.

after-acquired.definition: Acquired subsequent to a particular time or  event.

after-acquired property.definition: (1) Property a debtor acquires after  concluding an agreement putting up other property as security for  a loan. (2) Property acquired by a bankrupt after filing for bank-  ruptcy.

after-acquired title.definition: A doctrine providing that if someone sells  property he or she does not own but then acquires the title to it, the  buyer receives the good title.

after the fact.definition: Subsequent to a particular event. See also  accessory.

against interest.definition: Describes a statement or admission that is  disadvantageous to the person making it.

against the law.definition: Illegal; also describes a court decision made  despite insufficient evidence or material issues.

against the (manifest) weight of the evi.definition: Not supported by  the evidence presented at trial, or based on false evidence; the  evidence clearly leading to the opposite conclusion.

against the will.definition: Describes the state of mind of a victim of  robbery or rape.

age discrimination.definition: Unfair treatment in the workplace and in  hiring based on age; prohibited by the federal Age Discrimination  in Employment Act of 1967.

Age Discrimination in Employment Act.definition: A federal law that  prohibits discrimination against people on the basis of age. ABBRV.  ADEA.

agency.definition: (1) A relationship in which one person, the agent, is  authorized to act on behalf of the other, the principal. (2) A  department or group that performs a specific task for the govern-  ment. (3) A business that provides a specific service, often  arranging transactions between customers.

agent, insurance.definition: Someone authorized to sell insurance policies  on behalf of an insurance company.

agent, real estate.definition: Someone authorized to sell or rent property  on behalf of other people.

age of consent.definition: (1) The age at which a person may marry  without his or her parents’ permission. (2) The age at which a  person may consent to have sexual intercourse without causing his  or her sexual partner to be guilty of an offense such as statutory  rape or sexual assault.

age of majority.definition: The age at which a person becomes a legal  adult, responsible for his or her own actions, and able to enter into  contracts and vote.

age of reason.definition: The age at which a child is deemed responsible  for his or her own actions, usually 7 years of age.

aggravate.definition: To make worse. N. aggravation. See also simple.

aggravated assault.definition: Assault made worse than simple assault by  the addition of aggravating circumstances, such as extreme indif-  ference to human life or the use of a deadly weapon.

aggravating circumstances.definition: Circumstances that increase the  severity of a crime or tort.

aggregate.definition: The sum of several parts; the entire amount of some-  thing. V. To combine several elements into one whole, as when  several causes of action are joined into a single lawsuit or several  people are combined to form a group for a class action. N. aggre-  gation.

aggrieved.definition: Feeling anger at some injury or unfair treat-  ment.

aggrieved party.definition: Someone who has been injured, suffered a loss,  or whose rights or property are at risk.

aging of accounts.definition: Arranging accounts in chronological order of  days outstanding.

agrarian.definition: Having to do with land and agriculture.

agree.definition: To concur; to consent; to settle.

agreement.definition: A mutual understanding between two or more  parties; a meeting of minds. It often leads to a contract.

aid and abet.definition: To knowingly help someone commit a crime. See  also accessory, accomplice.

air rights.definition: Rights to use the air above land; air rights accompany  land ownership of land, with some limitations, e.g., a landowner  has the right to recover damages from airlines that interfere with  his or her use of the land, but is not allowed to pollute the air.

aka.definition: Also known as. See also alias.

alderman.definition: A city or town official or legislator; a councilman.

aleatory contract.definition: A contract whose performance depends on  some random event, such as an insurance contract.

alias.definition: A fake or alternate identity. ADV. Used to indicate the use  of a false name, e.g., “Samuel Clemens, alias Mark Twain.”

alibi.definition: A defense in which the defendant claims to have been someplace other than the scene of a crime when the crime was committed and produces evidence to prove it, thus proving that it was physically impossible for him or her to have committed the crime in question.

alien.definition: A foreigner; someone born in another country who has not  become a citizen of his or her country of residence. ADJ. (1)  Foreign. (2) Strange, unfamiliar. See also resident alien.

alienate.definition: To transfer property from one person to another. N.  alienation.

alienation of affections.definition: The tort of maliciously interfering  with a marriage, resulting in damage to the marital relationship.

alimony.definition: An allowance ordered by the court that one spouse  pays to the other after a divorce or separation for the support and  maintenance of the recipient.

allege.definition: To claim; to assert; to state in a pleading what one intends  to prove at trial. N. allegation.

allen charge.definition: A charge by the court to a deadlocked jury admon-  ishing them to work together to reach a verdict, and especially  encouraging the holders of a minority opinion to listen to the argu-  ments of the majority and to let themselves be convinced if  possible. This is prohibited in some states.

allocate.definition: To distribute for a particular purpose; to assign; to allot.  In taxation, to allot portions of income to different purposes. N.  allocation. ADJ. allocable.

allocution.definition: A speech by a trial judge to a criminal defendant asking the defendant if he or she would like to make a statement on his or her own behalf, or asking if the defendant has any legal reason why he or she should not be sentenced if the jury's verdict is to convict.

allot.definition: To apportion; to distribute portions of something to several  individuals; to allocate something to someone.

allotment.definition: The amount of something allocated to someone; a  portion or share.

allow.definition: To permit; to acquiesce; to accept as true.

allowance.definition: (1) The amount that is permitted. (2) A sum of  money paid to someone regularly.

alluvion.definition: Land formed by the accumulation of silt on the banks  or at the mouth of a stream or river. See also accrete.

alter.definition: To change or modify. N. alteration.

alteration, material.definition: A change in the language of a contract that  affects the rights defined by the contract.

alter ego.definition: (Latin) Other self; an alternative personality. In  corporate law, a doctrine that allows the court to find an individual  responsible for acts done in the name of a corporation.

alternative dispute resolution.definition: Methods for settling disputes  without recourse to litigation, including arbitration, mediation, and  conciliation.

alternative pleading.definition: A form of pleading in which the pleader  sets forth two or more different and possibly inconsistent versions  of his or her claim or defense.

ambiguity.definition: Uncertainty of meaning; doubt. ADJ. ambiguous.

amend.definition: To fix; to improve; to modify or revise a document. N.  amendment.

amends.definition: Reparations; something done to make up for a wrong  done to someone else.

American Bar Association.definition: A national association of lawyers  and law students. ABBRV. ABA.

American Civil Liberties Union.definition: A national organization dedi-  cated to protecting constitutionally guaranteed civil rights and  liberties. ABBRV. ACLU.

American Federation of Labor and Congres.definition: U.S. trade union organization made up of over  fifty national and international trade unions representing over ten  million workers. ABBRV. AFL-CIO.

Americans with Disabilities Act.definition: A federal statute prohibiting  discrimination against disabled people in jobs, transportation, and  services. ABBRV. ADA.

amicus curiae.definition: (Latin) Friend of the court; someone who is not  a party to a lawsuit but who has a strong interest in the subject  matter of a case and petitions the court for permission to file a  brief providing information on the matter to aid the court in  rendering its decision; such a brief is called an amicus curiae brief  or amicus brief.

amnesty.definition: An official pardon granted by a government to a group  of people forgiving them for past crimes, usually political crimes  such as treason or draft evasion.

amortize.definition: To spread out the payment of a debt by periodically  paying a portion of interest and capital; to pay off a debt such as a  mortgage in installments; to write off the cost of an asset over time.  N. amortization.

anarchist.definition: A person who wishes to bring about a state of  anarchy.

anarchy.definition: A political system characterized by an absence of  government and law-enforcement mechanisms and complete  freedom for individuals; a state of lawlessness or disorder resulting  from a lack of governing authority.

ancestor.definition: A person from whom one is descended.

ancient.definition: Old; having existed since the distant past.

ancient writings.definition: In evidence law, old (twenty or thirty years  old) documents kept in official custody, presumed to be genuine  without express proof.

ancillary jurisdiction.definition: Jurisdiction claimed by a federal district  court to adjudicate matters raised as part of an existing  controversy, even though the court would not ordinarily have juris-  diction over the matter. See also supplemental jurisdiction.

ancillary suit.definition: A suit that grows out of an existing or prior suit in  the same court.

annex.definition: To add something to something else; to join; to unite. N.  An addition to a building or document.

annexation.definition: The act of adding or joining one thing to another.

annotate.definition: To add notes to a text explaining it or commenting  on it; often done to court cases and statutes. N. annotation.  ADJ. annotated.

annual report.definition: A yearly financial report prepared by a corpora-  tion for its stockholders, audited by an independent certified  public accountant, and filed with the Securities and Exchange  Commission on Form 10-K, including a balance sheet, income  statement, and other information.

annuity.definition: A fixed sum paid to a person periodically for a set  period of time, often for life.

annul.definition: (1) To declare something invalid; to abolish; to make  nonexistent. (2) To declare that a marriage was never legally valid  and therefore never existed. N. annulment. See also divorce.

answer.definition: (1) A response; a reaction. (2) A pleading submitted by  a defendant in response to a plaintiff’s complaint, containing  denials of allegations, affirmative defenses, and counterclaims. V.  To respond; to file an answer to a complaint with the court.

antenuptial agreement.definition: An agreement entered into by prospec-  tive spouses before their marriage, to take effect upon marriage. It  usually contains provisions about support and distribution of  property in the event of divorce or separation. See also prenuptial  agreement.

anti-ballistic missile.definition: A defensive missile designed to meet and  destroy a ballistic missile, i.e., a missile carrying some nuclear,  chemical, biological, or other agent intended to inflict harm on its  victims. ABBRV. ABM.

anticipate.definition: (1) To do something before its proper time. (2) To  expect, to predict. N. anticipation. ADJ. anticipatory.

anticipatory breach of contract.definition: A breach of contract that  occurs when one party announces before the time scheduled for  performance that he or she will not perform according to the  contract.

anticipatory offense.definition: A crime whose purpose is to commit a  crime, such as conspiracy.

Anticybersquatting Consumer Protection A.definition: A federal law  enacted in 1999 that makes cybersquatting a wrongful act subject  to civil liability.

anti-lapse statute.definition: A law that provides for the heirs of a  devisee to inherit the devisee’s share of an inheritance if the  devisee dies before the testator, instead of allowing the bequest  to lapse as it would have under common law; e.g., if a mother  dies before her father, her children can inherit her share of  their grandfather’s estate.

a posteriori.definition: (Latin) Relating to knowledge gained through  recent observation or experience. See also a priori.

apparent.definition: Obvious; evident; true as far as one can see.

apparent authority.definition: The authority an agent gains when a prin-  cipal allows a third party to believe that the agent has such  authority, whether or not it is part of the agent’s actual authority.

apparent defect.definition: A defect that can readily be seen on inspection.

appeal.definition: To request a higher court to review a case that has been  decided by a lower court and render a new decision, either a  reversal or a new trial. N. appeal.

appeals court.definition: A court that can hear appeals; also called a court  of appeals or an appellate court.

appear.definition: To come into court as a party to a lawsuit and submit to  the court’s jurisdiction. N. appearance.

appearance, general.definition: An ordinary appearance in which the  party is subject to the court’s jurisdiction.

appearance, special.definition: An appearance in which the party appears  to attack the court’s jurisdiction over him or her.

appellate court.definition: A court that reviews decisions made by lower  courts or administrative agencies and does not hear new cases.

appellee.definition: The party against whom an appeal is filed; the  party who prevailed at trial in the lower court. See also respon-  dent.

application.definition: A computer program; software that performs a  particular task, such as a word processor, a spreadsheet, or a Web  browser.

appoint.definition: To select, choose, or designate; to assign someone a job.

appointment.definition: (1) The designation of a person to do a job. (2) A  person appointed to a position. See also power of appointment.

apportion.definition: (1) To divide something and assign or allocate  portions of it to different parties. (2) To distribute legislative seats  among the parties to be represented. N. apportionment.

appraise.definition: To assess the value of something; to evaluate. N.  appraisal.

appreciate.definition: (1) To grow in value. (2) To understand; to realize  fully the implications of something.

appreciation.definition: The increase in an item’s market value over a  period of time; the difference between an item’s cost basis and fair  market value.

appropriate.definition: (1) To take something for one’s own; often used to  describe theft. (2) To allocate money or other items for a particular  purpose. N. appropriation.

appurtenant.definition: Belonging to and necessary to the full use of a  larger property.

a priori.definition: (Latin) From theoretical deduction. See also a  posteriori.

arbiter.definition: A referee; someone appointed by a court to settle a  dispute by the rules of law or equity. See also arbitrator.

arbitrage.definition: A financial transaction in which securities or goods  are simultaneously bought in one market and sold in another; price  differences in the different markets produce a profit.

arbitrary.definition: At whim or at random instead of according to logic  or rules; capricious.

arbitrary and capricious.definition: Describes a decision made  according to whim and without regard to facts or law.

arbitration.definition: A form of dispute resolution in which a neutral third  party renders a decision after both parties speak for themselves at  a hearing. See also arbitrator.

arbitration clause.definition: A clause in a contract that requires disputes  under the contract to be submitted to arbitration; such clauses are  designed to avoid the litigation of disputes.

arbitrator.definition: A neutral person appointed or chosen to settle a  dispute by hearing arguments from both parties and then rendering  a decision at his or her own discretion, not bound by rules of law  or equity. See also arbiter.

archive.definition: A place where old records, articles, documents, or  books are kept; a place on a website or computer where old arti-  cles are stored and can be accessed.

arguendo.definition: (Latin) Arguing; for the sake of argument; hypo-  thetically. ABBRV. arg.

argument.definition: A set of reasons given in logical order intended to  persuade hearers of a particular conclusion; a speech given by an  attorney to the judge or jury in order to present a case and  persuade the listeners to believe it.

ARM.definition: Adjustable rate mortgage. See mortgage, adjustable  rate.

arm’s length.definition: Distant, not intimate; describes a good faith, fair  market transaction by parties with relatively equal bargaining  power, in which neither one forces the other to accept terms.

arraign.definition: In criminal law, to bring a defendant into court, charge  him or her with an offense, and allow him or her to plead.

arraignment.definition: The first step in the criminal process, in which  a defendant is called into court, charged with a crime,  informed of his or her rights, and allowed to plead guilty, not  guilty, or nolo contendere.

arrears.definition: Overdue payments; money owed but not yet paid.

arrest.definition: To use legal authority to deprive someone of liberty; in  criminal law, to stop someone suspected of a crime and take him  or her into custody. N. arrest.

arrest record.definition: The official record of times a person has been  arrested.

arson.definition: The crime of intentionally and maliciously setting prop-  erty on fire.

article.definition: One clause or paragraph of a legal document.

articles.definition: A collection of several clauses or rules that comprise a  statute, contract, or other legal document.

Articles of Confederation.definition: The document that formalized the  agreement between the original thirteen states of the Union. It was  written in 1777, enacted in 1781, and replaced by the Constitution  in 1789.

articles of impeachment.definition: A document outlining reasons for  removing a public official from office.

articles of incorporation.definition: A document that creates a corpora-  tion; sometimes called articles of association.

artifice.definition: A clever or cunning device, often used to commit fraud  or some trickery.

asbestos.definition: A silicate mineral that resists heat and can be woven  into fire-resistant material.

asbestosis.definition: A lung disease caused by inhaling asbestos particles.

Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation.definition: An international economic  treaty organization of nations surrounding the Pacific Ocean.  ABBRV. APEC.

as is.definition: In the present condition; goods sold “as is” are delivered  in the condition in which they were when the buyer inspected them  before purchase.

asportation.definition: Moving goods or a person from one place to  another, as in larceny or kidnapping.

assault.definition: To attack physically; to threaten or attempt to cause  injury to someone else. When contact occurs as well, the offense is  often called assault and battery. N. assault. See also aggravate,  battery, simple.

assault weapon.definition: A weapon used by soldiers in military opera-  tions; for the purpose of gun control laws, any automatic or semi-  automatic weapon that meets certain criteria.

assembly.definition: A group of people gathered together in the same place  for the same purpose; the meeting together of a group of people for  a common purpose.

assembly, right of.definition: A constitutional right allowing citizens to  gather for reasons connected with the government, including  protests and political rallies.

assembly, unlawful.definition: A gathering that results in undesirable  behavior by the group, such as noise or obstruction of roads.

assent.definition: To approve; to ratify; to agree. N. assent.

assess.definition: To evaluate; to determine a value or price for something; to  set a value on property for tax purposes. N. assessment.

asset.definition: Something of value; real or personal property worth  money.

asset, intangible.definition: Assets that have no physical presence, such as  trademarks or goodwill.

assign.definition: (1) To transfer legal rights or property to someone else.  (2) To select or designate. N. assignment.

assigned risk.definition: In automobile insurance, a category of drivers  that insurers would not choose to cover due to their poor driving  records or other risk factors, but that the insurers are required by  statute to insure.

assignee.definition: A person who receives property from another.

assignment of error.definition: A claim by an appellant that the trial judge  made an error at trial.

assignment of income.definition: Assigning income-generating property to  someone else in an effort to avoid receiving the income and thus  income taxes.

assignment of wages.definition: Assigning the right to collect wages to a  creditor of the wage-earner.

assignor.definition: One who transfers property to another.

assigns.definition: People who receive property from an assignor.

assize.definition: A kind of court that formerly heard criminal and civil  cases in England and Wales.

associate.definition: To be connected with or work with someone. N. A  colleague, often a subordinate one; in a law firm, associates are  lawyers who are employees of the firm, as opposed to partners,  who are owners.

association.definition: A group of people joined together for a particular  purpose; the act of joining together for a specific purpose.

assume.definition: (1) To take on a responsibility or power; to receive. (2)  To take on something deceitfully, such as a false name. (3) To  suppose something to be the case without any proof. N. assump-  tion.

assumpsit.definition: (Latin) He promised; a promise to do or pay some-  thing made by one person to another.

assumption of risk.definition: An affirmative defense stating that a plain-  tiff voluntarily risked injury by exposing him- or herself to a known  danger.

assurance.definition: A declaration of certainty; also a British term for  insurance.

assure.definition: To confirm; to make certain; to make someone else sure  of something.

asylum.definition: Refuge or shelter from danger; a safe place for the poor  and unfortunate to find help.

asylum, political.definition: Protection granted by a nation to someone  who has fled his or her homeland for political reasons.

ATF.definition: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.

atrocity.definition: An act of extreme cruelty, brutality, and wickedness.  ADJ. atrocious.

attach.definition: (1) To join or connect. (2) To seize a defendant’s property  before a judgment has been reached at trial as security for any  judgment that the plaintiff might receive. N. attachment.

attempt.definition: The act of trying to do something, usually unsuccess-  fully; in criminal law, an intentional effort to commit a crime that  failed but could have succeeded. V. attempt.

attendant circumstances.definition: The facts and circumstances  surrounding an event.

attest.definition: To declare something to be true; to sign a document as a  witness. N. attestation.

attorney.definition: A lawyer; more generally, an agent appointed to act for  another person.

attorney at law.definition: A lawyer admitted by a court to practice law in  a particular jurisdiction, including drafting legal documents and  representing clients in court.

attorney-client privilege.definition: In evidence law, the right of attorneys  and their clients to withhold information about confidential  communications made in the course of their professional relation-  ship.

attorney general.definition: An attorney who serves as the head of the  Department of Justice and chief legal adviser to the president  and who represents the United States in legal matters; each state  also has its own attorney general who performs the same func-  tions at the state level.

attorney’s fees.definition: The fees charged by a lawyer for services  rendered to clients. See also contingent.

attractive nuisance.definition: A doctrine in tort law that a person who keeps something on his or her property that is likely both to attract children and be a danger to them (such as a swimming pool) is under a duty to protect the children from the dangers (as by fencing in the pool).

auction.definition: A public sale of goods or property to the highest bidder.  V. auction.

auctioneer.definition: A person licensed to conduct auctions, usually  employed by the seller to act as the seller’s agent.

audit.definition: A systematic review of an organization’s or an individual’s  accounts. V. audit.

auditor.definition: A person who conducts an audit.

authenticate.definition: To prove that something is genuine or true; to  certify; to give something legal authority so as to allow it to be  admitted as evidence. N. authentication.

authorities.definition: Sources used in composing a legal document, such  as statutes, cases, restatements, and articles.

authorities, table of.definition: A list of citations to all the legal sources  used in a brief or other legal document.

authority.definition: (1) Power; the right to tell others how to act or to  make others obey; rights or powers delegated by one person or  body to another. (2) An expert.

authorize.definition: To approve; to give permission; to give someone legal  authority. N. authorization.

authorized stock issue.definition: The number of shares of stock a corpo-  ration is allowed to sell under its charter.

autopsy.definition: The dissection and physical examination of a dead  body to determine the cause of death.

aver.definition: To assert or state; to declare.

averment.definition: In a pleading, a positive assertion of fact.

avoid.definition: (1) To escape or evade. (2) To nullify; to make void; to  cancel; to destroy. N. avoidance.

avulsion.definition: The sudden removal of a large piece of land from one  property and its deposit onto another, as when a river changes its  course abruptly. See also accretion.

award.definition: To grant something; to give something as a prize or  compensation. N. Money or another object given as a grant or  compensation; also the decision rendered by a nonjudicial decider  such as an arbitrator.

back.definition: (1) To support or be in favor of. (2) To support financially.  (3) To endorse or countersign. ADJ. From the past; to the rear of.

background check.definition: An investigation performed on a person’s  history and background that can include criminal records, bank-  ruptcies, liens, civil court judgments, previous and current  addresses, property ownership, and other information.

back pay.definition: The difference between the wages received for a  period of work and retroactive higher wages granted by a court.

back wages.definition: Wages earned in the past but not yet paid.

bad check.definition: A check written on a closed account or on an  account with insufficient funds to pay it or on a closed account. See  also check kiting.

bad debt.definition: An uncollectible debt; a debt owed by an insolvent  debtor.

bad debt reserve.definition: An account used to estimate debts that ulti-  mately will not be paid and will thus eventually be deducted for tax  purposes.

bad faith.definition: Deceit; intent to defraud; dishonesty in dealing with  someone.

badges of fraud.definition: A fact or circumstance that makes it appear as  if a person intends to defraud someone else in a transaction and  justifies an inference of fraud; examples include transferring funds  in anticipation of a lawsuit, transferring all of a debtor’s property to  someone else, a confidential relationship between the parties, and  fake consideration.

bad title.definition: A title that does not convey any property to a  purchaser.

bail bond.definition: A contract between a prisoner, the state, and a third  party known as a bail bondsman, in which the bail bondsman  agrees to furnish bail for the prisoner in return for a fee and takes  the risk that the prisoner will not return for trial. See also bond,  bondsman.

bailee.definition: A person who holds goods or property for someone else  for a specific purpose, such as a mechanic keeping a car for  repairs.

bailiff.definition: (1) A court officer who keeps order and looks after jurors  and prisoners. (2) An agent or steward who is responsible for prop-  erty or goods.

bailment.definition: The delivery of goods to a bailee to be held in trust for  a specific purpose, such as repairs, often formalized with a  contract.

bailment for hire.definition: A bailment in which the owner of the goods  agrees to compensate the bailee.

bailor.definition: One who delivers personal property or goods to a bailee.  See also bailment.

bailout.definition: Financial assistance to an ailing business to save it from  failure. v. bail out.

bait and switch.definition: A kind of deceptive advertising in which a merchant advertises a low-priced product to lure customers and then disparages that product or fails to have it in stock in order to persuade them to buy a more expensive item; generally prohibited by statute.

balance.definition: (1) To compare the difference between debits and  credits in an account. (2) To compare the value of one thing with  another. (3) To distribute weights or values to create harmony or  equality. N. balance.

balance sheet.definition: A financial statement that includes debits,  credits, assets, and liabilities.

balancing test.definition: A test used by courts to weigh the rights of an  individual against the rights of the state; common in cases  involving freedom of speech or equal protection.

balloon mortgage.definition: A mortgage in which the buyer makes regular  payments at intervals and then must pay the rest of the balance at  the end of the mortgage period.

balloon note.definition: A promissory note in which the interest is paid in  periodic installments and then the remainder of the principal is  paid in one lump sum at the end of the term.

balloon payment.definition: The large payment due at the end of the term  of a balloon note.

ballot.definition: (1) A piece of paper or other object used by a voter to cast  a vote in an election. (2) The list of candidates or issues being voted  on.

ballot, secret.definition: A kind of voting in which a voter’s choices cannot  be identified as coming from him or her.

ballot box.definition: A sealed box in which voters deposit ballots.

bank.definition: A financial institution that holds money for customers,  invests it to earn interest, lends money at interest, issues promis-  sory notes, handles trusts, deals in negotiable securities, and  performs other financial services.

bank, commercial.definition: The most common kind of bank, which  provides a full range of banking services. See also savings and  loan association.

bank account.definition: A sum placed in a bank by a customer that is  available to be withdrawn upon the customer’s request.

bank draft.definition: An order for payment of money drawn upon a bank  by a bank officer, payable when the issuing bank accepts it.

bankrupt.definition: Insolvent and unable to pay debts as they come due.

bankruptcy.definition: A process in which a court declares a person or  business insolvent and orders the debtor’s assets to be sold to pay  off creditors, at which point the debtor is discharged from any  further obligation and may begin anew.

bankruptcy, Chapter 7.definition: Straight bankruptcy; a proceeding that  liquidates property, pays off debts, and leaves the debtor  discharged.

bankruptcy, Chapter 11.definition: Business reorganization in which a  court supervises an insolvent business while it continues to  operate and comes up with a plan for reorganization.

bankruptcy, Chapter 12.definition: A proceeding to allow for family  farmers and fisherman to create a repayment plan to pay off debts  over a specified period of time while being supervised by the court.

bankruptcy, Chapter 13.definition: A proceeding that lets a borrower with  enough income pay off bills over a specified period of time while  being supervised by the court.

bankruptcy court.definition: A federal court that handles only bankruptcy  cases, presided over by a bankruptcy judge.

bankruptcy trustee.definition: A person appointed by the court to handle a  debtor’s property during bankruptcy proceedings.

banner ad.definition: A small advertisement that appears on a website,  linking that website to the advertiser’s own website; the advertiser  pays the owner of the original website for every hit it receives  through the banner ad.

bar.definition: (1) The court; a particular place in the courtroom. (2) A body  of attorneys; such bodies are organized at the local, state, or  national level. See also bench. (3) An obstacle; something that  prevents an issue from being litigated. V. To prevent or forbid.

bargain.definition: An agreement between two parties to transfer goods or  property or to perform some act or service in exchange for consid-  eration. V. To negotiate the terms of a transaction or agreement.

bargain and sale.definition: A contract commonly used in real estate to  transfer title and use of property from buyer to seller; does not  include warranties against liens or encumbrances.

bargaining unit.definition: A group, such as a labor union, that represents  employees during collective bargaining.

barratry.definition: The offense of inciting lawsuits or quarrels.

barrister.definition: In the United Kingdom, a lawyer who conducts trials.  See also solicitor.

basis.definition: A taxpayer’s cost in acquiring an asset.

basis, adjusted.definition: Acquisition cost plus capital improvements  minus depreciation.

basis, carryover.definition: The basis of property acquired from a dece-  dent, valued at the basis of the property just before the decedent’s  death.

basis, stepped-up.definition: A basis that is increased to a certain level at  a particular date, usually in property transferred through inheri-  tance.

bastard.definition: A child born to a mother and father not married to one  another.

BATFE.definition: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and  Explosives.

battery.definition: A wrongful and intentional physical touching that  causes offense and possibly, though not necessarily, injury. See also  assault.

bearer.definition: One who possesses a document, instrument, or security.

bearer paper.definition: Commercial paper, such as a check, payable to  whoever has it; often written as “payable to bearer” or to “cash.”

bed (and board).definition: The right of a married couple to live together;  (Latin) a mensa et thoro. See also divorce.

belief.definition: A conviction that something is true; something that one  thinks or supposes after examining evidence and information. V.  believe.

bench.definition: (1) A court; the seat a judge occupies in a courtroom; the  office of judge. (2) The collective body of judges. See also bar.

bench trial.definition: A trial conducted by a judge without a jury. See also  jury trial.

bench warrant.definition: An order issued by a court for the arrest of a  person, often used to make that person appear in court.

beneficial.definition: Profitable; advantageous; often used to describe  entitlements enjoyed by the beneficiary of a trust who does not  hold legal title to a property but is allowed to use it.

beneficial interest.definition: In trusts, the benefit or profit from the use of  property enjoyed by a beneficiary who has an equitable interest in  it, as opposed to the legal title held by a trustee.

beneficial owner.definition: Someone who has equitable title of property  but not legal title.

beneficial use.definition: A right to use and enjoy property without  holding legal title to it.

beneficiary.definition: Someone who benefits from someone else’s act,  such as a person for whom property is held in trust, the recipient  of the proceeds of an insurance policy, or someone named in a will  as a recipient of property.

benefit, fringe.definition: Something given to an employee by an employer  as part of compensation in addition to salary, such as health insur-  ance; also called benefits.

bequeath.definition: To leave a gift of personal property to someone by a  will. See also bequest, devise.

bequest.definition: A gift of personal property made in a will; a legacy.

bequest, conditional.definition: A bequest that takes place when specified  conditions are met.

bequest, residuary.definition: A bequest of all items remaining in an estate  after specified legacies and debts are paid.

bequest, specific.definition: A bequest of a specific item or items.

Berne Convention.definition: An international agreement that establishes  rules for copyrighting artistic works and regulating international  copyright matters.

best evidence rule.definition: A rule requiring that the best and most reli-  able evidence be presented at trial instead of less reliable evidence;  thus, if the original version of a document or photograph is avail-  able, it should be presented instead of a copy.

bestiality.definition: Sexual activity between a human and an animal.

Better Business Bureau.definition: A private, nonprofit organization that  assists consumers and businesses with marketplace issues such as  advertising and selling practices.

beyond a reasonable doubt.definition: In a criminal case, proven by  evidence to the point that a reasonable man or woman would be  entirely convinced and morally certain that the defendant is guilty.

bias.definition: A preconceived opinion or prejudice; a condition that  renders someone unable to judge a matter impartially. V. To  influence in such a way as to render someone else’s judgment  prejudiced.

bid.definition: An offer to buy or sell goods or services at a stated price,  common at auctions or among builders or laborers seeking  contracts. V. bid.

bid, best.definition: The bid that best fits the needs of the one soliciting  bids.

bid, sealed.definition: A bid submitted under seal, not to be opened until  a stated time when all bids for the same thing are opened and  compared simultaneously; common in construction.

bid shopping.definition: A prime contractor’s disclosure of current low  bids to pressure subcontractors to submit even lower bids.

bigamy.definition: Knowingly being married to more than one spouse at  once.

bigot.definition: A person who is prejudiced against people who live differ-  ently or who hold different beliefs from him or her.

bigotry.definition: A sense of superiority over and intolerance of lifestyles  and ideas that are different from a person’s own.

bill.definition: (1) A statement of goods purchased or services rendered  and moneys owed for them. (2) A piece of paper currency. (3) A  draft of a proposed law introduced into the legislature for debate  and voting. (4) A pleading submitted by a plaintiff to an equity court  stating grounds for a trial. V. To issue a statement requesting  payment of moneys owed for goods or services.

bill of attainder.definition: A legislative act that inflicts capital punishment  on someone who has been accused of a serious offense but has not  been convicted at trial; prohibited by the U.S. Constitution.

bill of lading.definition: A receipt given by a carrier to someone who  entrusts goods to the carrier for shipment, serving as a contract  between shipper and carrier and giving its holder title to the goods  held by the carrier.

bill of lading order.definition: A negotiable bill of lading that can be sold,  and that causes title to the goods to vest in its holder, who can  collect the goods by presenting the bill of lading to the carrier.

bill of particulars.definition: In criminal law, a written statement by the  prosecution describing in detail its allegations against the defen-  dant and informing the court and the defendant what the prosecu-  tion’s case will be.

bill of rights.definition: A statement of fundamental rights of citizenship;  the first ten amendments of the U.S. Constitution.

bill of sale.definition: A written agreement that transfers ownership of  personal property from one person to another.

bind.definition: To obligate; to place legal duties upon someone. ADJ.  binding.

binder.definition: (1) A written agreement in which an insurer promises to  cover a prospective insured between the time of application for a  policy and when the policy is actually issued. (2) A written docu-  ment summarizing the key terms of a preliminary contract.

bind over.definition: To order a defendant to remain in custody or provide  bail pending trial.

Biological Weapons Convention.definition: An international treaty,  effective in 1975, that requires its signatories to prohibit the  creation and stockpiling of biological weapons such as  disease-causing microbes.

bipartisan.definition: Containing representatives from both of two sides  or parties.

blackacre.definition: The name of a fictitious piece of property used as an  example in teaching and discussing property law; sometimes the  term “whiteacre” is used as well.

black letter law.definition: An informal term for basic principles of law.  See also hornbook law.

blacklist.definition: A list of people to be shunned, usually due to noncon-  formity or untrustworthiness. V. blacklist.

blackmail.definition: Demanding money in exchange for performing a  duty or under threat of revealing injurious information or  causing injury to one’s person or property; extortion. V. black-  mail.

blank endorsement.definition: Endorsing a check or promissory note by  signing it without writing in a recipient’s name, leaving a space for  insertion of a recipient’s name.

blasphemy.definition: Irreverent or profane speech or writing about reli-  gion or sacred things. V. blaspheme. ADJ. blasphemous.

blog.definition: Web log; a website typically produced by an individual that  contains entries on particular topics, links to other websites, and  sometimes comments from readers.

board.definition: A group of people who manage a business or public  office.

board of directors.definition: The governing body of a corporation that is  elected by shareholders and that sets company policy and  appoints officers.

board of education.definition: A local or state agency that manages munic-  ipal or local schools.

board of health.definition: A municipal or state committee dedicated to  improving public health.

boilerplate.definition: Standardized text used in legal documents; text  always written identically in the same kind of document.

bona fide.definition: In good faith; genuine; not intending to deceive.

bona fide occupational qualification.definition: A qualification that is  truly necessary for performing a job, used to judge matters of  employment discrimination. ABBRV. BFOQ.

bona fide purchaser.definition: Someone who buys property in good faith  for valuable consideration and has no reason to believe anyone  else has rights to the property.

bond.definition: Written evidence of a debt issued by a company or govern-  ment in which the issuing body agrees to pay a fixed rate of interest  during the period of the loan and to repay the principal at a speci-  fied date, called maturity.

bond, bearer.definition: A negotiable instrument payable to its holder.

bond, coupon.definition: A bond sold with a book of coupons that may be  clipped when they come due and redeemed for interest.

bond, registered.definition: A bond registered in the name of the holder in  the issuing body’s books.

bond, surety.definition: A bond issued by a surety promising to perform an  obligation if the person who is supposed to perform it defaults.

bond issue.definition: Raising funds by offering bonds to investors; the  bonds issued for that purpose.

bondsman.definition: Someone who serves as surety for a bond. See also  bail bond.

bonus stock.definition: Common stock offered as an incentive with the  purchase of another kind of securities, such as preferred stock or  bonds.

book.definition: A document containing the accounts or records of a busi-  ness. V. To perform administrative tasks such as recording the  name and alleged crime of someone who has been arrested and  brought to the police station. N. booking.

book value.definition: An asset’s official value, usually cost minus depreci-  ation.

books, corporate.definition: A corporation’s written records of trans-  actions.

boot.definition: Taxable cash or property exchanged during an otherwise  nontaxable exchange; cash or property given by one party to equal  the value of items exchanged.

borrow.definition: To take money or property from someone else and use  it with the intent to return it. See also loan.

bound.definition: Obligated to by contract or moral constraint. N. The  edge of a piece of property; the limit or boundary. See also  metes and bounds.

brain death.definition: The point at which the brain stops functioning  irreversibly; this point is ambiguous, but some criteria for  assessing it include lack of response to stimulation, absence of  reflexes, cessation of breathing, and no brain activity for twenty-  four hours.

breach.definition: To break a promise; to fail to perform a duty or observe  an agreement. N. breach.

breach, constructive.definition: A breach occurring when one party does  something to prevent his or her fulfilling contractual duties or  announces beforehand that he or she will not fulfill contractual  duties. See also anticipatory breach of contract.

breach, material.definition: A serious breach of a contract that essentially  destroys the value of the contract, excuses the nonbreaching party  from further performance, and allows suit for breach of contract.

breach, partial.definition: A failure to perform some of the duties of a  contract, which gives the nonbreaching party the right to collect  damages but does not excuse his or her performance of contrac-  tual duties.

breach of contract.definition: Failure to perform acts promised in a  contract.

breach of duty.definition: Failure to perform legal or moral duties, or to use  the care that a reasonable man or woman would use in given  circumstances.

breach of trust.definition: A trustee’s failure to properly perform duties  required by the trust.

breaking.definition: Forcibly opening a door, window, or gaining other  access to a building; the force required can be very slight, as in  opening a closed and unlocked door.

breaking and entering.definition: Forcibly opening a building and then  entering it; two elements of the crime of burglary.

breathalyzer.definition: A device used to check the level of alcohol in a  person’s breath; breathalyzer test results are admissible as  evidence.

brethren.definition: Plural of brother, i.e., brothers; when used in a will,  also includes sisters; often used by Supreme Court justices to refer  to their fellow justices.

bribe.definition: To give someone money or a gift to induce or influence  him or her to act in a particular way, usually an improper or illegal  way. N. Money or a gift given to influence someone.

bribery.definition: The act of offering a bribe.

brief.definition: (1) A written document presented to the court and to the  opposing counsel by a lawyer that describes the facts of a case,  questions of law, and legal arguments in support of his or her  client’s position. (2) A summary or abstract of a case. V. (1) To  write a summary of a case. (2) To inform someone of the details  of something.

broker.definition: A person who brings parties together to negotiate trans-  actions between them in return for a commission or other compen-  sation; a middleman. V. To negotiate a deal between parties.

broker, real estate.definition: A person who negotiates transactions  involving the buying and selling of land or property.

Brown v. Board of Education.definition: A 1954 U.S. Supreme Court case that declared that "separate but equal" schools for black and white students were inherently unequal and thus violated the equal protection clause of the Fourteenth Amendment of the U.S. Constitution; this case overturned Plessy v. Ferguson.

browse.definition: To move from website to website on the Internet by  clicking from one to another; to surf the Web.

browser.definition: A computer program that allows a user to view and use  websites.

browse-wrap agreement.definition: A contract stating that a user agrees to  a website’s terms and conditions. It is created when the user  browses the website, without the user actively agreeing to the  terms and conditions. See also click-wrap agreement, shrink-wrap  agreement.

bug.definition: A tiny hidden microphone used to secretly monitor conver-  sations. V. To record conversations using a hidden microphone.

buggery.definition: Anal intercourse; sometimes includes bestiality. See  also sodomy.

bugging.definition: Electronic surveillance by using bugs to eavesdrop on  and record conversations, regulated by statute. See also wire-  tapping.

building code.definition: The state and local laws and regulations  governing the construction, maintenance, and operation of  dwellings.

bulk sale.definition: A transfer or sale of the majority of goods and  supplies owned by a business in one transaction; often done as part  of the liquidation of a business.

bulk sale acts.definition: Statutes that require a business planning a bulk  sale to first notify creditors, designed to protect creditors from  secret bulk sales.

bulk transfer.definition: A form of commercial fraud in which a business  sells most of its stock and then fails to pay creditors.

bundling.definition: The practice of combining two or more products and  selling them as one item.

burden.definition: (1) A duty; something oppressive. (2) A restriction on  the use of land.

burden of persuasion.definition: The requirement that the party with the  burden of proof convince the judge or jury of the validity of his or  her case.

burden of proof.definition: A party’s duty to furnish sufficient evidence to  sustain an allegation.

bureaucracy.definition: A form of government or organization with a hier-  archical structure of officials, authority delegated from top to  bottom, well-defined positions within the structures, inflexible  rules, and usually a fairly complicated administrative system.

Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and.definition: A  federal agency within the U.S. Department of Justice that handles  gun licensing and the investigation of traffic and crimes involving  alcohol, tobacco, firearms, ammunition, explosives, arson, and  bombings. ABBRV. ATF, BATFE.

Bureau of Labor Statistics.definition: An agency of the U.S. Department of  Labor dedicated to assembling information and statistics about the  U.S. workforce. ABBRV. BLS.

burglary.definition: Under common law, breaking into and entering a dwelling at night intending to commit a felony once inside; under modern statutes, the definition has expanded to include all buildings, not just dwellings, and to require that the actor must merely enter the building instead of breaking into it, and must intend to commit any crime, not just a felony; the crime involved is usually theft.

business.definition: An occupation or commercial activity done to earn  money; making money by engaging in commerce.

business expense.definition: An expense incurred in the process of  running a business to generate income.

business gain or loss.definition: Gain or loss resulting from sales or  exchanges done in the course of business.

business judgment rule.definition: A rule that exempts corporate execu-  tives from liability if there is reason to believe that they made the  decision in question in good faith, with due care, and thought they  were acting in the best interest of the corporation; their judgment  may not be second-guessed.

business purpose.definition: Something done specifically for business as  opposed to personal reasons.

but for test.definition: In tort law, a test used to decide whether an indi-  vidual is responsible for something; the court asks whether the  event would have occurred without, or “but for,” the defendant’s  action; not commonly used today because of the difficulty in  applying it.

buyer in ordinary course of business.definition: An ordinary buyer;  someone who buys something in good faith from someone in the  business of selling that particular item and without any knowledge  of a third party’s interest in the goods.

bylaws.definition: The internal rules and regulations made by a business or  organization to govern its internal workings.

cabinet.definition: The U.S. president’s board of advisors, composed of the  heads of the executive departments, the vice president, and occa-  sionally other officials; in other countries, a group of senior offi-  cials who advise the king or chief executive, or who control the  government themselves.

cafeteria plan.definition: A fringe-benefit plan that allows employees to  choose from a range of benefits, selecting them according to indi-  vidual needs.

calendar.definition: A chart describing the organization of days, months,  and years.

calendar, court.definition: The list of cases scheduled to be heard during a  court session.

calendar call.definition: A court session devoted to checking the status of  pending cases and setting trial dates.

call.definition: (1) In property law, a natural object that marks a boundary  and serves as a landmark in a deed. (2) A demand for payment. V.  (1) To demand payment. (2) To redeem bonds before maturity.

callable.definition: Able to be paid off before maturity.

callable bond.definition: A bond that may be called for payment before it  matures.

call option.definition: A contract giving its holder the right to purchase  stock at a specific price by a specific date.

calumny.definition: Slander or defamation; making false statements to  harm someone’s reputation; a slanderous statement.

campaign finance.definition: Money raised to pay for politicians’ election  campaigns.

cancel.definition: To annul; to revoke; to announce that something previ-  ously planned will not take place. N. cancellation.

canceled check.definition: A check that has been paid and charged to the  person who wrote it, and is marked in some way to indicate that  fact.

canon.definition: A rule or standard; a body of rules, particularly for  governing the conduct of a kind of professional; a rule suggesting  appropriate conduct for legal professionals.

canon law.definition: Roman Catholic law, created by popes and cardinals.

CAN-SPAM Act.definition: A law passed in 2003 that made certain commer-  cial emails, often called “spam,” illegal and empowered the Federal  Trade Commission to take action to prevent spam.
