capacity.definition: Legal competence; having the age and mental capacity  to understand the consequences of one’s actions and to make  rational decisions.

capacity, criminal.definition: The age and mental ability necessary to  intentionally commit a crime.

capacity, testamentary.definition: The mental ability necessary to write a  proper will.

capacity to contract.definition: The age and mental acuity necessary to  enter a binding contract.

capias.definition: A writ issued by the court demanding that a named  person be taken into custody.

capital.definition: Wealth in the form of money and assets; all the assets  owned by a business.

capital account.definition: An account that records a business’s capital  assets, expenses, and liabilities.

capital expenditure.definition: Money spent to acquire or improve a  capital asset.

capital gain or loss.definition: The profit or loss realized when a capital  asset is sold.

capital intensive.definition: Requiring a large amount of equipment for  production.

capitalization.definition: All stock, bonds, and other securities issued by a  corporation.

capitalize.definition: To convert present or expected income into an asset;  in business accounting, to record an expense (such as repairs to a  building) as a capital asset because it will produce long-term bene-  fits, thus allowing the expense to be written off over a period of  time.

capital offense.definition: An offense for which execution is a possible  punishment; also called a capital crime.

capital punishment.definition: The death penalty; punishment by death.

caption.definition: The heading of a legal document such as a brief, motion,  or pleading, containing the names of the parties, the court, the  action, the docket number, and any other required information.

carbon credit.definition: A tradable permit giving its holder the right to  emit a specified amount of carbon dioxide.

care.definition: (1) Attention, concern, or caution. (2) Custody. (3) Duty of  care; an obligation to conduct oneself in such a way as to avoid or  prevent injury to another.

care, great.definition: The care exercised by a reasonable person in very  important matters, or the care used by an extremely prudent  person in a similar situation.

care, ordinary.definition: The care a reasonably careful person would use  in a similar situation. See also reasonable care, due care.

care, slight.definition: The care a reasonable person exercises in unimpor-  tant matters, or the care used by a careless person in a similar situ-  ation.

carnal knowledge.definition: Sexual intercourse; penetration of a female’s  genitals by a male’s.

carrier.definition: A person or business that transports people or goods for  a fee.

carrier, common.definition: A carrier who makes transportation available  to the public, and who will transport any person or the goods of any  person who pays for the passage, as long as there is room available  and no legal reason to refuse.

carrier, private.definition: A carrier who transports people or goods by  contract and only in particular cases.

carryback.definition: In taxation, the process of applying a deduction or  credit from one year to the tax liability of a previous year.

carryover.definition: A deduction or credit from one year to the tax liability  of a future year.

carry over.definition: To extend past a stated deadline or boundary; in  the case of health savings accounts, to transfer from one fiscal  year into the next; in the case of taxes, it is the process of  applying a deduction or credit from one year to the tax liability  of a future year.

carte blanche.definition: Complete freedom to act as one chooses; unlim-  ited discretionary power.

cartel.definition: A coalition of independent producers, usually industrial  corporations, who band together to set prices and restrict compe-  tition. See also monopoly.

case.definition: (1) A legal action or lawsuit to be decided in a court of law  or equity. (2) The legal arguments and evidence used by one side of  a lawsuit to support its position.

casebook.definition: A textbook used in law school to teach a particular  type of law, containing court opinions from cases in that subject  and commentary by experts in the field.

case law.definition: The body of law derived from examination of previ-  ously judged cases, including their treatment of a subject and inter-  pretation of legislation. See also common law, civil law.

case system.definition: A method of teaching law by having law students  read and analyze cases.

cash.definition: Ready money, i.e., coins and bank notes. Sometimes  includes negotiable checks and bank accounts. See also credit.

cash flow.definition: The total money generated; the cash remaining when  all expenses are paid.

cash value.definition: Market value, the price an item would bring if sold  on the open market.

castle doctrine.definition: The principle that a person’s home is meant to  be safe from attack and that the person can therefore defend his or  her home with a degree of force that would not be legal in any  other setting. See also duty to retreat.

casualty.definition: In insurance, an accident; something destroyed or  badly damaged; a person killed. See loss, accident.

casus belli.definition: (Latin). Case of war; a reason to go to war.

causation.definition: Causing something; a cause; the connection between  cause and effect.

cause.definition: To make something happen. N. (1) That which makes  something happen. (2) A legal case; see also cause of action.

cause, direct.definition: The action that sets in motion a sequence of  events leading directly to a result, without any intervening actions  to assist or interfere with the chain of events.

cause, intervening.definition: Something that happens between the action  that sets in motion a series of events and the ultimate result.

cause, proximate.definition: A cause that directly produces a result, and  without which the result would not have happened.

cause, superseding.definition: An intervening cause that substantially  affects the ultimate outcome of the series of events and breaks the  chain of causation between the direct cause and the effect.

cause of action.definition: A set of facts that creates a valid legal claim that  can be grounds for a lawsuit.

cautionary instruction.definition: A judge’s instruction to the jury to  consider specific pieces of evidence only for instructed purposes,  not to allow extraneous information to influence their judgment,  not to speak about the case to anyone outside the courtroom, and  other matters.

caveat emptor.definition: (Latin) Let the buyer beware; the principle that  the buyer is responsible for examining merchandise and judging its  quality before buying it.

CDC.definition: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

cease and desist order.definition: An order by a court, agency, or judicial  body telling someone to stop doing a particular activity, usually  because the activity in question is illegal.

censor.definition: A person who examines published media, including print  and film, in search of objectionable or offensive content. V. To  examine published media looking for offensive content and  suppressing it when found.

censure.definition: A formal reprimand or expression of disapproval. V.  censure.

Centers for Disease Control and Preventi.definition: An agency within  the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services that works to  promote public health. ABBRV. CDC.

certificate.definition: An official document confirming a particular fact.

certificate, birth.definition: A certificate stating the date and place of a  person’s birth.

certificate, death.definition: A certificate stating the date and place of a  person’s death.

certificate, stock.definition: A certificate issued by a corporation to a  shareholder indicating that the shareholder owns a certain number  of shares of the corporation’s stock.

certificate of deposit.definition: A document issued by a bank to a  customer indicating that it has received a sum of money from the  customer and will repay it with interest at a specific date.

certificate of incorporation.definition: A document issued by a secretary  of state approving a company’s articles of incorporation and indi-  cating that the corporation exists.

certificate of occupancy.definition: A document issued by a local govern-  ment confirming that a building complies with local building codes.

certified check.definition: A check drawn on a bank account that has been  certified in writing by a bank official, thereby making the bank  responsible for it.

certify.definition: To confirm formally; to authenticate in writing. N. certi-  fication.

certiorari.definition: A writ issued by an appellate court to a lower court  requesting the official record of a decision made by the lower court  so that the appellate court can review it for errors.

cestui que trust.definition: The person who has a beneficial interest in a  trust; the beneficiary.

cestui que use.definition: The person who has a right to use property held  by another.

cestui que vie.definition: The person whose life determines the duration  of a trust or insurance contract.

chain of title.definition: The history of ownership of a property, listed from  the original owner to the present one.

challenge.definition: To object; to dispute the truth of a statement. N. (1)  An objection. (2) An objection by a lawyer to the inclusion on the  jury of a juror proposed by opposing counsel.

challenge, peremptory.definition: An objection to including a juror for no  particular reason; each party is permitted a certain number of  peremptory challenges.

challenge for cause.definition: An objection to a juror based on a specific  reason, which the judge must approve.

chamber.definition: A hall or room in which a judicial or legislative body  meets.

chancellor.definition: (1) A senior officer, especially at a university. (2) The  judge of a court of chancery. (3) In some European countries, the  head of the government.

chancery.definition: Equity, or equitable jurisdiction. Court of chancery. A  court of equity.

change.definition: To alter or modify; to substitute one thing for another,  often used in reference to money. N. change.

character.definition: A person’s moral qualities; reputation; disposition.

character evidence.definition: Evidence about a person’s reputation in  society.

character witness.definition: A witness who attests to a person’s reputa-  tion and moral qualities.

charge.definition: (1) To accuse someone of an offense. (2) To entrust  someone with a duty. (3) To request money for something  purchased or provided. (4) To issue instructions to a jury as to how  they should analyze the facts and arguments presented at trial. N.  charge.

charitable.definition: Done out of benevolence or a desire to help those  in need.

charitable contribution.definition: Donations of money or other valuable  items to charitable organizations; often tax-deductible.

charitable organization.definition: Under the Internal Revenue Code, a  corporation or business devoted to specific charitable purposes  and not operated for profit or political influence.

charitable trust.definition: A trust whose property must be used for chari-  table purposes.

charity.definition: (1) An organization whose focus is on charitable work  and public benevolence. (2) The donation of money or help to  those in need.

charity, public.definition: A charity that receives most of its support from  the public.

charter.definition: A document granted by the state permitting the creation  of a corporation, city, or university and defining its rights and priv-  ileges. See also articles of incorporation. V. To hire an airplane,  boat, or other such vehicle for private use.

chattel.definition: A possession; movable or personal property, as opposed  to land or real property; something that can be owned.

chattel mortgage.definition: A mortgage placed on personal property to  secure a debt; chattel mortgages have been mostly replaced by the  Uniform Commercial Code’s security arrangements.

chattel paper.definition: A document that records a debt and a security  interest or lease of specific goods.

check.definition: Examine; verify. N. A document indicating that the  amount of money noted on it is to be drawn from the writer’s bank  account when it is presented to the bank.

check, personal.definition: A check written on an account holder’s  personal account.

check, traveler’s.definition: A check purchased from a bank that functions  as cash but is protected against loss or theft. See also certified  check.

check kiting.definition: A scam in which an individual deposits worthless  checks in different banks and uses his or her good credit to with-  draw funds before the checks clear, attempting to deposit the  money into the account on which the check is drawn before the  discrepancy is discovered. See also bad check.

Chemical Weapons Convention.definition: An international agreement  effective in 1997 that prohibits its signatories from creating and  stockpiling chemical weapons such as nerve gases.

chief.definition: Most important; highest ranking. N. Leader or head.

chief justice.definition: The judge who presides over a court with more  than one judge.

child.definition: (1) Offspring, a son or daughter. (2) A person under the  age of majority; a minor. PL. children.

child abuse.definition: Physical, sexual, or mental mistreatment of a child.

child labor laws.definition: Laws regulating the hours, conditions, and  nature of work that may be performed by children.

Children’s Internet Protection Act.definition: A federal law enacted in 2000 that requires schools and libraries that receive federal funding to implement safeguards to prevent minors from viewing inappropriate content on the Internet. ABBRV. CIPA.

child support.definition: Financial contributions that a parent must make  toward the upbringing of a child, usually paid by the parent who  does not keep custody of the child after a divorce.

chilling effect.definition: Limits on constitutional rights, such as free  speech, created by fear of repercussions rather than outright  government prohibition.

Chinese wall.definition: A policy used by securities firms that prevents all  departments from accessing nonpublic information in order to  prevent insider trading and self-dealing.

choate lien.definition: A lien that has been perfected and can be enforced.

chose in action.definition: A claim that can be brought as a lawsuit.

chose in possession.definition: Something that is owned or can be owned.

churning.definition: Excessive and unnecessary trades of stock by a  broker in order to increase his or her commission.

C.I.F.definition: Cost, insurance, and freight. Also called C.F.I.

circuit.definition: A judicial division of a state or the country, usually  covering several counties or districts.

circuit court.definition: A court that hears cases from within a judicial  circuit, so-called because its judges traveled a circuit within the  region, hearing cases in different places. See also district court.

circumstantial evidence.definition: Evidence drawn from inference or  deduction; secondary evidence. See also direct evidence, indirect  evidence.

citation.definition: (1) A reference in a legal document or argument to a  legal authority such as a precedent or statute. (2) A summons  issued by a court ordering its recipient to appear in court at a spec-  ified date and time. V. cite.

citizen.definition: A legally recognized member of a nation or state, who  owes allegiance to the government and in return receives the  government’s protection.

citizenship.definition: The status of a citizen. See also naturalize.

civil.definition: Having to do with the concerns of citizens and the state;  the branch of law that handles private matters as opposed to crim-  inal ones.

civil action.definition: A lawsuit brought by a private citizen to protect a  private or civil right or to seek a civil remedy; a noncriminal action.

civil court.definition: A court handling civil actions, i.e., noncriminal  matters.

civil disobedience.definition: Deliberately refusing to obey a law as a way  of protesting its unfairness.

civilian.definition: A person not in the military; a private citizen.

civil law.definition: (1) Law concerned with citizens and private matters. (2) The body of jurisprudence created by a nation or state, as opposed to natural law or international law. (3) A system of jurisprudence practiced in Europe and in Louisiana, based on the codes of ancient Roman law as opposed to the precedents that form the authority of common law.

civil liberties.definition: Personal rights and immunities from government  oppression established and guaranteed by the Constitution,  including freedom of speech and freedom of association;  natural liberties that cannot be limited by the government. See  also civil rights.

civil procedure.definition: The laws governing procedure and practice  used in civil litigation.

civil rights.definition: The rights of all citizens to personal liberties,  freedom, and equality; rights specifically granted through laws  enacted by communities, as opposed to civil liberties, which are  rights that the government is not allowed to restrict.

Civil Rights Acts.definition: Federal laws enacted to protect personal  rights guaranteed by the Constitution, particularly freedom from  discrimination.

civil service.definition: The professional staff and workers of a govern-  ment.

Civil Service Commission.definition: A commission that exists to ensure  that civil service jobs are awarded on the basis of merit rather than  favoritism.

claim.definition: (1) To state the truth of something; to assert. (2) To assert  ownership of or right to money or property and demand it. N. (1) A  demand for something that one considers one’s own. (2) An appli-  cation for compensation filed with an insurer. (3) A cause of action,  including a right enforceable by the court, an injury, and damages.

class.definition: A group of people or things sharing common charac-  teristics.

class action.definition: A lawsuit brought by one or a few members of a  group on behalf of all the members, specially certified by the court  to confirm that all the members share a concern and meet other  requirements.

classify.definition: To put into groups on the basis of similarities and  shared characteristics. N. classification.

clause.definition: A paragraph, stipulation, or article in a legal document  such as a contract or pleading.

Clayton Act.definition: A federal statute that amends the Sherman Antitrust  Act by prohibiting price discrimination and regulating mergers and  contracts that might inhibit competition.

Clean Air Acts.definition: Federal and state statutes that regulate air pollu-  tion.

clean hands doctrine.definition: A doctrine holding that in order for  someone to receive equitable relief for a wrong, he or she must be  blameless in the transaction, and must have performed his or her  duties in good faith without wrongdoing. See also unclean hands.

clear.definition: Obvious or evident; easy to understand; without a doubt.

clear and convincing.definition: Highly probable, establishing a firm  belief that something is true; an evidentiary standard between a  preponderance of the evidence and beyond a reasonable doubt.

clear and present danger.definition: A constitutional doctrine that allows  the government to restrict freedom of speech in cases where free  speech would create an obvious and immediate danger that the  government has a duty to prevent.

clearinghouse.definition: A banking establishment where member banks  exchange checks and bills all at once, paying only the balances in  cash so as to settle daily accounts conveniently.

clear title.definition: A title free of encumbrances; good title.

clergy.definition: Religious professionals; those ordained for the ministry.

clerical.definition: Having to do with bookkeeping, records, filing, corre-  spondence, and similar duties.

clerical error.definition: A mistake made while drafting or copying a docu-  ment.

clerk.definition: (1) A person who works in an office handling documents,  records, and other administrative duties. (2) A person who works  in a store, selling and organizing merchandise, usually an assistant.  (3) A court officer who keeps court records, files pleadings and  motions, issues process, and enters judgment. V. To work as a  clerk, especially a law clerk.

clerk, law.definition: A law student or recent law school graduate who  assists an attorney or judge with research; writing of briefs, memo-  randa, and opinions; and, other tasks.

click-wrap agreement.definition: A contract formed when a user clicks a  box on a website agreeing to the website’s terms and conditions;  see also browse-wrap agreement, shrink-wrap agreement.

client.definition: An individual or organization that employs a professional  to provide that professional’s services; someone who employs an  attorney to represent him or her in court, to draft legal documents,  to advise, or to provide other legal services.

close.definition: (1) To complete or bring to an end. (2) To cover an  opening. ADJ. (1) Near, nearby. (2) Closed or restricted. N. (1) An  enclosed piece of land surrounded by a visible or invisible  boundary; see also inclosure. (2) The end of a period of time or  event.

closed shop.definition: A business in which a collective bargaining agree-  ment requires that all employees be members of a particular union.  See also open shop.

closing.definition: The conclusion of a sale of real estate in which the  buyer pays the purchase price, the mortgage is secured, and the  seller hands over the deed to the buyer.

closing argument.definition: An attorney’s final arguments to the judge and  jury at the conclusion of his or her case, summing up the facts  supporting the case and explaining why the opposing side’s case is  inadequate.

closing costs.definition: Expenses that must be paid on closing, including  fees for title searches and title insurance, deeds, appraisals, credit  reports, and escrow.

closing statement.definition: A document that records the details of the  sale, including purchase price, deductions, tax adjustments, and  credits, resulting in the net amount that the seller receives.

cloture.definition: A vote by a legislature to end excessive debate on the  matter at hand and vote on it. See also filibuster.

cloud on title.definition: An encumbrance or claim on a title that could  potentially impair the title but can be proven invalid by outside  evidence, and can be removed by an action to quiet title.

COBRA.definition: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act  of 1985.

code.definition: A systematic collection of laws, regulations, or rules.

code, civil.definition: The collection of laws based on the French Napoleonic  Code that governs Louisiana jurisprudence. Also known as code  civil.

code, penal.definition: A collection of laws dealing with criminal acts and  punishments. See also United States Code.

co-defendant.definition: Someone who is a defendant together with  someone else accused of the same crime or offense, or who is sued  with another defendant in a single action.

code of military justice.definition: The collection of laws that govern the  administration of military justice for all the armed services of the  United States.

code of professional responsibility.definition: A compilation of guidelines  for the professional conduct and ethical behavior of attorneys and  legal professionals; has been replaced by the Model Rules of  Professional Conduct.

codicil.definition: An addendum to a will, modifying, adding to, revoking,  or explaining portions of the original document.

codify.definition: To arrange laws or rules systematically. N. codifi-  cation.

cognovit judgment.definition: A debtor’s written confession of judgment in  the event of nonpayment.

cognovit note.definition: A promissory note written by a debtor author-  izing an attorney to confess judgment against him or her if the debt  described in the note is not paid when due.

cohabit.definition: To live together; to share a dwelling as a married couple  without being married. N. cohabitation.

co-heir.definition: One of two or more heirs inheriting from the same  estate.

coif.definition: A headdress; a white piece of cloth worn by top English  lawyers on top of their wigs.

Coif, Order of the.definition: An honor society for law students in the top  10% of their class.

coinsurance.definition: A form of insurance in which the insurer pays only  a portion of a claim for damage, sharing the risk with the insured.

collate.definition: (1) To collect information and arrange it in order. (2)  When someone dies intestate, to estimate the value of all advances  made by the deceased to his or her children before death so that  these advances can be considered in dividing the estate according  to law. N. collation.

collateral.definition: Something pledged as security for a loan, to be  forfeited if the debt is not paid. ADJ. Beside; parallel; additional. See  also lineal.

collateral attack.definition: An attempt to defeat a judgment through a  separate action instead of an appeal or other direct attack.

collateral estoppel.definition: A doctrine holding that a judgment on  issues litigated by two parties is binding on them for those issues  in all subsequent actions with different causes of action.

collateral source rule.definition: A rule stating that if an injured person  receives compensation from a source such as insurance, the  damages the injured person can receive from the tortfeasor are not  reduced by that amount.

collective bargaining.definition: Negotiation and dispute resolution over  employment matters between a representative group of employees  and the employer. See also union.

colloquium.definition: In an action for slander or libel, a declaration that  words spoken or written by the defendant were about the plaintiff  and intended by the defendant.

collude.definition: (1) Conspire; to agree secretly to commit some fraudu-  lent act. (2) In a divorce proceeding, for the husband and wife to  agree that one of them will commit some act that can be grounds  for divorce. N. collusion.

collusive action.definition: A lawsuit brought by people who do not actu-  ally have a grievance in order to see how the court will rule or to  establish a precedent; courts will not hear collusive actions  because they have no real controversy.

color.definition: Appearance as opposed to reality; disguise; hiding facts  behind a false legal theory.

color of law.definition: The appearance of legal authority without the  substance; something that appears to have the authority of law but  in fact does not.

color of title.definition: An instrument that appears to be a valid title but  in fact is not.

comity.definition: (1) Courtesy toward others. (2) The judicial practice of  one court recognizing the decisions of courts from other jurisdic-  tions out of respect. See also full faith and credit.

comity of nations.definition: Recognition by one nation within its own  territory of the laws of another nation.

comment.definition: An opinion or reaction to something; an observation.  V. comment.

commerce.definition: The exchange, buying, and selling of goods, services,  and property; trade and transportation of goods. ADJ. commercial.

Commerce Clause.definition: Article I, Section 8, Clause 3 of the U.S.  Constitution, which gives Congress the power to regulate  commerce with foreign nations, among the states, and with  American Indian tribes.

commercial paper.definition: A document in which the maker promises to  pay the bearer a certain sum on deposit or at a particular time; a  short-term unsecured loan issued by a company.

commercial speech.definition: Speech that has a specific commercial  purpose, such as advertising.

commercial unit.definition: An item or collection of goods that cannot be  divided without impairing its value, such as a machine, a suite of  furniture, or a bale of cotton.

commingling of funds.definition: A fiduciary’s mingling of a client’s or  beneficiary’s money with his or her own.

commission.definition: (1) A fee paid to an agent, broker, etc., for handling  a transaction, usually a percentage of the total transaction. (2) A  warrant issued by the government or a court authorizing its recip-  ient to perform specific duties. (3) A group of people authorized to  perform some function. (4) The act of committing a crime.

commissioner.definition: A member of a commission, or a person with a  commission.

commit.definition: (1) To do or perform a deed or act. (2) To entrust to the  care of someone else; to send someone to prison or a mental insti-  tution. (3) To bind oneself to someone or something.

commitment.definition: (1) The act of committing. (2) An obligation.

committee.definition: (1) A group of people appointed to perform a  specific duty. (2) Someone who has been committed to a mental  institution. (3) Someone appointed to care for an incompetent  person.

commodity.definition: (1) Something that can be sold; a thing of value. (2)  Raw materials or agricultural products that can be bought and sold.

common law.definition: A system of law based on judicial precedent and  custom rather than statute and code; the system of jurisprudence  used in England and most of the United States. See also civil law.

Commons, House of.definition: The British legislative house that repre-  sents the common people, all subjects except the nobility and  royalty.

common stock.definition: Shares of ownership of a company. See also  preferred stock.

commonwealth.definition: A political unit governed for the common good;  a commonwealth can take many forms, such as a nation, state,  republic, or union.

Communications Decency Act.definition: A law passed as part of the  Telecommunications Act of 1996 that attempted to regulate  obscenity on the Internet and was effectively ruled unconstitu-  tional by the U.S. Supreme Court in the late 1990s and early 2000s.  ABBRV. CDA.

community.definition: (1) A locality or the people who live in it. (2) A group  of people who share a religion, profession, or other characteristic.  (3) A group of states or nations with common interests. (4) The  sense of fellowship that comes from sharing a home, interests, or  characteristics.

community property.definition: Property owned equally by husband and  wife, with all earnings divided equally between spouses; the form  of marital property distribution used by a minority of states. See  also equitable distribution, marital property.

commute.definition: To replace or change; to replace a criminal sentence  with a lighter one. N. commutation.

compact.definition: An agreement between two or more parties, especially  states or governments, to work together on shared concerns.

Compact Clause.definition: Article I, Section 10, Clause 3 of the U.S.  Constitution, prohibiting states to enter into compacts with other  states or with foreign nations (i.e., interstate compacts) without the  consent of Congress.

company.definition: A group or association of people with the purpose of  running a business or commercial enterprise; a business.

company, joint stock.definition: A company that raises capital by pooling  the contributions of its members, which are then divided into  shares that are distributed to members to represent their share of  ownership of the company.

comparative negligence.definition: A system under which negligence is  computed in terms of percentage, and a party’s damages will be  reduced by the amount that his or her negligence contributed to  them; contributory negligence has largely replaced the doctrine of  comparative negligence.

compensate.definition: (1) To give someone money to make up for an  injury he or she has suffered. (2) To pay someone for work he or  she has performed. N. compensation.

competency.definition: Ability to stand trial or serve as a witness.

competent.definition: Able; capable; having the ability to do something;  capable of understanding.

competent evidence.definition: Evidence that is admissible because it is  material and relevant.

complainant.definition: A party who brings a legal complaint against  another.

compliance.definition: The practice within a business of ensuring that all  personnel are following applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

comply.definition: To follow orders or rules; to submit.

compos mentis.definition: (Latin) Of sound mind; sane and mentally  competent. See also non compos mentis.

compound a crime or felony.definition: For an injured party to agree not  to prosecute the person who has committed the crime against him  or her in exchange for some payment.

compound interest.definition: Interest paid both on principal and on any  interest already accumulated.

Comprehensive Test Ban Treaty.definition: An international treaty effec-  tive in 1997 that bans all nuclear explosions. ABBRV. CTBT.

compulsion.definition: Force or duress; forcing someone to do something.  V. compel.

compulsory arbitration.definition: Arbitration in which the parties are  forced to agree.

compulsory insurance.definition: Motor vehicle insurance that drivers are  required by law to purchase.

compulsory process.definition: A court’s use of subpoena, arrest, or  attachment to force a person to appear in court; a defendant’s  constitutional right to force witnesses to appear and testify at trial.

Computer Fraud and Abuse Act.definition: A federal law passed in 1986  that made hacking into computer systems illegal. ABBRV. CFAA.

concealed carry.definition: The practice of carrying a concealed weapon;  open carry. N. The practice of carrying a weapon in plain sight on  one’s person.

concealed weapon permit.definition: A license that allows a private citizen  to carry a concealed weapon.

conception.definition: (1) The beginning of pregnancy, when an egg is  fertilized by a sperm; the action of conceiving a child. (2) A notion  or idea; the beginning of an idea. V. conceive.

concerted action.definition: An act that has been planned and performed  by two or more people with a common purpose. See also  conspiracy, accomplice.

conciliation.definition: Amicable resolution of a dispute by the parties  themselves, often done before trial. See also arbitration,  mediation.

conclude.definition: (1) To end; to sum up. (2) To reach a judgment through  reasoning.

conclusion of fact.definition: A conclusion reached by examining only the  facts and evidence, without applying any law.

conclusion of law.definition: A court’s legal conclusions about a case,  arrived at by applying rules of law.

conclusive evidence.definition: Incontrovertible evidence; evidence that  establishes a proposition and that cannot be disproved.

concurrence.definition: A concurring opinion; an opinion written by a  judge or justice of an appellate court agreeing with the majority  conclusion in a case but describing a different path of legal  reasoning. See also dissent.

condemn.definition: (1) To find someone guilty of a crime; to sentence  someone to death. (2) To disapprove of publicly. (3) To officially  declare a building unfit for use. (4) To use eminent domain to take  private property for public use. N. condemnation.

condition.definition: Something that must happen or be the case before  something else can happen; a prerequisite. ADJ. conditional.

condition precedent.definition: A fact or state of affairs that must exist  before a particular contractual duty must be performed.

condition subsequent.definition: A future event that will end a contractual  obligation.

condominium.definition: A kind of dwelling arrangement in which individ-  uals own separate units in a multi-unit complex and all owners  share common facilities and areas. See also cooperative.

condonation.definition: In marriage, the forgiveness of some behavior that  would be grounds for divorce by resuming cohabitation on the  condition that the behavior not happen again.

condone.definition: To approve, albeit reluctantly; to allow undesirable  behavior to continue.

confederate.definition: United by a treaty or common agreement. N. An  accomplice.

confederation.definition: An alliance or league of states, each of which  retains full sovereignty and self-government, with a limited  central government.

confess.definition: To admit to having committed a crime or wrongful act.  N. confession.

confession and avoidance.definition: A pleading in which the party admits  the truth of the allegations in the complaint but then presents new  information that makes those allegations ineffectual. See also  affirmative defense.

confession of judgment.definition: Entry of judgment against a debtor  without a legal proceeding, done if the debtor signs a cognovit note  at the time of purchase.

confidence game.definition: A swindle in which a swindler earns the trust  of the victim and then takes advantage of it to cheat the victim out  of money; informally called a con game or simply con.

confirm.definition: (1) To show that a statement is correct; to state that a  fact is true. (2) To formally install someone in office; to ratify.

confirmation.definition: Formal approval; written ratification of some-  thing.

confiscate.definition: To take someone’s private property for public use;  during wartime, to take an enemy’s property; for the government to  appropriate private property without compensation. N. confisca-  tion.

conflict of interest.definition: An ethical dilemma in which a person is  entrusted with two duties at odds with one another, in which atten-  tion to one duty will harm the other.

conflict of laws.definition: The conflict that results when a controversy  occurs in more than one jurisdiction, thus raising the question of  which jurisdiction’s law to apply to the matter; the branch of law  that considers these questions, also called choice of law.

conform.definition: To comply with stated rules or standards. ADJ.  conforming. N. conformity. See also nonconformity.

conformed copy.definition: An exact copy of a document with handwritten  notations of items that were written by hand on the original; often  prepared and certified by a clerk of court.

confront.definition: To face someone; to come face to face with a problem  or an accusation. N. confrontation.

Confrontation Clause.definition: A provision of the Sixth Amendment of  the U.S. Constitution guaranteeing the accused the right to  confront his or her accuser and witnesses at trial, to hear his or her  testimony, and cross-examine him or her.

confuse.definition: To make blurry or perplexing; to intermingle things so  that they cannot be distinguished from one another. N. confusion.

confusion of goods.definition: Mixing the property of different owners so  that they can no longer identify their own goods.

conglomerate.definition: A corporate entity composed of several different  corporations merged together. See also merger.

congress.definition: (1) A country’s national legislative body; in the United  States, the body composed of the Senate and the House of  Representatives, usually capitalized to “Congress.” (2) A formal  meeting, usually by political or professional groups. (3) A union or  coming together, as in the phrase “sexual congress.”

conjecture.definition: A tentative conclusion or opinion based on limited  information; a guess. V. conjecture.

conjugal.definition: Having to do with the marital relationship.

conjugal rights.definition: Rights that each spouse can expect from the  other, particularly sexual rights but also the rights to society,  comfort, and affection.

conjugal visit.definition: A visit to a convict by his or her spouse during  which the couple is given the opportunity to have sexual inter-  course.

conjunctive denial.definition: A single collective denial of several facts  stated in a complaint.

con man.definition: Slang for a swindler. See also confidence game,  flim-flam.

consanguinity, collateral.definition: The relationship between those who  share an ancestor but are not descended in the same line, such as  the relationship between aunts and uncles, nieces and nephews,  and cousins.

consanguinity, lineal.definition: Kinship between people descended in a  direct line from one another, such as the relationship between  parent and child and grandchild.

conscience.definition: A personal standard that guides a person’s view of  what is right and wrong; moral sense.

conscience of the court.definition: An equity court’s application of  community standards of fairness and decency along with princi-  ples of equitable law and precedent to its resolution of a contro-  versy.

conscientious objector.definition: A person who refuses to serve in the  armed forces due to ethical, moral, or religious objections to war.  See also pacifist.

conscript.definition: One who has been compulsorily enrolled in the mili-  tary. V. conscript.

conscription.definition: The draft; compulsory enlistment in the mili-  tary.

consent.definition: To agree voluntarily; to give permission. N. consent.

consent, implied.definition: Consent that is expressed indirectly  through behavior and actions that make it appear that consent  has been given.

consent decree.definition: A binding decree issued by a judge that  expresses a consensual agreement between the parties to a  lawsuit.

consent judgment.definition: A judgment that the parties agree to.

consent search.definition: A search made by police after the subject of the  search has voluntarily consented to it freely, which absolves the  police of the need for a search warrant.

consequence.definition: A result; the result that naturally follows a cause.

conservator.definition: A guardian; someone appointed by a court to  manage an estate, a business, or the personal affairs of someone  unable to manage alone.

consideration.definition: The payment or reward essential to the forma-  tion of a contract and that persuades a person to enter the  contract; something of value given in exchange for a performance  or a promise.

consign.definition: To entrust goods to someone else; to deliver goods to the  custody of a carrier or agent, usually so that they can be sold.

consignee.definition: The recipient of consigned goods; the person named  as recipient in a bill of lading.

consignment.definition: (1) The act of consigning. (2) A batch of goods  consigned to someone.

consolidate.definition: To put together several things into one coherent  whole. N. consolidation.

consolidated appeal.definition: A single appeal filed by more than one  appellant whose interests are similar enough to make their combi-  nation feasible.

consolidation of actions.definition: Combining several related lawsuits  into a single action.

consolidation of corporations.definition: Combining two or more corpora-  tions by destroying them and creating a new corporation with the  assets and liabilities of the now nonexistent ones. See also merger.

consortium.definition: (1) A married person’s right to fellowship, help, and  affection from his or her spouse. (2) An association of several  companies that join together to pursue a common object for a  specified period of time, in which the members do not assume  liability for one another’s actions. PL. consortia.

consortium, loss of.definition: A cause of action that can be brought by  someone whose spouse has been killed or injured, based on loss of  material services, companionship, and sex.

conspiracy.definition: Two or more people who join together to plan and  commit an unlawful act. V. conspire.

conspirator.definition: A person who participates in a conspiracy.

constituent.definition: One who authorizes someone else to act for him or  her, usually used to name the members of a community that elect  and are represented by a legislator or politician.

constitution.definition: A collection of fundamental principles of law  according to which a nation or organization is to be governed.

constitutional.definition: According to principles defined in the  Constitution.

constitutional question.definition: A legal question that must be answered  by interpreting the Constitution, as opposed to being addressed by  a statute. See also unconstitutional.

construct.definition: An idea composed of various conceptual elements,  usually one that is not empirically verifiable or testable.

construction.definition: (1) The interpretation of a statute or legal authority,  particularly of ambiguous portions of it. See also construe.

construction, liberal.definition: Interpreting a statute expansively, looking  for the spirit of the law.

construction, strict.definition: Interpreting a statute solely on the basis of  what is written, bringing in nothing that is not expressed and  looking for the letter rather than the spirit of the law.

constructionist.definition: A person who interprets a law, especially the  Constitution, in a particular way. (2) Building houses and other  structures.

constructive.definition: Implied or inferred; inferred by construing facts in  a particular way.

constructive contract.definition: A contract not intentionally entered into  by the parties that arises legally to prevent injustice. Synonymous  with quasi contract.

constructive discharge.definition: A situation in which an employee finds  conditions at work so intolerable that he or she feels forced to  resign, without actually being discharged by the employer. See also  harass, discrimination.

constructive eviction.definition: A situation in which a landlord lets living  conditions get so bad that a tenant feels compelled to move out.

constructive possession.definition: The power to control an item without  actual physical possession of it.

construe.definition: To find the meaning in words or actions. See also  construction.

consultant.definition: An expert, often an independent contractor, paid by  a company to give it advice on some matter but not employed by  that company.

consumer.definition: A person who buys goods and services and consumes  or uses them.

consumer-driven health plan.definition: A health plan that is meant to give  consumers a higher degree of control over their own health care,  typically by allowing consumers to save money and use it to pay for  medical care as they choose. See also health savings account, high  deductible health plan.

consumer goods.definition: Goods bought for personal use by an indi-  vidual, household, or family, and not intended for resale; a category  of goods under the Uniform Commercial Code.

consumer protection laws.definition: Federal and state laws regulating  sales and credit practices, intended to protect consumers from  deceptive advertising, poor quality, dishonest credit and debt prac-  tices, and other dangers facing retail consumers who cannot  engage in arm’s-length bargaining with retailers and manufac-  turers.

contempt.definition: Disregard for authority; willful disobedience or disre-  spect.

contempt, civil.definition: Refusing to perform a duty ordered by the court  to benefit another party to the litigation, such as producing docu-  ments.

contempt, constructive.definition: Disregard for court authority that  occurs away from the court, such as refusing to obey an  injunction.

contempt, criminal.definition: An act that directly interferes with the  court’s operation or obstructs justice.

contempt, direct.definition: Contempt committed in the court or near  enough that it interferes with orderly proceedings.

contempt of court.definition: Disobedience to a court and its officers; any  act intended to interfere with the operation or dignity of the court.

contiguous.definition: Touching, next to, sharing a border.

Continental Congress.definition: A congress held by the American  colonies at the start of the American Revolution; Continental  Congresses were held in 1774, 1775, and 1776, and resulted in the  creation of the Continental Army.

contingency fee.definition: A fee charged by an attorney that is contingent  on a successful outcome of the case; often the attorney will take a  percentage of the client’s recovery if the client prevails, and will  receive nothing if the client loses; commonly used by attorneys  representing plaintiffs in personal injury lawsuits. Also called a  contingent fee.

contingent.definition: Depending on something; occurring if some spec-  ified condition occurs; provisional. N. A delegation; a group of  people that represents a larger group.

contingent beneficiary.definition: Someone who will receive the proceeds  of a trust or some other arrangement only if a specific event occurs.

contingent interest.definition: An interest that will only vest if a specific  event occurs.

contingent liability.definition: Potential liability; liability that will accrue if  some specific event occurs first.

contingent right.definition: A right that will only vest if a specific event  occurs.

continuance.definition: Postponement or adjournment of a trial or other  court proceeding to a later date.

contraband.definition: Illegal goods; goods that are illegal to possess, such  as narcotics or counterfeit money.

contract.definition: An agreement between two or more parties that is  enforceable by law; a transaction between two or more people in  which one or both promise to perform some duty in exchange for  consideration, and if a party fails to do what he or she promised, the  law allows the other party to seek a remedy. V. contract.

contract, bilateral.definition: A contract between two parties in which  each one promises something to the other.

contract, oral.definition: A contract that is executed verbally and not put  in writing. See also statute of frauds.

contract, unilateral.definition: A contract in which one party promises to  do something in exchange for performance by the other party.

contractor.definition: A person who contracts to do work for someone else, on an independent basis, using his or her own materials and methods and not under the control of the customer with regard to the details of how the work is done. See also independent contractor, subcontractor.

contribute.definition: To give; to donate something in order to help some  cause; to help or assist in bringing something about.

contribution.definition: A payment of a portion of a liability recovery by  one who shares liability, especially among insurers; the doctrine  that allows someone who has already paid a judgment to recover  part of the amount from another party who is also liable. ADJ.  contributory.

control.definition: Power over someone or something; power to direct a  person’s actions, manage a machine, or influence events. V.  control.

controlled substance.definition: A drug regulated by law whose distribu-  tion is restricted or prohibited because of potential for abuse,  including narcotics, stimulants, depressants, hallucinogens, and  marijuana.

controlled substance acts.definition: Federal and state laws that regulate  distribution and use of controlled substances.

controversy.definition: A dispute that can be litigated in court; a civil action  or lawsuit.

contumacy.definition: Willful disobedience of the court’s authority.

contumely.definition: Contemptuous insolence; rudeness or insulting  language.

convention.definition: (1) An agreement, usually among several nations,  that is less formal than a treaty. (2) A meeting of members of a  professional, political, or social organization.

convertible securities.definition: Bonds or preferred stock that can be  exchanged for common stock or another lesser security, generally  within the same company.

convey.definition: To transfer property from one person to another. N.  conveyance.

convict.definition: For a court to find someone guilty of a crime. N. One  who has been found by a court to be guilty of a crime.

conviction.definition: The legal act of finding someone guilty of a crime;  the end of the prosecution, including the judgment or sentence.

conviction, summary.definition: Finding a person guilty of a crime after a  trial without a jury.

cooperative.definition: A business owned and run by its members for the  purpose of mutual help, in which profits and costs are shared.

cooperative apartment.definition: An apartment in a complex of apart-  ments in which each owner has a share in the entire complex and  a lease of his or her own apartment. See also condominium.

copay.definition: A set fee a person pays for a medical service received as  part of a health insurance plan.

copyright.definition: The legal right to publish, perform, or display a work  of literature, art, music, drama, recording, or film, protected both  by common law and by statute, including the Copyright Act of  1976. See also infringe.

coroner.definition: A public official who investigates sudden, violent, and  suspicious deaths. See also inquest.

corporate opportunity.definition: The opportunity available to someone  closely related to a corporation to use corporate information or  appropriate corporate business opportunities for his or her own  personal gain; the corporate opportunity doctrine prohibits this  kind of opportunism.

corporation.definition: The legal entity created by law of an association of  people who hold shares of a business, existing as an artificial  person that can sue and be sued and whose liability is generally  limited to corporate assets.

corporation, C.definition: A corporation formed under Subchapter C of the  Internal Revenue Code; also called a Subchapter C corporation.

corporation, close.definition: A corporation whose shares are not publicly  traded but instead are held by a small group or a single shareholder  who also runs the corporation’s business; also called a closely held  corporation.

corporation, private.definition: An ordinary corporation formed to do busi-  ness and earn a profit.

corporation, public.definition: A corporation formed by the government  for a particular governmental purpose.

corporation, S.definition: A small corporation that can have its taxable  income taxed to its shareholders to avoid corporate income tax.

corpus.definition: (Latin) Body; a collection or mass; the principal mass of  a physical substance. In trust law, it is the principal or res of an  estate.

corpus juris.definition: (Latin) Body of law; a book that collects principles  of law.

correctional institution.definition: A jail, reformatory, prison, or other  institution where those convicted of crimes are sent.

correctional system.definition: The government system of correctional  institutions and parole systems.

corroborate.definition: To confirm or verify; to agree with or give support  to. N. corroboration.

corroborating evidence.definition: Evidence that agrees with or supports  evidence that has already been presented.

corrupt.definition: Willing to act dishonestly in exchange for money or  advantage; morally depraved. V. To spoil; to make someone turn  bad.

corruption.definition: Abuse of an official position for personal gain;  dishonest behavior by someone who holds official power. See also  bribe.

cosign.definition: To sign a document along with someone else, either to  guarantee the first signer’s obligation or to verify the authenticity of  the other signature.

cosigner.definition: One who cosigns a document. See also surety.

cost.definition: The price; the amount that must be paid for something.

cost, opportunity.definition: A potential benefit lost because of a decision  made.

cost basis.definition: The amount a taxpayer paid for an asset.

cost of completion.definition: In a breach of contract action, a measure of  damages calculated by the amount it would cost to pay for a  performance that would put the plaintiff where he or she would  have been had the contract not been breached.

cost of living.definition: The amount it costs to live from day to day, meas-  ured by the Consumer Price Index.

cost-plus contract.definition: A kind of contract, common in construction  projects, in which the contractor is paid for costs of material and  labor plus a percentage of those costs as profit.

costs.definition: The expenses involved in taking a case to trial, sometimes  awarded to the victorious party; costs usually do not include  attorney’s fees.

cotenancy.definition: A tenancy shared by two or more people at the same  time, including joint tenancy and tenancy in common.

cotenant.definition: Someone who shares a tenancy with someone else.

counsel.definition: (1) An attorney, a lawyer; also called counselor. (2)  Legal advice. V. To give legal advice.

counter.definition: Against. V. To oppose; to rebut.

counterclaim.definition: A claim made by a defendant to oppose the claim  brought by the plaintiff; a counterdemand.

counterclaim, compulsory.definition: A claim arising out of the same  subject matter as the plaintiff’s original claim and involving largely  the same people; it must be made when the defendant answers the  complaint.

counterclaim, permissive.definition: A claim not arising out of the occur-  rence that brought about the original complaint, and that can be  brought at the defendant’s choice.

counterfeit.definition: Fake; forged; copied with the intention of passing  off as authentic. V. counterfeit.

counterfeiter.definition: A person who makes fake copies of money or  other objects and passes them off as genuine.

county.definition: A locally governed territorial division of a state; called a  parish in Louisiana.

coupon.definition: A certificate attached to a loan instrument that can be  separated from the instrument and presented after a specified time  for the collection of interest.

coupon bond.definition: A bond that has coupons attached to it for the  collection of several installments of interest before the bond  matures.

course of dealing.definition: A series of previous acts and conduct  between the parties to a transaction that establishes an under-  standing that subsequent actions and conduct related to the same  or similar transactions will be interpreted the same way. See also  trade usage.

course of employment.definition: The normal duties and activities associ-  ated with a job; the activities that an employee performs as his or  her customary services to the employer.

court.definition: A tribunal in which a judge and/or jury hears and decides  civil and/or criminal cases; the governmental entity that applies the  state’s laws to cases and controversies and administers justice; also  called a court of law.

court, civil.definition: A court that hears civil cases.

court, criminal.definition: A court that hears criminal prosecutions.

court, out of.definition: Occurring before a case reaches trial or outside the  courtroom; usually used to describe a settlement.

court, trial.definition: The level of court at which a civil or criminal case  receives its first hearing.

court-martial.definition: (1) A military court that hears cases involving  members of the armed forces. (2) A trial or proceeding before a  military court. V. To try someone in a court-martial. PL. courts-  martial or court-martials.

court of appeals.definition: A court that hears appeals of decisions made  by trial courts. See also appellate court.

court of equity.definition: A court that hears cases in equity, particularly  cases that would not have a suitable remedy at law; most states no  longer have courts of equity. See also chancery.

court reporter.definition: A person trained and certified to record testi-  mony at trial or at a deposition and produce an official transcript  of it.

court stacking.definition: The practice by the president of appointing to  federal benches only judges who espouse a particular ideological  viewpoint, in order to increase the likelihood that courts will rule  in that direction.

covenant.definition: A contract or formal agreement; often produced in  writing and signed by all parties. V. To enter into an agreement.

covenant, affirmative.definition: A covenant in which one party agrees to  do something.

covenant, negative.definition: A covenant in which one party promises not  to do something.

covenantee.definition: A person for whom a covenant is made.

covenant for further assurance.definition: A covenant in which a seller of  land agrees to do whatever is necessary to perfect the buyer’s title.

covenant not to compete.definition: An agreement made as part of a  contract for employment in which the covenantor agrees not to  compete with the covenantee in a particular area for a specified  time.

covenantor.definition: A person who makes a covenant.

covenant running with the land.definition: A covenant that is attached to  a piece of land and is binding on those who come into possession  of it.

cover.definition: (1) In insurance, to protect against loss or liability; to  furnish money to pay for the financial consequences of an accident  or misfortune. (2) In commercial law, after a seller breaches a  promise to deliver goods, to find an alternative source of the same  goods on the open market.

coverage.definition: In insurance, the amount that the insurer is prepared  to pay for a particular loss.

coverture.definition: In common law, the legal state of a married woman,  which was generally merged with that of her husband, leaving her  without the right to own property or enter contracts on her own.

Creative Commons.definition: A nonprofit organization dedicated to  making creative work more freely available by providing free  licensing services to creators of literary works, enabling copyright  holders to grant some of the rights to their work to the public.

credibility.definition: The quality, especially in a witness, of being believ-  able and likely to tell the truth.

credible evidence.definition: Evidence that is likely to be true because  it is natural and probable in light of other circumstances  already known.

credible witness.definition: A trustworthy witness whose testimony is  believable.

credit.definition: (1) The ability of a person or business to obtain money,  goods, or services because of the likelihood that he or she will pay  back any debt on schedule, usually based on a past history of  payment and likelihood of future success. (2) Money lent on credit.  (3) In accounting, a sum received; the opposite of debt.

creditor’s bill (suit).definition: A proceeding brought in equity by a  creditor who has secured judgment against a debtor to enforce  payment of the debt if the creditor cannot reach the debtor’s  property at law.

credit rating.definition: A number calculated by examining a person’s  assets, liabilities, and financial history that tells a lender how likely  that person is to pay back a loan.

crime.definition: An act that violates criminal law.

crime against humanity.definition: An attack on or persecution of a group  of people who are part of a widespread practice or governmental  policy that results in serious attacks on human dignity or degrada-  tion of the victims, including widespread rape, murder, torture, or  racial, political, or religious persecution.

crime against nature.definition: Sexual intercourse that violates statutorily  mandated standards of behavior. See also buggery, sodomy.

crime malum in se.definition: A crime that is illegal because it is wrong in  itself. See also malum in se.

crime malum prohibitum.definition: A crime that is illegal because it is  prohibited by statute. See also malum prohibitum.

crime of passion.definition: A crime committed in the heat of sudden  passion.

criminal.definition: One who commits a crime; one who violates a criminal  law. ADJ. Related to a crime; done with malice or intent to injure.

criminalize.definition: To decide that some act is a crime; to pass a law  stating that an act is a crime; see also decriminalize.

criminal justice system.definition: The government’s system of law  enforcement that apprehends, prosecutes, and punishes criminals,  including law enforcement officers, courts, and correctional insti-  tutions.

criminal law.definition: The branch of law that deals with the prosecution  and punishment of criminals; the body of law that determines what  constitutes a crime and what constitutes suitable punishment.

criminal maintenance.definition: Helping a party to a lawsuit prosecute or  defend the case in such a way as to obstruct justice and promote  needless litigation.

criminal mischief.definition: The crime of maliciously injuring property.

criminal procedure.definition: The body of law that governs actions in  criminal courts, including investigation, prosecution, and  punishment.

criminal record.definition: An official history of a person’s arrests, convic-  tions, sentences, and paroles; also called a rap sheet.

cross-claim.definition: A claim brought by a party to a lawsuit against a co-  party, out of an occurrence that is part of the subject matter of the  original action. See also counterclaim.

cross-examination.definition: At trial or a deposition, the examination  of a witness by the party opposed to the side that produced  him or her, regarding testimony already raised on direct  examination and matters of witness credibility.

cruel and inhuman treatment.definition: As a grounds for divorce, treat-  ment by one spouse that causes the other spouse suffering and  makes life together completely unbearable.

cruel and unusual punishment.definition: Punishment that offends ordi-  nary people due to its excessiveness or cruelty, or that is far out of  proportion to the offense it punishes; forbidden by the Eighth  Amendment of the Constitution.

culpable.definition: Guilty; deserving blame or punishment. N. culpa-  bility.

culprit.definition: A person who commits a crime or wrongful act; a person  accused of a crime.

cumulative.definition: Increasing with new additions; piling up.

cumulative voting.definition: A system of shareholder voting in which a  shareholder is allowed to apply all of his or her votes to one candi-  date for a board of directors instead of having to cast separate  votes for each office, allowing minority shareholders a chance for  meaningful representation.

curative.definition: Able or intended to correct a legal error.

curative instruction.definition: An instruction given by a judge to a jury  intended to correct an erroneous instruction or to fix the jury’s  interpretation of improper evidence.

curative statute.definition: A statute passed to correct a defect in a previ-  ously enacted law. See also declaratory.

currency.definition: Coined money and banknotes in general  circulation.

current.definition: Of the moment; in the present time.

current assets.definition: Property of a business that could or will be  converted into cash as part of normal operations in the immediate  future, usually within the year.

current liabilities.definition: Liabilities that are part of normal business  operations and that will be repaid within a year.

curtesy.definition: A common-law right of a husband to take a life estate in  all of his wife’s lands if she dies before him, usually only applicable  if they had children together; curtesy has been abolished or modi-  fied in most states. See also dower.

curtilage.definition: The land surrounding and attached to a house, often  enclosed in some way.

custodian.definition: One who cares for something, ranging from buildings  to financial assets.

custody.definition: (1) Responsibility or guardianship of a person or thing.  (2) Imprisonment.

custody, joint.definition: Custody in which both parents share responsi-  bility for caring for and raising a child.

custody, temporary.definition: Custody awarded to one parent for a brief  period, pending a decision on permanent custody.

custody of children.definition: The daily nurture and control of a child,  usually awarded to one parent in a case of divorce or separation.

custom.definition: The traditional, ordinary, and accepted way of doing  things in a particular community.

customary.definition: Ordinary; according to usual practices or custom;  habitual.

customer.definition: Someone who buys goods or services from a business.

customs.definition: (1) The government agency that collects tariffs and  taxes on imports; also the place at an airport, port, or border  crossing where imported goods are inspected. (2) Tariffs or taxes  on imported goods; also called customs duties.

cybercrime.definition: A crime committed using computers, networks, or  the Internet.

cyberlaw.definition: The field of law that encompasses issues involving  computers, networks, and information technology.

cyberspace.definition: The nonphysical territory occupied by the Internet  and computer networks.

cybersquatting.definition: Registering a trademarked name as a domain  name with the intention of selling it to an individual or company  that actually owns the trademarked name.

cy-près.definition: (French) As near; as near as possible; the doctrine  that if a bequest is illegal or impossible, the court will try to  replace it with one as close to the testator’s intentions as possible.  ADV. cy-près.

damages.definition: Money awarded as compensation for injury or loss.

damages, actual.definition: Damages for actual losses and injuries  sustained; also called compensatory damages or general damages.

damages, consequential.definition: Damages for an injury that arises from  the consequences of an act, for loss that arises from an injury but  is not directly caused by the injury and would not necessarily  occur; also called special damages.

damages, double or treble.definition: Damages awarded in particular  kinds of cases in which the court will award two or three times the  amount awarded by the jury.

damages, incidental.definition: Damages for expenses that arise out of an  occurrence that gives rise to a claim for actual damages.

damages, liquidated.definition: A sum that a party to a contract agrees to  pay if he or she breaches it; meant to be a reasonable estimate of  the loss that the breach would cause.

damages, punitive.definition: Damages over and above compensation for  actual injury, intended to punish the wrongdoer for malicious  conduct; also called exemplary damages.

damnum absque injuria.definition: (Latin) Harm without injury; a loss or  injury that does not give rise to a legal action to recover damages.

damnum fatale.definition: (Latin) Harm due to fate; an injury caused by  an act of God or some force beyond human control. See also act  of God.

dangerous.definition: Able to harm or injure; risky or hazardous.

dangerous weapon.definition: A device that can inflict serious injury or  death; see also deadly weapon.

day in court.definition: The day on which a litigant is appointed to appear  in court and be given the opportunity to seek relief from an injury  or to defend him- or herself from charges.

dead hand.definition: The continuing control by the dead over the affairs  of the living; see also mortmain.

deadly force.definition: Force that could kill the person against whom it is  used.

deadly weapon.definition: A weapon or instrument that is capable of  killing someone, whether because of its inherent nature (such as a  gun) or the way it is used (such as a hammer). See also weapon.

deal.definition: To conduct business; to buy and sell a particular  commodity. N. (1) An arrangement between two or more parties  that works to their mutual benefit. (2) A transaction that involves  buying or selling something.

dealer.definition: A person who buys and sells a particular commodity; one  who buys something in order to resell it. See also retail, broker.

death.definition: The moment at which life ceases; the condition of being  dead; see also brain death.

death, natural.definition: A death that results solely from natural causes.

death, violent.definition: A death caused by human actions.

death benefit.definition: An amount paid to a beneficiary when an insured  person dies.

death certificate.definition: An official document issued to certify that  someone is dead, usually including cause and time of death.

death penalty.definition: Capital punishment; punishing someone for a  serious crime such as murder by killing him or her.

death records.definition: Official records of deaths kept by a municipal  authority.

death tax.definition: Any tax imposed on property or its transfer after its  owner dies.

debauch.definition: To corrupt; to destroy one’s morals or manners; to  seduce.

debauchery.definition: Overindulgence in pleasures of the senses; sexual  immorality.

de bene esse.definition: (Latin) Conditionally; in preparation for a  possible future need; usually used to describe a legal proceeding  that is done provisionally and let stand for the time being, but that  can be challenged in the future.

de bene esse, deposition.definition: A deposition of a witness done just  in case the witness cannot appear at trial, so that the litigant  will have the testimony regardless. Also called examination  de bene esse.

de bene esse, evidence.definition: Evidence admitted provisionally by the  court on the understanding that its admissibility will be shown  later.

debenture.definition: An unsecured loan instrument issued by a company  and backed by a promise to pay or general credit rather than a

debit.definition: In accounting, a sum of money that is charged and due; the  opposite of credit.

debt.definition: Something, usually money, that is owed; an obligation to  pay a certain amount; see also bad debt.

debt, secured.definition: Debt tied to collateral, such as a mortgage.

debt consolidation.definition: The practice of combining debts from  various sources into one account.

debtor.definition: (1) A person who owes a debt to someone else; see also  creditor. (2) A person subject to bankruptcy proceedings.

debtor, judgment.definition: Someone who is the subject of a judgment  awarding a sum to a creditor.

deceased.definition: Someone who has died; a dead person. ADJ. Dead.

deceit.definition: A fraudulent misrepresentation used to cheat someone;  the act of deceiving someone by lying or misrepresenting the truth.  ADJ. deceitful.

deceive.definition: To make someone believe something that is not true;  to mislead.

decide.definition: To come to a conclusion or resolution after deliberation  and consideration; to determine something.

decision.definition: A conclusion reached after considering facts and  applicable law if necessary; a judicial determination or judgment.  See also opinion.

declaration.definition: (1) A formal announcement or statement. (2) At  common law, the first pleading presented to the court by a plain-  tiff, similar to a complaint in structure and purpose. (3) In  evidence, an unsworn statement made by a witness, a party to a  lawsuit, or a person who subsequently died. (4) When entering a  country, a statement of goods brought into the country by the  person entering.

declaration, dying.definition: A statement made by someone who thinks  he or she is about to die about the circumstances of his or her  death; dying declarations are sometimes admissible as evidence.

declaration against interest.definition: A statement made out of court that  is so disadvantageous to the person making it that it is presumed to  be true; declarations against interest are admissible as an excep-  tion to the hearsay rule.

Declaration of Independence.definition: A document written by Thomas  Jefferson and adopted by the thirteen colonies on July 4, 1776,  listing the colonies’ grievances against England and declaring the  colonies a separate nation free from allegiance to Great Britain.

declaration of war.definition: A formal public announcement by the lead-  ership of a nation that it is at war with another nation.

declaratory.definition: Explanatory; meant to clarify an ambiguity.

declaratory judgment.definition: A judgment issued by a court in which it  determines the legal rights of the parties or expresses an opinion

declaratory statute.definition: A statute enacted to clarify the law on a  particular matter, intended to end any doubts as to its meaning. See  also curative.

declare.definition: (1) To announce something formally, such as war; to  make a pronouncement about the state of something, such as  naming something a fire hazard. (2) In taxation, to admit to having  received income; when going through customs upon entering a  country, to admit to possessing goods on which duty is owed.

decree.definition: The judicial decision of a court in equity made after  hearing testimony and determining the rights of the parties, equiv-  alent to a judgment by a court of law, though the term “judgment”  may also be used in reference to courts of equity.

decree nisi.definition: A conditional judgment that will be made perma-  nent unless a party can show cause why it should not be; common  in actions for divorce. See also consent decree.

decriminalize.definition: To legalize an act that was formerly criminal.

dedicate.definition: For a private owner to convey land or an easement to  the public for public use. N. dedication.

deduct.definition: To subtract something from a total; in taxation, to  subtract the cost of items from total income to determine taxable  income. N. deduction.

deductible.definition: Able to be deducted; items that may be deducted  from gross income are described as tax-deductible. N. The amount  of money that an insured party must pay for a loss before an  insurer will pay a claim.

deduction, itemized.definition: Expenses individually listed and deducted  from gross income.

deduction, standard.definition: A standard amount that a taxpayer may  deduct from gross income instead of itemizing individual expenses.

deed.definition: A written instrument, signed and delivered, by which one  person conveys land to another.

deed of release.definition: A deed that releases property from an encum-  brance, such as a mortgage.

deed of trust.definition: A deed that transfers the title of property to a  trustee as a security; used instead of a mortgage in some states.

deed poll.definition: A deed made by and binding a single party.

deep rock doctrine.definition: In corporate bankruptcy, a principle that  prevents controlling shareholders from presenting their claims  before or with those of other creditors.

de facto.definition: (Latin) In fact, in reality; used to describe a situation  that is for all practical purposes the case, though it might not be  legal or official; see also de jure.

de facto government.definition: A government that sets itself up in place  of the legal government, often by use of force.

de facto judge.definition: Someone who performs the duties of a judge but  who does not have legitimate judicial authority.

de facto marriage.definition: A union of two parties who live together as  husband and wife but in which the legality of the marriage is in  some way defective.

defamation.definition: An intentional publication or public statement of  false information that damages someone’s reputation. V. defame.  ADJ. defamatory. See also libel, slander.

default.definition: Failure to perform a legal duty or meet an obligation.

default judgment.definition: Judgment entered against a defendant who  fails to defend himself or herself by responding to the plaintiff’s  complaint or appearing in court.
