Federal Trade Commission.definition: A federal agency that promotes  free trade and fair competition in interstate commerce by  preventing price-fixing, advertising, and general unfair competition.  ABBRV. FTC.

federation.definition: A union of states that share a central government but  also govern themselves independently.

fee.definition: (1) Payment made to someone in exchange for services. (2)  An estate of complete ownership of land; ownership of a piece of  property.

fee simple.definition: Complete ownership of a property, with no time limit  or restrictions on what can be done with it, that will pass to the  owner’s heirs if he or she dies intestate; also called fee simple  absolute.

fee simple conditional.definition: An interest in property that is given by a  grantor to the owner that will become a fee simple if a particular  condition is met, usually having to do with the estate passing to  specific heirs of the owner, and if the condition is not met the  estate goes back to the grantor when the owner dies.

fee simple defeasible.definition: An estate that can end if a particular  event does or does not happen; also called a fee simple deter-  minable.

fee tail.definition: A conveyance of property that establishes a specific  line of succession, limiting the transfer of the property to a  particular category of descendants of the recipient, and that goes  back to the grantor if there are no suitable descendants; also  called an estate tail.

fee tail male or female.definition: An estate limited to male or female lines  of descent.

fellow servant.definition: A coworker; someone who works for the same  employer.

felon.definition: One who has committed a felony.

felony.definition: A serious crime; most felonies are defined by statute, and  often include those that are punishable by death or by more than  one year of imprisonment. ADJ. felonious. See also misdemeanor.

felony murder.definition: A killing that occurs while committing a felony;  whether the killing occurs accidentally or not, under the felony  murder doctrine it could be prosecuted as first-degree murder; this  doctrine varies from state to state.

fence.definition: (1) A structure made of wood, metal, or some other  material that encloses or divides a piece of land. (2) Someone  who receives and sells stolen property. V. To buy and sell stolen  property.

feudalism.definition: A social, economic, and governmental system  common in medieval Europe, under which nobles gave land to  vassals who fought for them, and peasants farmed the land and  gave much of their produce to their lords, who in return gave them  protection. ADJ. feudal.

fiat.definition: An arbitrary and authoritative command; a decree.

fiduciary.definition: Involving trust, confidence, and good faith;  describes the relationship of a trustee to a beneficiary or a guardian  to a ward. N. Someone entrusted with a duty to act on behalf of and  in the best interest of someone else, especially the management of  someone else’s property; a trustee.

fiduciary capacity.definition: The condition of acting, transacting busi-  ness, or handling money on behalf of someone else.

fiduciary duty.definition: The duty of a person or institution entrusted with  someone else’s property to act in that person’s best interest while  putting aside personal interests; the highest duty imposed by law.

Fifth Amendment.definition: An amendment added to the Constitution as part of the Bill of Rights that provides important protections from government actions, including a guarantee that no person will be required to answer for a capital crime unless first indicted by a grand jury; no one will suffer double jeopardy; no one must testify against him- or herself; no one will be deprived of life, liberty, or property without due process of law; and, no property will be taken for public use without just compensation.

fighting words.definition: Words that in and of themselves cause injury or  could provoke a violent response from listeners; fighting words are  not protected under the First Amendment, and uttering them can  constitute the tort of assault.

file.definition: The documents that comprise a case, including pleadings,  motions, briefs, affidavits, and other papers. V. To deposit a docu-  ment with the court or other proper authority so that it becomes  part of the official record.

file sharing.definition: The practice of sharing computer files over a  computer network such as the Internet.

filibuster.definition: An excessively long speech intended to obstruct  debate and voting on a piece of legislature, especially in the Senate.  V. filibuster.

filing.definition: A document that has been filed as part of a lawsuit. See  also file.

final decision.definition: A decision by the court that ends a dispute  between parties and settles their respective rights, preventing  further litigation of the matter unless it is reversed or set aside; also  called a final judgment.

finance charge.definition: A fee charged for the extension of credit and the  deferral of payment of a loan.

financial institution.definition: A business that deals in money, such as a  bank or trust company, a thrift institution, a currency exchange, a  securities trading company, a credit card company, an insurance  company, etc.

financial statement.definition: A report of the financial condition of a  person or institution, including a balance sheet, an income state-  ment, and charges.

finder.definition: A middleman who helps people find things such as jobs,  business opportunities, or loans in return for payment of a  finder’s fee, and then lets the parties come to their own business  arrangements.

finding.definition: A court’s or jury’s conclusion about a matter of law or  fact.

finding of fact.definition: A court’s or agency’s determinations about the  facts of a case after hearing testimony, examining evidence, and  deliberating.

finding of law.definition: A court’s determination of how to apply law to  the facts of a case.

fine.definition: Money that a person must pay as a penalty for a crime  or wrongful act. V. To punish someone by making him or her  pay a fine.

fine print.definition: Details of a contract, insurance policy, or other docu-  ment that are printed in small type and placed where they will not  be easily noticed or read, making it difficult for someone to learn  about conditions that might be unfavorable.

fingerprint.definition: The unique pattern of lines and whorls on the tip  of a finger, used to identify individuals. V. To take someone’s  fingerprint.

fire.definition: To end or terminate someone’s employment.

firearm.definition: A gun; a device that uses an explosive substance to  propel a projectile from a barrel.

fire sale.definition: A sale of merchandise at reduced prices, usually done  after an emergency such as a fire or flood that destroys or damages  the commercial premises.

firewall.definition: A device or program that limits access to a computer  network.

firm offer.definition: An offer by a merchant to buy or sell goods made in  writing and signed, assuring the other party that the offer will be  held open for a specified period of time; regulated by the Uniform  Commercial Code.

First Amendment.definition: An amendment to the Constitution added with  the Bill of Rights, guaranteeing freedom of speech, assembly, reli-  gion, press, and the right to petition the government for redress of  grievances.

first-degree murder.definition: Killing someone deliberately and intention-  ally, having planned it beforehand, with extreme cruelty, or while  committing another felony.

first impression.definition: The first consideration of something, particu-  larly the first time a case appears before a court and presents an  entirely new question of law that has never been considered  before, and thus has no precedents to guide the decision.

first-in, first-out.definition: An accounting method that assumes goods are  sold in the order purchased; see also last-in, first-out. ABBRV. FIFO.

fiscal.definition: Concerning financial matters, particularly matters of  government finance and the public treasury.

fiscal year.definition: A twelve-month period used by a business as a tax  and accounting period; the accounting year.

fit for a particular purpose.definition: An item’s condition of being suitable  for the purpose for which it is intended, usually as promised by the  seller. See also warranty.

fix.definition: (1) To establish something permanently or for the long-term.  (2) To decide or settle something; to set a price. ADJ. fixed.

fixed assets.definition: The property that a business uses in its daily oper-  ations and will not convert into cash, such as equipment.

fixed capital.definition: The money permanently invested in a company.

fixed income.definition: Regular income that does not fluctuate over time;  income from a retirement benefit or annuity. See also price fixing.

fixture.definition: An object that is permanently attached to a building or  property and is thus regarded as part of the property.

fixture, trade.definition: An object used in business attached to a rented  space by a tenant who runs a business there; trade fixtures remain  the property of the tenant and do not become part of the property.

flee.definition: To run away, usually to escape danger. See also flight.

flee from justice.definition: For someone who has committed a crime to  leave home or the jurisdiction where the crime was committed in  order to avoid arrest.

flexible benefits.definition: An employee benefits program that allows  employees to choose from a range of possible benefits to create  personalized benefits packages. See also cafeteria plan.

flexible spending account.definition: A savings account in which an  employee can save tax-free a portion of his or her earnings to meet

a specific type of expense.definition: FSA. See also cafeteria plan,  health savings account.

flight.definition: The act of running away or trying to escape something;  fleeing. See also flee.

flight from prosecution.definition: The act by someone who has  committed an unlawful act of leaving the scene of the crime in an  effort to avoid arrest.

flim-flam.definition: A type of fraud or confidence game performed by two  perpetrators, one of whom persuades the victim to give money to  the other as part of a get-rich-quick scheme, whereupon both  perpetrators disappear with the money.

float.definition: The time between when a check is deposited and when the  money it represents is deducted from the writer’s account.

floating capital.definition: Capital kept to meet current expenses, often  kept in the form of current assets.

floating debt.definition: Short-term debt held by a business or government.

floating interest rate.definition: A variable interest rate that changes based  on the money market.

FMLA.definition: Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993.

Food and Drug Administration.definition: A federal agency that regulates  the safety of food, drugs, cosmetics, and other household prod-  ucts as part of the Department of Health and Human Services.  ABBRV. FDA.

force.definition: Compulsion; power exerted to make something happen. V.  To make someone do something against his or her will.

force, deadly.definition: Force likely to cause death or serious bodily  injury.

force, unlawful.definition: The use of force against someone who has not  consented to its use.

forced heirs.definition: People who cannot be disinherited by a testator’s  will, such as a spouse or children.

forced sale.definition: A sale made by order of the court to enforce a judg-  ment against the property owner.

force majeure.definition: (French) Superior strength; unforeseeable and  unavoidable circumstances outside the control of a party to a  contract that render him or her incapable of carrying out contrac-  tual duties.

forcible.definition: Done by force, compulsion, or violence.

forcible detainer.definition: (1) Using force to hold someone in custody.  (2) A statutory remedy allowing someone who owns property to  take possession of it from someone who refuses to relinquish it;  see also detainer.

forcible entry.definition: Using force or threat of force to illegally enter  property that belongs to someone else against the owner’s will.

forcible entry and detainer.definition: A statutory proceeding that returns  property to someone who has been wrongfully deprived of it.

foreclose.definition: To end someone’s right to a property; to take posses-  sion of mortgaged property as a result of the property owner’s  failure to make payments. N. foreclosure.

foreign.definition: From another country, state, or jurisdiction.

foreign corporation.definition: A corporation that does business in one  state but is incorporated in another.

foreign court.definition: A court in another country or state.

foreign national.definition: A person who is in one country but who is a  legal permanent resident of another.

foreman.definition: (1) A juror who leads jury proceedings and speaks to  the court on the entire jury’s behalf. (2) A worker in a leadership  position over his or her coworkers. Also called a foreperson.

forensic engineering.definition: A branch of engineering that applies engi-  neering principles to legal matters.

forensic medicine.definition: A branch of medicine that applies medical  principles to the investigation of legal matters, especially the inves-  tigation of crimes that result in death.

forensics.definition: The application of scientific tests and techniques to  the investigation of crimes.

foresee.definition: To predict; to know in advance what is likely to occur in  a given situation. ADJ. foreseeable. N. foreseeability.

foreseeable risk.definition: The risk that a person of ordinary intelligence  and prudence should reasonably expect to occur.

forfeitable.definition: Able to be given up or taken as a punishment.

forfeiture.definition: The taking of property without compensation, usually  as a punishment for breaking the law.

forge.definition: To make a copy or imitation of something intending to  pass it off as the genuine article; see also counterfeit.

forgery.definition: The copying of a document, signature, work of art, or  bank note with the intention of deceiving someone into thinking it  is genuine.

form.definition: (1) A model or frame of a legal document with blank  spaces to be filled out with information that applies to an individual  situation. (2) Customary procedure or formalities; the way some-  thing is expressed as opposed to what is expressed.

Form 1040.definition: The form that most U.S. taxpayers must fill out to  file their income tax returns.

forms of action.definition: The categories of legal actions available under  common law, such as trespass, debt, etc., and divided into actions  at equity and actions at law; now all forms of action are gathered  together under the Rules of Civil Procedure into the single heading  “civil action.”

fornication.definition: Sexual relations between two unmarried people, or  by an unmarried person (who commits fornication) and a married  person (who commits adultery); definitions vary by jurisdiction. V.  fornicate.

forthwith.definition: Right away; immediately; at the first opportunity.

fortuitous.definition: Happening by chance rather than design. N.  fortuity.

forum.definition: A court or tribunal; a place where people can try to get a  judicial or administrative remedy for some wrong.

forum non conveniens.definition: (Latin) An inconvenient court; a court’s  discretionary power to refuse to hear a case that is within its juris-  diction if it would be more convenient for the parties or better  serve the interests of justice for it to be heard elsewhere.

forum shopping.definition: An attempt by a litigant to have the case tried in  a specific jurisdiction that appears likely to rule in his or her favor.

found.definition: (1) To establish something, usually an institution or  charity, often by providing an endowment for it. (2) To serve as a  basis for something.

foundation.definition: (1) The lowest part of a building that supports the  weight of the entire structure. (2) The establishment of an institu-  tion, hospital, charity, school, etc. (3) A fund that supports an insti-  tution, hospital, charity, school, etc. (4) Evidence and questions of  a witness used to establish the relevance of other evidence, thus  making it admissible.

founder.definition: One who establishes a charity or institution by  endowing it or supplying initial funds.

Founding Fathers.definition: The men who created the U.S. Constitution in  1787.

four corners rule.definition: A rule requiring that the meaning of a docu-  ment and the intention of its creator be determined by considering  it as a whole and not in isolated parts.

franchise.definition: (1) The right to vote. (2) A privilege granted by the government or a company to a person or company, allowing it to engage in a specific activity not permitted to most citizens; a license from the owner of a trademark to sell products under that name, under which the franchisee agrees to follow certain rules and the franchisor promises assistance through advertising and other services. (3) A business owned by a franchisee.

franchisee.definition: A person who owns a franchise of a business.

franchisor.definition: A business or other entity that sells the right to  engage in a particular activity or run a particular business.

fraternal benefit association.definition: An organization whose members  either belong to the same professional field or who are working  toward the same goal that exists to promote a common cause and  assist its members with benefits for death, injury, or illness; also  called a fraternal benefits society.

fraud.definition: Intentional misrepresentation of the truth done to cause  someone else to rely on that misrepresentation and be deceived  into surrendering a legal right or otherwise being injured. ADJ.  fraudulent. V. defraud.

fraud, actual.definition: A misrepresentation done specifically to cheat  someone; also called fraud in fact.

fraud, constructive.definition: An act or omission that might not be  intended to deceive, but does anyway; also called fraud in law.

fraud, extrinsic.definition: Fraud that prevents a party from knowing  about and therefore defending or pursuing all legal rights available  to him or her.

fraud, intrinsic.definition: Fraudulent information, such as perjury, that is  considered by the court in reaching a judgment.

fraud in the factum.definition: Fraud that arises when a person signs a  document that is not the document he or she believes is being  signed.

fraud in the inducement.definition: Fraud in which a person is misled  about the details of a transaction or contract and enters into an  agreement under a false impression about what is involved.

fraudulent concealment.definition: Hiding a material fact that legally or  morally should be disclosed.

fraudulent conveyance.definition: A transfer of property done to hinder a  creditor or place the property out of a creditor’s reach.

fraudulent misrepresentation.definition: A false statement about a mate-  rial fact made by a speaker who knows it is false but intends the  hearer to believe it and rely on it, and that injures the hearer if he  or she does rely on it.

freedom.definition: Liberty; absence of restrictions or restraints.

freedom of contract.definition: The right to enter into contracts and agree-  ments with others, guaranteed by Article I of the Constitution.

Freedom of Information Act.definition: A federal law, 5 U.S.C.A. section  552, that attempts to prevent abuse of governmental power by  requiring federal agencies to make documents and information  about agency matters available to the general public, with a few  exceptions. ABBRV. FOIA.

freedom of press.definition: The right to publish views and opinions  without interference by the government, guaranteed by the First  Amendment of the Constitution.

freedom of speech.definition: The right to express one’s opinions verbally  or in writing without interference or restriction from the govern-  ment, guaranteed by the First Amendment of the Constitution.

free exercise clause.definition: A clause in the First Amendment of the  Constitution stating that Congress will pass no law prohibiting the  free exercise of religion; see also establishment clause.

freehold.definition: A piece of land held in fee or as a life estate; property  that the owner holds at least for life and might also be able to pass  on after death; see also leasehold.

free on board.definition: Describes a contractual agreement by a seller  to deliver goods to a specified place without expense to the buyer,  in which the seller assumes all delivery expenses and risks; such a  contract is called an f.o.b. contract. ABBRV. FOB.

freeware.definition: Software made available by its copyright holder for  use for an unlimited time free of charge. See also public domain,  shareware.

freeze-out.definition: A process in which a close corporation tries to force  minority shareholders to sell their stock on terms favorable to the  majority shareholders by preventing the minority shareholders  from getting any profit from their investment. See also squeeze-out.

freight.definition: (1) Goods transported by a carrier such as a truck, train,  aircraft, or ship. (2) The cost of transporting goods by a carrier. V.  To transport goods by a carrier.

freighter.definition: (1) A ship or airplane used to transport cargo. (2) A  person or company in the business of transporting goods.

freight forwarder.definition: A business that consolidates small shipments  of goods so they can all be sent as a single lot and assumes respon-  sibility for their transport.

frequently asked questions.definition: A list of questions frequently asked  about a topic followed by their answers. ABBRV. FAQ.

fresh pursuit.definition: Pursuit by police officers of a suspected felon  fleeing the scene of the crime done immediately after the criminal  act and without delay, which provides the officers with the  common law right to cross state lines to make the arrest; also  called hot pursuit.

friendly suit.definition: A lawsuit brought by parties who agree on a partic-  ular settlement but need a judicial decision to make it binding; see  also declaratory judgment.

fringe benefit.definition: A benefit that supplements an employee’s pay,  such as health insurance, profit-sharing plans, or recreational facil-  ities.

frisk.definition: A quick physical search of a person to feel for concealed  weapons done by running hands over the body over clothing.

frivolous.definition: Unimportant; not serious; lacking in substance;  presenting no legitimate legal question.

frivolous appeal.definition: An appeal that has no merit, presents no justi-  ciable question, and has no hope of success.

fruit of the poisonous tree doctrine.definition: The principle that evidence  obtained for a criminal trial through an illegal search or illegal  interrogation of a witness is tainted and therefore inadmissible  against the defendant because it violates the guarantee of due  process.

fruits of a crime.definition: The items acquired by a criminal through crime.

frustration of purpose.definition: A situation that arises when a condition  that was part of a contract does not occur, through no fault of  either party, but the condition was the reason for one party  entering the contract in the first place; can be cause for terminating  the contract.

fugitive.definition: Someone who flees or hides in an attempt to evade  capture.

fugitive from justice.definition: A person who flees to another state or  hides after committing a crime, hoping to avoid arrest.

full faith and credit.definition: The constitutional requirement that all  states must recognize the laws, judicial decisions, and public  records of all other states and must give them the same degree of  recognition that their own laws, judicial decisions, and public  records receive.

fund.definition: An amount of money saved or intended for a particular  purpose. V. To supply money for a particular purpose.

fund, general.definition: A fund that is not restricted to a specific purpose  but can be used for general expenses. See also mutual fund,  sinking fund.

funded debt.definition: A debt that has a fund allocated for its payment.

fund in court.definition: An amount of money deposited with the court  either because the parties to a lawsuit both claim it, or to keep it in  reserve to pay for potential liability.

fungible.definition: Interchangeable; describes goods that are essentially  identical to one another and that can replace one another, e.g., oil,  grain, or money.

future interest.definition: An interest in real or personal property that will  begin at some specified time in the future.

futures contract.definition: An agreement to buy shares of a commodity  at a fixed price but with delivery and payment occurring at an  agreed-upon date in the future; if the price of the commodity  rises during that period, the seller pays the buyer the difference  between the agreed price and the current price, and if the price  of the commodity drops, the buyer pays the seller the difference.

gag order.definition: (1) An order issued by a judge prohibiting participants  in a lawsuit from discussing the case publicly in order to protect  the litigants’ right to an impartial trial; gag orders may not usually  be imposed on the press. (2) An order to restrain or silence a  disruptive defendant in a criminal trial.

gain.definition: Profit; growth of wealth, property, or value. V. gain. ADJ.  gainful.

gainful employment.definition: A job or an occupation that earns money or  generates profit; employment for pay that is suited to the abilities  of the worker; also called gainful occupation.

gamble.definition: To bet; to play games of chance for money. N. A risky  enterprise that could turn out as either a profit or a loss.

gambling.definition: Betting; playing games of chance for money.

game.definition: Wild animals that can be hunted.

game laws.definition: Federal and state laws that regulate the hunting of  animals for sport or food.

game of chance.definition: A game in which the outcome is determined  primarily by luck and chance rather than skill.

garnish.definition: To seize someone’s money, property, or wages to settle  a debt or claim.

garnishee.definition: The third party who is ordered by the court to  surrender money or property owed to a debtor so that it can be  used to pay the debtor’s debts.

garnishment.definition: A court order that takes the property or money  that a third party owes to a debtor (typically wages owed to the  debtor by an employer) and gives it to a plaintiff creditor.

GATT.definition: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade.

General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade.definition: An international  agreement that states guidelines and rules for international trade.  ABBRV. GATT.

gender.definition: The condition of being female or male, with emphasis on  cultural implications of masculinity or femininity rather than  biological and physical differences; the members of the female or  male sex; see also sex.

general.definition: Not specialized; concerning an entire class or cate-  gory as opposed to individuals. N. The highest-ranking kind of  officer in the army.

general bequest.definition: A bequest to be paid out of the general assets  of an estate rather than a particular thing or money.

general election.definition: An election taking place in an entire state to  elect offices that come open regularly.

general exception.definition: An objection to a pleading for lack of  substance.

general jurisdiction.definition: Jurisdiction covering all types of cases that  can be brought before a court.

general law.definition: A law that affects all people of a state or class.

general public license.definition: A free software license used by software  producers to ensure that all versions of software licensed by  general public license will remain freely available. See also open  source. ABBRV. GPL.

general reputation.definition: The character of a witness as portrayed by  testimony of people who know him or her.

general welfare.definition: The health, peace, safety, and morality of the  citizenry, promoted by Article I of the Constitution.

generation.definition: (1) The period of time it takes a person to be born,  grow up, and have children of his or her own, usually between  twenty and thirty years. (2) A set of family members all born  around the same time and at the same stage of descent from a  common ancestor.

generation-skipping transfer.definition: A transfer of wealth that passes  over one generation to go down to a younger one, e.g., a transfer  from grandparents straight to grandchildren.

generation-skipping trust.definition: A trust created to facilitate a  generation-skipping transfer.

generic.definition: General, not specific; covering a whole class of  things.

generic drug laws.definition: Laws that allow pharmacists to substitute  generic drugs for equivalent brand-name drugs in order to save  consumers money.

generic mark.definition: A name of an item that is so general and  commonly used as to lack the distinctiveness necessary to make it  eligible for trademark protection.

Geneva Conventions.definition: Four treaties created in Geneva,  Switzerland, between 1863 and 1949 that set standards for how  member nations should treat prisoners of war and civilians during  wartime. See also jus in bello, laws of war.

genital mutilation.definition: The practice of modifying genital organs by  cutting off portions of them; usually done to young girls.

geopolitical.definition: Having to do with the combined effects of geog-  raphy and politics.

gerrymander.definition: To manipulate boundaries in dividing a state or  territory into political divisions in order to secure an advantage for  a certain party in elections. N. gerrymander.

gift.definition: Something given to someone else willingly and without  expectation of payment; a voluntary and intentional giving of prop-  erty by a competent donor that is delivered to and accepted by the  recipient without exchange of consideration.

gift, absolute.definition: A gift given while the donor is still alive; also  called a gift inter vivos.

gift in contemplation of death.definition: A gift that is promised to  someone by a donor who expects to die soon that will take effect  if the donor does die as expected, but for which the donor retains  the title while he or she is still living, and that becomes invalid if the  donor recovers and does not die of the illness that he or she  suffered from when the gift was made; also called a gift causa  mortis.

gift over.definition: A gift that occurs after an intermediate estate ends; e.g.,  if A gives B a life estate with a gift over to C, then C takes B’s estate  when B dies.

going concern.definition: A company or enterprise currently conducting  its ordinary business.

going concern value.definition: The value of a firm that is currently  conducting business, which includes goodwill and other aspects  that make it more valuable than its mere assets.

good cause.definition: A good reason for doing or not doing something; a  reason for one’s behavior that is substantial enough to constitute a  legal excuse, determined by the context of an individual case.

good faith.definition: Sincerity, honesty, lack of deceit; a sincere intention  to do what is promised.

good Samaritan.definition: Someone who voluntarily aids a person who is  in imminent danger or has been injured without expectation of  compensation; a good Samaritan is not held liable for injury to the  victim unless he or she fails to use reasonable care and the victim  is injured further by the efforts at assistance.

good title.definition: A title to property that is valid and can be sold without  worry; a title free from doubts, encumbrances, and defects, and  that is not the subject of litigation; also called merchantable title.  Synonymous with clear title, marketable title.

goods.definition: Personal property, chattels; all property that is not land  or buildings, including animals and crops and all other movable  items.

goods, durable.definition: Items that are not consumed when used and  thus last a long time, such as household appliances; also called  hard goods.

goods, soft.definition: Goods made of fabric, such as clothing or bedding,  or textiles themselves; also called dry goods. See also consumer  goods.

goodwill.definition: An intangible business asset composed of a good repu-  tation with customers, suppliers, and the community at large, good  employee morale, the ability to attract customers and clients, good

govern.definition: To control and regulate the affairs of an organization,  institution, nation, state, or group of people.

governing body.definition: A group or entity that controls the actions and  policies of an institution, nation, state, group, or organization.

government.definition: The body that controls, influences, and regu-  lates the affairs of a nation, state, or community. ADJ. govern-  mental.

governmental act.definition: An act done by the government or its agents  that uses legislative, judicial, or administrative powers for the  public good.

governmental immunity.definition: A doctrine limiting the federal govern-  ment’s power to tax states and the states’ power to tax the federal  government.

governor.definition: The elected executive leader of a state.

grace period.definition: A period between an official due date and the time  when an actual penalty will be imposed for failure to pay or  perform; in insurance, the time between the due date of a premium  and the time when insurance will actually be cut off if payment is  not made.

graded offense.definition: A crime that has different degrees and penalties  depending on level of guilt; e.g. murder can come in first, second,  or third degree. See also degree of crime.

graft.definition: Corrupt practices used by public officials to unlawfully  take public money; money obtained by corruption.

grandfather clause.definition: A clause in a new statute that exempts those  who are already engaged in the enterprise or activity it regulates  from the new requirements.

grand jury.definition: A group of people who are selected to investigate an  alleged crime and indict suspected criminals. See also petit jury.

grant.definition: To give; to bestow something on someone; to agree (to a  request). N. (1) A legal conveyance of property. (2) A sum of money  given by the government or an organization for a particular  purpose.

grant, land.definition: A donation of land by the government to a state or  institution such as a university.

grantee.definition: A person who receives a grant.

gratuitous.definition: (1) Done for free; given without expectation of  payment. (2) Unjustified; done without good reason.

gratuitous promise.definition: A promise made without consideration,  making it unenforceable as a legal contract.

gratuity.definition: Money or other property given freely, usually in return  for some service or favor.

gravamen.definition: The essence or most serious part of a complaint,  cause of action, charge, etc.

gray market.definition: An unofficial trade in something, especially of unis-  sued shares of stock, controlled or scarce goods, or goods manu-  factured abroad that use U.S. trademarks without permission of  the trademark holders.

grievance.definition: A complaint; an allegation that someone has  committed some injury or injustice that deserves recompense; in  labor law, a complaint about working conditions filed by a worker  or a union in order to seek relief.

gross.definition: (1) Of income or profit, total, before deductions. (2)  Extreme; blatant. Gross income. N. All income from all sources. See  also negligence.

gross national product.definition: The total value of goods and services  produced by a country in one year plus net income from foreign  exports and investments.

ground lease.definition: A lease of vacant land with no buildings on it,  usually rented in order to build structures such as office buildings  or hotels on it; ground leases often run for many years and are  renewable by the lessor.

ground rent.definition: Rent paid for the use of land, usually to build on it.

growth stock.definition: A security that is likely to increase in market  value.

guarantee.definition: (1) A promise or formal statement that specific  conditions are the case or will be met. (2) A person who receives a  guarantee. V. guarantee.

guarantee clause.definition: (1) A clause in the Constitution in which the  U.S. government guarantees each state a republican form of  government and promises to protect each of the states against  invasion and against domestic violence. (2) A clause in a contract  in which one party promises to pay someone else’s obligations if  necessary.

guarantor.definition: A person or organization that guarantees something;  one who makes a guarantee.

guardian.definition: A person who looks after the legal and financial affairs  or takes care of someone who is unable to look after his or her own  because of age or disability; see also ad litem, ward.

guardian by election.definition: A guardian chosen by a child who lacks  parents.

guardian, testamentary.definition: A guardian appointed by the will of a  child’s father or mother.

guest.definition: Someone who receives hospitality; someone invited into  another person’s home for entertainment or driven by another  person in a car without paying for the ride.

guest statute.definition: A statute that absolves a driver from liability  injury to a passenger in the car if the injury was the result of ordi-  nary negligence, requiring that the driver exhibit gross or willful  negligence, reckless disregard, or some other higher level of care-  lessness to be liable; many states have repealed these statutes.

guilt.definition: (1) The condition of having committed a crime or wrongful  act. (2) A feeling of remorse after committing a wrongful act or  failing in some obligation.

guilty.definition: (1) Having committed a crime; culpable; having been  determined by a jury to have committed a specific crime. (2)  Remorseful; see also innocent.

guilty plea.definition: A voluntary admission of having committed a crime  made to a court by a criminal defendant, punishable in the same  way as a guilty verdict after trial.

gun control law.definition: A law regulating the sale, possession, and use  of guns and firearms.

habeas corpus.definition: (Latin) You have the body; a writ that institutes  a court proceeding to determine whether a criminal defendant has  been lawfully imprisoned, or to test the constitutionality of a  conviction; also used in cases of child custody and deportation.

habitable.definition: Referring to a building or dwelling, of good enough  quality to be lived in or occupied without serious inconvenience or  danger to health and safety. N. habitability.

habitual drunkenness.definition: The practice of frequently drinking to the  point of intoxication.

habitual offender.definition: A person who regularly commits crimes; also  called a habitual criminal. Synonymous with recidivist.

hack.definition: To break into a closed computer network by using soft-  ware or clever programming. N. A quick fix to a computer program-  ming problem; a modification to a program that allows the user to  enter an area of the program or network that was previously closed  to the user.

hale.definition: To drag forcibly; to summon forcefully into court.

halfway house.definition: A temporary residence for people who have left  an institution, such as a prison or mental hospital, to help them  readjust to life in society.

handwriting.definition: Writing done by hand with a hand-held implement  such as a pen or pencil; the unique writing produced by a particular  person and identifiable as his or her production.

hanging.definition: A form of capital punishment in which the convicted  person has a rope placed around his or her neck and is then hung  until dead. V. hang.

harassment, hostile environment.definition: Harassment that is deter-  mined by a pattern of sexual behavior by supervisors or fellow  employees that creates an environment that the victim finds  intolerable.

harassment, quid pro quo.definition: Sexual harassment in which a supe-  rior demands sexual favors from an employee as a condition of his  or her continued employment, threatening to fire the employee if  the demands are not met.

harassment, sexual.definition: Employment discrimination that involves  sexual demands and acts, which can involve perpetrators and  victims of either sex.

harbor.definition: (1) To provide shelter to someone. (2) To hide or take in  a criminal or alien, usually secretly. N. (1) A protected body of  water for anchoring ships. (2) A safe place; a refuge.

hard case.definition: A legal case in which the judicial decision accommo-  dates the extreme hardship faced by one party by deviating from  strict legal principles.

hardship.definition: Suffering, privation, difficulty, oppression. See unnec-  essary hardship.

hardware.definition: Computer equipment; the physical devices that make  up a computer system. See also software.

harmless error.definition: An error made by an appellate court during a  trial that is usually trivial or academic, does not prejudice the rights  of the party affected by it, and does not necessitate the reversal of  the judgment.

headnote.definition: A short summary of a case and its legal holdings  placed at the beginning of the case report.

head of household.definition: (1) A person who lives with and supports  one or more people living in the same home, and generally  manages household affairs. (2) An unmarried taxpayer who houses  and supports one or more dependents.

healthcare.definition: The prevention and treatment of diseases and  injuries by medical professionals and institutions.

Health Insurance Portability and Account.definition: A federal  law enacted in 1996 that allows many workers to maintain health  insurance coverage when they lose or change jobs and that  protects the privacy of health data. ABBRV. HIPAA.

health maintenance organization.definition: A form of health insurance  consisting of a group of physicians, hospitals, and other health care  providers that join together to provide health care services for  people who pay a fee to the group. ABBRV. HMO.

health plan.definition: A health insurance plan; a benefits plan that  provides medical care through a predetermined network of  doctors, clinics, and hospitals. See also insurance.

health savings account.definition: A savings account available to people  who participate in a high deductible health plan that allows them  to save pretax earnings to pay for qualified medical expenses and  carry over their savings from year to year. ABBRV. HSA.

hearing.definition: A legal proceeding, usually less formal than a trial, in  which the parties to a case are given an opportunity to present  evidence and testimony to a judge or other official who determines  the facts and makes a decision based on the evidence presented.

hearing, preliminary.definition: An initial hearing in a criminal case in  which a magistrate or judge decides whether there is sufficient  evidence to justify detaining a person accused of a crime.

hearing de novo.definition: A hearing in which a matter that has already  gone through trial is presented again as if for the first time, starting  over from the beginning.

hearsay.definition: A report made by a witness of something that another  person said or communicated nonverbally, usually not admissible  as evidence.

heat of passion.definition: The mental and emotional state caused by  sudden anger, hatred, terror, or extreme excitement that provokes  someone to commit a crime.

hedge.definition: To protect an investment by making a compensatory or  counterbalancing transaction. N. hedging.

hedge fund.definition: A mutual fund or partnership of investors that uses  hedging techniques such as arbitrage, futures contracts, and selling  short in an attempt to maximize profits.

heir.definition: A person legally entitled to inherit an estate if its owner dies  without a will.

heir, collateral.definition: An heir who is not directly descended from a  decedent but instead related collaterally, such as a cousin, niece, or  nephew.

heir, lineal.definition: An heir descended directly from the decedent, such  as a child or grandchild.

heir apparent.definition: A person who has a legal right to inherit an estate  if he or she outlives its owner, and whose right to the property  cannot be defeated.

heirloom.definition: A valuable object that is passed down through gener-  ations of a family.

heir of the body.definition: An heir who is the natural child or direct lineal  descendant of the decedent.

heir presumptive.definition: A person who would inherit a property if the  owner were to die immediately, but whose right could be defeated  if a nearer heir is born first.

heirs and assigns.definition: A phrase used in conveying an estate in fee  simple, used to define and limit the class of people who can inherit  the estate.

hereditament.definition: A thing that can be inherited.

hereditary.definition: Transmittable through or based on inheritance.

hereditary succession.definition: Inheritance; the passing of estates  through the laws of descent and distribution.

hereunder.definition: Within or further on in the document.

hidden asset.definition: An asset recorded on a company’s books at much  less than its market value.

hidden tax.definition: A tax built into the price of an item, usually incurred  at some point in manufacturing or distribution.

high deductible health plan.definition: A health insurance plan that  attempts to reduce costs by imposing a high deductible on medical  expenses, on the reasoning that if consumers must pay for most  medical care themselves they will avoid unnecessary care and shop  for the lowest prices when they do seek treatment. ABBRV. hdhp.  See also health savings account.

hijack.definition: To commandeer a vehicle such as an airplane, bus, or  ship, while in transit, in order to take it to a different destination, to  steal its cargo, or to take hostages. N. hijacking.

HIPAA.definition: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act.

hire.definition: To give someone a job; to take someone on as an employee.  N. A person who has been given a job.

hit and run.definition: Describes an automobile accident in which the  driver of one of the vehicles involved immediately leaves the scene  without identifying himself or herself or providing statutorily  required information to the police or other drivers and passengers.

hoard.definition: To acquire and keep goods or money beyond what is  needed, often done in times of scarcity. N. hoarding.

hobby.definition: An activity engaged in for pleasure during leisure time,  not done for profit.

hobby loss.definition: A loss incurred from a hobby, usually not deductible.

holder.definition: A person with legal possession of a document of title,  promissory note, check, or other instrument, and who is entitled to  receive payment on it.

holder in due course.definition: A bona fide purchaser who acquires an  instrument in good faith, for value, and without notice of any claim  against the instrument.

hold harmless.definition: To assume the liability for a situation, thereby  absolving another party of any responsibility for it. See also indem-  nify, save harmless.

holding.definition: (1) A court’s ruling on an issue presented at trial; a legal  principle produced by a court in deciding a case. (2) Property or  stocks owned by someone.

holding company.definition: A company that exists solely to own the stock  of or manage the affairs of another company.

holding period.definition: The period of time an asset must be held before  its sale or exchange will result in a capital gain or loss.

holdup suit.definition: A lawsuit without a legal basis instituted to delay or  obstruct some occurrence.

holograph.definition: A will written by hand by a testator, signed by the  testator, and not witnessed; also called a holographic will.

home equity loan.definition: A loan in which a lender gives money to a  borrower in exchange for a lien on the borrower’s house, using the  borrower’s equity in his or her house as collateral.

Homeland Security.definition: The U.S. government’s collective efforts to  keep the territory of the United States free from hazards, including  natural disasters such as hurricanes and man-made disasters such  as terrorist attacks.

home port doctrine.definition: A doctrine in maritime law holding that a  vessel engaged in foreign and interstate commerce is subject to  property tax only at its home port.

home rule.definition: A means of apportioning power between state and  local governments that allows local entities a certain degree of  independent self-government, allowing them to pass laws without  first getting permission from the state.

homestead.definition: The residence of a family, including house, land, and  outbuildings.

homestead, probate.definition: A home taken from a probate estate by the  court for the use of the surviving spouse and children of the  deceased in order to guarantee them a place to live.

homestead exemption.definition: A doctrine that allows a homeowner to  designate his or her home as a homestead and thereby exempt it  from claims by creditors.

homicide.definition: The killing of one human being by another.

homicide, excusable.definition: The killing of a person by accident or in  self-defense.

homicide, justifiable.definition: The intentional killing of a person without  evil intent in circumstances that make the killing necessary, such as  self-defense or the execution of a criminal sentenced to death.

homicide, negligent.definition: The killing of a person through the killer’s  negligence.

homicide, vehicular.definition: The killing of a person through the negli-  gent or reckless operation of a motor vehicle such as an automo-  bile, airplane, or boat.

honor.definition: To accept a check, note, credit card, or other form of  payment. V. A title of courtesy given to a judge, as in the phrase  “Your Honor” or the adjective “Honorable.”

honorable discharge.definition: A declaration by the government that a  soldier is leaving military service with a good record.

honorarium.definition: A payment of money or a valuable item rendered to  a professional in exchange for a service for which a fee is tradition-  ally or legally not paid; a gift given instead of pay as consideration  for services.

honorary.definition: Conferred as an honor or reward without the usual  requirements or responsibilities.

hornbook.definition: A short treatise that summarizes the fundamental  principles of a branch of law; a primer.

hornbook law.definition: General principles of law that are widely known  and not questioned; see also black letter law.

hostage.definition: A person held by a criminal or an enemy who threatens  to kill or harm the hostage if demands are not met or promises are  broken. See also kidnap.

hostile possession.definition: Occupying and claiming ownership of a  property without permission of the owner.

hostile witness.definition: A witness who is so obviously antagonistic to the party who has called him or her that the party is allowed to cross-examine him or her as if he or she had been called by the opposing party.

house.definition: (1) A building or structure used as a dwelling. (2) A  legislative body. V. To provide a home for someone.

House of Commons.definition: The lower house of the British Parliament,  consisting of representatives from all the common people of the  realm.

House of Lords.definition: The upper house of the British Parliament,  consisting of representatives from the nobility.

house of prostitution.definition: A building in which prostitutes work.

House of Representatives.definition: The larger of the two legislative  bodies that make up Congress, consisting of elected representa-  tives from each state whose number is set by population; often  called the House.

house of worship.definition: A church or other building used for religious  services.

humanitarian.definition: Working to promote the welfare and preserve  the dignity of human beings.

human resources.definition: The management of personnel and issues  surrounding employees, such as hiring, firing, pay, discipline, and  benefits.

human rights.definition: The basic rights to which all humans are generally  considered to be entitled, which can include life, liberty, freedom  of speech, freedom of religion, due process, equal rights, and  dignity.

hung jury.definition: A jury that cannot agree on a verdict.

husband-wife privilege.definition: Privilege covering spouses, who are not  required to reveal confidential communications between them  while married.

hypnosis.definition: A state of consciousness in which the subject loses the  power to control his or her actions and becomes very sensitive to  suggestion. V. hypnotize.

hypothecate.definition: To pledge money or property for a particular  purpose, often as security for a debt.

hypothesis.definition: A supposition; a proposed explanation for an occur-  rence based on available evidence, serving as the beginning of  further exploration of the question. See also theory.

hypothetical.definition: Based on assumptions but not necessarily true.

hypothetical question.definition: A question in which the questioner asks  someone to offer an opinion based on the assumption that certain  conditions are true.

ICANN.definition: Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and  Numbers.

ignorance.definition: The state of being unaware of something; lack of  knowledge. ADJ. ignorant.

illegal.definition: Forbidden by law or not according to law; unlawful.

illegal immigrant.definition: A foreign national present in a country  without legal permission to be there.

illegally obtained evidence.definition: Evidence acquired by violating a  person’s constitutional protection against illegal searches and  seizures; evidence obtained without a warrant or probable cause.

illegitimate.definition: (1) Not authorized by law; illegal. (2) Born to  parents not married to one another at the time of conception.

illusory.definition: Not real; deceptive; having a false appearance.

illusory promise.definition: A promise with such vague terms that it actually promises nothing because the person making it is allowed to choose whether or not to do the promised act and does not in fact agree to do anything.

immaterial.definition: Irrelevant; not essential; of no material impor-  tance. See also material.

immaterial evidence.definition: Evidence that has no real bearing on the  issue in question.

immigrant.definition: A person who moves to a foreign country to live  there.

immigrate.definition: To move to a foreign country with the intention of  living there permanently. N. immigration.

Immigration and Naturalization Services.definition: The federal agency  that registers immigrants and oversees the process of making them  into citizens. See also naturalize. ABBRV. INS.

immoral.definition: Against accepted moral standards; dissolute. N.  immorality.

immoral conduct.definition: Conduct that is willful, flagrant, or shameless,  and in disregard of the moral opinions of respectable members of  the community.

immunity.definition: Exemption or protection from something such as  prosecution, duty, or penalty.

immunity, official.definition: Personal immunity given to public officials,  protecting them from liability for injury caused by acts they have  done as part of their official duty. See also sovereign immunity.

impanel.definition: To list or enroll the jurors who have been selected for  a trial.

impartial.definition: Fair, unbiased; not favoring one side over another.

impeach.definition: To accuse a public official of misconduct; to question  the validity of something, such as a judgment, or the integrity of  a witness.

impeachment.definition: A criminal procedure in which a public official is  charged with misconduct.

implication.definition: A conclusion that can be drawn from information  but that is not expressly stated; a likely consequence.

implied.definition: Strongly suggested by circumstances and evidence  but not directly stated; see also express.

implied authority.definition: Power given to an agent by a principal that is  not expressly stated but that can be assumed as a necessary part of  the agent’s job.

implied powers.definition: Powers not expressly stated or enumerated but  that can be implied as necessary to do a particular job, sometimes  found by the Supreme Court in its interpretation of the  Constitution.

imply.definition: To suggest strongly that something is true without stating  it directly. See also infer.

import.definition: To bring goods into a country for sale. N. Goods brought  from another country for sale. See also export.

importer.definition: A person who imports goods. See also customs.

impossibility.definition: A situation that makes it impossible to do something; circumstances that make it impossible to perform a contract, such as the destruction of something necessary to the performance or the death of one of the parties, which relieves a party of his or her duty to perform under the contract. ADJ. impossible. See also impracticable.

impossibility, legal.definition: A condition arising when a law makes it  impossible for a person to do a particular act; e.g., a minor cannot  make a valid will, so any will made by a minor is invalid because it  is a legal impossibility.

imposter.definition: Someone who pretends to be someone else with the  intention of deceiving others, usually to gain some benefit.

impound.definition: To seize and take legal custody of something, such as  a vehicle; to capture animals and shut them in an enclosure.

impracticable.definition: So inconvenient or difficult as to be essentially  impossible. N. impracticability.

imprimatur.definition: (Latin) Let it be printed; a license to print and  publish a book.

imprison.definition: To put in prison; to keep someone restricted to a place  that essentially functions as a prison; to confine; to deprive of  liberty.

improvement.definition: A change that makes something better; an addi-  tion or change to buildings or land that goes beyond mere repairs  and enhances its value, utility, or attractiveness.

impute.definition: To attribute; to assign someone responsibility for the act  of another person over whom the first person exercises control.

imputed knowledge.definition: Knowledge that is attributed to a person  because he or she had a duty to know it and it was available.

in absentia.definition: (Latin) In absence; not physically present at an  event.

inadmissible.definition: Not able to be admitted under the rules of  evidence.

inalienable.definition: Not able to be taken away or given up without  consent of the possessor.

inalienable interest.definition: A property interest that cannot be sold or  transferred.

inalienable rights.definition: Rights such as freedom of speech, equal  protection of the laws, and due process that cannot be taken away  from or given up by anyone without his or her consent.

in camera.definition: (Latin) In chambers; done in a judge’s private  chambers.

incapacity.definition: Inability to do or understand something; lack of  physical, mental, or legal ability to do a particular task.

incarcerate.definition: To put in jail or prison; to imprison. N. incarcera-  tion.

incendiary.definition: Able to start a fire. N. A person or device that starts  fires.

incest.definition: Sexual contact between people who are so closely  related to one another by blood that they are prohibited by law  from marrying.

inchoate.definition: Not yet fully developed; incomplete. See also choate.

inchoate crime.definition: A crime that could constitute the initial stages  of another crime; e.g., assault could be an inchoate form of battery.

inchoate interest.definition: A potential future interest in property.

incident.definition: (1) An event. (2) Something that is inseparably  connected with something else. ADJ. Attached to something in a  subordinate way; dependent on something.

incidental.definition: Accompanying something else, usually in a subor-  dinate way; occurring in connection with something else by chance  or as a consequence.

incidental beneficiary.definition: A person who is indirectly benefited by  a contract formed between other people.

incidental powers.definition: Powers that are a necessary accompaniment  to powers expressly granted.

incident of ownership.definition: Partial or complete ownership or control  of a life insurance policy.

inclosure.definition: A piece of land surrounded by a fence, wall, or other  barrier; also called an enclosure.

income.definition: Money received as compensation for work, through  investments, or as profits from a business.

income, accrued.definition: Income earned but not yet received.

income, deferred.definition: Income received before it is actually earned.

income, earned.definition: Income brought in through work or business,  as opposed to income from investments.

income, imputed.definition: Value acquired by a taxpayer through use of  his or her own property, or by performing work services for himself  or herself, and not included in gross income.

income, net.definition: A business’s income minus operating expenses and  taxes.

income in respect of a decedent.definition: Income earned by a taxpayer  but received by his or her heirs or personal representatives after  his or her death.

income statement.definition: A statement of financial gains and losses over  a twelve-month period; also called earnings report, operating state-  ment, profit and loss statement.

income tax.definition: Tax levied on a person’s income by the federal  government and some state governments.

incompetent.definition: Lacking the skills or capability necessary to do  something; not legally qualified to perform a particular duty. N. A  person who lacks the ability or qualification to do something. N.  incompetence.

incorporate.definition: (1) To combine something into something else  as part of one whole. (2) To create a corporation. N. incorpo-  ration.

incorporeal.definition: Having no physical or material existence; intan-  gible.

incorporeal property.definition: Property that exists as a legal right  without physical substance, such as a copyright.

incorrigible.definition: Unable to be reformed or corrected. N. A person  who refuses to be reformed; an unmanageable person, especially a  juvenile.

increment.definition: An increase, usually part of a scale of regular  increases.

incriminate.definition: To suggest or charge that someone is guilty of a  crime. N. incrimination. ADJ. incriminating.

incriminating evidence.definition: Evidence that helps establish the guilt  of an accused person. See also self-incrimination.

inculpatory.definition: Helping to establish guilt or incriminate a criminal  defendant.

incumbent.definition: A person who currently holds a particular office. ADJ.  Necessary; required as part of duty or responsibility.

indecent.definition: Offensive, vulgar, against modesty or proper  behavior, obscene or lewd. N. indecency.

indecent exposure.definition: Lewdly exposing private parts of the body in  a public place. See also obscene.

indefeasible.definition: Not able to be defeated, annulled, or revoked;  usually describes an estate or a right that cannot be revoked.

indefinite.definition: Vague; not clearly expressed or defined; tempo-  rary.

indefinite failure of issue.definition: A dead person’s lack of living children  or descendants that can occur at any time after the decedent’s  death, however far in the future; see also failure of issue.

indemnify.definition: To pay or reimburse someone for a loss or injury; to  take on legal responsibility for someone else’s actions; to insure.

indemnity.definition: Money given as compensation or reimbursement for  a loss or injury; security against legal responsibility for one’s own  actions; the benefit provided by an insurance policy.

indemnity against liability.definition: A promise to take on another  person’s liability or compensate him or her if and when that liability  arises.

indemnity against loss.definition: Compensation given to a person when  he or she suffers a particular loss. See also insure, subrogation.

indenture.definition: (1) A formal agreement conveying real estate from  one party to another and binding both parties with obligations. (2)  An agreement issuing corporate bonds and debentures, often  between a corporation and an indenture trustee, who holds title to  the trust property and carries out the terms of the agreement. (3)  Historically, a legal contract made into several copies with the

independent contractor.definition: A person who does a job for another  person independently, using his or her own methods and is not  under the control of the employer in regard to how the work is  accomplished.

independent counsel.definition: A person or group of people appointed to  investigate accusations of criminal conduct by a high-level public  official.

index.definition: (1) An alphabetical list of entries or topics in a book or  series, including the pages on which they appear. (2) A statistical  method of tracking fluctuations in the value of stocks, mortgages,  inflation, the economy, etc., over time.

index fund.definition: A mutual fund that chooses its stocks according to a  stock market index number.

indexing.definition: Tying wages, mortgages, or other financial issues to  inflation in order to keep values relatively constant.

Indian.definition: For the purposes of many U.S. laws and treaties, an  indigenous American; a Native American or American Indian.

Indian Affairs, Bureau of.definition: A federal agency in the Interior  Department that oversees dealings between American Indians and  the federal government.

Indian Claims Commission.definition: A commission that hears claims  brought against the federal government by Indian tribes or other  groups of American Indians.

Indian reservation.definition: Public land set aside for the use of American  Indians, supervised by the federal government.

Indian title.definition: A right granted to American Indian tribes by  the federal government to use and occupy certain territories  that they have occupied for a long time, though without any  right of ownership.

Indian tribe.definition: A distinct group of American Indians of the same or  similar race, occupying a specific territory, and under the same  leadership.

indicia.definition: Indications, signs; circumstances indicating that some-  thing is probably true; synonymous with circumstantial evidence.

indicia of title.definition: A document showing title to a piece of prop-  erty.

indict.definition: To formally charge someone with a crime.

indictable offense.definition: A crime that can be prosecuted by indict-  ment, usually a serious crime.

indictee.definition: A person who has been indicted.

indictment.definition: A formal, written accusation presented by a grand  jury to the court charging someone with a specified crime.

indigenous.definition: Native, indigenous people. N. An ethnic group that  was present in a nation before the current population was created  by colonization from a foreign nation, which generally maintains a  culture, language, and social organization distinct from that of the  current surrounding population.

indigent.definition: Poor; poverty-stricken; destitute. N. A poor or needy  person.

indignity.definition: In divorce law, abusive or cruel treatment directed at  the mind and emotions rather than the body, such as vulgarity,  harsh criticism, ridicule, neglect, or abusive language.

indirect.definition: Circuitous or roundabout; not going straight from one  point to another; not directly caused by something.

indirect evidence.definition: Evidence that makes a hypothesis appear  plausible but does not actually prove it. See also circumstantial  evidence, direct evidence.

indispensable evidence.definition: Evidence that is absolutely necessary  to prove a particular fact.

indispensable party.definition: A party who has such an important interest  in a lawsuit that the judgment cannot be issued without his or her  presence; see also joinder.

individual retirement account.definition: A tax-advantaged personal  retirement account that an individual acquires and funds himself or  herself, not through an employer. ABBRV. IRA.

indorse.definition: To endorse; to sign the back of a check or negotiable  instrument in order to transfer its payment to someone else. N.  indorsement.

indorsee.definition: The person to whom a check or instrument is trans-  ferred by indorsement.

indorsement, accommodation.definition: An indorsement made without consideration for the benefit of the person holding the instrument, allowing that person to receive credit or a loan on the basis of the indorsement.

indorsement, blank.definition: An indorsement that does not name a recip-  ient, allowing any bearer to place his or her own name on the  instrument and collect the funds it transfers.

indorsement, restrictive.definition: Words placed on an instrument that  limit the circumstances in which it can be paid out, e.g., “for  deposit only.”

indorsement, special.definition: An indorsement that specifies the person  to whom the instrument is payable.

indorser.definition: The person who signs the check or instrument to  transfer it to another.

induce.definition: To cause; to bring about; to influence or persuade.

inducement.definition: The benefit that persuades a party to enter a  contract; the motive that causes someone to commit a crime.

industrial relations.definition: Relations between workers and their  employer on issues of compensation, safety, hiring, etc.

industry.definition: Commercial activity conducted for profit; economic  activity that uses a great deal of labor, capital, and raw materials for  the manufacture of goods. ADJ. industrial.

inebriation.definition: A person under the influence of too much alcohol.

ineligible.definition: Not legally allowed to hold a position or conduct an  activity; disqualified. N. ineligibility.

infamous.definition: Well-known for some misdeed or bad reputation;  notorious; wicked. N. infamy.

infamous crime.definition: Historically, a crime that renders its perpetrator  infamous; under the modern view, a crime punishable by death or  imprisonment in a state penal institution for more than one year.

infamous punishment.definition: Punishment by imprisonment or by  hard labor.

infancy.definition: Childhood; minority; the period before which a person  reaches legal majority.

infant.definition: A person below the age of legal majority, or before  marriage. See also emancipate.

infanticide.definition: Killing a baby soon after birth.

infer.definition: To deduce or conclude through a process of reasoning  based on facts and logic. N. inference. See also imply.

inferior court.definition: A court whose decisions are subject to review by  an appellate court; see also trial court.

infirmity.definition: Weakness or disability, especially caused by age or  disease; in insurance, a disease or illness that substantially impairs a  person’s health, and if revealed will discourage an insurer from  issuing that person a life or health insurance policy.

informal.definition: Unofficial; not drafted or executed in the proper legal  form. N. informality.

informal contract.definition: A contract that is valid without being  executed formally, such as an oral contract.

in forma pauperis.definition: (Latin) In the form of a pauper; the right  given an indigent person to sue without having to pay court fees  or costs.

informal proceeding.definition: (1) A trial or hearing less formal than a  regular trial, such as a proceeding in a small claims court. (2) In  probate court, a proceeding that admits a will to probate without  first providing notice to all interested parties.

information.definition: A written document filed with the court accusing  someone of a crime that serves essentially the same function as an  indictment but without a grand jury, informing the accused of the  charge against him or her.

information and belief.definition: A legal term used to describe testimony  that is not based on a witness’s firsthand knowledge but that the  witness nevertheless sincerely believes to be true, and that is based  on a reasonable, good faith effort to know the truth.

information technology.definition: The field of study and work that  concerns itself with computers, networks, and the electronic trans-  mission of information. ABBRV. IT.

informed consent.definition: Consent given after learning and under-  standing all relevant facts needed to make an intelligent decision,  required in situations where someone is giving up rights, as when  being arrested, or in potentially risky situations, such as surgery.

informer.definition: A person who confidentially gives information about a  crime to police officers; also called an informant.

infra.definition: (Latin) Below; used to refer to information further on in  a document. See also supra.

infraction.definition: Violation of a law, agreement, or duty.

infringe.definition: To encroach or trespass on something; to violate the  terms of a contract, right, or law; to violate a copyright, patent, or  trademark by using it without permission or payment to the owner.  N. infringement.

ingress.definition: Entrance; the act of entering or the right of entrance;  opposite of egress.

inhabit.definition: To live in a place; to dwell, to reside.

inhabitant.definition: Someone who lives in a place.

inherent.definition: Existing as a permanent, essential, and intrinsic part  of something.

inherent defect.definition: A defect built into an object that exists regard-  less of how it is used.

inherent powers.definition: Powers and authority that are an intrinsic part  of an office or position and that exist without being expressly  granted.

inherent right.definition: A right that a person has simply by virtue of being  a person and that is not granted from some outside source.

inherit.definition: To receive property or money as an heir after the owner's death; technically refers to receiving property by the rules of descent and distribution when the owner dies without a will, but has also come to mean receiving property through a will. N. inheritance.

inheritance tax.definition: Tax imposed on someone who receives prop-  erty and money through inheritance.

inhuman.definition: Cruel, brutal, lacking normal human compas-  sion.

inhuman treatment.definition: Treatment that is so physically or mentally  cruel or brutal as to endanger the health or life of its victim.

initial public offering.definition: The first set of shares a new company  sells on a public stock exchange, usually in an effort to raise money  from investors in order to enlarge the company. ABBRV. IPO.

initiative.definition: A process in which citizens file a petition proposing a  new piece of legislation and submit it to a vote by the legislature or  the electorate. See also referendum.

injunction.definition: A court order prohibiting someone from doing a  specified act in order to prevent future injury. ADJ. injunctive.

injunction, mandatory.definition: An injunction that requires the defen-  dant to do a particular act instead of prohibiting an act. See also  restraining order.

injunction, permanent.definition: An injunction actively sought by a party  to a trial and issued at its conclusion.

injunction, temporary.definition: An injunction issued as a preliminary  preventive measure, pending trial.

injure.definition: To harm or damage a person or property.

injury, irreparable.definition: An injury for which damages cannot be  assessed accurately, usually addressed by injunction.

injury, personal.definition: An injury done to someone’s body or personal  rights, as opposed to reputation or property.

injustice.definition: An absence of fairness; an unfair occurrence; a mistake  made by a court that results in unfair and unjust treatment.

in kind.definition: Of the same kind, category, or type; used to describe  the act of exchanging one thing for another thing of the same or  similar type. ADV. in kind.

in loco parentis.definition: (Latin) In the place of a parent; describes a  situation in which someone (e.g., a teacher or camp counselor)  supervising a minor temporarily assumes the legal obligations  normally held by a parent or guardian. ADV. in loco parentis.

inmate.definition: A person confined to a prison, hospital, or other  institution.

innocent.definition: Not guilty; in good faith; without intention of  committing a wrong or knowledge that a wrong is being  committed. N. innocence.

innovate.definition: To create something new. N. innovation.

innuendo.definition: A suggestive remark or hint; in a libel action, the part  of the pleading in which the plaintiff explains the meaning of the  allegedly libelous words.

in person.definition: Describes a situation in which a person actually  attends a trial, hearing, or other event, and speaks for himself or  herself, instead of allowing an attorney or other representative to  appear and speak for him or her. ADV. in person.

in personam.definition: (Latin) Against a person; describes actions  concerning the personal rights and liability of a person or jurisdic-  tion over a particular person, as opposed to jurisdiction over prop-  erty. See also in rem. ADV. in personam.

in personam jurisdiction.definition: The jurisdiction a court holds over an  individual defendant, as opposed to jurisdiction over his or her  property.

inquest.definition: A judicial investigation into the facts surrounding a  death, conducted by a coroner or medical examiner; a court’s  inquiry into the facts surrounding an incident, often made by a jury;  sometimes called an inquisition.

in re.definition: (Latin) In the matter of; concerning; in regard to; used  to entitle judicial proceedings that do not involve adversaries but  instead concern themselves with disposing of some situation, such  as the settling of an estate.

in rem.definition: (Latin) Against a thing; describes actions or proceed-  ings concerning property rather than people, used in cases to deter-  mine title to or interests in property within the court’s territorial  jurisdiction and that are brought against the property itself, not  against the people who own it. ADV. in rem.

in rem, quasi.definition: Describes proceedings brought against a defen-  dant personally but that concern property, usually claims for  money damages such as foreclosures or attachments. ADV. quasi  in rem.

insanity.definition: Madness or mental illness; the condition of being  mentally ill to the point that one’s perceptions and behavior are  seriously impaired and one is not responsible for one’s actions. ADJ.  insane.
