insanity defense.definition: A defense to criminal prosecution based on  the claim that the defendant cannot be guilty because he or she  lacked the ability to behave correctly and the capacity to under-  stand that his or her act was criminal.

insider.definition: In securities and corporations, someone who has access  to information about a business that is not available to the general  public, such as corporate directors, officers, and major stock-  holders; insiders are not allowed to buy and sell stocks in such a  way as to take advantage of their privileged position and use it for  personal gain.

insider information.definition: Information about a corporation that is  available only to insiders and not revealed to the general public.

insider trading.definition: The illegal buying and selling of stock based on  information available only to insiders.

insolvent.definition: Financially unable to pay debts. N. insolvency.

inspect.definition: To examine closely, looking for defects and quality,  often done to make sure the object meets some official standard;  to scrutinize. N. inspection.

inspection of documents.definition: A process done during discovery in  which a party examines and copies documents held by the  opposing party that are relevant to the lawsuit.

inspector.definition: (1) An official charged with issuing permits and  inspecting objects or places, such as buildings or restaurants, to  ensure that they adhere to legal standards. (2) In some jurisdic-  tions, a police officer.

Inspector General.definition: A high-ranking administrative official in  charge of monitoring operations and finding and eradicating fraud  and waste.

installment.definition: A sum of money that serves as partial payment of a  debt, usually as part of a payment plan that divides payment up into  several portions spread out over a period of time; partial satisfac-  tion of an obligation.

installment contract.definition: A contract that divides its obligation, such  as delivery of goods or payment, into portions that are performed  periodically over a specified period of time.

installment sale.definition: A contract in which a consumer purchases an  item and receives it at once but pays for it over a period of time in  installments.

institute.definition: To begin, establish, or inaugurate; to commence legal  proceedings. N. (1) An organization with a particular purpose,  often an authority in a particular field. (2) A commentary on  legal principles.

institution.definition: (1) The act of starting or establishing something; the  act of beginning a legal action. (2) An organization with a profes-  sional, charitable, educational, or similar purpose. (3) An organiza-  tion or place that houses people with special needs, such as mental  patients or prisoners.

instructions.definition: Directions given by a judge to a jury, telling them  how to apply the law to the facts of the case; see also charge.

instrument.definition: A formal written document; a writing that formally  expresses some legal agreement, such as a contract, lease, will,  deed, or bond. See also negotiable instrument.

insufficient.definition: Inadequate; not meeting needs. N. insufficiency.

insufficient evidence.definition: The condition of not having enough  evidence to support a claim at trial, determined by the court and  resulting in a directed verdict for the defendant.

insufficient funds.definition: The condition of having less money in a bank  account than has been drawn on that account.

insular.definition: On an island; having to do with islands; isolated;  provincial.

insurable interest.definition: A connection or relationship with a person or  thing that is substantial enough to bring a real benefit or advantage  to the person who wants to insure it, generally because the person  or thing provides him or her with money or some other valuable  thing, thus justifying the need or desire for insurance coverage.

insurance.definition: A contract in which one party, the insurer, agrees to  compensate the other party, the insured, for specified losses or  damage to property or people in exchange for consideration,  usually the payment of a premium.

insurance, automobile.definition: Insurance that covers the owner or  drivers of a vehicle from loss or damage to the vehicle and liability  for personal injury caused by operation of the vehicle.

insurance, health.definition: Insurance that covers illness, injury, and  medical care for the insured.

insurance, life.definition: A contract in which the insurer agrees to pay a  specified sum to a named beneficiary when the insured dies.

insurance adjuster.definition: A person who investigates and settles insur-  ance claims.

insurance agent.definition: A person who represents an insurance  company, selling policies on its behalf.

insurance commissioner.definition: A public official who supervises the  insurance business within a state.

insurance defense.definition: Legal work done by a law firm that specializes  in defending claims brought against or covered by an insurance  company, usually with a special fee arrangement giving the insur-  ance company discounted rates for high volume work.

insure.definition: To promise to compensate someone for the loss of or  damage to someone or something in exchange for payments of  money; to assume the risk of loss or damage; to underwrite; see  also indemnify.

insured.definition: A person covered by an insurance policy.

insurer.definition: A company or individual that insures people or things;  an underwriter.

insurrection.definition: A violent uprising against the government or  authority; a rebellion by citizens against the government.

intangible.definition: Having no physical presence; difficult to define or  grasp. See also tangible.

intangible asset.definition: Property that has no physical substance but  that nevertheless exists as a right, such as a patent, copyright, or  goodwill; also called intangibles.

integrate.definition: (1) To combine two or more things into one whole. (2)  To desegregate.

integrated contract.definition: A written contract that contains the entire  and final agreement of the parties, and to which oral evidence  cannot be admitted; also called an integration.

intent.definition: Plan, resolve, or intention; desire to bring about a partic-  ular result; the state of mind of a person who wants to do a certain  act and accomplish a particular result. V. intend. See also motive.

intent, general.definition: In criminal cases, an intention to break a law but  not necessarily to cause the specific result that occurred.

intent, legislative.definition: A legislature’s intended purpose in enacting a  piece of legislation.

intent, specific.definition: In criminal cases, an intention to commit a  particular crime.

intention.definition: A plan, desire, or aim; something that is intended.

intentional.definition: Deliberate; done consciously and purposely with  an awareness of probable consequences; not accidental.

intentional infliction of emotional dist.definition: A deliberate act  done expressly to inflict emotional distress on its victim.

intentional tort.definition: A tort committed deliberately with an express  desire to harm.

inter alia.definition: (Latin) Among other things; used to avoid listing  all the details of a statute or other document while noting that  they exist.

intercept.definition: To catch or obstruct something on its way to a desti-  nation; to use electronic or other devices to obtain the contents of  a communication done through some telecommunications device,  such as a wiretap. N. interception.

interest.definition: (1) A right, claim, or title to something. (2) A share or  stake in some undertaking; a personal stake in a matter. (3) Money  paid at a specified rate on a regular basis for the use of a loan or to  delay the date of repayment of a loan.

interest, absolute.definition: An interest in property that cannot be taken  from its owner without his or her consent, no matter what  happens.

interest, compound.definition: Interest paid on top of interest already  accrued, in addition to interest paid on principal.

interest, simple.definition: Interest paid only on the principal of a loan.

interest, vested.definition: A present interest in property that the owner is  allowed to transfer in the present but might not be allowed to actu-  ally possess or enjoy until some time in the future.

interested party.definition: A person with a legally recognized private  interest in some matter.

interim.definition: In the meantime; during intervening time; temporary.

interim financing.definition: A short-term loan held until long-term or  permanent financing can be arranged for an enterprise; also called  a bridge loan.

interim order.definition: A temporary order issued to handle a matter until  a specified event happens or until a final order is issued. See also  interlocutory.

interlocking directors.definition: People who serve on the boards of direc-  tors of two or more corporations simultaneously, resulting in a situ-  ation called an interlocking directorate, which can be used to  restrict competition and is often illegal under antitrust law.

interlocutory.definition: Temporary; provisional; issued as a temporary  stopgap measure while a lawsuit proceeds.

interlocutory order.definition: An order issued by the court while a trial is  still in process, determining an intermediate issue before coming to  a final decision for the entire case.

internal network.definition: A computer network restricted to a given  workplace or facility, not accessible by the general public.

Internal Revenue Code.definition: A federal statute in Title 26 of the U.S.  Code setting forth laws on federal taxes, including income tax,  estate tax, gift tax, excise tax, etc. ABBRV. IRC.

Internal Revenue Service.definition: The federal agency that administers  federal tax laws, assesses and collects taxes, audits taxpayers,  and handles appeals by taxpayers who dispute their tax liability.  ABBRV. IRS.

international agreement.definition: An agreement between two or more  nations that creates legal rights or obligations among them; a  treaty, convention, or other such agreement between nations.

International Court of Justice.definition: The United Nations’ court, seated  at The Hague, Netherlands, that provides advice on questions of  law and the writing of treaties and settles legal disputes between  nations; its decisions are enforceable by the U.N. Security Council.

international law.definition: The body of law that governs relations  between different countries, composed of custom and practice,  rules and statutes, international treaties, and other sources.

international trade.definition: Trade in goods or services across national  borders.

Internet.definition: A global network of computers, networks, and  websites that allows the exchange of information through domain  names, IP addresses, email, file sharing, and other mechanisms.  See also World Wide Web.

Internet Corporation for Assigned Names.definition: A  nonprofit corporation in California that manages the assignment of  IP addresses and domain names for the U.S. Department of  Commerce. ABBRV. ICANN.

Internet Protocol.definition: (1) The set of rules governing communication  among computers across a computer network or the Internet. (2)  Intellectual property. ABBRV. IP.

Internet service provider.definition: A company that sells consumers  access to the Internet. ABBRV. ISP.

interpleader.definition: An action in equity in which two or more parties  who each claim property held by a third party submit the claim to  a court to settle their rights, brought by the third party who fears

interpolate.definition: To insert words into a document, thereby changing  its meaning. N. interpolation.

interpret.definition: To ascertain or explain the meaning of something, such  as a statute, contract, or other document; to explain the meaning of  the language of a law or other legal document without venturing  into legal intentions and consequences. N. interpretation. See  also construe, construction.

interrogate.definition: To question; to ask questions of someone such  as a suspected criminal, often in a close or formal way. N.  interrogation.

interrogatories.definition: During discovery before trial, written ques-  tions about the case presented by one party to the opposing  party that must be answered under oath and returned to the  questioning party.

interstate.definition: Existing in more than one state; carried on in or  between two or more states.

interstate commerce.definition: Trade, transportation of people or prop-  erty, and all forms of commerce that involve the territory of more  than one state in the United States.

Interstate Commerce Commission.definition: A federal commission that  regulates surface transportation between states, including trucks,  trains, freight forwarders, oil pipelines, and other commercial  carriers, in order to facilitate interstate commerce by ensuring that  the public will have access to transportation that is reliable and  reasonably priced. ABBRV. ICC.

interstate compact.definition: An agreement between two or more states  on a matter of interest to each of them.

intervene.definition: To come between two things in such a way as to delay,  obstruct, or change the course of events. N. intervention.

intervening cause.definition: An independent event that occurs between a  wrongful act and an injury and changes the expected course of  events so that the result is not the one that would have been  predicted, thus relieving the person who committed the original  wrongful act of liability for the injury. See also superseding cause.

intervenor.definition: A person who enters a lawsuit by intervention.

intervention.definition: A procedure in which someone who is not origi-  nally a party to a lawsuit enters it to defend his or her own interest  in the matter.

inter vivos.definition: (Latin) Between the living; describes gifts or  transfers done from one living person to another. ADV. inter vivos.

intestacy.definition: The condition of dying without a valid will.

intestate.definition: Without a valid will at the time of death. N. A person  who dies without a valid will.

intestate succession.definition: Disposition of the property of someone  who dies intestate according to the rules of descent and distri-  bution.

in the course of employment.definition: Occurring while an employee is  at work or otherwise in his or her employer’s service.

intoxicated.definition: Drunk; under the influence of alcohol.

intoxication.definition: Drunkenness; a state of losing control of one’s  physical or mental faculties due to the consumption of alcohol or  another intoxicating substance, which renders one incapable of  acting like a reasonable person of ordinary prudence would under  the same circumstances.

intramural.definition: Within the walls; within the confines or boundaries  of an organization or institution. See also extramural.

Intranet.definition: An internal network; a computer network restricted to  a secure area and specific users.

intrinsic evidence.definition: Evidence produced by questioning a witness  at trial.

intrinsic fraud.definition: Fraud that occurs during trial through perjury,  forged documents, or hiding or misrepresenting evidence, which  affects the outcome of the trial.

intrinsic value.definition: The inherent value of a thing, which remains  constant regardless of place, time, or special features that affect its  market value.

inure.definition: (1) To take effect; to benefit someone; to vest. (2) To  accustom someone to something.

invalid.definition: Not valid; not legally adequate; void.

invasion of privacy.definition: The wrongful and unwarranted intrusion  into or publicizing of someone’s private affairs by another person  or the government.

invent.definition: To design or create some new thing that did not exist  before; to create a story, often intending to deceive.

invention.definition: The process of creating some new thing, especially a  device or machine; something invented.

inventory.definition: A detailed list of property, such as goods in stock or  the complete contents of an estate. See also first-in, first-out, last-  in, first-out.

invest.definition: (1) To spend money on an enterprise in the hope that it  will result in a profit; to give time or energy to a cause in the hope  that it will generate good results. See also investment. (2) To  bestow on someone an office or a right. (3) In feudal times, to  bestow a grant of land on someone in a ceremony called investi-  ture.

investee.definition: A company whose shares are bought by an investor.

investigate.definition: To examine the facts surrounding an incident as part  of a formal inquiry into the matter. N. investigation. ADJ. investi-  gatory.

investigatory powers.definition: Powers given to governmental agencies to  investigate violations of laws and the possible effects of proposed  laws.

investment.definition: The expenditure of money on an enterprise in the  hope of making a profit; assets or securities bought in the hope of  earning a profit.

investment company.definition: A company primarily in the business of  trading securities, using its capital to invest in other compa-  nies.

investor.definition: Someone who buys shares in a company or otherwise  invests money.

invitee.definition: A person who enters another’s property after being  invited.

invoice.definition: A list of goods contained in a shipment along with the  price due for them; a bill. V. To send an invoice to someone for  goods or services provided.

involuntary.definition: Done unwillingly, without choice; accidental or  unintentional.

involuntary confession.definition: A confession made unwillingly and  without free choice by someone whose rights against self-incrimi-  nation have been violated by threat of violence, coercion, improper  promises, etc.

involuntary conversion.definition: The unintentional loss of property  through theft, destruction, seizure, or condemnation.

involuntary servitude.definition: Slavery; forcing someone to work for  someone else.

iota.definition: The Greek letter “i,” i.e., the smallest letter of the Greek  alphabet; a miniscule amount, the smallest possible amount of  something.

IP address.definition: Internet Protocol address; a computer address; the  unique set of digits that computers use to identify a specific  computer or device in a network.

ipso facto.definition: (Latin) By the very fact itself; through the effect of  a fact or act.

irrelevant.definition: Not applicable to the matter at hand; immaterial. N.  irrelevance, irrelevancy.

irreparable injury.definition: An injury for which no adequate legal remedy  exists, usually addressed by an injunction.

irrevocable.definition: Unable to be withdrawn or revoked.

issue.definition: (1) A problem or question; a topic for debate. (2) The act  of sending something out formally; promulgation. (3) Children;  descendants. (4) The act of offering corporate securities for sale. V.  To send out or distribute; to promulgate; to offer something up for  sale.

issue of fact.definition: A disagreement about facts in a legal controversy,  i.e., when one party suggests one version of facts and the other  disagrees.

issue preclusion.definition: The barring of an issue from further litigation  because it has already been settled conclusively at trial.

J.definition: “Judge” or “justice”; used as a title for a person holding  that office.

jail.definition: A building used to confine people held in legal custody  either awaiting trial for a criminal offense or serving a prison  sentence after conviction; usually used for short-term confinement.  V. To imprison; to put someone in jail. See also prison, correctional  institution.

jailhouse lawyer.definition: An inmate in a correctional institution who  has no formal legal training but has studied the law independently,  who helps fellow inmates prepare appeals and provides legal  advice to them.

Jencks Act.definition: A law requiring the government to provide a crim-  inal defendant in a federal case with copies of witness statements  made against him or her in order to prepare a defense.

jeopardy.definition: Risk or danger; the danger of being convicted that is a  natural accompaniment to being a defendant in a criminal trial. See  also double jeopardy.

Jim Crow laws.definition: Laws used in the first half of the twentieth  century to segregate the races. See also segregate, desegregate.

job.definition: (1) A position of employment. (2) A task, usually paid. (3) A  crime such as robbery. V. To buy and sell goods or stocks.

jobber.definition: (1) A person who buys and sells stocks or goods; a  wholesaler or middleman. (2) A person who works casually doing  odd jobs for pay.

join.definition: To combine or unite; to unite with another party in a  lawsuit; to add a cause of action or issue to a lawsuit. See also  joinder.

joinder.definition: The act of uniting parties or causes of action to a  lawsuit.

joinder, compulsory.definition: The mandatory addition of a party to a  lawsuit if his or her presence is necessary to provide complete  relief to the current parties, or if his or her interests will be substan-  tially affected by the lawsuit.

joinder, permissive.definition: The voluntary joining of a lawsuit by a party  who is not indispensable to the action but who does have a claim  for relief arising out of it.

joinder of actions.definition: Combining two or more causes of action into  a single lawsuit.

joinder of parties.definition: The addition of one or more parties to a  lawsuit.

joint.definition: Shared; united. N. A marijuana cigarette.

joint and several liability.definition: Liability for an enterprise shared by  two or more people that can be applied to the group as a whole or  to individuals, so that each is individually responsible for  performing the entire obligation and each can be sued independ-  ently for failure to fulfill it, while at the same time the group is also  responsible for the obligation and can be sued as a whole.

joint bank account.definition: A bank account shared by two or more  people who each have equal right to it.

joint enterprise.definition: An enterprise undertaken by two or more  people who agree on a common purpose and share control of the  project and a financial interest in it.

joint liability.definition: Liability owed by two or more people together.

joint venture.definition: A commercial enterprise similar to a partnership,  in which two or more parties join together for the purpose of  carrying out a single commercial enterprise, for which they share  assets, liability, and control.

journal.definition: (1) A daily record of transactions, business, proceed-  ings, appointments, events and occurrences, etc. (2) A periodical  publication covering a particular profession or field.

journalists’ privilege.definition: A privilege that protects journalists and  other media professionals from being forced to reveal confidential  sources of information in some cases.

joyriding.definition: Taking an automobile without the owner’s permission  in order to use it temporarily but not intending to keep it perma-  nently.

judge.definition: An officer who presides over and decides cases in a  court. V. To decide a legal case; to form an opinion about a matter.  ABBRV. J.

judge advocate.definition: A military lawyer or legal officer; an officer in  the Judge Advocate General’s corps.

judge-made law.definition: Case law created by judges who interpret  statutes in what some consider to be an extreme or ideologically  motivated way.

judgment.definition: (1) The ability to form opinions and make decisions.  (2) The court’s final decision in a trial; the amount of money  awarded to the prevailing party by the court.

judgment, final.definition: A judgment that ends a legal controversy by  conclusively stating whether or not the plaintiff is entitled to relief.

judgment, foreign.definition: A judgment issued by a court in another state  or country.

judgment, money.definition: A judgment that awards a party a sum of  money.

judgment notwithstanding the verdict.definition: A judgment by the court  for one party after receiving a verdict for the other, following a  motion for a directed verdict; also called judgment n.o.v., non  obstante veredicto.

judgment on the merits.definition: A judgment rendered after the parties  have presented their cases and the court has determined which  party is in the right on the basis of facts and evidence presented.

judgment proof.definition: Describes a person not worth suing for  monetary damages because he or she is insolvent, has no prop-  erty within a jurisdiction, or is otherwise protected from a judg-  ment for money recoveries.

judicial.definition: Related to the office of judge, judgments, the admin-  istration of justice, or courts; related to the interpretation and  application of laws.

judicial activism.definition: A form of legal action in which a judge writes  opinions and renders decisions based on progressive ideas of  social justice instead of basing his or her decisions strictly on judi-  cial precedent and a restrained interpretation of legislation.

judicial authority.definition: The power that comes as part of the office of  judge; the power to hear cases and render decisions; also called  judicial power.

judicial branch.definition: The branch of government consisting of courts  and other entities that exist to interpret and enforce laws.

judicial immunity.definition: A judge’s immunity from civil liability for  anything done in his or her official capacity.

judicial notice.definition: A practice in which the court will recognize  certain facts without requiring one of the parties to produce  evidence proving them, usually in the case of facts that are univer-  sally acknowledged to be true or facts that the judge or jury already  know.

judicial review.definition: (1) The power of the courts to review the acts  of other branches of the government, usually in order to determine  that the law is properly applied to a matter, especially in constitu-  tional matters; review by the Supreme Court of acts by the legisla-  ture, as established in the case Marbury v. Madison. (2) Review of  a trial court’s decision by an appellate court.

judiciary.definition: A collective term for judges; the bench; the judicial  branch of the government.

jump bail.definition: For a criminal defendant to leave the jurisdiction or  fail to appear at trial after bail has been posted for him or her,  causing whoever posted bail to forfeit it. See also bail, bondsman.

jural.definition: Relating to the law, justice, rights, or obligations.

jurisdiction.definition: The power to make judicial decisions; a court’s or  judge’s power to investigate the facts of a matter, apply law to  them, and declare a judgment. (2) The territory in which a partic-  ular court can exercise its authority; the system of courts within  a particular area. ADJ. jurisdictional.

jurisdictional amount.definition: The amount of money required to be  involved in a controversy in order to give a court jurisdiction  over it.

juris doctor.definition: The degree received by someone who completes  law school. ABBRV. JD.

jurisprudence.definition: (1) The philosophy or science of law. (2) The  body of law formed by cases and interpretations of them; a system  of law.

jurist.definition: A scholar of the law; a lawyer or judge.

jury.definition: A group of people selected and sworn to hear the evidence  in a case and decide what the true facts are; usually composed of a  cross section of the community. See also grand jury, hung jury,  petit jury.

jury, blue ribbon.definition: A jury composed of experts, professionals, or  other people who are exceptionally qualified, rather than a cross  section of all members of a community.

jury challenge.definition: A party’s rejection of a potential juror while  selecting a jury for trial.

jury instructions.definition: A statement made to the jurors by the judge at  the conclusion of testimony but before the jurors begin deliber-  ating, in which the judge describes the law that applies to the  matters in controversy and explains how the jury should apply it.

jury panel.definition: The group of people summoned to court on a partic-  ular day to serve as jurors if chosen.

jury selection.definition: The process of choosing members of a jury from  a panel of potential jurors, done by the parties to a lawsuit at the  start of trial.

jury trial.definition: A trial that takes place before a jury, in which the jury  hears the testimony and sees evidence and then renders a decision  about the facts in accordance with the judge’s instructions. See  also bench trial.

jus cogens.definition: (Latin) Compelling law; a peremptory norm; in  international law, a principle that is widely accepted to be true by  a large number of states and individuals, such as the belief that  genocide and slavery are wrong.

jus in bello.definition: (Latin) Law in war; law governing actions during  wartime.

just cause.definition: A legitimate or fair reason for doing something; a  reason that will legally justify an action.

just compensation.definition: Compensation given to an owner whose  property is taken through eminent domain that is fair both to  the owner, who is losing property, and to the public, who is  paying for it.

Justice Department.definition: The federal department that enforces  federal laws and represents the federal government in legal  matters, under the direction of the attorney general.

justice of the peace.definition: A magistrate with minor local authority,  who hears minor criminal and civil cases, performs marriages, and  handles other civil matters; most states have replaced justices of  the peace with other courts and officers. ABBRV. J.P.

justiciable.definition: Able to be tried in court; presenting real interests  instead of merely hypothetical or abstract ones.

justiciable controversy.definition: A controversy involving a real issue that  can be settled by a court, involving a present claim made by one  party and another party disputing it.

justifiable.definition: Excusable; defensible; able to be proven to be  reasonable or correct. See also homicide, justifiable.

juvenile.definition: A child; a person below the age of legal majority. ADJ.  Childish or youthful; relating to children or young people.

juvenile court.definition: A court that hears cases involving children and  teenagers, particularly over juvenile delinquents and neglected  children.

juvenile delinquent.definition: A minor who commits a crime or engages  in regular criminal activity. See also delinquent child.

kangaroo court.definition: A court that is completely biased against one  party and therefore disregards that party’s rights and delivers a  judgment or verdict that is unfair to him or her; a trial in a kangaroo  court is considered a sham without legal authority.

Keogh plan.definition: A retirement plan for self-employed people with  benefits similar to those enjoyed by employees with employer-  sponsored retirement plans.

key number system.definition: A system of organizing published cases by  numbering each point of law with a key number, making it easy for  legal researchers to locate relevant topics and cases.

kickback.definition: A payment made by a seller to a buyer or agent in  order to persuade him or her to enter into the transaction; illegal in  most cases.

kidnap.definition: To unlawfully and forcibly take and carry away a person.  N. kidnapping.

kidnapping for ransom.definition: The abduction and holding of a person  in order to demand money in return for the victim’s release. See  also abduct, false arrest, ransom.

kite.definition: To write fraudulent checks; to write checks on an account  with insufficient funds in the hopes that funds sufficient to cover  the check will be deposited before the check is presented to the  bank for payment. N. kiting, check kiting. See also float.

know.definition: To be aware of; to have information about something; to  understand.

knowingly.definition: With awareness; consciously; with understanding  of the circumstances and the implications of an act.

Kyoto Protocol.definition: An amendment to the United Nations  Framework Convention on Climate Change that requires signa-  tory nations to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions to an  assigned level.

labor.definition: (1) Work, especially manual work. (2) A collective term for  workers. V. To work, especially at hard physical jobs.

labor dispute.definition: A disagreement between employees and their  employer about terms and conditions of employment, including  pay, safety, hours, etc.

laborer.definition: A person who engages in physical work for wages.

labor relations.definition: The relationship between employers and  employees, usually as personified by management and employees.

labor union.definition: An organization of workers in a particular field that  exists to handle disputes between the workers and their  employers, working under the assumption that the workers have  more power when they join forces and negotiate as a unit; also  called a labor organization.

laches.definition: An unreasonable delay in asserting or enforcing a claim.

laches, estoppel by.definition: A doctrine that prevents a person from  suing for some grievance that happened far in the past or otherwise  seeking to enforce some right that he or she should have addressed  long ago, on the assumption that the party with the claim has been  negligent in not bringing it, that the defendant would have a hard  time defending against it because it is so old, and that often the  defendant has been put at a disadvantage because the would-be  plaintiff has delayed so long.

laissez-faire.definition: (French) Allow to do; an economic doctrine  stating that the free market should be allowed to operate free of  governmental interference.

lame duck.definition: A public official still in office after the election but  before the inauguration of his or her successor.

land, tenements, and hereditaments.definition: A legal term encom-  passing all types of real property.

landlord.definition: A person who owns real property and rents it to  tenants. See also lessor.

landmark.definition: (1) A feature of the landscape or an object placed on  it that marks the boundary of a piece of property. (2) A building or  natural object that is easy to spot, or that has some historical or  cultural significance. (3) An important event that marks a turning  point or a significant moment.

landmark decision.definition: A decision by the Supreme Court that  changes the established law in an area.

land mine.definition: An explosive device laid on the surface of the earth  or hidden just underneath it, intended to explode when a person or  vehicle touches it.

land trust.definition: A form of trust in which a trustee holds legal title to  a piece of property but the beneficiary has the power to direct the  trustee, manage the property, and receive income from it; also  called an Illinois land trust.

lapse.definition: The expiration of a right or privilege due to failure to exer-  cise it or otherwise maintain it; the end of a legacy or right due to  the death of the person who held it or to the expiration of a speci-  fied period of time; failure of a bequest or testamentary gift; the  termination of an insurance policy if the policyholder does not pay  the premium. V. lapse.

larceny.definition: Taking the property of another person without the  owner’s consent and with the intention of making it the property of  someone else.

larceny, grand.definition: The taking of property with a high monetary  value, set by statute.

larceny, petit.definition: The taking of property with a low monetary value,  set by statute. Also called petty larceny. See also burglary, theft,  robbery.

last clear chance.definition: A doctrine that allows a plaintiff to recover  from a defendant if the defendant had the last clear chance or  opportunity to avoid the accident, regardless of the plaintiff’s own  negligence.

last-in, first-out.definition: A method of inventory that assumes that the  goods most recently bought are the first ones to be sold, and that  goods still in inventory at the end of the year are the ones bought  long ago. See also first-in, first-out. ABBRV. LIFO.

last will and testament.definition: A will; the most recent will created by a  deceased person; traditionally, the word “will” referred to the  disposition of real property and the word “testament” to the dispo-  sition of personal property.

latent.definition: Hidden; existing but not yet obvious or apparent.

latent ambiguity.definition: Language that appears to be clear and under-  standable but that in fact presents ambiguities or confusion when  someone tries to apply it.

latent defect.definition: A defect in an object that is not apparent on  reasonable inspection or through the use of ordinary care.

law.definition: (1) A system of rules created by a society to regulate  behavior and punish crimes. (2) A statute. (3) The professional  field concerned with the rules that regulate society.

law, at.definition: Pertaining to law; related to the law or the legal profes-  sion.

law enforcement.definition: Seeing that people follow laws and catching  and punishing lawbreakers; the work done by police.

lawless.definition: Not conforming to law; not controlled by law.

law of the case.definition: The doctrine that if a court decides an issue of  law, the determination will stay the same throughout subsequent  trials of the same facts unless a higher court reconsiders the issue  and issues a new determination.

law of the land.definition: The established law of a society, especially the  principles of due process and other rights too fundamental to be  abolished by government; in the United States, the Constitution  and laws made by its authority.

Law of the Sea.definition: United Nations Convention on the Law of the  Sea; an international agreement that sets rules for the use of the  world’s oceans, establishing territorial boundaries and governing  the management of marine resources.

law review.definition: A professional journal published by a law school containing scholarly articles on legal topics by experts and students, usually compiled and edited by a staff of students chosen for their excellent academic record.

Law School Admission Test.definition: A test that is required for admission  to most law schools. ABBRV. LSAT.

lawsuit.definition: An action at law or equity; a dispute brought before a  court for determination.

lawyer.definition: An attorney; a person who has studied law or who prac-  tices law.

lay.definition: Not professional or expert in a particular field; nonecclesi-  astical or nonclergy.

lay judge.definition: A judge who has not studied law.

layman.definition: A person who is not a professional or expert in a partic-  ular field, e.g., in legal situations, a nonlawyer.

layoff.definition: The firing of one or many employees in response to busi-  ness conditions, not for any wrongdoing of the employee; the  temporary or permanent termination of one or more workers. See  also downsize.

lay witness.definition: A witness who is not an expert in the field on which  he or she is called to testify; see also expert.

lead counsel.definition: The head lawyer on one side of a lawsuit who is  in charge of managing the case and all other attorneys and other  people working on it.

leading case.definition: A case that is recognized as determining the law  on a particular topic and is often cited for that purpose.

lease.definition: A contract in which the owner of a piece of property  allows someone else to possess it exclusively for a specified period  of time in return for payment. V. To allow someone to use property  for a period of time in exchange for payment; to rent. See also let,  lessor, lessee, sublease.

lease, month-to-month.definition: A tenancy without an actual lease, in  which the lessor pays rent monthly and either party can terminate  the arrangement with one month’s notice.

lease, proprietary.definition: A lease held by a resident in a cooperative  apartment.

leasehold.definition: An estate in real property held by lease, usually of a  fixed duration; see also freehold.

leave of absence.definition: A period of time in which a person has  permission to be absent from work or duties, after which he or she  intends to return.

leave of court.definition: The court’s permission to do a particular act.

legacy.definition: A bequest; money or property left to someone by a will.

legacy, absolute.definition: A bequest that comes into effect immediately  without any conditions or restrictions.

legacy, alternate.definition: A bequest in which the testator leaves a recip-  ient one of several things without specifying which.

legacy, contingent.definition: A bequest that will take effect when and if a  specified event occurs.

legacy, demonstrative.definition: A bequest of money that is to be paid out  of a specific fund.

legacy, general.definition: A bequest of money or goods to be paid out of  the general assets of the estate.

legacy, residuary.definition: A bequest of all personal property not other-  wise disposed of by will, i.e., the residue.

legacy, specific.definition: A bequest of a specific item designated by the  testator.

legal.definition: (1) Having to do with law; related to principles of law as  opposed to equity. (2) In accordance with law; lawful. ADV. legally.

legal age.definition: The age at which a person can enter into contracts  and conduct business on his or her own behalf, or the age at which  a person can marry; majority.

legal aid.definition: A nonprofit system that provides legal assistance to  people who cannot afford to pay for lawyers themselves.

legal capacity to sue.definition: The right to bring a lawsuit; the condition  of being of sound mind, being of legal age, and suffering from no  legal restraints or other impediments to bringing a lawsuit.

legal cause.definition: The conduct or event that directly causes an injury;  proximate cause.

legal detriment.definition: Injury or disadvantage caused to someone who  acted in such a way as to change his or her legal position or assume  a liability or duty based on a promise that another person made but  did not keep.

legal duty.definition: An obligation required by law, such as the duty of  parents to care for their children or the duty arising from a  contract; also called a legal obligation.

legal fiction.definition: An assumption that certain facts are true that is  made by the court in order to render a legal decision without delay.

legal name.definition: A person’s official name registered with the govern-  ment, usually consisting of one or more given names recorded on  the person’s birth certificate, the parents’ or father’s family name,  and possibly a new family name acquired upon marriage.

legal secretary.definition: A secretary with special expertise in legal  matters.

Legal Services Corporation.definition: A corporation established by  Congress in 1974 that provides funding for legal assistance in  noncriminal proceedings for people who cannot afford to pay for  legal counsel. See also legal aid.

legal tender.definition: Money that can be used to pay for things; all coins  and currencies of the United States, regardless of when they were  issued.

legatee.definition: A person who receives a legacy.

legislate.definition: To create or pass a law or resolution.

legislation.definition: The act of making laws; laws created by a legis-  lature.

legislative.definition: Holding the power to create laws; pertaining to the  creation of laws. N. legislative act, legislative branch, legisla-  tive history, legislative immunity, legislative power.

legislator.definition: A member of a body that creates laws.

legislature.definition: The group of people who make laws for a state  or country.

Legum Magister.definition: (Latin) Master of laws; a post-graduate degree  offered by law schools to students who have done advanced study  in a legal specialty or to foreign lawyers who have taken a certain  number of classes at an American law school. ABBRV. LL.M.

lemon laws.definition: Laws passed by many states that provide relief to  people who purchase new automobiles that turn out to be defec-  tive.

lend.definition: (1) To let someone use something temporarily, on the  understanding that it will be returned; to allow someone to borrow  something. (2) To provide someone with money that he or she must  eventually pay back, usually with interest.

lender.definition: A person or organization that lends something, particu-  larly money.

lending.definition: The act or business of lending something, especially  money. See also loan.

lessee.definition: A person who holds property through a lease; a person  who rents property; a tenant. See also lease, lessor.

lesser included offense.definition: A crime that is inherently part of a  more serious crime; e.g., assault is an inherent part of the more  serious crime of assault and battery, or attempt is an inherent part  of any crime that is actually completed.

lessor.definition: A person who rents or leases property to someone else;  a landlord.

letter of credit.definition: A promise made by a bank or other financial  institution to another bank or financial institution that it will honor  demands for payment on behalf of a specified customer under  specified conditions.

letter of intent.definition: A document that records the intention of the  parties to it to enter into a contract or take some other action but  that is not itself a formal contract.

letter of the law.definition: A strict, literal, word-for-word interpretation of  laws and statutes. See also spirit of the law.

leverage.definition: (1) The use of borrowed money to try to earn much  more through investment, in the hopes that the profits will  outweigh the interest owed on the debt. (2) The ratio of a busi-  ness’s debt to equity.

leveraged buyout.definition: A method by which outside investors or a  corporation’s own management buy the outstanding stock of a  corporation using money borrowed from outside sources, and  using the purchased company’s assets as security for the loan.

levy.definition: (1) To impose a tax, fee, or fine on someone; to raise or  collect. (2) To seize money or property. (3) To begin to wage war.  N. (1) A tax or other sum collected from people. (2) Raising money  through seizing property and selling it. (3) The enlisting of troops;  a group of enlisted soldiers.

lewd.definition: Sexually indecent; provoking lust. See also lascivious,  indecent.

lewdness.definition: Sexual indecency, especially performed in public or in  the presence of people who might be offended by it.

lewd and lascivious cohabitation.definition: The crime of a man and  woman living together without being married.

LexisNexis.definition: A privately owned online legal research system that  provides access to reported decisions, federal and state statutes,  law review articles, and many other materials; also called Lexis.  See also Westlaw.

liability.definition: The condition of being responsible for something;  something for which someone is responsible; an obligation or duty  to do something or pay a sum of money; a possible claim against a  person or business.

liability insurance.definition: Insurance that covers an individual against  claims against him or her made by third parties.

liable.definition: Responsible; legally answerable for something; obli-  gated or accountable.

libel.definition: A written, printed, or published false and malicious state-  ment that injures someone’s reputation; the written form of  defamation. ADJ. libelous. See also defamation, slander.

liberal.definition: Open to new opinions and interpretations; not strict,  conservative, or narrow-minded; in favor of individual liberty;  generous. See also construction.

liberties.definition: Specific instances of freedom from restrictions; rights  or privileges. See also civil liberties.

license.definition: (1) A permit that allows someone to do some act;  permission to do something that would be illegal without that  permission. (2) Permission to use property owned by someone  else, which does not give the licensee an exclusive right of posses-  sion; see also lease.

license fee.definition: A fee imposed by the government for granting a  license or a privilege to do something; also called a license tax.

licensee.definition: A person who receives a license; a person who has the  right to use property or do some action through a license.

licensor.definition: A person who grants a license.

lie.definition: (1) To intentionally make a false statement; to tell an untruth.  (2) For an action or claim to be sustainable or admissible. N. A  falsehood or untruth told intentionally to mislead someone.

lien.definition: A claim placed on property to act as security for a debt  owed by the owner, held until the owner pays the debt and kept if  the owner never pays.

lien, artisan’s.definition: A lien that allows an artisan or craftsman to hold  an item he or she has made until receiving payment for it.

lien, equitable.definition: A lien enforceable in equity, not at law.

lien, floating.definition: A lien that affects not only inventory and assets  owned by the debtor at the time the agreement is made, but also  property acquired afterward.

lien, judgment.definition: A lien that a judgment creditor is entitled to file  against a judgment debtor’s property, often in order to sell the prop-  erty at a sheriff’s sale.

lien, mechanic’s.definition: A lien that allows builders, tradesmen, suppliers,  and others who provide materials or labor for construction or  maintenance of buildings to attach the land or buildings in question  to secure payment for their work and materials.

lien, tax.definition: A lien that the state may place on a taxpayer’s property  if he or she fails to pay taxes.

life estate.definition: An estate in property that lasts until the person  holding it dies, or until some other specified person dies.

life expectancy.definition: The number of years a person is expected to  live, based on age and sex and calculated by actuarial tables and  statistics.

life interest.definition: A claim to or interest in property that ends at the  death of the person holding the interest or of some other person;  see also life estate, life tenant.

life tenant.definition: A person who holds a tenancy on land that ends at  his or her death or at the death of another person; such a tenancy  is called a life tenancy. See also life estate.

like-kind exchange.definition: An exchange of an item of property for  another item of property of a similar type and value, usually  nontaxable.

limitation.definition: A restriction; a restriction of the time in which a  lawsuit can be filed; a restriction of who can receive an estate in  property or the uses to which an estate may be restricted. See  also statute of limitations.

limited.definition: Restricted in size, time, or extent.

limited jurisdiction.definition: Jurisdiction over only specific kinds of cases  or otherwise restricted by statute; see also special jurisdiction.

limited liability.definition: Liability for corporate losses that is restricted in  some way, usually to the amount that an investor or shareholder  has placed in the corporation and not reaching personal assets.

limited liability company.definition: A business organization, often  managed by its members, with limited liability and limited ability to  transfer ownership. ABBRV. L.L.C.

limited partnership.definition: A form of partnership in which one or more  general partners manage the business and are personally respon-  sible for its debts, and one or more limited partners contribute  money and earn profits but do not run the business and are not  liable for its debts.

lineal.definition: In a direct line from a particular relative, such as grand-  parent to parent to child; see also collateral.

lineal descendant.definition: A person who is directly descended in a  straight line from an ancestor.

lineal heir.definition: A person who inherits directly from a lineal ancestor  or ascendant; see also descent.

line of credit.definition: An amount of credit available for a specified time  to a consumer that the consumer may access as he or she chooses;  a standing loan available for a preset time period.

lineup.definition: A procedure used by police to identify criminal suspects,  in which a suspect and several similar people are lined up in front  of a wall and a witness is asked to identify the one who committed  the crime.

link.definition: To connect one website or computer with another. N. A  place on a website that, if clicked, will connect the user to another  website.

liquid.definition: In cash or readily converted into cash; holding a large  amount of cash and working capital.

liquid assets.definition: Cash or items that can be quickly converted into  cash.

liquidate.definition: (1) To convert assets into cash; to sell assets. (2) To  settle; to pay debts; to settle the affairs of a business by deter-  mining debts and apportioning assets. N. liquidation.

liquidated damages.definition: The amount of damages that a party to a  contract agrees to pay if he or she breaches it.

liquidity.definition: The amount of cash available to a person or business;  the ease with which a person or business can obtain cash.

lis pendens.definition: (Latin) A pending lawsuit; a doctrine that  requires purchasers of property or other people with an interest  in property subject to a lawsuit to be bound by the outcome of  the litigation.

lis pendens, notice of.definition: A public notice informing the community that a particular property is subject to litigation and that prospective purchasers of the property will be bound by any judgment affecting it.

list.definition: A document containing several items of the same type, such  as a court calendar or schedule, or a registry of voters. V. To  compile a list; to register; to put a property up for sale with a real  estate agent.

listed stock.definition: Stock in a company that meets the Securities and  Exchange Commission’s requirements and is traded on an organ-  ized stock exchange; also called a listed security.

listing.definition: (1) Putting real estate up for sale; entering an agency  agreement with a real estate agent to sell a piece of property.  (2) Entering into a contract to place a company’s stock on a  stock exchange.

list price.definition: The publicly advertised price of an item.

litigant.definition: A party to a lawsuit; a plaintiff or defendant.

litigate.definition: To bring a dispute to a court of law; to bring or defend a  lawsuit in court.

litigation.definition: A lawsuit or legal action; the act of bringing or  defending a lawsuit. ADJ. litigious.

litigator.definition: A lawyer who represents parties in lawsuits in courts of  law; a specialist in litigation.

living wage.definition: A wage sufficient to meet ordinary living expenses.  See also minimum wage.

living will.definition: A document created by a person while still in relatively good mental condition stating in advance his or her wishes for medical treatment if and when he or she is no longer able to communicate and provide informed consent; see also advance directive, durable power of attorney.

Lloyd’s of London.definition: A group of private insurance underwriters  based in London, England, that originally specialized in marine  insurance but now will agree to underwrite a wide variety of risks.

loan.definition: Something that is borrowed, especially money; the act of  lending something to someone, with the intention of having it  returned at some future time. V. To lend; to borrow.

loan, personal.definition: A secured or unsecured loan to be used for  personal purposes as opposed to commercial ones.

loan, secured.definition: A loan that is secured with property or securities;  also called a collateral loan.

loan commitment.definition: A promise made by a bank or other lending  institution to a prospective purchaser of real estate that it will lend  him or her a specified sum at a specified interest rate to purchase  a particular property if it is done within a specified period of time.

loaned servant.definition: An employee hired out to another employer  for a particular job, who comes under the control of the  temporary employer and for whom the temporary employer  becomes liable.

loan shark.definition: A person who lends money at extortionate interest  rates, usually illegally.

loansharking.definition: The act of lending money at excessively high  interest. See also extort, usury.

lobbyist.definition: A person who works to influence politicians on partic-  ular issues and to persuade them to support or argue against partic-  ular items of proposed legislation.

local government.definition: The government of a geographical region  smaller than a state, such as a city or county, with control over  local matters.

lockdown.definition: Confining prisoners to their cells temporarily in order  to restore security after a riot or other emergency.

lockout.definition: An employer’s refusal to allow employees to work  during periods of negotiation; the employer’s version of a strike.

locus contractus.definition: The place where a contract was made.

locus delicti.definition: The place where an offense occurred.

locus in quo.definition: The place in which, i.e., the place where something  occurred.

logrolling.definition: A method used by legislators to pass legislation  without actually persuading fellow lawmakers of the validity of the  proposed law, done by combining several unrelated matters into  one bill in an effort to attract the votes of people who support indi-  vidual parts of it, or by exchanging favors such as agreeing to vote  for one another’s bills.

loiter.definition: To stand around idly; to move slowly and purposelessly. N.  loitering.

long arm statute.definition: A state statute that gives the state personal  jurisdiction over nonresident defendants in matters that affect  local residents and property.

long-term.definition: Occurring over a long period of time.

long-term capital gain or loss.definition: A gain or loss that occurs from  the sale of capital assets that have been held for the required length  of time, usually at least one year.

long-term debt.definition: Debt with a maturity date at least one year in  the future.

long-term financing.definition: A loan or mortgage that has a term of one  year or more.

lookout.definition: (1) A person who stands near the place where a crime  is being committed to watch for police or other witnesses; a person  stationed to watch for potential danger. (2) The watchfulness and  diligence that a driver of an automobile must use while driving.

loophole.definition: An ambiguity in a law; an inadequacy in a law that  allows a person to legally avoid complying with it, especially in  tax laws.

loss.definition: The fact or act of losing something; something that is lost;  the detriment or damage resulting from the destruction or ruin of  something; injury; expense.

loss, casualty.definition: The destruction of property by an unexpected  cause such as fire or flood.

loss of earning capacity.definition: Damage to a person’s ability to earn  money in the future.

lot.definition: (1) An item or collection of items delivered or sold as one  unit. (2) A plot of land. (3) A set of shares of stock to be traded; see  also odd lot.

lottery.definition: A game of chance in which people pay money for a  chance to win a prize that is awarded at random.

love and affection.definition: The emotional support and kind feelings  shared by close relatives such as parents and children or husbands  and wives; love and affection can serve as adequate consideration  for a gift between relatives.

loyalty oath.definition: An oath of allegiance to a government or other insti-  tution, required of some public officials.

lump sum.definition: A single payment of an entire amount due. See also  installment.

lying in wait.definition: The act of hiding in order to ambush someone or  attack suddenly.

lynch.definition: For a mob to kill someone for a suspected but unproven  offense, usually by hanging, without the approval or authority of  the law.

Maastricht Treaty.definition: The treaty signed in Maastricht, the  Netherlands, in 1992 that created the European Union.

machination.definition: Scheming or plotting, usually to carry out some  evil purpose.

mafia.definition: A body of organized criminals who use extortion, threats,  and violence to influence business or government; see also organ-  ized crime, mob.

magistrate.definition: A public official with judicial, executive, or legisla-  tive power granted by the government, often functioning as a judge  over minor matters or a justice of the peace.

magistrate’s court.definition: A court over which a magistrate presides,  handling small claims and minor matters.

Magna Carta.definition: (Latin) Great Charter; an agreement signed by  King John in England in 1215, guaranteeing political rights and  liberty to his nobles and forming the foundation of English rights  and privileges.

Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act.definition: A federal statute that requires  warranties for consumer products to be written in clear, easily  understandable language.

mail.definition: Letters or packages transported from sender to recipient  through a postal system. V. To address a package or letter, place the  proper postage on it, and deliver it into the keeping of a postal  system.

mailbox rule.definition: A doctrine in contract law stating that an offer is  accepted at the time a written acceptance is deposited in the mail.

maim.definition: To injure so as to cause permanent damage to some part  of the body; to cripple someone.

maintain.definition: To support; to continue; to keep something in a condi-  tion to continue and to prevent decline or decay; to continue to  prosecute a lawsuit once beginning it.

maintenance.definition: (1) The upkeep of property. (2) Providing financial  support for another person.

majority.definition: (1) A number greater than half of a total; the larger  group or number. (2) The age at which a person becomes an adult.

majority rule.definition: A form of government in which the desire of the  largest group within the whole determines how the entire state,  nation, or organization will act.

majority vote.definition: A vote in which the person who receives the  greatest number of votes is declared the winner.

maker.definition: A person who makes or creates something; a person who  creates a law; a person who signs a check or promissory note.

malice.definition: Evil intention; an intention to do a wrongful act or  commit a crime without any justification or excuse.

malice aforethought.definition: A state of mind in which a person deliber-  ately plans to commit a crime before actually performing it, char-  acterized by cruelty and a disregard for the consequences to  human life and social duties.

malicious.definition: Intending to do harm; done out of ill will and  without justification.

malicious abuse of legal process.definition: Intentionally using the legal  process for an improper purpose.

malicious arrest.definition: Arresting on criminal charges a person who is  known not to have committed a crime or arresting a person  without probable cause.

malicious prosecution.definition: Criminal or civil litigation brought  without probable cause and out of malice; if the defendant  prevails in such a case, he or she may bring an action for the tort  of malicious prosecution.

malinger.definition: To pretend to be sick or disabled to get out of work or  to keep receiving disability compensation.

malpractice.definition: Incompetence or improper conduct by a profes-  sional performing professional duties.

malpractice, legal.definition: Failure by an attorney to use the degree of  skill or care that should ordinarily be exercised in a situation.

malpractice, medical.definition: Failure by a physician to use the degree  of skill or care that should ordinarily be exercised in a situation.

malum in se.definition: (Latin) Bad in itself; describes an act that is  inherently wrong in itself without regard to what the law says.

malum prohibitum.definition: (Latin) Bad because it is prohibited;  describes an act that is wrong because it is prohibited by law.

mandate.definition: (1) A judicial order or command directing that some  action be taken, especially from a higher court to a lower one. (2)  Authority granted to a person to do some act, usually granted to an  elected official by the electorate. V. (1) To require that something  be done. (2) To give someone authority to do something.

manifest.definition: Evident; obvious. N. A document listing the cargo  and passengers on a ship or aircraft.

manifesto.definition: A public declaration of political principles issued by  a sovereign or politician; a formal document issued by an executive  officer of a state declaring its reasons for taking some international  action.

manipulate.definition: To influence someone unfairly; to control someone’s  actions; to buy or sell a security in order to give a false impression  about its value and thus influence the purchasing or selling deci-  sions of others. N. manipulation. ADJ. manipulative.

Mann Act.definition: A federal statute that makes it a crime to transport a  woman or girl across state lines for immoral purposes such as  prostitution; also called the White Slave Traffic Act.

manslaughter.definition: The unlawful and unjustifiable killing of another  person without premeditation or malice aforethought.

manslaughter, involuntary.definition: Accidental or unintentional killing of  a person through negligence or recklessness.

manslaughter, voluntary.definition: The intentional but not premeditated  killing of a person in the heat of passion.

margin.definition: (1) An edge or border; the place where water meets  land. (2) The difference between two prices, such as the selling  price of an item and its cost of production. (3) The amount paid to  a securities broker by a customer when using the broker’s credit to  buy securities. ADJ. marginal.

margin transaction.definition: The purchasing of securities using a combi-  nation of cash and a loan extended by the broker making the  purchase.

marijuana.definition: Cannabis; a plant whose leaves can be smoked as  an illegal drug; also spelled marihuana; also called pot, grass,  weed, Mary Jane.

marital agreement.definition: An agreement or contract between a  married couple or a couple about to get married, usually stating  which partner owns what marital property.

marital communications privilege.definition: The doctrine that a person  does not have to disclose confidential communications made by  his or her spouse during a legal marriage, and that such communi-  cations are inadmissible at trial; see also husband-wife privilege.

marital property.definition: Property acquired by either spouse during a  marriage, which is subject to equitable division if the parties  divorce unless they made a marital agreement that allocated it; also  called marital estate.

marital status.definition: The condition of being single, married, divorced,  separated, or widowed.

maritime.definition: Related to the sea and seafaring; connected with all  bodies of navigable water and commerce upon them.

maritime law.definition: The body of laws governing travel and commerce  on navigable waters.

market.definition: (1) A place where goods and services are bought and  sold. (2) The demand for a particular good or service; the state of  commerce in general. (3) The actions of supply and demand on  commerce; the free market.

marketable.definition: Able to be sold. N. marketability.

marketable title.definition: Title to property that is free from encum-  brances such that a reasonable person would accept it, and that  can be sold or transferred without impediment.

marketplace.definition: The world of commerce and trade.

market price.definition: The price for an item that a buyer will pay and a  seller will accept on the open market; the current price for an item;  also called market value.

marriage.definition: A legally recognized union between a man and a  woman as husband and wife. V. marry.

marriage, common law.definition: A union between a man and a woman  who have not been married by ceremony but who have agreed to  live together as husband and wife and have assumed marital duties.

marriage, same-sex.definition: A union between two men or two women  who live together as spouses; not legally recognized by most states.  See also domestic partner; marriage, common law.

marriage certificate.definition: A document certifying that a couple is  legally married.

marriage license.definition: A document issued by the state authorizing  two people to marry within a stated period of time.

marshal.definition: A public officer who enforces laws; the head of a police  or fire department; a sheriff. V. To arrange; to put in order. See also  United States Marshal.

marshalling assets.definition: Arranging assets into the order in which  they will be used to pay debts.

martial law.definition: Government by the military instead of civilians  during times of war or extreme civil unrest.

master.definition: (1) A person with authority over another; an employer.  (2) The presiding officer in a court of equity, also called a master in  chancery or master in equity. (3) An officer appointed to represent  a court in some matter, also called a special master.

master and servant.definition: The relationship between an employer and  an employee in which the employee performs services for the  employer in return for pay, and the employer directs the employee  and has the right to fire him or her.

master plan.definition: The long-term plan for some governmental project,  such as the plan for the urban development of a city.

material.definition: Essential; important; relevant to establishing a  cause of action or arriving at a judgment; see also immaterial.  N. materiality.

material alteration.definition: A change to a document that alters its orig-  inal meaning or its legal effect.

material evidence.definition: Evidence that influences the judge’s or jury’s  perception of the facts in a case; evidence with a real bearing on  the issues.

material fact.definition: A fact that is relevant and important to the matter  at hand and that would influence the actions of other parties if  made known to them; a fact that would cause someone to decide  to enter or not enter a contract.

material witness.definition: A witness who is the only person, or one of  very few people, who can testify about a particular fact.

maternal.definition: Having to do with mothers and motherhood.

maternal line.definition: A line of descent traced through mothers.

maternity.definition: The condition of motherhood. See also paternity.

maternity leave.definition: Leave granted a worker during pregnancy, or  after the birth or adoption of a new child.

matricide.definition: The killing of a mother by her child; a person who  kills his or her own mother.

matrimonial action.definition: A lawsuit related to the marital status  of the parties, such as an action for divorce, separation, or  annulment.

matrimony.definition: The state of marriage; the ceremony in which two  people are married. ADJ. matrimonial.

matter.definition: A situation or state of affairs; the facts that comprise a  cause of action or defense.

matter in controversy.definition: The situation that is the basis of a lawsuit.

matter of fact.definition: The factual issues to be ascertained at trial; also  called a question of fact.

matter of law.definition: A question of the proper law to apply to the facts  of a case, to be determined by the judge; also called a question of  law.

matter of record.definition: An issue that can be proven by the official  record on which it has been entered.

mature.definition: Fully developed; due for payment. N. maturity.

maturity date.definition: The date on which a note or other commercial  obligation comes due.

maturity value.definition: The amount due for a mature note or other obli-  gation.

maxim.definition: A short statement of a principle or rule of law, often used  in deciding equity cases.

mayhem.definition: (1) Violent disorder or chaos. (2) The crime of mali-  ciously injuring, maiming, or dismembering someone so as to  render the victim permanently unable to fight.

means.definition: (1) Resources, especially financial. (2) The action or  method that allows something to happen.

mediate.definition: To intervene in a dispute in order to help the parties  resolve it.

mediation.definition: A form of alternate dispute resolution in which a  neutral third party, the mediator, hears the testimony of both  parties to a dispute and tries to help them agree on a solution, but  cannot impose a decision on them.

Medicaid.definition: A health insurance program run by the U.S.  Department of Health and Human Services that pays for health  care for low-income individuals, the disabled, and several other  categories that people may fall under.

Medicare.definition: A health insurance program run by the U.S.  Department of Health and Human Services that pays for health  care for people who are at least 65 years old and certain other  individuals.

meeting of minds.definition: The state of agreement about the terms and  conditions of a contract that the parties to it must reach for it to be  validly created.

member.definition: A person or business that belongs to a larger group or  organization.

member bank.definition: A bank that is affiliated with the Federal Reserve  System.

member firm.definition: A securities brokerage firm that belongs to a  particular stock exchange.

memorandum.definition: (1) An informal document that records the details  of a transaction, event, or agreement, or discusses some matter. (2)  A document that examines the facts of a case and applies the law  to it to see whether it has merit or not, usually written by a law  clerk or low-level attorney at the request of a higher-level attorney,  and considered attorney work product; also called a memorandum  of law or a memo.

memorandum of understanding.definition: A legal document that  expresses an agreement between two parties but that is not as  formal or binding as a contract. ABBRV. MOU.

menace.definition: To threaten, especially with the prospect of injury. N.  A threatening person or thing; a danger. ADJ. menacing. See  also assault.

mens rea.definition: The mental state that occurs when committing a  crime, i.e., guilt, criminal intent, or knowledge that a crime is being  committed; one of the four mental states in which a crime may be  committed, i.e., intentionally, knowingly, recklessly, or negligently.

mens rea, general.definition: A general intention to commit a crime.

mens rea, specific.definition: The specific mental state that is necessary  to convict someone of particular crimes.

mental anguish.definition: Extreme distress, anxiety, and any other mental  pain that is extreme enough to merit damages for the victim.

mental capacity.definition: A person’s ability to understand what he or she  is doing or the effects that other people’s actions will have on him  or her; see also capacity.

mercantile.definition: Related to merchants, trade, and commerce;  commercial.

mercantile law.definition: Commercial law; the law that governs  merchants, trade, and commerce.

merchant.definition: A person who buys and sells goods, usually buying  wholesale and selling at retail; a trader; a person who imports and  exports goods for sale.

merchantable.definition: Salable; able to be sold; of the type described  and fit for the purpose for which it is sold. N. merchantability.

Mercosur.definition: A free trade agreement between Argentina, Brazil,  Paraguay, Uruguay, and several other South American nations that  are associate members.

mercy killing.definition: Euthanasia; killing a person or animal because  continued life would be intolerable due to a painful, incurable  illness or severe injury.

merge.definition: To combine two or more things together into one whole.

merger.definition: (1) The combination of two or more entities, rights, or  other things into a single whole, especially the absorption of the  minor entities, rights, or things into the largest of them. (2) The  combining of two or more corporations into one, with one of them  absorbing the others into itself. (3) The absorption of one estate  into another when one person comes into possession of both of  them. (4) Sentencing a person convicted of two or more crimes for  only one of them. (5) After a plaintiff receives judgment in his or  her favor, the combination of that judgment with other possible

merits.definition: The legal substance of a claim or defense.

mesne profits.definition: (1) Profits occurring between two points in time.  (2) Profits earned from land by a person who has no right to it,  recoverable by the landowner.

message board.definition: A type of Internet forum in which users can post  and respond to messages on various topics.

meta tags.definition: Codes written in a computer language such as HTML  or XML that identify particular types of text or images on a web  page or document.

metes and bounds.definition: Boundaries of property; a method of meas-  uring and describing land boundaries using distances and angles  from surrounding properties and landmarks.

military.definition: Related to war, the armed forces, and soldiers.

military jurisdiction.definition: As listed in the Constitution, the three  kinds of military authority over civil matters: military law,  which governs the military during peacetime and war; military  government, in which a military commander appointed by the  president supersedes civilian laws during times of civil and  foreign war; and, martial law, ordered by the president during  times of dire emergencies.

militia.definition: An organization of citizens, civilians or military that  defends a locality, state, or nation, but that is not part of a standing  army; an organization of so-called citizen soldiers, such as the  National Guard.

mineral.definition: A solid inorganic substance naturally occurring in the  earth, often obtained by mining, drilling, or quarrying, such as coal,  oil, gas, iron, gold, uranium, etc.

mineral rights.definition: The right to take minerals out of a particular  piece of land.

minimum contacts.definition: The minimum level of contact with a forum  state that a nonresident defendant must have to be subjected to  personal jurisdiction in the courts of that state without offending  due process of law; usually intentional business activity in the  forum state is enough.

minimum wage.definition: The federally established lowest hourly wage  that employers engaged in interstate commerce must pay their  employees, or the equivalent established by an individual state, set  at a level to allow workers to maintain their health and well-being.

mine.definition: An opening made in the earth to allow the extraction of  minerals.
