mining.definition: The process or business of extracting minerals from the  earth.

mining lease.definition: A lease that allows the lessee to enter land to  search for minerals on it and to extract them if found.

minister.definition: (1) A member of the clergy, particularly of Protestant  Christian denominations. (2) The head of a governmental depart-  ment. (3) A diplomatic official who represents one state or nation  to another. (4) (Archaic) An agent; someone who acts for someone  else. V. To care for; to meet someone’s needs.

ministerial act.definition: An act done by an agent or subordinate who is  acting on orders from a superior.

minor.definition: A person below the age of majority; a child or juvenile.  ADJ. Small; of less importance.

minority.definition: (1) The smaller group or number of a whole; a number  less than half of a total; the opposite of majority. (2) The condition  of being a minor; childhood. (3) A disadvantaged group of people;  a group of people that comprises a relatively small part of the  whole population and that suffers due to its race, religion, sex, etc.

minority stockholder.definition: A stockholder who holds very few  shares in a corporation and thus is unable to vote effectively for  corporate directors.

minutes.definition: A transcript of a meeting or event; a record of court  proceedings.

Miranda Rule.definition: A rule requiring that before a person is interro-  gated about a crime he or she be informed of legal protections  against self-incrimination and the right to counsel, a procedure  called the Miranda warning or reading someone his or her rights;  evidence obtained from questioning done without first reading the  Miranda warning is inadmissible in court (except for impeachment  purposes).

misappropriate.definition: To dishonestly take something that belongs to  someone else and use it for oneself; to use someone else’s property  for a purpose for which it was not intended; also called misapply.  N. misappropriation.

miscarriage.definition: (1) An unsuccessful outcome, especially due to  mismanagement. (2) The premature termination of a pregnancy  before the fetus is able to survive outside the womb. V.  miscarry.

miscarriage of justice.definition: Errors made by a court that damage the  rights of a party sufficiently to warrant reversal of the decision.

miscegenation.definition: The genetic mingling of races; interracial  marriage.

mischief.definition: Harm; trouble; the act of damaging property or injuring  a person intentionally or recklessly.

misconduct.definition: Improper behavior; neglect of duties; mismanage-  ment.

misconduct in office.definition: Unlawful or corrupt behavior by a public  official in the conduct of official duties.

miscreant.definition: A person who breaks a law or a rule; a person who  behaves badly. ADJ. miscreant.

misdelivery.definition: Delivery of goods to the wrong recipient or the  wrong destination; delivery of goods damaged by the carrier  entrusted with their transport; failure to deliver goods at all.

misdemeanor.definition: A minor crime, less serious than a felony and  usually punished by less severe penalties.

misfeasance.definition: Doing a legally required duty in the wrong way.

mislead.definition: To give someone the wrong idea or impression about  something; to deceive. ADJ. misleading.

misnomer.definition: An inaccurate name; a mistake made in recording  someone’s name.

misrepresent.definition: To make a false or inaccurate statement; to give  the impression by words or behavior that a situation is different  from what is in fact the case. N. misrepresentation.

mistake.definition: An incorrect or wrong action or belief; an act or  omission caused by ignorance or misconception about the true  situation.

mistake, mutual.definition: A mistake made by both parties to an  agreement.

mistake, unilateral.definition: A mistake made by only one party to an  agreement.

mistake of fact.definition: An error caused by ignorance or misconception  of a fact.

mistake of law.definition: An error occurring when a party understands the  facts but is ignorant or confused as to how the law applies to them.

mistrial.definition: An invalid trial; a trial terminated before a judgment  is reached due to circumstances such as a hung jury, lack of juris-  diction, or another fundamental problem.

mitigate.definition: To make something less severe or serious. N. miti-  gation.

mitigating circumstances.definition: Circumstances that make a crime or  offense less serious without excusing the act.

mitigation of damages.definition: A doctrine requiring the victim of a tort  or breach to use reasonable diligence and ordinary care to mini-  mize injury or damage.

mob.definition: (1) A large and unruly group of people, especially a group  engaged in illegal and destructive behavior; see also riot. (2) The  mafia; organized crime in general. V. For a large group of people to  swarm over and overwhelm an area.

Model Rules of Professional Conduct.definition: A set of rules created by  the American Bar Association and adopted by many states that  govern the conduct of attorneys.

modus operandi.definition: (Latin) A way of operating; a way of doing  things; especially used to refer to a criminal’s habitual methods;  also called m.o.

molest.definition: To engage in sexual touching, up to and including sexual  intercourse, with an unwilling partner.

money.definition: Coins, paper currency or banknotes, and other mediums  of exchange authorized by a government; wealth.

money demand.definition: A claim made by a plaintiff for recovery of a  specified sum of money.

money judgment.definition: A final judgment by a court ordering the defen-  dant to pay a sum of money to the plaintiff.

money order.definition: A negotiable instrument purchased by the person  who wants to pay a sum of money from a bank, post office, or other  third party that makes its credit available, and made payable to a  named recipient.

money supply.definition: All money in the economy at a given time.

monopoly.definition: Exclusive possession or control of a supply of some  commodity or an industry by one or a few people or businesses; a  market condition in which a single person or company controls the  entire trade in something. V. monopolize.

Montreal Convention.definition: An international treaty created in 1999  that establishes rules for the compensation of victims of air  disasters.

moot.definition: Uncertain or unsettled; subject to debate. V. To raise an  issue for debate; to discuss.

moot case.definition: A case that seeks a decision on some issue that has  no actual controversy or no practical application because the issue  has already been resolved.

moot court.definition: A mock court set up by a law school to try hypothet-  ical cases as an exercise in oral advocacy for law students.

moral.definition: Concerned with character, the distinction between right  and wrong, proper social interactions, and ethics.

moral certainty.definition: Certainty about something that is beyond a  reasonable doubt but not quite absolute; enough certainty about an

moral turpitude.definition: Depravity, dishonesty, or vileness; conduct that  grossly violates acceptable standards of morality and behavior.

moratorium.definition: A temporary stop to or prohibition of a particular  activity; a temporary period in which debtors may postpone  payment of debts.

mortal.definition: Deadly or fatal; related to death.

mortality.definition: Death; the number of deaths from a particular  cause or at a particular time or place; the condition of being  subject to death.

mortality tables.definition: Actuarial tables that insurance companies use  to determine how long a person is likely to live.

mortgage.definition: An interest in real property held by a creditor who  lends money to a debtor to purchase the property and takes title to  or a lien on the property as security for the loan. V. To give a cred-  itor an interest in property as security for a loan.

mortgage, adjustable rate.definition: A mortgage in which the interest rate  is tied to an index and periodically moves up or down as the index  does. ABBRV. ARM.

mortgage, balloon payment.definition: A mortgage in which the borrower  pays regular interest payments for a period and then must pay the  entire principal at once.

mortgage, fixed-rate.definition: A mortgage with an interest rate that  remains the same throughout the life of the mortgage.

mortgage broker.definition: A firm or person who arranges mortgages  between borrowers and lenders.

mortgagee.definition: A person who lends money to another through a  mortgage.

mortgagor.definition: A person who borrows money to acquire title to  property and pledges that property as security for the debt.

mortmain.definition: (French) Dead hand; the state of property owned by  an ecclesiastical or other organization.

most favored nation.definition: A nation that is accorded the most favor-  able rights and privileges, especially trade rights, that can be  granted by another nation. ABBRV. MFN.

motion.definition: A formal application to the court asking for a rule or  order in favor of the applicant, such as a grant of summary judg-  ment, of judgment notwithstanding the verdict, to dismiss a  complaint, or a new trial. See also move.

motion in limine.definition: A motion made before trial asking the court to  prevent the other party from introducing evidence that would be  too prejudicial to the party making the motion.

Motion Picture Association of America.definition: A nonprofit trade asso-  ciation that lobbies on behalf of film studios, particularly in the  realm of copyright, digital rights management, and free speech.  See also Digital Millennium Copyright Act. ABBRV. MPAA.

motion to suppress.definition: In a criminal case, a request to exclude  evidence that has been obtained illegally.

motive.definition: The reason for doing some act; the idea or circum-  stances that cause someone to do something.

multifarious.definition: Of various types; having multiple parts.

multifarious suit.definition: A lawsuit in which dissimilar causes of action  or parties are joined incorrectly.

multilateral.definition: Involving the participation of at least three  parties, especially governments or nations.

multiplicity.definition: A large number or great variety of something.

multiplicity of actions.definition: Several lawsuits on the same matter  brought by different plaintiffs against the same defendant; also  called multiplicity of suits.

municipal.definition: Related to a city or town or its government, or  another local government; occasionally used in reference to state  or national government.

municipal bonds.definition: Securities sold by a city, county, state, or other  local government body to raise money for municipal expenses and  projects, payable at a specified future date with interest that is  exempt from federal and some state income tax.

municipal court.definition: A city court with jurisdiction over local matters,  including small civil matters and crimes occurring within the city.

municipality.definition: The population of a specific area that has been  incorporated and governs itself; a city, village, or town; also called  a municipal corporation.

municipal ordinance.definition: A law enacted by a municipality to regu-  late local affairs.

murder.definition: The unlawful killing of a person by another person with  malice aforethought. ADJ. murderous.

murder, first-degree.definition: An unlawful killing that is willful, delib-  erate, and premeditated.

murder, second-degree.definition: An unlawful killing without delibera-  tion or premeditation but with malice aforethought.

murder, serial.definition: The murder by one killer of several victims over  a period of time.

mutilate.definition: To injure badly; to deprive someone of the use of a  limb; to inflict serious, disfiguring damage on someone or some-  thing; to damage or alter a document so that it becomes imperfect.  N. mutilation.

mutiny.definition: A rebellion by soldiers or sailors against their  commanding officers. V. mutiny. ADJ. mutinous.

mutual.definition: Held in common by both parties; reciprocal.

mutual company.definition: A company owned by members who also do  business with the company, and who receive profits in proportion  to their business with the company.

mutual fund.definition: A fund run by an investment company that pools  the contributions of various investors who purchase shares in the  fund, and invests them in a selection of publicly traded securities.

mutual insurance company.definition: An insurance company owned by  the people it insures, who share in the company’s profits.

mutuality.definition: A mutual or reciprocal action, quality, or condition.

mutuality of remedy.definition: The principle that one party cannot receive  an equitable remedy that the other party could not also receive.

n/a.definition: (1) Not available; not applicable. (2) (Latin) Non allo-  catur; not allowed.

name.definition: The word or words used to identify a person, animal,  business, or other entity. V. To give a name to someone or  something.

named insured.definition: The person designated on an insurance policy as  having a contract for insurance.

narcotic.definition: A pain-relieving drug that also causes drowsiness or  stupor and is often addictive, many of which are used medically but  also abused illegally. ADJ. narcotic.

NASDAQ.definition: National Association of Securities Dealers  Automated Quotations.

National Association of Securities Deale.definition: A computerized information system used in trading  securities. ABBRV. NASDAQ.

National Guard.definition: A militia trained and equipped by the U.S. Army  and Air Force that serves as reserve forces and can be mobilized by  the president to help the armed forces with military operations or  during states of emergency.

National Labor Relations Board.definition: An independent agency estab-  lished by Congress through the National Labor Relations Act that  works to prevent unfair labor practices by either employers or  labor unions, oversees union elections, and adjudicates claims.  ABBRV. NLRB.

national origin.definition: The country in which a person was born or from  which his or her immediate ancestors emigrated.

native.definition: A person who was born in a particular place; a person  who is a citizen of a place because he or she was born there, or  because his or her parents are citizens. ADJ. native.

natural.definition: Caused by nature or existing in nature; not arti-  ficial.

natural affection.definition: The emotional state that is supposed to exist  naturally between parents and children, spouses, and siblings, and  that can function as valuable consideration for a contract.

natural child.definition: A child that is related to his or her parents by  blood, not adoption.

natural death.definition: Death from natural causes, such as illness or old  age, and not caused by violence or accident.

naturalization.definition: The process of becoming a citizen of a country if  one was not born there.

naturalize.definition: To become a citizen of a country other than one’s  place of birth.

naturalized citizen.definition: A person who has earned citizenship by  fulfilling residence and other requirements, as opposed to merely  being born in a nation or to citizen parents.

natural law.definition: Moral rules and principles believed to govern  human behavior naturally, as an inherent part of human nature,  regardless of laws enacted by people; see also positivism.

natural life.definition: A person’s actual physical life that ends with phys-  ical death.

natural person.definition: A human being; an actual person instead of an  artificial entity such as a corporation.

navigable.definition: Able to be traveled; describes waters accessible to  boats, or roads accessible to vehicular traffic.

navigable waters.definition: Waterways that can be traveled down for  commerce.

n.b.definition: (Latin) Nota bene, meaning note well, observe; used to  draw a reader’s attention to an important point.

necessary and proper clause.definition: Language in Article I of the U.S.  Constitution authorizing Congress to pass laws that are necessary  and proper to carrying out the powers granted to the government  by the Constitution.

necessary parties.definition: Parties who must be included in a lawsuit  because complete relief would be impossible without them.

necessity.definition: Something that is required or must be done; the state  of being required.

negative.definition: (1) Characterized by absence rather than presence.  (2) Expressing refusal, denial, or disagreement.

negative condition.definition: A requirement in an agreement that some-  thing must not happen.

negative covenant.definition: A covenant that prevents someone from  doing something, such as selling goods in the same region as  someone else.

negative easement.definition: An easement that prevents a landowner  from doing certain things to the property.

neglect.definition: To fail to do something; to fail to care for someone or  something; to disregard or ignore. N. The state of being uncared for  or ignored; failure to do something.

neglected child.definition: A child whose caregivers fail to provide the  required level of care for his or her physical, mental, and emotional  needs.

negligence.definition: Failure to use the proper care in doing something,  i.e., the amount of care that an ordinarily prudent person would use  under the same circumstances. ADJ. negligent.

negligence, contributory.definition: A plaintiff’s negligence that is at least  a partial proximate cause of the injury.

negligence, criminal.definition: Negligence that can constitute a crime;  such carelessness or disregard for the safety of others that it makes  the actor criminally liable.

negligence, gross.definition: Failure to use even the slightest care.

negligence, willful, wanton, or reckless.definition: Negligence done with  complete disregard to the risks and with conscious indifference to  the consequences. See also comparative negligence.

negligence per se.definition: Conduct that is treated as negligence  because it violates a statute.

negotiable.definition: Able to be transferred to another person;  modifiable.

negotiable instrument.definition: A legal document signed by its maker  that promises unconditionally to pay a specified sum at a specified

negotiate.definition: To discuss a matter in an effort to reach agreement,  often suggesting different possibilities for consideration and  acceptance or refusal; to bargain. (2) To transfer the legal owner-  ship of a negotiable instrument. N. negotiation.

nepotism.definition: The practice by a public official or other person with  power and influence of giving choice jobs to close relatives; prefer-  ential hiring of relatives.

net.definition: The total amount remaining after all expenses and  deductions.

net asset value.definition: The value of a share of stock in a mutual fund  or corporation, calculated by subtracting total liabilities from total  assets and dividing by the number of outstanding shares; book  value.

net cost.definition: The total cost of an item minus any financial gain  from it.

net estate.definition: A gross estate minus funeral expenses, claims against  the estate, mortgages and other debts, and any other proper deduc-  tions that must come from the estate during settlement.

net loss.definition: The situation arising when expenses are greater than  income; negative income.

net operating loss.definition: The loss resulting when the expenses  involved in operating a business are greater than the revenues it  generates.

net worth.definition: The total assets of a person, business, etc., minus total  liabilities; also called net assets.

neutral.definition: Unbiased, disinterested; not taking either side of a  dispute; neither positive nor negative. N. neutrality.

neutrality laws.definition: Laws that govern warfare as it concerns neutral  nations; laws passed by Congress that govern the United States  when it functions as a neutral party while other nations are at war.

new.definition: Novel; fresh; appearing more recently than another  similar item or person.

newly discovered evidence.definition: Evidence discovered after a trial is  concluded and judgment rendered that could not have been discov-  ered before trial and that can be grounds for a new trial if it prob-  ably would result differently.

new matter.definition: In pleading, a new issue with new facts that has not  been already alleged by a party and added by an amended or  supplemental pleading.

next friend.definition: A person who acts on behalf of an infant, incompe-  tent, or other person who is not sui juris and is therefore unable to  represent himself or herself adequately in the action; the next  friend is not a party to the suit and acts as an officer of the court;  see also ad litem, guardian.

next of kin.definition: The living person or people most closely related by  blood to someone; the person or people who will take a decedent’s  estate under the rule of descent and distribution.

nil.definition: (Latin) Nothing; shortened form of Latin word “nihil,”  meaning “nothing”; used in writs and judgments to convey various  negative meanings.

nisi.definition: (Latin) Unless; used to describe an order, judgment, etc.,  that will take effect unless some specific thing happens.

no fault.definition: Used to describe a situation in which the fault of the  parties is irrelevant to the outcome, such as an insurance policy in  which the insurer will pay for damage regardless of whether the  insured was at fault in causing it; see also divorce.

nolo contendere.definition: (Latin) “I do not want to contend,” or “I am  unwilling to contest it”; a plea used by a defendant in a criminal  case that neither admits nor denies charges but has most of the  effects of a guilty plea.

nominal.definition: Existing in name only, having no real presence;  merely stated, not actually existing; so small as to be strictly  symbolic.

nominal damages.definition: A very small sum awarded as damages to a  plaintiff who has suffered no real compensable injury.

nominal party.definition: A plaintiff or defendant joined to an action for  jurisdictional or procedural reasons, not because of any injury or  liability.

nominee.definition: A person who has been nominated for a position.

non compos mentis.definition: (Latin) Not of sound mind; insane. See  also compos mentis.

nonconforming use.definition: A use of property that began lawfully  before the creation of a zoning restriction that makes it unlawful;  see also variance.

nonconformity.definition: An aspect of some object that does not conform  to the prevailing standard for objects of that kind.

noncontestability clause.definition: A clause in an insurance contract that  prevents the insurer from contesting a policy due to mistake or  fraud after a specified period of time.

nondelivery.definition: Failure by a carrier to deliver goods to the proper  recipient.

nondisclosure.definition: Failure to disclose information or reveal facts.

nonexempt.definition: Not exempt; eligible for overtime pay under the  National Labor Relations Act.

nonfeasance.definition: Failure to perform a duty; failure by an agent to  perform some obligation he or she has agreed to do for a principal;  failure of a public official to do some duty required by law. See  also malfeasance, misfeasance.

nonperformance.definition: Failure to perform some duty or obligation;  failure to perform according to the terms of a contract.

nonprofit.definition: Conducted for purposes other than making a profit,  such as charity or scientific research.

nonprofit corporation.definition: A corporation that does not distribute its income to shareholders or officers as profit and that engages in a purpose such as religion, charity, education, public safety, etc.

nonrecourse debt.definition: A debt for which the borrower is not person-  ally liable and that is instead secured by the purchased property,  which the creditor can repossess if the debtor defaults.

non sequitur.definition: (Latin) It does not follow; a statement that does  not logically follow the statement or argument preceding it; ABBRV.  non seq.

nonstock corporation.definition: A corporation that does not issue shares  of stock but instead grants members ownership through a member-  ship charter or agreement, common among mutual companies and  charities.

nonsuit.definition: A judgment rendered against a plaintiff who fails to  proceed to trial or prove his or her case by not producing enough  evidence to support it; nonsuit is not judgment on the merits and  does not preclude the plaintiff’s bringing the same lawsuit again.

nonsupport.definition: Failure to provide financial support to someone to  whom a duty of support is owed, such as the failure of a parent to  support a child.

normal.definition: Regular; typical; conforming to an established  standard.

North American Free Trade Agreement.definition: A free trade aggreement  between the United States, Canada, and Mexico. ABBRV. NAFTA.

no strike clause.definition: A clause in an agreement between labor and  management prohibiting employees from striking and instead

notary public.definition: A person with the authority to perform a limited  range of legal functions such as administering oaths, witnessing  signatures, drawing up contracts or deeds, taking depositions, etc.

note.definition: (1) A written document in which a borrower promises to  pay back a debt to a specified recipient at a specified time. (2) A  short treatise on some legal subject written by a law student for a  law review.

note, installment.definition: A note that is paid in several installments over  a period of time.

note, mortgage.definition: A note promising payment for a property that  serves as security for the debt.

not guilty.definition: (1) A plea given by a criminal defendant who denies  committing the crime in question. (2) A verdict given by the jury in  a criminal trial if it finds that the state has not proved its case  against the defendant beyond a reasonable doubt.

notice.definition: Notification; warning or knowledge that a fact exists or  that something will occur.

notice, actual.definition: Information expressly given to a person so that  there is no question whether or not it has been received, or infor-  mation that he or she should have had because there was sufficient  evidence available.

notice, constructive.definition: Information that a person is assumed to  have because he or she should have discovered it through  normal diligence.

notice, legal.definition: Notice that the law requires to be given, such as by  advertising in a newspaper; also, notice implied by law such as  actual notice or constructive notice.

notice act.definition: A recording act that gives priority to a bona fide  purchaser who is the first to record the claim and who has no  knowledge of other claims.

notice of dishonor.definition: Notice that a check or other negotiable  instrument has been dishonored.

notice to airmen.definition: A notice filed at an airport to notify pilots of  hazards in the area. ABBRV. NOTAM.

notice to appear.definition: Notice summoning a defendant to appear in  court.

notice to quit.definition: Notice given by a tenant informing the landlord  that the tenant intends to move out; also, notice given by a landlord  informing the tenant that he or she must move out.

notify.definition: To inform someone of something; to give notice. N.  notification.

notorious.definition: (1) Famous; well-known, especially for some bad  behavior or evil deed. (2) Open; easy to observe and not concealed.

notorious possession.definition: Open and unconcealed possession of real  property; see also adverse possession.

N.O.W. account.definition: A kind of interest-bearing checking account.

NSF check.definition: A check written for an amount greater than the sum  in the account on which it is written.

Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty.definition: An international treaty created  in 1968 that requires its members not to make new nuclear  weapons, not to assist other nations in making nuclear weapons,  and to get rid of their own nuclear weapons, while still being  allowed to develop and use nuclear fuel for power generation.  ABBRV. NPT.

nuclear proliferation.definition: The spread of nuclear weapons and  related materials to nations that have not previously had  nuclear weapons.

Nuclear Regulatory Commission.definition: A federal agency that regu-  lates the civilian use of nuclear materials. ABBRV. NRC.

nugatory.definition: Ineffectual or invalid; useless; of no importance.

nuisance.definition: A person, thing, or activity that causes inconvenience  or annoyance; anything that prevents a person from freely enjoying  the use of his or her own property, endangers his or her health, or  offends. See also abate.

nuisance per se.definition: Something that is a nuisance all the time under  any circumstances.

null.definition: Invalid; void; with no legal force; also called null and void.

nullify.definition: To invalidate; to make null and void. N. nullification.

nullity.definition: Something that is legally invalid; the state of being legally  invalid.

nurture.definition: To care for; to raise a child.

oath.definition: The act of swearing that something is true; a promise to tell  the truth in court or perform some act, often sworn before a  witness or invoking a supreme power.

oath, under.definition: Describes the state of a person after swearing an  oath.

oath of allegiance.definition: An oath binding the swearer to some nation  or leader.

oath of office.definition: An oath sworn upon taking some office, prom-  ising to perform the duties of the office properly.

obiter dictum.definition: (Latin) Something said in passing; incidental  remarks or opinions made by a judge that are not essential to judg-  ment in a case.

object.definition: Purpose; goal; aim. V. To protest; to express disapproval;  in court, to protest that the opposing party’s action or statement is  improper or illegal.

objection.definition: The act of protesting an action or statement of the  opposing party in order to draw the court’s attention to illegality  and impropriety and to preserve the issue for appeal.

objective.definition: Purpose or goal. ADJ. Unbiased, not influenced by  outside sources.

obligation.definition: A duty; something that a person is required to do; a  binding agreement that requires a person to do something; a  document containing a binding agreement. V. obligate. ADJ.  obligatory.

oblige.definition: To make someone legally bound to do something or pay  a sum of money.

obligor.definition: The person bound to perform some duty or pay money;  a promisor.

obloquy.definition: Blame; public criticism or disgrace; reproach; public  verbal abuse reproaching someone for some act.

obscene.definition: Morally offensive; against accepted standards of  decency, especially in a sexual way; appealing to sexual interests  and without serious political, literary, scientific, or artistic value. N.  obscenity.

obscure.definition: Hidden; not easily discovered; hard to understand. V.  To conceal; to make difficult to understand.

obsolete.definition: No longer used; out of date; still in existence but no  longer observed due to irrelevance to changed circumstances.

obstruct.definition: To hinder; to get in the way of something; to block or  impede. N. obstruction.

obstruction of justice.definition: Hindering the process of justice in court  or of judicial acts, such as by influencing jurors or preventing an  officer of the court from performing his or her duty.

obvious.definition: Clear; easily seen or understood; apparent.

obvious risk.definition: A risk that is easily discovered by a person of ordi-  nary intelligence.

occupancy.definition: Occupying or taking possession of a property with  title to it.

occupant.definition: A person who occupies a property; a person who  possesses and uses property; a person who holds a particular  public office.

occupation.definition: (1) A job or profession. (2) The act of taking posses-  sion of a property or land; the condition of being possessed.

occupational.definition: Related to a particular job or profession; related  to work.

occupational disease.definition: A disease caused by a particular job or  resulting from long-term exposure to conditions at a particular job.

occupational hazard.definition: A risk that is an accepted aspect of a  particular job, as a natural consequence of working conditions and  actions.

Occupational Safety and Health Act.definition: A federal law intended to  reduce workplace injuries, illnesses, and deaths, administered by  the Occupational Safety and Health Administration. ABBRV. OSHA.

occupy.definition: (1) To reside in; to hold; to fill. (2) To conquer and  inhabit a land or nation with an army.

occurrence.definition: Something that happens; an event or incident.

odd lot.definition: A unit of stock containing fewer shares than the usual  one hundred or ten shares that make up units for sale.

of counsel.definition: Describes an attorney who assists in the prepara-  tion of a case but is not the lead attorney on it, or an attorney who  is semi-retired from a firm or works at it part-time.

offend.definition: To commit an illegal act; to break a rule.

offender.definition: One who commits an illegal act.

offense.definition: An illegal act; an act that violates criminal laws; a felony  or misdemeanor.

offense, criminal.definition: A violation of a criminal law; a crime.

offer.definition: To present something to someone to be accepted or  rejected. N. An expression of willingness to do something or pay a  sum of money; a promise to enter into an agreement if the promise  is accepted.

offer and acceptance.definition: The two necessary components of a  contract; a proposal by one party that is accepted by the other.

offering.definition: A quantity of stock issued for sale at a fixed price.

offer of proof.definition: A presentation of evidence at trial for acceptance  by the court; often done in situations where an objection to a  party’s line of questioning has been sustained but the court gives  the party the opportunity to show privately, away from the jury,  evidence showing the relevance of the question.

office.definition: (1) An official position of authority, often with a  government or corporation. (2) A room or building in which  business is transacted or in which a professional works or  maintains headquarters.

office, home.definition: A room or area in a dwelling set aside as a profes-  sional workplace.

office, public.definition: An official governmental position that gives a  person holding it some degree of governmental powers to be used  to benefit the public.

officer.definition: (1) A person who holds an office. (2) A member of the  armed services with authority to command. (3) A member of  the police force. (4) A person entrusted with the management  of a corporation.

official.definition: A person who holds an office. ADJ. With the authority of  a public body; done by an officer.

offshoring.definition: The practice of a business having some or all of its  operations performed in a foreign country by local workers,  usually for cost reasons.

of record.definition: Officially recorded; entered onto official records.

oligarchy.definition: Rule of a nation, institution, etc., by a few, i.e., by a  small group of people.

oligopoly.definition: A market condition in which only a few sellers control  the entire trade in some product; see also monopoly.

ombudsman.definition: An official in a government or company whose job  is to hear, investigate, and remedy complaints.

omission.definition: The failure to do some duty or required action; the  exclusion or leaving out of something or someone; overlooking  something or someone. V. omit.

omnibus bill.definition: A piece of proposed legislation that addresses  several different matters, often combined in order to force the  executive to accept laws that he or she does not want in order to  avoid defeating the whole package.

on demand.definition: When requested or on presentation, usually used  to describe a negotiable instrument that is payable when presented  instead of on a specified date.

one person, one vote.definition: A form of allocating legislators by district  so that every citizen gets equal representation, in that seats in the  legislature are apportioned by population.

on its face.definition: Upon immediate inspection; used to describe a  law that is void or unconstitutional by its very wording, usually  because it does not address the issue it is intended to address.

online.definition: On a computer network; on the Internet.

on or about.definition: Approximately; in the vicinity of; used to describe  an act that happened around a particular time without having to  specify a date or time, or something that is near a person without  necessarily being physically on him or her.

on the merits.definition: Based on the facts of a case and the rights of  the parties involved.

on the person.definition: In physical contact with the body or in the  clothing being worn.

open.definition: (1) To allow access to something. (2) To create or estab-  lish, e.g. a business, bank account, or legal case. (3) To begin a trial.  ADJ. (1) Visible; frank; not hidden or secretive. (2) Allowing access;  not closed.

open account.definition: (1) An unsettled account; an account intended to  be used for future transactions. (2) An account on which a buyer  can make repeated transactions on credit.

open and notorious.definition: Conspicuous, public, with no attempt at  concealment; see also adverse possession.

open court.definition: A court open to the public, to which anyone  conducting themselves properly may be admitted.

opening statement.definition: A speech made by an attorney at the start of  a trial describing the facts and issues of the case and arguments  that the attorney will make.

open shop.definition: A workplace that employs people without regard to  whether they belong to a union; see also closed shop.

open source.definition: With regard to software, freely distributed, with  source code that is freely available and modifiable, without any  restrictive licenses.

operate.definition: To function; to run something. N. operation.

operating expenses.definition: The money required to run something, such  as a business.

operating system.definition: The software that performs the essential  tasks a computer must do in order to function, such as allocating  memory, controlling devices, supporting software applications,  and providing access to files. ABBRV. OS.

operation of law.definition: The automatic effects of law; used in situa-  tions when the rights or liabilities of parties are determined by  application of law, and not through their own private agree-  ments.

opiate.definition: A drug derived from opium; a narcotic; a substance or  drug with addictive properties and other effects similar to  morphine.

opinion.definition: (1) A statement written by a judge explaining his or her  decision in a case. (2) A statement written by an attorney for a  client explaining his or her evaluation of the facts and law in a case.  (3) Personal beliefs, views, or judgments on a matter.

opinion, concurring.definition: An opinion written by a judge who is part  of a panel of judges, agreeing with the conclusion of the majority  opinion but not necessarily with the reasoning.

opinion, dissenting.definition: An opinion written by one of a panel of  judges disagreeing with the majority opinion.

opinion, expert.definition: A statement made by an expert explaining his  or her evaluation of facts from a qualified perspective.

opinion, majority.definition: An opinion shared by a majority of the judges  on a panel.

opinion, plurality.definition: An opinion shared by less than the majority of  the judges on a panel as to reasoning but that agrees with the  conclusion of the majority.

oppress.definition: To force someone to stay in a position of hardship; to  dominate; to use one’s authority to inflict harm or cruelty on  someone. N. oppression.

option.definition: The right to choose something; something that can be  chosen.

option contract.definition: A contract in which one party promises to  keep an offer open (such as an offer to sell property) for a  specified period of time, usually in return for consideration.  See also stock option.

oral.definition: Verbal; stated in speech, not in writing.

oral argument.definition: A verbal statement made by counsel before a  court arguing in support of a position.

oral confession.definition: A verbal statement confessing to having  committed a crime.

oral contract.definition: A contract that is made verbally, or partly verbally  and partly in writing.

oral evidence.definition: Evidence provided through the speech of a  witness.

order instrument.definition: A negotiable instrument made payable to a  particular person, through language such as “pay to the order of”;  also called order paper.

ordinance.definition: A law, statute, or rule, usually enacted by a municipal  or other local government.

ordinary and necessary expenses.definition: Expenses that are normal,  expected, and appropriate to the conduct of a business.

ordinary course of business.definition: The usual activities and events that  occur during the usual conduct of a particular business or of  commercial activity in general.

ordinary income.definition: Earnings from work or business as opposed to  income from investments.

organic law.definition: The fundamental law of a state or country that  serves as a basis for all other laws and social organization; it may  be written, such as a constitution, or unwritten.

organized crime.definition: Systematic, large-scale crime organized  and executed by professional criminals; see also mafia, mob,  racketeering.

original.definition: First; present at the beginning; created directly by a  particular person, not copied.

original document rule.definition: A rule that a party must produce the  original of a document offered as evidence unless it is not available.

original jurisdiction.definition: Jurisdiction to try and judge a case at its  beginning, as opposed to on appeal.

ouster.definition: Wrongfully forcing someone off his or her own property.  V. oust.

outcome test.definition: The principle that a diversity case should have the  same outcome whether brought in federal or state court.

outline.definition: A summary of the key points of a document, argument,  or topic, arranged as a list; a summary of the key points of a law  school class done by a law student or a commercial publisher,  created as a study aid. V. outline.

outline group.definition: A group of law students who collaborate on an  outline for a class, such as by each contributing an outline of one  week of class so as to compile a complete set of notes for the  semester.

out-of-court.definition: Arranged privately, outside of court or trial, by  parties to a lawsuit.

out-of-court settlement.definition: An agreement reached privately by the  parties to a lawsuit, settling their grievances and ending the lawsuit  without intervention by the court.

out-of-pocket.definition: Having to do with the expenditure of cash,  especially of a financial loss paid personally.

out-of-pocket expense.definition: A necessary expense paid directly by a  person who intends to recover the cost from someone else at a  later time (such as when a tenant pays for repairs to rental prop-  erty and expects reimbursement from the landlord).

out-of-pocket loss.definition: In a warranty or breach of contract case, the  difference between the purchase price and the actual value of an  object.

outrage.definition: The tort of intentional infliction of severe emotional  distress; an extreme insult or violent injury.

outsourcing.definition: The practice of a business hiring another company  or outside individuals to perform some of its work; subcontracting.  See also independent contractor.

outstanding balance.definition: The money still to be paid on a debt.

outstanding check.definition: A check that has been written and given to  its recipient but has not yet been deducted from a bank account.

overbroad.definition: Used to describe a statute that covers too many  situations, i.e., that, while legitimately forbidding certain conduct,  inadvertently also prohibits constitutionally protected activities. N.  overbreadth. See also void, vague.

overdraft.definition: The condition of drawing more money out of an  account than it has in it, such as by writing a check for too great an  amount.

overdue.definition: Not having been done or paid by the appointed date;  delayed.

overhead.definition: Operating expenses of a business aside from cost of  materials or labor.

overreaching.definition: Using fraud or a superior bargaining position to  take advantage of someone else in a commercial transaction.

overrule.definition: To reverse or annul; to overturn or void the decision of  another court.

overt act.definition: In criminal law, an open act done as part of a crime.

over-the-counter market.definition: A securities market of dealers who  buy and sell securities that are not listed in a securities exchange,  done primarily over the telephone or online.

overtime.definition: Time worked in addition to normal working hours.

overtime pay.definition: Wages paid for overtime worked, usually at a  higher rate than ordinary hourly pay.

own.definition: To possess; to hold title to property.

owner.definition: A person or business that has legal title to property.

ownership.definition: The state of owning property, with a right to possess,  use, and dispose of it.

oye.definition: A word meaning “hear ye,” cried by a bailiff to mark the  beginning of a court session; pronounced “oh yes.”

ozone layer.definition: A portion of the atmosphere surrounding the  Earth that contains a high concentration of ozone, a molecule  that plays an important role in absorbing excess ultraviolet  radiation from the sun.

p.a.definition: (1) Professional association. (2) Per annum, meaning  yearly.

pacifist.definition: A person who thinks war and violence are always unjus-  tifiable, and who refuses to participate in war or military activities.  See also conscientious objector.

pact.definition: An agreement between two or more parties.

pain and suffering.definition: Physical and mental distress or injury, for  which damages may be recovered in a tort action.

palimony.definition: Financial support similar to alimony given to a partner  in a terminated nonmarital relationship; see also domestic partner.

pander.definition: To cater to someone’s immoral lusts and desires, such  as by providing pornography; to procure a prostitute for someone.  N. A pimp.

panel.definition: A group of people joined together for some united  purpose, such as a group of judges who sit together to hear the  same case, or the group of jurors summoned to court for jury duty.

paper.definition: A document, including letters, contracts, account books,  promissory notes, and anything else that can be written or printed  on paper; a written or printed instrument.

paper money.definition: Banknotes; documents printed by a government,  backed by its credit, and used as currency.

paper profit or loss.definition: Profit or loss that exists on the books but  has not been converted into cash.

par.definition: (1) A standard or norm used as a benchmark for comparison, such as the value of one nation's currency compared to another's (also called par of exchange), or the normal number of strokes used to complete a golf course. (2) The face value of a stock, bond, or negotiable instrument, as opposed to market value; also called par value.

par, above.definition: Selling for more than face value.

par, at.definition: Selling at face value, when face value and market value  are equal.

par, below.definition: Selling for less than face value.

paralegal.definition: A person with some legal training who assists an  attorney in preparing cases, documents, and other legal tasks.

paramount.definition: Most important; of highest rank; supreme.

paramount title.definition: The superior of two titles to the same property,  i.e., the one that will prevail over the other.

paramour.definition: A lover, usually of a married person committing  adultery.

paraphernalia.definition: (1) Equipment used for a particular activity. (2)  (Archaic) A married woman’s own personal property, apart from a  dowry, that she could keep after the death of her husband.

parcel.definition: (1) A package. (2) A quantity of something, such as a  piece of land or an estate. V. To divide something into portions and  distribute them to various recipients.

pardon.definition: To forgive; to release a convicted criminal from liability  for his or her crime; to absolve. N. An act by a governor, president,  or other government official releasing a convicted criminal from  punishment for a crime and reinstating his or her former level of  civil liberties.

pardon, conditional.definition: A pardon that becomes or remains valid as  long as the pardoned person does or does not observe some spec-  ified condition.

pardon, full.definition: A pardon removing all legal consequences from a  conviction.

pardon, general.definition: A pardon issued to a group of people who have  all committed the same offense, usually political; see also amnesty.

parens patriae.definition: (Latin) Parent of the country; the doctrine that  it is the role of the government to look out for those who cannot  take care of themselves.

parent.definition: (1) A father or mother, natural or adoptive. (2) A  company or organization that controls several subsidiaries, often  called a parent company. ADJ. parental.

parental liability.definition: Parents’ liability for the actions of their minor  children.

parental rights.definition: A parent’s rights in regard to his or her children,  including the right to custody, discipline, control over a child’s  earnings and property, and to be supported by a grown child.

parish.definition: (1) An administrative district of a Christian church. (2) In  Louisiana, a territorial division equivalent to a county.

parity.definition: (1) Equality, particularly of pay or status; equivalence of  goods. (2) A method of regulating prices for farm goods by  making them equivalent to prices set on a particular date. (3)  One country’s value in terms of another’s at a particular rate of  exchange. See also par.

parliament.definition: A legislature, such as the highest legislature of the  United Kingdom. ADJ. parliamentary.

parody.definition: A work such as a film, literary work, or song that  imitates another work in a humorous, ironic, or satirical way.

parole.definition: The release of a convict from prison before the end of his  or her sentence on the condition that the former prisoner follow  certain rules and commit no more crimes.

parolee.definition: A person released from prison on parole.

parol evidence.definition: Verbal or spoken evidence; spoken testimony by  a witness.

parol evidence rule.definition: A rule that prevents the parties to a  written agreement from altering it with spoken provisions or oral  agreements.

partial.definition: Not complete or entire; affecting only part of  something.

partial loss.definition: Loss of only part of something, as opposed to  total loss.

participate.definition: To take part in something; to do something with  other people; to share. N. participation.

partisan.definition: Associated with and espousing the views of a partic-  ular political party.

partition.definition: To divide into sections. N. The division of property  between co-owners so that each gets an individual interest in a  part of it.

partner.definition: (1) A person who shares the profits and risks of some  business or other enterprise with one or several other people.  (2) A spouse or domestic partner. V. To join with someone else  as partners.

partner, general.definition: A partner who participates in all management,  profits, and losses, and is personally liable for all partnership debts;  also called a full partner.

partner, limited.definition: A partner whose liability for partnership debts  is limited to his or her share of contributed capital, and who has  only a limited share of profits or losses.

partner, silent.definition: A partner who shares profits and losses but does  not participate in the management of the enterprise; also called a  dormant partner.

partnership.definition: An association between two or more people who  call themselves partners and share the profits and risks of a busi-  ness or some other undertaking.

partnership, general.definition: A partnership in which partners share  profits, losses, and management equally, regardless of the contribu-  tions to capital made by each of them.

partnership, limited.definition: A partnership with at least one general  partner and at least one limited partner.

party.definition: (1) A person or entity involved in some transaction or  matter; a person or entity on one side of a lawsuit or other dispute.  (2) An organized political group that tries to make its own views  the law of the land by participating in elections and working for  common goals in government; also called a political party. See also  third party.

passport.definition: A document issued by a national government that  identifies its bearer as a citizen of that country with permission to  travel abroad and return home under the home nation’s protection.

passport control.definition: A security checkpoint at an entrance point into  a country where officials inspect and approve the passports of  those wishing to enter.

patent.definition: (1) An exclusive right granted by the government to  manufacture and sell an invention for a specified period of time. (2)  A document granting land from a government to a person. V. To  obtain a patent for an invention. ADJ. Obvious; readily apparent.

patent pending.definition: A notice placed on an object informing others  that its inventor has applied for a patent on it and is awaiting the  government’s decision.

Patent and Trademark Office.definition: The federal agency that issues  patents and trademarks as part of the Department of Commerce.

patent troll.definition: An individual or business that acquires patents  without planning to manufacture the patented items but intending  to sue anyone who infringes on the patents.

paternal.definition: Having to do with fatherhood and fathers; showing  the care expected of a father.

paternity.definition: Fatherhood; the state of being a father. See also  maternity.

paternity suit.definition: A lawsuit brought to identify the father of a child  and demand child support payments from him.

patient-physician privilege.definition: A privilege exempting confidential  communications between patients and their doctors from  discovery.

patient’s bill of rights.definition: A statement of patients’ rights adopted by  many hospitals and health care providers, and made law by the  federal government and several states, that covers topics such as  confidentiality, consent to treatment, and dignity.

Patient Self-Determination Act.definition: A federal law requiring health  care facilities to inform patients of their rights under state law  to create advance directives to dictate how they should be  cared for if they become incapacitated; see also living will,  durable power of attorney.

patricide.definition: The killing of one’s own father; a person who kills his  or her father.

patrimony.definition: Property inherited from one’s father; property inher-  ited from either parent or other ancestors; heritage.

patron.definition: (1) A person who supports or protects someone or some-  thing, usually with financial contributions. (2) A regular customer  of a business. V. patronize.

patronage.definition: (1) The support given by a patron. (2) Regular busi-  ness given by customers to a store, theater, etc.

patronage, political.definition: The (generally improper) use of a political  office to provide friends and relatives with government jobs or  protection.

pauper.definition: A destitute person; a person so poverty-stricken that he  or she must be supported by the public. See also indigent.

pay.definition: To give money in exchange for goods or services, or to  discharge a debt. N. Compensation; wages; payment.

payable.definition: Due to be paid; required to be paid.

payment.definition: The amount paid for something; the act of paying for  something; satisfaction of a debt or obligation.

payment in due course.definition: Payment to the holder of a negotiable  instrument on or after its maturity date in good faith and with no  known defects in title.

payment into court.definition: A party’s depositing with the court money or  other property while a lawsuit is in progress, after which the court  will pay out the money and property according to the judgment or  other settlement.

payout.definition: A taxable payment, such as a dividend, drawn from a  company’s earnings and made by that company to its shareholders.

payroll.definition: A list of all the employees in a company and the amounts  they are paid; the total amount a company pays its employees.

payroll tax.definition: Taxes paid on wages, salary, or income, including  income tax, Social Security tax, and unemployment tax.

peaceable.definition: Peaceful; free from arguments, conflict, or  violence.

peaceable possession.definition: Continuous possession of property  without any adverse claims on it.

peculate.definition: For a public officer to steal public funds entrusted to  him or her; to embezzle. N. peculation.

pecuniary loss.definition: A loss of money or any anticipated economic  benefit, such as a child’s support by a parent.

penal code.definition: The codification of criminal law; criminal statutes.

penalize.definition: To impose a punishment on someone.

penalty clause.definition: A clause in a contract or agreement that  describes a penalty for a breach, default, or other infraction,  usually not enforceable by a court.

pendente lite.definition: (Latin) Pending the lawsuit; describes matters  that must be put on hold to await the outcome of a lawsuit that will  determine how they are settled.

pendent jurisdiction.definition: Discretionary jurisdiction by a federal  court over a state law claim that arises out of the same matter that  simultaneously creates a federal claim.

pending.definition: About to happen; begun but not yet completed;  awaiting settlement.

penitentiary.definition: A prison, usually for the incarceration of people  convicted of felonies or serious crimes.

pen register.definition: A device that records the numbers dialed on a tele-  phone; see also eavesdrop, wiretapping.

pension plan.definition: A plan established by an employer to provide  retirement benefits to employees.

per capita.definition: (Latin) By heads; according to the number of  people; counting individuals and dividing something among them  equally. ADV. per capita.

per curiam.definition: (Latin) By the court; by a court’s or judge’s  decision.

per diem.definition: (Latin) By the day. ADV. Daily. N. A daily allowance  of money.

peremptory norm.definition: A principle of international law that is so  important and so widely accepted that no state is permitted to  deviate from it, such as the prohibitions on genocide, slavery, or  piracy.

perfect.definition: Complete; without flaw or defect; having fulfilled the  requirements to make something enforceable at law. V. To make  perfect; to finish; to fulfill all requirements necessary for some  transaction.

perform.definition: To do some act in fulfillment of a promise; to take the  required steps to meet an obligation; to accomplish something. N.  performance.

performance bond.definition: A bond given as surety by a building  contractor to a customer to ensure that the contractor will  complete the project, and that the customer may use to pay for  completion if the contractor defaults.

peril.definition: Danger; risk; the risk that is covered by an insurance  policy.

periodic alimony.definition: Alimony paid in a specific amount at intervals,  such as monthly; also called permanent alimony if it lasts for the  recipient spouse’s lifetime.

periodic tenancy.definition: A tenancy that lasts for a specified period,  such as a year, and that cannot be terminated except at the end of  the rental period.

perjury.definition: The crime of intentionally lying under oath during a judi-  cial proceeding, such as at trial.

permanent.definition: Fixed; continuing or lasting without interruption;  intended to stay in the same place or the same condition  indefinitely.

permanent abode.definition: A fixed residence; a long-term home that the  resident intends to stay in indefinitely.

permanent disability.definition: A disability that impairs a person’s earning  capacity for the rest of his or her life.

permanent employment.definition: Employment that will continue until  terminated by either employee or employer.

permission.definition: Being allowed to do something; authorization.

permit.definition: To authorize or consent to something; to allow. N. An offi-  cial document authorizing someone to do something.

perpetrate.definition: To commit a crime or other wrongful act. N. perpe-  tration.

perpetrator.definition: A person who commits a crime.

perpetual.definition: Never ending; continuing without interruption  or end.

perpetuity.definition: (1) Something that lasts forever; the condition of  lasting forever. (2) A restriction on property that makes it  impossible to transfer forever, or for a period beyond that  allowed by law; an estate with such a restriction on it. See also  rule against perpetuities.

perquisites.definition: The fringe benefits and special rights and privileges  that go with a position; also called perks.

per se.definition: (Latin) In itself; intrinsically; by its very nature.

person.definition: A human being; an individual, corporation, partnership,  trustee, labor organization, government, or other entity with  defined legal responsibilities and rights.

personal.definition: Related to or belonging to an individual.

personal effects.definition: Movable property and chattels owned by an  individual, particularly all goods owned by a person at the time of  death; also called personal property or personalty.

personal holding company.definition: A company with few shareholders  and a large percentage of income through passive sources such as  rents or royalties.

personal injury.definition: Physical injury to a person’s body; also refers to  a branch of law specializing in torts arising out of physical injuries.

personal injury lawyer.definition: A lawyer who specializes in bringing  lawsuits on behalf of plaintiffs who have suffered physical injuries.

personal liability.definition: Liability for a matter extending to an indi-  vidual’s personal funds and property.

personal representative.definition: A person who handles the affairs of  someone who cannot do so him- or herself, such as a child, an inca-  pacitated person, or a deceased person; see also executor.

persona non grata.definition: (Latin) A person who is not pleasing; an  unwelcome, undesirable, or unwanted person.

per stirpes.definition: (Latin) By roots or by shoots, as of a tree; a form  of distribution of an estate in which each recipient takes a share  according to the proportion that would have fallen to his or her  deceased ancestor; the opposite of per capita. E.g., Grandmother  has three daughters, A, B, and C. A is still alive but B and C are  dead. A has one child, B has one child, and C has four children. If  Grandmother dies and leaves $300,000, under a per stirpes  arrangement, A will inherit $100,000 and her child will inherit  nothing; B’s child will take his dead mother’s share of $100,000;  and C’s four children will divide their mother’s share of $100,000,  each receiving $25,000.

persuade.definition: To use arguments and reasoning to induce someone to  adopt a particular opinion on a matter. N. persuasion.

petitioner.definition: (1) A person who presents a petition to some  authority. (2) A person who brings a legal action by means of a peti-  tion either in a court of equity or a court of appeals.

petit jury.definition: A trial jury that has the job of hearing testimony,  seeing evidence, and using the information to determine issues of  fact and reach a conclusion about them, such as a verdict in a crim-  inal case. See also grand jury.

petty.definition: Small; unimportant; trivial; an English corruption of the  French word “petit.”

phishing.definition: Using email and misleading websites to entice users to  provide their credit card numbers, bank account numbers, pass-  words, usernames, and other confidential information in order to  use that information for criminal purposes such as theft.

picket.definition: A group of people carrying signs and standing or  marching outside a place of business in a peaceful protest against  working conditions, labor practices, union grievances, or other  sources of disagreement. V. To participate in a picket.

picketing.definition: The act of participating in a picket.

pierce the corporate veil.definition: To impose personal liability for corpo-  rate activities on individuals associated with the corporation, doing  away with the protection against personal liability that ordinarily

pimp.definition: A person who finds clients for prostitutes in exchange for  a share of their earnings. V. To work as a pimp. See also pander.

piracy.definition: (1) The practice of attacking and robbing ships at sea; see  also hijack. (2) The unlawful copying and distribution of copy-  righted works such as software or recordings of music.

pirate.definition: A person who commits acts of piracy.

place of business.definition: A place where a business is run or where  someone works; see also domicile.

place of business, principal.definition: The main or most important loca-  tion of a business’s operations; the management headquarters or  center of operations.

plagiarize.definition: To copy someone else’s words or ideas and pass them  off as one’s own. N. plagiarism. See also copyright.

plain error.definition: An obvious error made by a trial court that substan-  tially affects a defendant’s rights and warrants a reversal of the  judgment and a new trial.

plain meaning.definition: An interpretation of a statute arrived at by reading  its words and interpreting them according to their general, common  meaning, usually without considering legislative history or other  evidence to determine the intent of the people who drafted it.

plaintiff.definition: A person who files a complaint to start a lawsuit.

plaintiff in error.definition: An appellant; one who brings an appeal; “appel-  lant” is the term more commonly used today.

plaintiff’s firm.definition: A law firm that specializes in bringing lawsuits  on behalf of plaintiffs, usually for a contingency fee. See also  defense firm.

plain view.definition: Those things that can be immediately seen without  special effort because they are sitting out in the open and are  unconcealed; incriminating evidence in plain view can be taken by  police without a search warrant.

plan.definition: A benefits plan or health plan; a package of employee  benefits.

plan year.definition: The one-year period in which a given benefits package  remains in place, after which members and plan components may  be modified.

plat.definition: A map showing the property lines, roads, and other land-  marks of an area; also called a plot.

plea.definition: (1) In criminal law, a defendant’s response to the charges  brought against him or her. (2) Formerly, at common law or equity,  one of several kinds of pleading that have now been made obsolete  under the Rules of Civil Procedure.

plea bargain.definition: An agreement made between a criminal defendant  and the prosecutor in which the defendant agrees to plead  guilty to a lesser offense in exchange for a lighter punishment  after conviction.

plead.definition: (1) In criminal law, to answer charges brought by the pros-  ecution. (2) To make or file a pleading in a lawsuit; to file a pleading  in response to a plaintiff’s complaint.

pleading.definition: A document containing a party’s side of a lawsuit, such  as a plaintiff’s complaint or a defendant’s answer, in which the  party lists the facts that support his or her side of the case and pres-  ents them to the court at the beginning of a lawsuit.

pleading, affirmative.definition: An answer filed by a defendant that states  the existence of facts that contradict the plaintiff’s allegations,  rather than simply denying those allegations.

pleading, amended.definition: A pleading that either corrects or expands  an earlier pleading.

pleading, code.definition: Pleading done according to the requirements of  a procedural code. See also Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

pledge.definition: (1) A promise. (2) An object given as security for a  promise, contract, debt, or other obligation.

pledgee.definition: A person who receives a pledge as security.

pledgor.definition: A person who gives a pledge to someone else.

plenary action.definition: A full trial on the merits; see also summary.

plenary session.definition: A meeting of a legislature or other group  attended by all members.

plurality.definition: (1) In voting for more than two candidates, the number of votes for the candidate who wins more than any other but does not receive a majority. (2) On an appellate court with a panel of judges, an opinion joined by the greatest number of judges without being a majority; i.e., on a panel of nine judges, if four judges agree on an opinion, two judges agree with the result but not the reasoning, and three dissent, the opinion with four judges is a plurality.

plutocracy.definition: Government by the wealthy; a group of wealthy  people who control the government.

plutocrat.definition: A wealthy person who uses his or her wealth to gain  political power.

pocket veto.definition: A president’s or governor’s method of rejecting a  bill submitted by the legislature toward the end of a legislative  session by simply refusing to accept or reject it and letting it die  through inaction, rather than rejecting it outright with a veto.

point.definition: (1) An argument; a distinct proposition or issue of law; the  relevance or reason of some argument. (2) In real estate, a fee of  1% of a loan amount paid to the lender when the loan is created. (3)  In securities, a measure of value, often worth one dollar.

point reserved.definition: A difficult legal issue set aside at trial for later  consideration so that the court can proceed with other issues.

police.definition: An organization maintained by a government to preserve  public order, prevent crimes, and apprehend criminals. V. To main-  tain order; to enforce laws.

police court.definition: A local or municipal court with jurisdiction over  minor criminal matters.

police power.definition: A government’s power to preserve the peace and  public order through the use of a police force and criminal laws  that restrain personal freedom and property rights.

policy.definition: (1) The principles that guide the actions of a government,  business, or other entity or individual. (2) A contract of insurance.  (3) A lottery.

policyholder.definition: A person who owns an insurance policy.

political.definition: Related to government and public affairs.

political question.definition: A question that a court will not decide  because it should be decided by another branch of government,  such as the legislature.

political subdivision.definition: Any geographical area that is governed  by a particular governmental entity, such as a county or school  district.

politician.definition: A person engaged in politics, particularly one elected  or running for public office.

politics.definition: The activities of a government and those associated  with it.

poll.definition: (1) To record votes or opinions from people. (2) To ask  the members of a jury individually what their own decision is  before the jury’s verdict is recorded. N. A record of votes cast in  an election; a place where votes are cast (also called polls); a  list of voters.

poll tax.definition: A tax paid by a person in exchange for the right to vote  in elections; a tax on individuals of a particular class, such as all  males over 21 years of age.

polygamy.definition: Simultaneous marriage to more than one person.

polygraph.definition: A lie detector; a device that monitors heart rate,  breathing, and other physical aspects of a person being questioned

ponzi scheme.definition: A kind of pyramid scheme in which a perpetrator  promises high returns on an investment and uses money submitted  by later investors to pay off earlier investors, but eventually runs  out of money or disappears and the scheme collapses. See also  Securities and Exchange Commission.

pool.definition: (1) A group of people or organizations, especially those  involved in the same business, who combine resources to benefit  one another and to reduce competition. (2) A common fund  contributed to and shared by a number of people, either in legiti-  mate business or in gambling. V. To combine resources or funds.

pop-under ad.definition: An advertisement that opens a new window  under the active window when the user visits a particular website.  See also banner ad.

pop-up ad.definition: An advertisement that opens in a new window on top  of the active window when a user visits a particular website.

pornography.definition: Literature, photography, or other printed or visual  material that depicts sex organs or sexual activity, intended to  cause erotic stimulation rather than education. ADJ. porno-  graphic. See also obscene, prurient interest.

port.definition: A harbor; a place where ships can load and unload, and  where customs and duties are collected; a city or town with such  a harbor.

portfolio.definition: The collective investments held by a person or  institution.

port of departure.definition: The port or airport from which a ship or  aircraft begins its journey.

port of entry.definition: A place where a ship, aircraft, or other vessel, and  its passengers or cargo, enter a country, where customs and duties  are paid and immigration procedures observed.

positive law.definition: Law created by a government and human institu-  tion to run society; see also natural law.

positivism.definition: The belief that laws exist to run society and are valid  because they are enacted by humans, and ideals or worries about  justice should not limit their application.

possess.definition: To own; to hold without owning; to control something;  to occupy physically.

possessory action.definition: A lawsuit in which the plaintiff’s objective is  to recover possession of real property, as opposed to recovering  title to it.

possessory interest.definition: The right to possess property, i.e., to  occupy it or control it and to exclude others from it.

possibility.definition: Something that could happen but will not necessarily  happen; a contingent interest in property.

possibility of a reverter.definition: A chance that an estate will return to  the person who granted it if certain conditions occur.

post.definition: (1) Mail; letters and packages and the system that delivers them. (2) A job; an assigned duty or position, such as that held by a soldier. (3) A military base. V. (1) To mail a letter or package. (2) To hang a sign or notice in some public place, such as a sign warning away trespassers from property. (3) To enter an amount in a bookkeeping ledger; to accept a negotiable instrument, pay it, and record the payment. (4) To furnish bail. ADV. (Latin) After.

post-conviction.definition: After conviction of a criminal offense.

post-conviction relief procedures.definition: Court actions that allow a  criminal defendant to challenge a conviction on constitutional  grounds; ABBRV. PCR actions.

post-date.definition: To enter a date on an instrument that is later than the  date on which it is written.

post hoc ergo propter hoc.definition: (Latin) After this, therefore  because of this; the mistaken reasoning that if something  happens after an event, it was necessarily caused by that event.

posthumous child.definition: A child born after the death of his or her  father.

post-mortem.definition: After death. N. An examination of a dead body to  determine cause of death.

post-nuptial agreement.definition: An agreement made between spouses  settling their property rights if they should divorce or if one should  die. See also prenuptial agreement.

pound.definition: (1) A place for the detention of illegally parked automo-  biles or stray animals. (2) A unit of weight used in the United States  and the United Kingdom. (3) The basic unit of currency in the  United Kingdom and several other nations.

pourover.definition: A provision in a will directing that estate property be  placed in a previously established trust, or that property be moved  from a trust to a will.

power.definition: Authority; the ability or liberty to do something.

power, constitutional.definition: The authority to act in particular matters  granted to branches of the government by the Constitution; see  also implied powers, enumerated powers, Commerce Clause,  necessary and proper clause.

power, corporate.definition: A corporation’s power to act in particular  matters that are necessary to its operation.

power of acceptance.definition: The power held by a person who receives  an offer to create a contract by accepting it.

power of appointment.definition: Authority given by a donor or testator  allowing a donee to choose the beneficiaries of a fund, trust, or  estate; title to the transferred property passes directly from the  donor or testator to the beneficiaries chosen by the donee.

power of appointment, special.definition: A power of appointment that  limits the possible beneficiaries to members of a specified group.

power of attorney.definition: The authority to act for someone else in legal  matters; a document conferring that authority to someone.

practicable.definition: Possible under given circumstances; feasible in  actual practice.

practice.definition: (1) Customary action or procedure; the established  procedures of law and court proceedings. (2) The exercise of a  profession or religion. V. To carry out the daily activities of a profes-  sion such as law or medicine, or of a religion; to work as a profes-  sional in a particular field.

preamble.definition: An introductory statement; an introductory section of  a statute, constitution, etc., that explains the reason for its creation  and the objects it is meant to achieve.

precarious.definition: Uncertain; likely to break, fall, or fail; not securely  held.

precarious loan.definition: A loan that must be repaid whenever the lender  demands it; a loan that is likely not to be repaid.

precatory.definition: Expressing a request, wish, or recommendation, as  when a testator expresses a wish in his or her will.
