sale.definition: The act of exchanging a commodity for money; a contract  between a buyer and a seller in which the seller transfers property  to the buyer in exchange for money or the promise of money.  V. sell.

sale, bulk.definition: The sale of all of a merchant’s stock, materials,  merchandise, and equipment.

sale, cash.definition: A sale in which the buyer pays the full purchase price  at the time of sale.

sale, forced.definition: A sale made without the owner’s consent, ordered  and administered by the court as part of a proceeding, such as fore-  closure.

sale, judicial.definition: A court-ordered sale of property administered by  an officer of the court.

sale, tax.definition: A court-ordered sale of a person’s property to collect  money for the owner’s unpaid taxes. See also auction, installment,  retail, sheriff’s sale.

sale and leaseback.definition: The sale of an item to a buyer who imme-  diately leases it back to the seller, done to free up the seller’s capital  or to gain a tax deduction.

sales tax.definition: A tax paid on purchased goods, usually a percentage  of the cost of an item, collected by the seller on behalf of the  government.

salvage.definition: The goods retrieved after the destruction or damage of  a building or other property, or the value of those goods; the cargo  retrieved from a wrecked ship or other vessel; the goods remaining  after some destruction that are claimed by the insurer after it pays  the owner for the loss; the act of rescuing goods from destruction.  V. salvage.

same.definition: Identical; not different; equal or equivalent; unchanged;  of the same type.

sample.definition: An example or model; a specimen of some commodity  offered as an example of the quality or design of the commodity as  a whole.

sample, sale by.definition: A sale in which the buyer examines only a  sample of the item purchased, which creates an express warranty  that all goods of that type are of the same quality and design as the  sample.

sanction.definition: (1) Official approval of some action. (2) A penalty or  threatened penalty for disobeying a law or rule; penalties taken by  one nation against another, such as trade restrictions, intended to  force it to comply with some standard. V. sanction.

sane.definition: Of sound mind; in possession of one’s mental faculties;  not insane. N. sanity.

sanity hearing.definition: A hearing in which the court determines whether  a criminal defendant is mentally competent to stand trial.

Sarbanes-Oxley Act.definition: A federal law passed in 2002 that increased  auditing and reporting requirements for corporations. ABBRV.  Sarbox, SOX.

satisfy.definition: To fulfill an obligation or pay off a debt. N. satisfaction.  ADJ. satisfactory. See also accord and satisfaction.

save.definition: (1) To exempt; to reserve. (2) To rescue someone or  something from danger; to keep safe. (3) To keep; to accumulate  something such as money; to avoid expending or wasting some-  thing.

save harmless.definition: To indemnify; to repay another party if a specific  loss occurs. See also hold harmless.

saving clause.definition: A clause inserted in a statute to make an excep-  tion to the statute’s provisions, such as a grandfather clause; a  clause that prevents the statute from affecting those who currently  enjoy rights that the statute takes away.

savings.definition: Money that is accumulated and held for the future.

savings account.definition: A bank account in which money is held and  accumulated, as opposed to being spent regularly as in a  checking account.

savings and loan association.definition: A mutually owned cooperative  institution that exists primarily to make loans to members, partic-  ularly for the purchase of real estate, and to provide members with  savings accounts, but that may offer other banking services.

scandal.definition: (1) An event that is considered morally wrong and  causes public outrage; the outrage provoked by a shocking  event. (2) Malicious gossip about a shocking event that causes

schedule.definition: (1) A plan for conducting events; a list of planned  events that occur regularly. (2) An appendix attached to a docu-  ment, such as a statute or tax return, describing in detail matters  mentioned briefly in the document itself. V. To arrange for some-  thing to happen at a particular time.

school district.definition: A government entity that oversees the operation  of public schools in a defined area.

scienter.definition: (Latin) Knowingly; used to describe situations  where a defendant had guilty knowledge that a crime was being  committed, such as knowledge that a misrepresentation he or she  made was not true; often used as a noun to mean guilty knowledge  or a mental state intending to deceive.

scintilla.definition: (Latin) A spark; the tiniest particle; the merest trace  of something.

scintilla of evidence.definition: A very insignificant or small particle of  evidence tending to support an issue, which is nevertheless enough  to allow the matter to go to the jury.

scope.definition: Range; boundaries; the area encompassed by some  concept, activity, etc.

scope of authority.definition: Authority that has been delegated to an agent  to allow him or her to carry out the assigned task, including both  powers actually authorized by the principal and those that are  implied in the task.

scope of employment.definition: The activities done by an employee as  part of his or her employment; duties that are part of  someone’s job.

scrivener.definition: A scribe, clerk, or notary; a person who prepares  written instruments such as contracts and deeds for other people  for a fee.

scrutiny.definition: Close observation; critical and thorough examination.  V. scrutinize.

seal.definition: An impression in wax or another substance, or an  impression on paper, placed on a document to verify that it has  been legally executed. V. To place a seal on a document; to close  a document and place a seal on it that must be broken for it to  be read.

sealed and delivered.definition: Words used to describe a conveyance  that has been properly executed, signed, witnessed, sealed, and  delivered to the proper recipient.

sealed bid.definition: A bid for a job submitted by a contractor in a sealed  envelope; all sealed bids are opened at the same time and the job is  awarded to the lowest bidder.

sealed instrument.definition: An instrument signed and sealed by its  maker.

sealed verdict.definition: A verdict placed in a sealed envelope by a jury if  the court is not in session when the jury reaches a decision, which  it presents to the court when it reconvenes.

sealing of records.definition: The sealing of a person’s criminal records so  that they can only be examined by court order, often done with  juvenile criminal records to prevent their impeding the person in  his or her efforts to become a productive adult.

search.definition: To look for something; to explore hidden places in a  person’s house, vehicle, or other place in a quest for evidence of a  crime. N. search.

search engine.definition: A software application that allows users to  search for the appearance of particular words or images on  websites.

search warrant.definition: A written order issued by a judge or judicial offi-  cial in the state’s name, authorizing a sheriff or other officer of the  law to search a particular place for evidence of a crime and seize it  if found.

seasonable.definition: At the appropriate time; within a reasonable time;  at the time agreed upon.

seasonal.definition: Occurring during a particular season of the year.

seasonal employment.definition: Employment that exists only at partic-  ular times of the year, such as harvest work.

secede.definition: To withdraw formally from a union, an alliance, or a  political or religious group. N. secession.

secondary.definition: Coming after something; less important than some-  thing else; subsequent.

secondary distribution.definition: A public sale of stock that has already  been issued and sold once, as opposed to a new offering.

secondary party.definition: A person who is responsible for paying a debt  if the original debtor fails to; a person who has secondary liability  for a debt.

second mortgage.definition: A mortgage placed on property that  already has a mortgage on it, which follows in priority behind  the first mortgage.

secret.definition: Not known by others; hidden from view or concealed.  N. secret.

secretary.definition: (1) A clerk; an employee who handles correspon-  dence, keeps records, types documents, and performs other admin-  istrative tasks; see also legal secretary. (2) An officer of an  organization who handles correspondence, records, etc. (3) An  official who heads a governmental department.

secretary general.definition: The head of the United Nations.

secretary of state.definition: (1) The head of the State Department of the  United States, top-ranking officer in the Cabinet, responsible for  advising the president on matters of foreign policy. (2) An officer  in a state who supervises state business affairs and licenses  corporations.

Secret Service.definition: A government agency in the Treasury  Department whose duties include protecting the president and  investigating counterfeiting and credit fraud.

secure.definition: To make something safe; to guarantee; to guarantee  repayment of a loan by obtaining collateral. ADJ. Safe; backed by  collateral.

secured debt.definition: A debt that is guaranteed by certain items that the  creditor may take if the debtor fails to pay back the loan.

secured transaction.definition: A transaction that involves security, i.e., a  transaction secured with collateral that can be taken by the cred-  itor if the debtor defaults.

securities acts.definition: Federal and state statutes that govern registra-  tion of and trade in corporate securities, including the Securities  Act of 1933 and the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, administered  by the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Securities Investor Protection Corporati.definition: An organization estab-  lished by federal statute to help brokers and dealers in corporate  securities who find themselves in financial difficulties.

security.definition: (1) Safety; protection; the state of being protected from  danger, loss, or threat. (2) Collateral; an item promised as a forfeit  in case of default. (3) A stock, bond, debenture, or other interest  that represents a share in ownership of a company or evidence of  the company’s indebtedness; an instrument that gives its owner the  right to money or property; PL. securities. See also stock, bond,  Securities and Exchange Commission.

Security Council.definition: A group of fifteen United Nations member  nations that work to prevent war and maintain peace throughout  the world.

security deposit.definition: Money or property given to a creditor by a  debtor as a pledge of payment, usually returned to its owner if  payment is completed satisfactorily; e.g., money given to a landlord  by a tenant as a guarantee that the tenant will pay rent and observe  other terms of the lease, to be forfeited if the tenant damages the  property or otherwise breaks the bargain.

security interest.definition: An interest in real or personal property that  exists because the property has been offered as security for an obli-  gation or debt.

sedition.definition: Speech or actions intended to persuade the people to  rise up against a government and, if possible, overthrow it; advo-  cating treason. ADJ. seditious.

seduce.definition: (1) To tempt or entice someone into sexual activity. (2)  To tempt someone into doing something he or she knows is wrong  or ill-advised. N. seduction.

segregate.definition: To separate; to keep certain things or people away  from one another; to separate people based on race, religion, sex,  etc. N. segregation. See also desegregate.

seisin.definition: Ownership of property; possession of a freehold estate  in land.

seize.definition: To take someone’s property by force; for a police officer  authorized by a search warrant to take the real or personal prop-  erty of someone who has broken the law or who has been ordered  to forfeit that property by the court; to take a person into physical  custody. N. seizure. See also search and seizure.

Selective Service System.definition: An agency of the executive branch of  the U.S. government that administers the draft, requiring all males  between the ages of 18 and 26 to register and inducting them into  the armed services when necessary.

self-dealing.definition: A fiduciary’s use of property or funds entrusted to  him or her for personal benefit, such as a trustee’s using trust prop-  erty for himself or herself.

self-defense.definition: The act of defending oneself against threatened  injury; the right to protect oneself or one’s family from immediately  threatened harm, which can serve as a defense in a criminal or tort  action arising out of injuries caused by an act of self-defense.

self-employed.definition: Working for oneself, performing jobs for and  receiving pay directly from clients instead of working for an  employer for wages or a salary.

self-executing.definition: Coming into effect automatically when  specific criteria are met.

self-help.definition: Handling some matter that could involve legal authori-  ties without using the normal legal process, such as taking posses-  sion of collateral when a debtor defaults or evicting a tenant for  nonpayment of rent without involving the police.

self-incrimination.definition: The act of testifying against oneself or impli-  cating oneself in a crime, which the Fifth Amendment forbids the  government to require of anyone.

sell.definition: To give someone property in exchange for money; to be in  the business of selling something. N. sale.

sell short.definition: To sell shares of stock or other commodities  borrowed from someone else, intending to buy them back at a  lower price later so as to make a profit and still be able to return  the shares or their value to their original owner. N. short sale.  See also hedge.

semiautomatic weapon.definition: A gun that fires only one bullet with  each trigger pull and automatically loads a new bullet into the  chamber with each shot, eliminating the need to cock or reload the  gun after firing.

Senate.definition: The upper chamber of the U.S. Congress; the upper  chamber of a state legislature; a governing body of a nation, univer-  sity, or other organization.

seniority.definition: The condition of having been in a job longer than  someone else, which can result in preference for promotions and  layoffs.

sentence.definition: The punishment given by the court to a criminal  defendant who has been found guilty of a crime. V. sentence.

sentence, concurrent.definition: A sentence served simultaneously with  another sentence.

sentence, deferred.definition: A sentence that is postponed to some future  time and possibly never imposed if the defendant does not violate  the terms of probation.

sentence, definite.definition: A sentence set by law for a particular crime,  not modifiable by the judge; also called determinate sentence or  fixed sentence.

sentence, indefinite.definition: A sentence that has a prescribed range of  punishments set by law, for which the court and other officials  have some discretion in sentencing and that may be ended early at  the discretion of the parole board; also called indeterminate  sentence.

sentence, interlocutory.definition: A temporary sentence imposed on a  defendant between conviction and final sentencing.

sentence, life.definition: A sentence to prison for the rest of one’s natural  life.

sentence, mandatory.definition: A sentence set by law for a particular  offense, with no room for judicial discretion.

sentence, split.definition: A sentence divided into a period of incarcera-  tion and a period of probation.

sentence, suspended.definition: A sentence that is not imposed on the  defendant on the condition that he or she does not violate terms  specified at sentencing.

sentences, cumulative.definition: Multiple sentences that are served one  after another; also called consecutive sentences.

separable.definition: Able to be divided or separated. N. separability.

separable controversy.definition: A cause of action brought as part of a  larger suit that could be separated and could stand alone as its own  lawsuit, which can serve as a basis for removing the entire lawsuit  from state to federal court.

separate.definition: Not connected with something; distinct; forming an  entity or unit by itself. V. (1) To divide; to move apart; to place a  boundary between things. (2) For a judge to order witnesses at a  trial to stay out of the courtroom except for when they are testi-  fying. (3) For a husband and wife to move into different homes  intending to no longer live together as a couple, often as a  precursor to divorce; if the separation is ordered by a court, it is  called a legal separation. N. separation.

separate but equal.definition: Describing a doctrine, now generally  believed to be unconstitutional, that held that the races could be  treated equally although segregated into different facilities.

separation agreement.definition: A written agreement between a husband  and wife who have separated and intend to divorce, arranging for  division of property, custody of children, alimony, child support,  and any other necessary matters.

separation of powers.definition: The division of the U.S. government and  many state governments into three branches—executive, legisla-  tive, and judicial—each of which wields a particular set of powers  unique to it and not shared by the other branches, and that the  other branches are not permitted to use.

SEP-IRA.definition: Simplified Employee Pension Individual  Retirement Account.

sequester.definition: (1) To isolate; to separate or segregate; to hide  away; to isolate a jury during a trial. (2) To seize property  pending the outcome of litigation or to hold until a debt is paid;  to impose spending restrictions on a government; to declare  someone bankrupt. Also called sequestrate. N. sequestration.

servant.definition: A person who works for someone else and is controlled  by him or her; an employee; a person employed by a government,  also called a civil servant. See also civil service, independent  contractor.

serve.definition: (1) To work for someone, especially as an employee; to  hold a position in the armed forces. (2) To deliver a legal document  such as a summons formally and in an official capacity.

service.definition: The act of working for someone; the work that is  performed by someone.

service, constructive.definition: Any form of service other than personal  service.

service, personal.definition: Actual delivery of the document to the person  named on it.

service, substituted.definition: Service in which the document is  presented to someone authorized to receive it on the named  party’s behalf.

service by publication.definition: Publication of a summons or other docu-  ment as an advertisement in a newspaper named by the court, used  in cases where the defendant cannot be found.

servicemember.definition: A member of the military; a soldier in the armed  forces.

service of process.definition: The formal delivery to a defendant of the  complaint, summons, or other legal document to notify him or her  that a lawsuit has been brought.

servient estate.definition: Land subject to an easement; land that the  owner of a dominant estate is allowed to use for some specific  purpose.

servitude.definition: (1) The condition of being enslaved; the condition  of a person who is bound to work for another person as a  servant, either voluntarily or involuntarily; see also indenture.  (2) An obligation or encumbrance on land that takes the form of  a burden or restriction on the landowner, as opposed to an ease-  ment, which is a right or privilege enjoyed by the owner of the  estate that claims it.

servitude, equitable.definition: A servitude that is enforceable in equity.

session.definition: A meeting of a court, legislature, or other body; the  period when a court sits to hear cases.

session laws.definition: A bound volume of the laws passed during a  legislative session, compiled in the order of their enactment.

set aside.definition: To vacate, annul, or reverse a judgment, order, or  proceedings.

setback.definition: A building’s distance from a curb, building, property  line, or other boundary.

set-off.definition: A counterclaim brought by a defendant against a plaintiff  that arises out of a different matter from that raised in the plaintiff’s  complaint, and that the defendant hopes to use to recover some  amount from the plaintiff, thereby reducing or canceling the  damages due to the plaintiff.

settle.definition: (1) To resolve a matter; to conclude an estate; to finalize  accounts; for the parties to a lawsuit to resolve their dispute on  their own before a court reaches a final judgment on the matter  after trial, thereby allowing the trial to be canceled and the lawsuit  terminated. (2) To dispose of finally, such as after death; to give  property to someone. N. settlement.

settlor.definition: A person who creates a trust; a person who gives prop-  erty to someone; see also donor.

sever.definition: To cut; to divide or separate; to end a relationship; to  separate causes of action or defendants that have been joined  together to try them instead in individual lawsuits. N. sever-  ance.

severable.definition: Able to have portions removed without destruction  of the whole; able to be divided. N. severability.

severable contract.definition: A contract that contains two or more inde-  pendent parts, one or more of which can potentially be breached  without invalidating the entire contract.

severable statute.definition: A statute that remains valid after portions of  it are declared invalid and removed.

several.definition: (1) Three or more but not many. (2) Separate;  regarded separately or individually. ADV. severally; see also joint.

several liability.definition: Liability for something held by every individual  involved with some matter, which allows for a separate lawsuit to  be brought against each party.

severalty.definition: The ownership of property by one person, who does  not share title with anyone else.

severance pay.definition: A sum of money paid to a terminated employee  as compensation for firing him or her.

severe.definition: Extreme; very bad; strict or harsh.

sex.definition: (1) Sexual activity; sexual intercourse. (2) A physical cate-  gory, either male or female, determined by reproductive organs;  members of a group that have the same reproductive organs; the  state of being a male or female; see also gender. ADJ. sexual.

sex crime.definition: A crime involving sex, either with a sexual motive or  involving a sexual act, such as rape, sexual assault, or indecent  exposure.

sex offender.definition: A person who has been convicted of a sex crime.

sexual abuse.definition: Illegal sexual contact between a minor and an  adult.

sexual assault.definition: Forcible sexual contact inflicted on an unwilling  victim; see also rape.

shall.definition: A modal verb used to create a future tense; as used in  statutes and legal documents, generally has an imperative sense,  meaning “must,” though it can also be used in a permissive sense,  meaning “may.” In traditional English usage, “shall” was the future

sham.definition: Something that is false; a fake; a pretense. ADJ. False; not  what it is purported to be.

sham pleading.definition: A pleading that appears genuine but is not based  on any real issue of material fact, and is so clearly false that it does  not raise a bona fide issue.

sham transaction.definition: A transaction that appears to be genuine  but in fact is intended to deceive its creditors or the govern-  ment.

share.definition: To partake of a portion of something along with others, or  to give a portion of something to another. N. A portion; a part of a  larger thing; a unit of stock.

share and share alike.definition: To divide something into equal portions  for each person or entity that is entitled to a share.

shareholder.definition: A person who owns shares of stock; synonymous  with stockholder.

shareware.definition: Software that a user may acquire and try free of  charge, usually distributed with a request for voluntary payment.  See also open source.

Sharia.definition: Islamic law, based on interpretation of the Quran and  other sources of Muslim doctrine.

sheriff.definition: An officer elected by a county to keep the peace, enforce  laws, serve process, execute judgments, and perform other such

sheriff’s sale.definition: A sale of property by a sheriff or court officer  ordered by a court to satisfy a judgment, such as a foreclosure,  often conducted as an auction.

Sherman Antitrust Act.definition: A federal statute passed in 1890 to  prevent monopolies and restrictions on free and open interstate  and foreign commerce; see also antitrust, Clayton Act.

shield law.definition: A law that prevents a court from requiring witnesses  to reveal certain kinds of information, such as a law that prevents  opposing counsel from questioning a victim of a sex crime about  his or her past sexual behavior, or a law that allows journalists to  refuse to disclose confidential information in a legal proceeding;  see also privilege.

shift.definition: To move something; to change position or emphasis; to  transfer a burden of proof from one party to another.

shop.definition: (1) A place of business where goods are sold. (2) A place  where goods are manufactured; a factory, plant, or workshop.

shop steward.definition: A union official who is elected to represent his or  her fellow workers in negotiations with management.

short.definition: (1) Covering a small distance or lasting a brief time;  below the standard or required length. (2) Having sold borrowed  shares or property in the expectation of being able to repurchase  them at a lower price; see also sell short.

short-term.definition: For a short time; current; due within a short period  of time, usually within a year.

show.definition: To illustrate; to prove; to exhibit or display; to produce  evidence of something.

show cause order.definition: An order by the court made upon the motion  of one party requiring the opposing party to appear before it and  explain why the court should not take a particular action.

show-up.definition: A face-to-face confrontation arranged by law enforce-  ment officers between a suspect and a witness to or victim of a  crime, usually done just after the crime has been committed and  intended to allow the witness or victim to identify the person who  has committed the crime.

shrink-wrap agreement.definition: A contract or licensing agreement that  is packaged inside a product and that cannot be read until the  product is purchased and opened. See also browse-wrap agree-  ment, click-wrap agreement.

sick.definition: Physically or mentally ill; can also include incapacity due  to physical injury.

sick leave.definition: A leave that an employee is allowed to take from  work when either the employee or a close family member is sick or  injured, either with or without pay but without loss of seniority or  benefits.

sick pay.definition: Compensation paid to an employee while on sick leave  either temporarily or permanently, often as part of disability  compensation.

side-bar.definition: A place at the side of a judge’s bench where the judge  and attorneys can discuss matters privately, in the presence of the  court reporter who records their conversation, but without the  jury, parties, witnesses, or spectators hearing what they say.

sight draft.definition: A negotiable instrument that is payable on sight, i.e.,  when presented; a draft that is payable on demand.

signatory.definition: A party who signs his or her name to an agreement; a  nation that signs on to an international treaty or other agreement.

signature.definition: The act of signing one’s name; one’s name written by  one’s own hand, or affixed in some other way such as printing,  engraving, stamping, allowing someone else to write one’s name, a  mark used instead of a name, etc., as long as the person whose  name appears on the document intends it to be placed there to  make the document effective.

signing statement.definition: An official statement issued by a president  when he or she signs a bill into law, explaining his or her reasons  for signing and opinions about the new law.

silent.definition: Not speaking; knowing something but not saying  anything or acting accordingly. N. silence.

silent partner.definition: An investor in a business who contributes capital  and shares profits and losses but does no active management.

similar.definition: Almost the same; resembling; alike in some ways.

similar sales.definition: Sales of properties similar to the one in question,  used to determine fair market value.

simple.definition: Pure; uncomplicated; used to describe a crime that is  not aggravated or compounded. See also aggravate.

Simplified Employee Pension Individual R.definition: An individual retirement account in which an individual may  contribute a set percentage of pre-tax income. ABBRV. SEP-IRA.

simulate.definition: To imitate; to assume an appearance; to fabricate or  counterfeit. N. simulation.

simulated judgment.definition: A judgment on a case of bad debt that  appears valid but actually is meant to give unfair advantage to one  of the parties or to perpetrate a fraud on third parties.

simultaneous death act.definition: A law stating that in cases where distri-  bution of property depends on the time that a person dies, and  where two people die simultaneously or close enough together that  it is impossible to determine who died first (such as when husband  and wife perish together in an airplane crash), the law will presume  that each died before the other and their property should be distrib-  uted accordingly.

sine qua non.definition: (Latin) Without which not; something without  which something else cannot happen; a requirement or neces-  sity.

sinking fund.definition: A fund used to accumulate money to replace  equipment as it wears out, to repay debts as bonds mature, or for  other long-term financial obligations.

sit.definition: To hold a session of a court, a legislature, or other body.

situs.definition: (Latin) Site, position; the location of a thing; the location  of property for tax purposes; the place where a crime or other  action takes place.

slander.definition: Making a false and defamatory oral statement about  someone that can injure his or her reputation; oral defamation. V.  slander. See also libel, defamation.

slander of title.definition: Criticizing a product in order to lower public  opinion of it or to persuade a customer to purchase a different  product.

slave.definition: A person owned by and forced to work for another  person. See also enslave.

slip and fall.definition: An accident in which a victim slips on something,  such as liquid on the floor or a banana peel, and is injured, which  serves as the basis for a lawsuit against the owner of the premises;  a similar kind of accident is a trip and fall. See also personal  injury, plaintiff’s attorney.

small business.definition: A business with a small number of employees,  typically fewer than fifty or one hundred, or with low sales or  profits.

small claims court.definition: A court that handles matters involving small  amounts of money in an informal, inexpensive, and usually fairly  quick manner.

sober.definition: Not under the influence of drugs or alcohol. N.  sobriety.

Social Security.definition: A national program of social insurance in which  employers and employees all contribute funds that are pooled and  paid out to retired and disabled workers and to the surviving  spouses of dead workers, administered by the Social Security  Administration.

sodomy.definition: Oral or anal sexual intercourse, or sexual intercourse  with an animal; definition varies by state.

software.definition: The programs a computer uses to perform particular  tasks; applications. See also hardware.

solemn.definition: Formal; observing the proper form or ceremony.

solemnize.definition: To make formal through an official ceremony; to  perform a marriage in public before witnesses. N. solemnization.

sole proprietor.definition: A person who owns a business alone, without  co-owners or partners.

solicit.definition: (1) To try to get something from someone; to ask for  something; to request or entreat; to encourage someone to commit  a crime. (2) To offer one’s services as a prostitute. N. solicitation.

solicitor.definition: (1) The head legal officer of a city, town, department,  or other body. (2) In Britain, a lawyer who drafts wills, prepares  conveyances, and handles all other legal matters aside from  arguing cases in court, though in modern times solicitors do occa-  sionally appear in court; see also barrister.

solicitor general.definition: The attorney who ranks just below the  attorney general of the United States and whose responsibilities  include representing the U.S. government before the Supreme  Court and other federal courts.

solitary confinement.definition: Imprisoning someone alone in an isolated  cell, without contact with fellow prisoners or without any human  contact at all, even from prison employees.

solvent.definition: Able to pay debts; having enough money to cover  one’s expenses. N. solvency. See also insolvent.

sound.definition: In good condition; without defects; healthy. N.  soundness.

sound and disposing mind and memory.definition: Language often used in  wills to mean the mental capacity necessary to create a will.

sound in.definition: To aim at; to be connected with.

sound in contract.definition: For a lawsuit to appear to be an action related  to a contract, although it was brought as an action in tort.

sound in damages.definition: For an action to be brought for damages  only, not for recovery of property.

sound mind.definition: Sane and mentally capable, with unimpaired  faculties.

source.definition: Origin; the place or person from which something  comes; the person or thing that starts something.

sovereign.definition: A ruler; a monarch or king; the supreme authority of  a country. ADJ. sovereign.

sovereign immunity.definition: A doctrine that prohibits lawsuits being  brought against the government without its consent.

sovereign state.definition: A state or nation that governs itself and is not  subject to the authority of any other state or nation.

sovereignty.definition: The power by which a government rules its state or  nation.

spam.definition: Unsolicited emails sent to large numbers of email  addresses in the hope of enticing some readers to respond.

speaker.definition: (1) The head of a legislative body. (2) A person who is  speaking.

Speaker of the House.definition: The chief officer of the House of  Representatives.

special.definition: Unusual; better than or different from something  else; meant for a particular purpose; belonging to a particular  person or thing.

special counsel.definition: An attorney who works for the attorney  general on a particular matter.

special interest group.definition: A group of people with a common  interest who attempt to influence legislators to pass laws favorable  to them.

special jurisdiction.definition: A court’s ability to hear only a limited cate-  gory of cases.

special law.definition: A law that affects only particular people and not the  general public; a private law.

special use.definition: A use of property that is not allowed under zoning  laws but is permitted as an exception in a particular case.

specie.definition: Money in the form of metal coins.

specie, in.definition: (1) In coins. (2) A contract performed exactly  according to stipulated terms.

specific.definition: Clearly stated or identified; precisely defined;  explicit. V. specify. N. specification.

specific performance.definition: An equitable remedy in a case of breach  of contract, in which the breaching party is required to perform the  obligation under the contract. See also bequest, intent.

speculate.definition: (1) To guess; to theorize or come to a conclusion  about something without adequate evidence. (2) To engage in risky  investments; to buy or sell stocks in the hopes that prices will rise  or fall dramatically. N. speculation.

speedy trial.definition: A criminal trial conducted according to proper  regulations and without unnecessary or arbitrary delays, a right  guaranteed by the Sixth Amendment.

spendthrift.definition: A person who spends a great deal of money quickly  and without prudence; a person who wastes his or her estate  through excessive spending, gambling, laziness, or other irrespon-  sible behavior.

spendthrift trust.definition: A trust set up to safeguard the funds of  someone who would be expected to waste them by restricting the  beneficiary’s access to money and placing it out of the reach of  creditors.

spirit of the law.definition: The actual intention of a law; the meaning it is  meant to convey, arrived at through interpretation and possibly  consideration of the intentions of those who wrote it; see also letter  of the law.

split.definition: (1) To break into separate parts; to divide between two or  more parties or entities. (2) To divide a single cause of action into  several parts, each of which becomes the basis for its own action.  ADJ. split.

spoliation.definition: The act of destroying something; destruction of  evidence that might prove unfavorable; destruction or modification  of a document or instrument. V. spoil.

spontaneous.definition: Done on a sudden impulse without prior  planning.

spontaneous exclamation.definition: A statement made immediately  after some exciting occurrence by someone who saw it or  participated in it, before he or she has the chance to think about  it; see also res gestae.

spouse.definition: A husband or wife; a person who is married to another.  ADJ. spousal.

spouse, surviving.definition: A spouse who remains alive after his or her  partner has died; see also widow.

spyware.definition: Software that is secretly installed on a computer  without the owner’s knowledge or consent in order to collect  personal information, to use the computer to send spam, to hijack  the Web browser, or to conduct other illegal activities.

squatter.definition: A person who sets up residence on property that does  not belong to him or her. V. squat.

squeeze-out.definition: Eliminating minority shareholders from a  corporation by buying out minority shares or issuing many  new shares that dilute the minority interest; also called a  freeze-out.

stack.definition: To apply multiple insurance policies to one loss.

stake.definition: (1) An interest, such as in a business. (2) A wager; an  amount of money or item of property placed by two people in the  care of a third party who will give it to the party who becomes enti-  tled to it based on the outcome of some event, such as the winner  of a game or lawsuit.

stakeholder.definition: (1) A person with an interest in a business or trans-  action. (2) A third party who holds property or money that is the  subject of a contest between two other people, such as a lawsuit or  a wager. See also interpleader.

standard.definition: A norm; an established practice; a level, quality, or idea  used as a benchmark for comparison. ADJ. Accepted; normal.

standard of care.definition: The degree of care that an ordinary person of  reasonable prudence would use in a specific situation.

standard of proof.definition: The level of proof that must be offered to  prove a case, such as “beyond a reasonable doubt” in criminal  cases or a “preponderance of the evidence” in civil cases.

standing.definition: (1) Position or status in a community; reputation. (2)  Having a strong enough interest in a matter to be allowed to  bring a lawsuit based on it; also called standing to sue. ADJ.  Permanent; regular.

standing order.definition: An order that is carried out as part of a regular  routine, used to regulate procedures in a court or other body.

stand mute.definition: For a criminal defendant to refuse to plead when  charged.

stare decisis.definition: (Latin) Stand by things decided; the principle that  courts in common law will follow previously decided cases as  much as possible and avoid upsetting precedents.

state.definition: To say; to express something in words, either spoken or  written. N. (1) Condition. (2) An organized political unit with one  government; one of the United States. (3) The government; the  people who live in a state. (4) Ceremonies and pomp that accom-  pany high governmental functions.

state action.definition: A claim brought by a private citizen against the  government, protesting governmental intrusion into his or her  affairs and violation of his or her civil rights, usually brought under  the due process clause of the Fourteenth Amendment and the Civil  Rights Act.

state law.definition: Laws passed by and enforced within a state, as  opposed to federal law.

statement.definition: A written or verbal expression of some fact or belief;  an assertion or allegation made by a witness. See also opening  statement, prior inconsistent statement.

state of mind.definition: The mental condition at the time that some event  occurs; a person’s reasons for acting in a particular way.

state police.definition: The police force hired by a state to keep the peace  and enforce laws within a state.

status.definition: (1) Social standing or rank; official classification. (2)  Condition or state.

status crime.definition: A crime based on a person’s character or condition  rather than on actually committing a wrongful act, such as the  condition of being a drug addict; laws defining status crimes are  generally held to be unconstitutional.

status quo.definition: The usual state of things at a particular time.

status quo ante.definition: The normal state of things before a particular  event occurred.

statute.definition: A formal written law passed by a legislature.

statute of frauds.definition: A statute requiring that certain kinds of  contracts be written in order to be enforced, common under  English law and now codified in the Uniform Commercial Code.

statute of limitations.definition: A statute that limits the time in which a  lawsuit may be brought for an injury or a crime, after which the  party with a grievance loses the right to sue; see also laches.

statutory.definition: Related to statutes; required by or governed by a  statute.

statutory construction.definition: Interpretation of a statute, often done  by a judge when a statute is invoked during a lawsuit; see also  construe, construction.

stay.definition: To stop; to put on hold. N. A judicial order that puts some  action on hold temporarily.

stay of execution.definition: A judicial order that prevents a judgment from  being executed for a period of time.

steal.definition: To take something that belongs to someone else without  the owner’s consent, intending to keep it or use it and never return  it. ADJ. stolen. See also larceny, theft.

stealth.definition: Cautious and secretive movement that is meant to go  unnoticed. ADJ. stealthy.

stepchild.definition: A natural child of one’s spouse from a previous  marriage or liaison.

stepparent.definition: A person who is not a child’s natural parent but who  is married to the child’s natural mother or father.

stipulate.definition: To require or demand something as part of an agree-  ment; for the parties or attorneys on opposing sides of a case to  agree in writing on how to handle certain parts of the lawsuit in  order to limit issues and speed up the proceedings. N. stipulation.

stock.definition: (1) The capital of a business raised by selling shares in the  business to people inside or outside the company; an ownership  interest in a company or business; see also security. (2)  Merchandise and goods for sale held by a business. (3) A person’s  ancestry. V. To keep merchandise available for sale.

stock, no-par.definition: Stock issued with no face value, or with an  extremely low face value.

stockbroker.definition: A person employed by a customer to buy and sell  securities on the customer’s behalf.

stock certificate.definition: A document that indicates that its holder has  an ownership interest in a corporation.

stock exchange.definition: A place or organization where shares of stock  and other securities are bought and sold.

stockholder.definition: A person who owns shares of stock; synonymous  with shareholder.

stock market.definition: A stock exchange; the entire marketplace of secu-  rities trading facilities; the economic concept of securities trading;  also called the market.

stock split.definition: The splitting of single shares into two or more  shares apiece, resulting in an increased total number of shares and  in the shares held by individual shareholders. See also broker,  preferred stock, common stock, Securities and Exchange  Commission, security.

stonewall.definition: To refuse to answer questions or to give evasive  answers in order to delay or block some action, particularly a polit-  ical action.

stop.definition: To prevent someone from moving; to restrain someone’s  liberty. N. stop.

stop and frisk.definition: The action by a police officer of stopping a  person suspected to be armed and dangerous and running his or  her hands over the outside of the suspect’s clothing to locate any  concealed weapons.

straddle.definition: The securities trading practice of placing a contract to  buy (put option) and a contract to sell (call option) on the same  commodity or stock, hoping to profit from a large change in price  in a volatile market. See also hedge.

Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Partici.definition: A lawsuit filed  by a large corporation against a much smaller adversary in order to  silence the adversary by swamping it with legal burdens and costs.  ABBRV. SLAPP.

straw man.definition: (1) An argument placed in a brief or opinion solely  for the purpose of refuting it. (2) A third party who acts as a

streaming.definition: Constantly delivered over a telecommunications  network.

street name.definition: The name of a broker, rather than that of a  customer; refers to securities that are held in a broker’s name  instead of in the name of the customer who owns them.

strict.definition: Rigid; adhering closely to rules without deviation, relax-  ation, or room for interpretation.

strict liability.definition: (1) Tort liability imposed regardless of actual  fault on a person or company that sells a product for injuries due  to defective or dangerous products; see also products liability.  (2) Criminal liability imposed regardless of criminal intent on a  person who commits a certain type of criminal act, such as a  parking violation.

strict scrutiny.definition: A test that courts apply to determine the consti-  tutionality of statutes that classify people by type (such as race or  national origin) in ways that affect fundamental rights, in which the  government has the burden of proving that there is a compelling  state interest served by the classification and that the classification  is the only way of achieving that purpose.

strike.definition: A refusal to work by a group of employees, usually done  to force management to grant some request made by the workers  after negotiations have not produced the desired result; a refusal  by a group of people to do some action that is expected of them as  a way of forcing compliance with their demands. V. (1) To partici-  pate in a work stoppage. (2) To remove someone from a group; to  eliminate a prospective juror.

strike off.definition: (1) To remove a case from a court’s docket due to lack  of jurisdiction. (2) To finalize a sale at an auction by the auctioneer  bringing down his or her hammer or otherwise announcing that the  highest bidder is entitled to pay the amount of the bid and take the  property.

sua sponte.definition: (Latin) Of one’s own volition; voluntarily; without  prompting.

subcontractor.definition: A person or business hired by a general  contractor to perform a portion of a contracted project. See also  contractor.

subdivide.definition: To divide a part of something into smaller parts; to  divide a plot of land into smaller parcels or lots. N. subdivi-  sion.

subject.definition: (1) A topic; a person or thing that is being considered or  discussed. (2) A citizen; a resident of a state or nation, excluding  the monarch or ruler. V. To inflict; to make someone undergo some-  thing. ADJ. (1) Depending on. (2) Under some authority or control.

subject matter.definition: A matter or topic presented for consideration or  debate; the right or property that is the foundation of a dispute or  lawsuit.

subject matter jurisdiction.definition: A court’s power to hear cases  related to a particular kind of subject matter.

sub judice.definition: (Latin) Under a judge; under consideration by  a court.

sublease.definition: A lease of property to a third person by a person who  is already leasing it; the original lessee remains responsible for the  original lease. V. sublet.

submit.definition: (1) To present something to someone else for considera-  tion or decision; to present a proposal to the court for approval. (2)  To yield to someone else’s desires or authority; to obey.

subordinate.definition: Below someone or something else; of lower rank  or importance. N. A person ranked below another. V. To rank some-  thing below something else; to give one debt or claim lower priority  than another debt or claim. N. subordination.

suborn.definition: To persuade someone to commit perjury through bribery  or other inducement. N. subornation.

subpoena.definition: An official court document ordering a person to  appear in court or at a judicial proceeding at a specified time; also  called subpoena ad testificandum.

subpoena duces tecum.definition: A subpoena commanding a witness to  bring certain documents and other relevant items to court or to a  deposition.

subprime loan.definition: A loan made to an individual who cannot obtain  a loan from a traditional lender at market rates due to a low credit  rating, typically made at a higher interest rate to offset the risks  incurred by the lender.

subrogation.definition: The substitution of one person for another with  respect to an obligation, legal claim, or debt, with the substituted  person assuming all duties of payment or performance and all  rights that accompany the claim. V. subrogate.

subrogee.definition: A person who is substituted for another.

subscribe.definition: (1) To write one’s name on a document as a means of  authenticating it or making it binding upon him or her; see also  sign. (2) To agree to buy shares in a corporation. (3) To give money  to a cause, such as a charity. (4) To pay a sum of money in order to  receive something regularly, such as issues of a magazine. N.  subscription.

subscription rights.definition: The right of a person who already holds  shares in a corporation to buy at a favorable price additional shares  of the same type of stock.

subsidiary.definition: Under the control of another. N. A company  controlled by another company that owns a majority of the shares  of its stock; also called a subsidiary corporation.

subsidy.definition: A grant of money from the government or a public body  to assist an industry that provides services that are needed by the  community. V. subsidize.

substance abuse.definition: Using drugs or alcohol to the point that they  detrimentally affect the life of the user and those around him or  her.

substantial evidence.definition: Evidence adequate to support the propo-  sition for which it is offered.

substantial performance.definition: Performance that does not exactly  meet the terms of an agreement but that does accomplish the

substantiate.definition: To provide evidence to support a contention or  prove a fact; to verify; to back up a story.

substantive.definition: (1) Related to actual rights or duties rather than  rules or procedures. (2) Based in reality; having some real meaning.

substantive due process.definition: The requirement found in the Fifth  and Fourteenth Amendments that the substance and content of  laws must be fair and reasonable and that they must not be applied  arbitrarily or unreasonably.

substantive evidence.definition: Evidence offered to prove a fact at issue  in a trial, as opposed to character evidence about a witness.

substantive law.definition: The part of law that defines rights and duties  rather than procedural methods of enforcing those rights, i.e., the  law of contract, tort, property, trusts and estates, etc., as opposed  to civil procedure.

substitute.definition: To replace one thing or person with another. N. A  replacement.

subtenant.definition: A person who leases property from someone who is  already leasing himself or herself; a person who enters into a  sublease.

subterfuge.definition: A ploy to escape, evade, or conceal something; a  deceitful means of accomplishing some goal.

subvert.definition: To work to undermine established authority or over-  throw the government. N. subversion. ADJ. subversive.

succeed.definition: (1) To achieve a desired goal. N. success. ADJ.  successful. (2) To take the place of someone or something in a

succession.definition: (1) Several people or things of the same type  following one after the other. (2) Inheriting a title or right to prop-  erty through a will. (3) Following someone in a position, office, or  other situation. See also succeed.

successor.definition: A person or thing that takes the place of another in a  position, elected office, or other situation.

successor in interest.definition: The next person who would have title to  or control over a property if the current owner were to lose it. See  also succeed.

sue.definition: To initiate and carry out legal proceedings against someone;  to bring a lawsuit against someone. (2) To appeal to someone to  grant something. N. suit.

suffer.definition: (1) To experience pain or misery. (2) To tolerate.

sufferance.definition: Tolerance; consent to some action implied by failing  to object rather than explicitly approving.

sufficient.definition: Adequate; enough for the purpose for which it is  required. N. sufficiency.

sufficient evidence.definition: Enough evidence to justify the legal action  in question.

suffrage.definition: The right to vote in a public election.

suffrage, women’s.definition: The right of women to vote in political elec-  tions, granted by the Nineteenth Amendment in 1920.

suicide.definition: The act of killing oneself. V. commit suicide.

sui generis.definition: (Latin) Of its own kind; unique; the only example  of its type.

sui juris.definition: (Latin) Of one’s own right; emancipated; of legal age;  having the right to handle one’s own affairs and not under the legal  control of anyone else.

suit.definition: (1) A lawsuit; a legal action in which a plaintiff appeals to a  court to grant him or her a remedy for some injury. (2) An entreaty  or appeal to an authority to grant some request.

suitor.definition: (1) A person who applies or appeals to do something,  such as a man who hopes to persuade a woman to marry him or a  person who hopes to buy a business. (2) A person who brings a  lawsuit; a plaintiff.

sum certain.definition: A specified or fixed sum of money.

summary.definition: A concise statement of the main points of a longer  document. V. summarize. ADJ. (1) Concise. (2) Conducted  without the usual formalities. (3) Done by a judge or magistrate  without a jury.

summary judgment.definition: A judgment that ends a lawsuit without trial  in a case where a judge finds that there is no genuine issue of mate-  rial fact and thus no need to send the matter to a jury.

summation.definition: A last step in a jury trial before the jury begins delib-  erations, in which the attorneys for both sides sum up the evidence  presented and call attention to the important points in their argu-  ments; also called summing up.

summer associate.definition: A law student who works at a law firm for  the summer. See also law, clerk.

summon.definition: To notify a defendant that he or she has been sued and  must appear in court on a particular day.

Sunday closing laws.definition: Local or state laws that prohibit certain  businesses from operating on Sunday; also called blue laws.

sunset clause.definition: A clause in a statute stating that it will cease  to be effective on a certain date unless it is renewed by the  legislature.

sunshine laws.definition: Laws requiring governmental agencies and  departments to open their meetings to the public and make records  of those meetings available to the public.

superior.definition: Above; better than; entitled to priority over some-  thing else.

superior court.definition: A court of general jurisdiction, usually wider  jurisdiction than an inferior court, though meaning varies by state.

supersede.definition: To take the place of someone or something; to void  one thing and replace it with something else.

superseding cause.definition: Something that happens in between an  action and an injury that interrupts the chain of causation and  prevents the initial actor from being liable for the injury.

supervene.definition: To occur after an event, in such a way as to change  the circumstances.

supervening cause.definition: Something that happens between an action  and an injury that changes the circumstances enough that it  becomes the cause of the injury.

supervisor.definition: An employee who oversees the work of other  employees.

supplement.definition: Something added to something else to improve,  enhance, or complete it. V. supplement. ADJ. supplemental,  supplementary.

supplemental act.definition: A legislative act that clarifies, extends, or  completes an existing law.

supplemental jurisdiction.definition: The discretionary jurisdiction of a  federal court to hear and decide matters that are related to claims  already within the court’s original jurisdiction, such as claims that  arise out of the same case or controversy as one already before the  court or claims that add parties to the original parties.

supplemental pleading.definition: An answer, complaint, or other  pleading that adds new facts to an original pleading without  changing it; see also amend.

supplemental proceeding.definition: A proceeding done after a court has  issued judgment against a debtor, to identify the debtor’s property  and use it to satisfy the judgment; also called supplementary  proceeding.

support.definition: (1) To provide someone with money, a home, and the  necessities of life. (2) To encourage; to approve of; to defend; to  aid. (3) To corroborate; to back up with evidence. (4) To hold  something up; to bear the weight of something. N. support. See  also alimony, child support, maintain.

suppress.definition: To end something forcibly; to prevent information  from being revealed; to control or restrain. N. suppression.

suppression of evidence.definition: (1) A court's refusal to admit evidence acquired by unlawful means. (2) A party's refusal or failure to furnish opposing counsel with evidence that might prove unfavorable.

supra.definition: (Latin) Above; used in a document to refer to some-  thing mentioned earlier in the document; see also infra.

supranational.definition: Transcending national boundaries, concerns,  or governments.

Supremacy Clause.definition: A clause in Article IV of the U.S.  Constitution that makes the acts of the federal government  the supreme law of the land, superior to all state laws and  constitutions.

supreme.definition: The highest, most important, or most powerful;  superior to all others. N. supremacy.

Supreme Court.definition: The highest court in a state or in the United  States; the court of last resort in many states and in the United  States; also used as a name for a court of general jurisdiction in  some states.

surcharge.definition: (1) A charge added on top of a price or payment. (2)  A financial penalty imposed on a fiduciary who fails to perform his  or her duties properly. V. surcharge.

surety.definition: A person who takes responsibility for someone else’s  debt, agreeing to pay it if the debtor fails to do so, usually bound by  the same original instrument as the debtor and thus responsible for  the debt from its inception. See also guarantee.

surgeon general.definition: A federal officer who heads the U.S. Public  Health Service; the head of a military medical service.

surplus.definition: Excess; something left over after most of an asset or  fund has been used for its designated purpose; a corporation’s  assets remaining after expenses and debts have been paid.

surplus, earned.definition: Surplus earnings and profits retained by a  corporation instead of being paid to shareholders as dividends.

surplus, paid-in.definition: A corporation’s surplus obtained by selling  stock at a price above par value.

surprise.definition: (1) To catch, capture, or discover someone unawares.  (2) To astonish or amaze. N. (1) Coming upon someone unawares.  (2) An unexpected event; a revelation or situation that a party to a  trial has not expected and could not reasonably have prepared for,  which potentially can be grounds for a new trial.

surrender.definition: To give up; to yield; to hand something over to  someone else; for a tenant to give up a lease before its term with  the understanding that the landlord will terminate the lease. N.  surrender.

surrender value.definition: The amount that an insurer will pay a policy-  holder if he or she gives up a life insurance policy, i.e., the policy’s  current value.

surrogate.definition: (1) A substitute or deputy. (2) A judicial officer who  handles matters such as probate, adoptions, and guardianships at  the local level, such as within a county.

surrogate mother.definition: A woman who bears a child for another  couple, either by having her own egg fertilized with the husband’s  sperm or by having an embryo made from the couple’s gametes  implanted in her uterus, and after birth surrenders her parental  rights to the couple.

surrogate parent.definition: A person other than a child’s parent who  assumes parental responsibilities toward that child; an advocate  appointed for a child by a juvenile court.

surtax.definition: An additional tax imposed on something that has already  been taxed.

survival action.definition: An action that survives the death of the party  who brought it.

survival statute.definition: A statute that allows the estate of a deceased  person to maintain a cause of action for that person’s pain and  suffering up until the moment of death.

survive.definition: To remain living; to live after someone else has died. N.  survival. ADJ. surviving.

survivor.definition: A person who remains alive after the death of someone  else or after some event.

survivorship.definition: Becoming entitled to property by outliving someone  else who had an interest in it; the right of a joint tenant to take the  entire property when his or her co-tenant dies.

suspect.definition: A person who is believed to have committed a crime. V.  To believe without proof that someone has committed a crime or  misdeed; to have some slight idea that something is the case  without having any proof. ADJ. Untrustworthy; unreliable; poten-  tially dangerous.

suspect classification.definition: A classification of people that seems to  have no legitimate purpose, such as grouping people by race or sex  when those traits could not be relevant to the matter at hand.

suspend.definition: (1) To put on hold; to postpone. (2) To prevent  someone from performing a job or attending school for a period of  time as a punishment. N. suspension.

suspended sentence.definition: A sentence for a criminal offense that is  postponed indefinitely unless the convicted person commits  another crime within a specified period.

suspicion.definition: The act of suspecting; a belief that someone has  committed a crime or misdeed. V. suspect. ADJ. suspicious.

sustain.definition: (1) To support. (2) To uphold; to affirm the validity of  something. (3) To suffer something unpleasant, such as an injury.

swear.definition: To bind oneself or someone else with an oath.

swearing in.definition: The act of administering an oath to a witness at a  trial, to a public official, or to a person undergoing some other  formal proceeding. ADJ. sworn.

sweepstakes.definition: (1) A contest that awards prizes based on random  drawings of entries, often used as a promotion of products. (2) A  horse race or other gambling contest in which the winner or  winners receive all the stakes placed on the race.

swindle.definition: To use fraud to cheat someone out of money. N.  swindle, swindling.

syllabus.definition: A headnote at the beginning of a reported case,  summarizing the key legal points contained within.

symbolic speech.definition: Conduct or actions without words that  express some sentiment or opinion, which may or may not be  protected under the First Amendment.

syndicate.definition: (1) A group of people or companies that form an  alliance, permanent or temporary, to conduct some business or  activity together. (2) An organization that supplies material to a large  number of periodical publications. V. (1) To manage a syndicate;  to be managed by a syndicate; to sell something to a syndicate.

syndication.definition: The act of forming a syndicate; the condition of  being owned or managed by a syndicate.

table.definition: A list or chart that includes many items of information in  a concise display. V. To postpone or suspend consideration of  something, such as a proposed item of legislation.

table of cases.definition: An alphabetical list of the cases mentioned in a  legal book or document.

tacit.definition: Implied but not openly expressed; understood without  being explicitly stated.

tack.definition: To add something to something else, such as to add  together periods of time in which a property is under adverse  possession by different occupants in order to meet the statutory  requirement for ownership, to combine coverage from two or more  insurance policies in a claim for damages, or to unite two or more  liens so as to increase the priority of one of them. N. tacking.

Taft-Hartley Act.definition: A federal law enacted in 1947 as an addition to  the National Labor Relations Act, giving employers increased  rights in labor disputes.

tail.definition: A limitation on the ownership of land, usually restricted  to one person and his or her heirs. ADJ. Limited as to tenure or  ownership.

tail, estate in.definition: Inheritance that must be passed on to the recip-  ient’s children and grandchildren in a direct line. See also fee tail.

take.definition: To grasp or lay hold of something; to seize a person or  thing; to steal something; to assume ownership of something; to  receive title to property.

take over.definition: To acquire control over or management of a company.  N. takeover.

take the fifth.definition: To invoke the Fifth Amendment as justification for  refusing to answer a question in a criminal prosecution.

tamper.definition: To interfere with something so as to change it, especially  in a destructive or unauthorized way; to attempt to influence a jury  through bribery or other illegal means.

tangible.definition: Real; with a physical presence; perceptible to touch  and sight. See also intangible.

tangible evidence.definition: Evidence that can be seen or touched, as  opposed to testimony.

tariff.definition: A tax or duty imposed on imports and exports; see also  customs.

tariff, protective.definition: A fee imposed on imported goods of a certain  type to protect producers and sellers of domestic goods of the  same type.

tax.definition: A charge imposed by the government on people and busi-  nesses on various activities and possessions, such as income, prop-  erty, purchased goods, or inheritances, used by the government to  fund itself and its programs. V. (1) To impose a tax. (2) To assess  the costs of a legal action.

taxable estate.definition: The amount of an estate that is subject to taxes,  i.e., the gross estate minus any allowable deductions.

taxable gift.definition: A gift that is subject to taxation.

taxable income.definition: The amount of income subject to taxes, i.e.,  gross income minus credits and deductions.

taxable year.definition: A twelve-month period used for calculating taxes;  see also fiscal year.

taxation.definition: The act of taxing; money paid as tax. See also ad  valorem tax, estate tax, gift tax, income tax, payroll tax, sales tax.

tax benefit rule.definition: A rule stating that an amount deducted in an  earlier year but then recovered in a later year is to be included in  the income of the later year.

tax bracket.definition: A category of income that is subject to a particular  tax rate.

tax court.definition: A court that has jurisdiction over tax matters.

tax credit.definition: An amount subtracted from a taxpayer’s tax liability.

tax deduction.definition: An amount subtracted from a taxpayer’s taxable  income.

tax evasion.definition: The crime of paying less in taxes than the amount  required by the government, such as by fraudulently reporting a  lower income than what was in fact received.

taxing power.definition: The power granted to Congress by the  Constitution to levy taxes.

taxpayer.definition: A person or business that pays taxes; the person or  entity subject to taxes on a particular transaction.

tax rate.definition: The percentage of income or purchase price that must  be paid in tax; see also tax bracket.

tax shelter.definition: A fund, transaction, business arrangement, or other  method used by a taxpayer to reduce taxable income or tax  liability.

telecommunication.definition: Using signals to transmit information over  a distance, such as over a telephone or over a computer network.

telecommute.definition: To perform one’s job at some location other than  the traditional workplace, such as at home, and using information  technology to communicate with the workplace.

teleworker.definition: An employee who performs his or her job at some  location other than the traditional workplace, using information  technology to communicate with the workplace.

temporary.definition: Not permanent; provisional; lasting for a limited  time.

temporary disability.definition: Disability due to illness or injury that  prevents a person from working for a period of time but that is  expected to end when the person recovers.

temporary restraining order.definition: An order issued by a court forbid-  ding a party to take some action as a temporary measure to protect  a plaintiff until a formal hearing on an injunction can take place.  ABBRV. TRO.

tenancy.definition: The condition of being a tenant; an interest in land held  by a tenant.

tenant.definition: (1) A person who rents property from a landlord. (2) A  person who owns or possesses property.

tenant, life.definition: A person who holds an interest in property until the  end of a life, either the tenant’s or someone else’s.

tenants, joint.definition: Two or more people who share an interest in  property, each of them owning the whole interest; when a joint  tenant dies, his or her interest in the property ends.

tenants by the entirety.definition: A husband and wife who hold property  as joint tenants, with one spouse taking the whole title when the  other dies.

tenants in common.definition: Two or more tenants who hold an undivided  interest in property but who each have a separate title to a propor-  tional share; when a tenant in common dies, his or her share of the  property goes into his or her estate.

tender.definition: To offer or present something to someone; to present  someone with money as payment; to offer to perform some service  for a price. N. tender.

tender, legal.definition: Valid money; money that a creditor must accept as  payment of a debt.

tender of delivery.definition: A seller’s actual delivery of goods to a buyer;  a seller’s placing goods at the buyer’s disposal and notifying the  buyer of that fact.

tender offer.definition: An offer to purchase shares of stock, often above  market price, in a company made by a person or business that  wished to acquire control of the company.

tenement.definition: A building, house, dwelling, or other structure affixed  to land; a building divided into separate residences that are rented;  a dilapidated or run-down apartment building.

tenure.definition: (1) A right to hold property; the condition of holding  property. (2) The time that a person spends in a position or an

term.definition: (1) A word or expression for something. (2) The duration  of something; a fixed period of time; a period of time in which a  court is in session. (3) A condition or requirement in a contract or  agreement, usually used in the plural, i.e., terms. V. To give a name  to something.

terminable interest.definition: An interest in property that ends when a  particular event occurs or does not occur.

terminate.definition: To end; to expire; to fire an employee. N. termination.

terms of use.definition: The rules presented by the creator or producer of  a work such as a software application to its users, explaining the  rights and obligations that accompany use of the work.

territorial court.definition: A court in a U.S. territory that functions as both  a state and a federal court.

territorial integrity.definition: The principle that a state’s borders are  sacrosanct; the idea in international law that one state or nation  should not aid actions that could reform the borders of another  state or nation.

territorial jurisdiction.definition: A court’s or government’s power over a  specified territory or region.

territorial sea.definition: The waters that are considered the territory of  the state off which coast they lie; a band of waters parallel to the  coast that can be no more than twelve nautical miles wide. See also  Law of the Sea.

territorial waters.definition: All waters contained within a state or country  and all ocean waters within a three-mile border around its coast.

territory.definition: A defined area of land; an area or region under the  control of one government or ruler; the area in which a judge has  jurisdiction; a region that is organized and governed according to  the terms of a particular nation but has not yet been admitted to  statehood in that nation. ADJ. territorial.

terror.definition: Extreme fear or dread, which can lead to intimidation; the  fear typical of someone who is afraid of being hurt or killed.

terrorism.definition: The use of violence to achieve political ends in order  to intimidate citizens and governments into acquiescing to the  demands of the terrorists.

terrorist.definition: A person who engages in terrorism. ADJ. terroristic.

terrorize.definition: To frighten someone terribly; to use violence or threats  to fill someone with dread and alarm.

testament.definition: A will; the part of a last will and testament that  disposes of someone’s personal property.

testamentary.definition: Related to or given by a will.

testamentary capacity.definition: The mental ability required to compose  and execute a valid will, i.e., the ability to understand who one is,  what one owns, and what the will will do.

testamentary disposition.definition: A gift of property from a deceased  person to a living one, done through a will.

testamentary intent.definition: A testator’s true intention in writing a will  or naming a recipient of property.

test case.definition: A lawsuit that serves as a test of the validity of a law,  principle, or cause of action; one lawsuit chosen out of many  similar ones brought at the same time to be heard by the court and  decided as a test of the right of all the similar cases; also called a  test action.

testify.definition: To speak under oath; to give evidence as a witness in a  deposition or lawsuit; to serve as proof of something.

testimony.definition: The spoken evidence given by a witness under oath  in court or at a deposition, or written evidence provided by a  witness under oath through an affidavit.

theft.definition: The act of taking something that belongs to someone else,  without the owner’s permission and with no intention of returning  it; stealing; see also larceny, steal.

theorize.definition: To create a theory; to generate a possible explanation  for some situation.

theory.definition: A set of principles or ideas that serve as the basis or  explanation for some action or situation; a generally accepted body  of principles based on known facts that explains some circum-  stance; an idea or premise about something; a legal or factual prin-  ciple that serves as the basis for a case. See also hypothesis.

third degree.definition: Thorough and prolonged questioning administered  to a criminal suspect by the police with the intention of getting the  person to confess to a crime. ADJ. A category of crime less serious  than first degree or second degree.

third party.definition: A person who is not directly involved in a transac-  tion; someone who is not a party to an agreement.

third party beneficiary.definition: A person who is not a party to a contract  but who will benefit from it and has rights under it.

third party complaint.definition: A complaint filed by a defendant in an  existing lawsuit against someone who is alleged by the defendant  to be liable for damages to the plaintiff.

threat.definition: A statement of an intention to harm someone or damage  his or her property; a person or thing that seems likely to cause  damage or danger; a menace. V. threaten.

thrift institution.definition: A savings and loan or savings bank; also called  a thrift.

time.definition: (1) Duration; how long it takes to do something. (2) A  specific moment.

time-and-a-half.definition: An hourly pay rate commonly paid for overtime  work, calculated by adding 50% of the usual hourly rate to the usual  hourly rate.

time deposit.definition: A certificate of deposit; an account from which a  depositor must wait a specific period of time before withdrawing.

time is of the essence.definition: A phrase used in contracts in which  performance by one of the parties at or by a specific time is an  essential term. See also reasonable time.

timeshare.definition: A property, often a vacation home, that is owned by  several parties, each of whom is entitled to use it for a specified  period of time every year.

title.definition: (1) A right or claim of ownership of property. (2) The offi-  cial name of a job or position; the formal address given to a  person in a position or job or of particular social status. (3) The  name of a book, article, artwork, or other creation. (4) The name  of a legislative bill or legal action. V. To name a book or other  composition or creation.

Title IX.definition: Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, a federal  law banning sex discrimination in schools and universities, both  academic and athletic.
