Title VII.definition: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, a federal law  that prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of sex, race,  religion, or national origin.

title company.definition: A company that checks titles to property in  order to find defects and encumbrances, and that also issues title  insurance.

title insurance.definition: Insurance issued by a title company that has  investigated the title to a property, guaranteeing that it has no  known defects and insuring against claims based on a defective  title.

title of record.definition: The official title to a property, found through a  title search. See also bad title, cloud on title, good title.

title search.definition: An investigation done by a title company in which  it examines the history of a title in the registry of deeds and then  prepares an abstract summarizing the transfers of the title and the  absence of defects on it.

Tobacco Master Settlement Agreement.definition: A civil settlement in  which several major tobacco companies agreed to pay money to  the states to pay the health care costs of people injured by smoking  and to limit the advertising of tobacco products.

to have and to hold.definition: A phrase used in a conveyance expressing  the fact that the transferor intends for the transferee to receive the  property; also used in many marriage ceremonies to convey the  parties’ intention to be joined to one another.

toll.definition: A payment for the right to use something once, such as the  right to drive across a bridge. V. To suspend; to deny or take away.

tombstone ad.definition: An advertisement in a newspaper announcing  that a corporation is going to put up for sale an offering of stock.

tonnage.definition: Capacity of a ship; the amount of cargo a ship can  carry; the amount of cargo a ship is carrying.

tonnage duty.definition: A tax imposed on ships according to tonnage,  assessed when they enter a country.

tort.definition: A private injury or wrong; a violation of a socially recog-  nized duty owed to a plaintiff that results in injury to the plaintiff;  torts can be caused intentionally, through negligence, or under  strict liability. ADJ. tortious.

Tort Claims Act.definition: A federal law that makes the U.S. government  liable for damages in tort, waiving sovereign immunity except in  specific cases; many states also have tort claims acts.

tortfeasor.definition: A person who commits a tort against another.

torture.definition: The infliction of severe mental or physical pain on a  person in order to intimidate or punish the victim or to elicit infor-  mation. V. torture.

totality of the circumstances test.definition: A test that examines all the  circumstances surrounding an event to determine whether a  person’s constitutional rights have been violated by a search and  seizure.

total loss.definition: Complete destruction of property, such that it can no  longer be used at all and retains little or no value.

to wit.definition: (Middle English) To know; namely; that is to say.

trade.definition: (1) Buying and selling goods or services. (2) An exchange;  a one-to-one transfer. (3) A skilled job, especially one that involves  manual work. (4) A particular kind of business; the people who  work in a particular kind of business or profession. V. To buy and  sell; to exchange. See also commerce.

trade agreement.definition: An agreement made by two nations regulating  trade between them.

trade dress.definition: The appearance and image associated with a partic-  ular product, including labels, colors, size, shape, and advertising  techniques unique to the product.

trademark.definition: A name, word, symbol, or device used by the manu-  facturer of a product to identify that product and distinguish it  from other similar ones produced by competitors, which can be  registered with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office; also called a  mark. See also copyright, patent, trade name.

trademark, arbitrary.definition: A name for a product that is in no way  descriptive or suggestive of the product, such as “Apple”  computers, that is easily registered but difficult to educate  consumers about.

trademark, descriptive.definition: A name for a product that is descriptive  of what it is or does, which cannot be registered unless it has taken  on a distinctive meaning over time.

trademark, fanciful.definition: A name invented for a particular product,  such as “Exxon,” that is easily registered but difficult to educate  consumers about.

trademark, generic.definition: A name for a product that has become  synonymous with the product itself, such as “aspirin,” which  cannot be registered for trademark protection.

trademark, strong.definition: A trademark that is inherently distinctive,  such as an arbitrary or fanciful one, which is entitled to greater  protection than a weaker one, such as a generic or descriptive  mark.

trademark, suggestive.definition: A name for a product that evokes char-  acteristics of the product, and that is considered inherently distinc-  tive and thus allowed to be registered.

trade name.definition: A name used by a business to identify itself, which  stands for the business’s reputation and goodwill.

trade secret.definition: A secret formula, pattern, plan, process, machine,  or method of compiling information known and used exclusively  by one business that could give competitors an advantage if they  acquired it.

trade usage.definition: A practice or custom accepted by the practitioners  of a particular trade that they have every reason to expect to  be followed by other practitioners of that trade in a particular

traffic.definition: Trade in some commodity; buying, selling, or trading of  goods and services, often used regarding trade in illegal commodi-  ties such as drugs. V. traffic.

transact.definition: To conduct business; to carry on some activity.

transaction.definition: One instance of conducting business, such as  buying or selling an item; an agreement or act involving at least two  people that alters their legal rights in relation to one another.

transaction or occurrence test.definition: A test used to determine  whether a cause of action arises out of the same transaction or  event that is the subject matter of an existing claim brought by the  opposing party, in which case it must be brought as a counterclaim  or forever be barred from litigation.

transcribe.definition: To record spoken words in writing.

transcript.definition: An official, certified, written record of a trial or other  proceeding prepared by a court reporter.

transfer.definition: To move a person or thing from one place to another; to  move an object from the possession of one person to the posses-  sion of another; to change the title of property from one owner to  another; to convey. N. transfer.

transfer agent.definition: A person or company that oversees the sale of  shares of publicly traded corporations and keeps records on share-  holders.

transferee.definition: A person who receives something that is trans-  ferred.

transferee liability.definition: Tax liability imposed by the Internal  Revenue Service on a person who has received transferred prop-  erty in cases where it cannot find the transferor who actually  owes the taxes.

transferor.definition: A person who transfers something to someone else.

transfer pricing.definition: The practice of transferring goods, services,  loans, intellectual property, or other commodities to other  jurisdictions or countries in order to set prices at local levels  and gain tax advantages.

Transportation Security Administration.definition: A federal agency within  the Department of Homeland Security created in 2001 to oversee  security of transportation systems within the United States. ABBRV.  TSA.

treason.definition: The act of betraying one’s country, such as by aiding an  enemy of the state or plotting to overthrow the government. ADJ.  treasonous.

treasure trove.definition: Treasure found; a cache of money, precious  metals, or other valuable items with no known owner found buried  in the earth or in a hidden place; the person who finds a treasure  trove is entitled to it before anyone except the true owner.

treasury.definition: A place where wealth is stored, especially public funds;  the branch of the government responsible for saving and distrib-  uting revenue.

treasury bill.definition: A promissory note issued by the U.S. Treasury with  a maturity period of one year or less, which yields no interest but  is sold at a discount and paid in full on maturity.

treasury note.definition: A note issued by the U.S. Treasury for an inter-  mediate term, i.e., maturing in one to five years, which pays  interest by coupon.

treasury stock.definition: Stock issued and then later repurchased by a  corporation.

treaty.definition: A formal agreement between two or more nations  concerning some matter of public welfare.

treaty clause.definition: A clause in the Constitution that gives the  president the power to make treaties with other nations and the  Senate the right to approve them.

treble.definition: Triple. V. To triple; to multiply by three.

treble damages.definition: An award of damages that is tripled as further  punishment for the wrongdoer.

trespass.definition: To enter or disturb property owned by someone else  without the owner’s permission. N. trespass.

trespass, continuing.definition: A permanent state of trespass, as when  someone builds a house on his or her own land that encroaches  onto the property of someone else.

trespass, criminal.definition: Trespassing on property that has been  posted with signs warning away trespassers, or remaining on  someone else’s property after being ordered to leave.

trespass on the case.definition: A common law action that allowed  someone to recover for injury that resulted indirectly from a defen-  dant’s actions; also called case.

trial.definition: A formal judicial proceeding in which a judge and some-  times a jury hear the evidence in a case and decide the rights of the  parties in a civil case or the guilt or innocence of the defendant in  a criminal case. See also bench trial, jury trial.

trial court.definition: The court in which a case is first presented, as  opposed to an appellate court.

tribunal.definition: A judicial court; a judge’s seat or bench; a judge or  group of judges with jurisdiction in an area.

truancy.definition: Refusal to attend school by a minor required to attend,  without permission or justification.

truant.definition: A student who refuses to attend school.

true.definition: Accurate; reliable; in agreement with the facts; genuine;  loyal. N. truth.

true bill.definition: An indictment; the endorsement of an indictment by  the grand jury who has made it.

true copy.definition: A copy of a document that is not necessarily an exact  duplicate of the original but is close enough to be clearly under-  stood and recognized.

true value.definition: The fair market value of property.

trust.definition: (1) Confidence that someone will act in a particular way;  firm belief in someone’s reliability. (2) A legal arrangement in  which one person or company (the trustee) holds title to property

trust, active.definition: A trust that must be actively managed by the  trustee.

trust, charitable.definition: A trust created to benefit a class or the public  for educational, charitable, scientific, or religious purposes.

trust, passive.definition: A trust that does not require any action by the  trustee.

trust, resulting.definition: A trust that has not been explicitly created but  that arises when it seems that a person transferring property to  another must have wanted the person who receives title to the  property to use it to benefit someone else. See also cestui que.

trust company.definition: A company that acts as a trustee for people who  want to create trusts.

trustee.definition: The person who holds the property of a trust and admin-  isters it in a fiduciary capacity for the benefit of the beneficiaries.

trustee in bankruptcy.definition: A person who handles the administration  of bankruptcies, managing the financial affairs of the bankrupt  person or company and paying creditors.

trust fund.definition: Property or money held in a trust, administered by a  trustee and intended to benefit someone who does not own it.

trust indenture.definition: A document containing the terms and condi-  tions governing a trust.

trustor.definition: A person who creates a trust; a settlor.

trusty.definition: An inmate in a correctional institution who, due to good  behavior, is allowed a degree of freedom and responsibility denied  to other prisoners.

truth-in-lending act.definition: A federal law requiring lenders to reveal to  consumers applying for commercial credit the true cost of that  credit, in order to enable consumers to shop around for the most  favorable credit terms.

try.definition: To examine a case in a judicial trial; to subject a criminal  suspect to a trial.

turn-key contract.definition: A building contract in which the contractor  assumes all risks and performs all work, leaving the owner of the  project nothing to do toward its construction but “turn the key” to  open the door once it is finished.

turnover.definition: The rate of replacement of goods, funds, or personnel;  in a company, the rate at which employees leave and are replaced  by others.

turntable doctrine.definition: Another term for “attractive nuisance,” so-  called because railroad turntables are known to attract children  who can be harmed by playing on them.

tying arrangement.definition: A seller’s refusal to sell a buyer a desired  item unless the buyer also purchases a specific other item, whether  the buyer wants the additional item or not.

UGMA.definition: Uniform Gifts to Minors Act. See Uniform Transfers  to Minors Act.

ultimate facts.definition: The facts that ultimately form the basis of the  decision in a case.

ultrahazardous.definition: Extremely dangerous; involving a serious risk  of harm to people or property that cannot be avoided no matter  how careful one is.

ultrahazardous activity.definition: An activity that is so abnormally  dangerous that no amount of care can prevent risk of harm, and  that therefore gives rise to strict liability.

ultra vires.definition: (Latin) Beyond the powers; outside one’s official  authority; used especially to describe actions by a corporation that  exceed the powers granted to it by its charter or by state law.

unanimous.definition: Agreed to by all concerned parties without a nega-  tive vote or opinion. N. unanimity.

unauthorized.definition: Done without official authorization or permis-  sion.

unauthorized use.definition: Using an automobile without permission of  the owner.

unborn.definition: Not yet born; can refer to a fetus in the womb or a  child who has not yet been conceived.

unclean hands.definition: The equitable doctrine that a plaintiff’s own fault  must be considered when determining the remedy he or she should  receive from a defendant. See also clean hands doctrine.

unconscionable.definition: Unreasonably detrimental; grossly one-sided;  so unfair or oppressive to the interests of a party to a contract as to  render the contract unenforceable. N. unconscionability.

unconstitutional.definition: Conflicting with the Constitution of the  United States or another constitution.

uncontestable.definition: Not able to be challenged or contested.

uncontestable clause.definition: A clause in a life insurance policy that  says the insurer may not contest a claim after the policy has been  in force for a specified period of time.

under control.definition: Of a vehicle, able to be stopped quickly when  necessary.

under protest.definition: Unwillingly; done under compulsion while  making one’s unwillingness known; used to describe an action  done unwillingly and with objection.

under the influence.definition: Intoxicated; suffering from mental and  physical impairment due to consumption of alcohol or drugs.

underwrite.definition: (1) To insure; to accept the liability for something. (2) To agree to buy all unsold shares from an issue of stocks or bonds. (3) To provide financial support for some enterprise. N. underwriting.

underwriter.definition: A person, bank, insurance company, or other insti-  tution that underwrites something.

undivided.definition: Not divided or broken up into portions.

undivided interest.definition: An interest in property held by tenants in  common, joint tenants, or tenants by the entirety, each of whom  has a right to use the entire property; also called an undivided right;  see also tenant.

undue.definition: Improper; unwarranted; exceeding what is necessary.

undue influence.definition: Excessive or improper influence exerted on a  person writing a will or giving away property by someone who  wants to take away the person’s free will and dominate the action;  influence that interferes with someone’s free will or judgment and  subjugates it to someone else’s wishes.

unearned interest.definition: Interest paid to someone by a financial insti-  tution before it is actually earned.

unearned surplus.definition: All surplus that is not earned, including  surplus from the revaluation of assets, surplus from contributions  by shareholders or others, and paid-in surplus. See also income,  unearned.

unemployment insurance.definition: Insurance that pays benefits to  temporarily unemployed workers; in the United States, an  insurance program that federal law requires each state to fund  and administer.

unfair competition.definition: The tort of fraudulently trying to pass off  one’s goods as those of another company, or to use the reputation  of another company to sell one’s own product, as through  misleading advertising or packaging.

unfair labor practices.definition: Acts forbidden to both employers and  employees under the National Labor Relations Act, including for a  union to coerce employees and employers, or to refuse to bargain,  or for an employer to interfere with the employees’ attempts to  exercise their rights.

unfit.definition: Not suitable for a particular purpose; not qualified;  incompetent.

uniform.definition: Conforming everywhere to the same standard;  remaining the same everywhere; applying equally to everyone  concerned.

Uniform Code of Military Justice.definition: A compilation of laws  governing the conduct of military personnel.

Uniform Commercial Code.definition: A compilation of laws governing  commercial transactions, including the sale of goods, commercial  paper, banking, and secured transactions. ABBRV. UCC.

Uniform Consumer Credit Code.definition: A law adopted by some states  that regulates consumer credit. ABBRV. UCCC.

uniform laws.definition: Compilations of laws that address various subject areas and attempt to make the law in those areas uniform, such as the Uniform Commercial Code, Uniform Consumer Credit Code, and Uniform Transfers to Minors Act; also called uniform acts.

Uniform Resource Identifier.definition: A commonly used synonym for  Uniform Resource Locator. ABBRV. URI.

Uniform Resource Locator.definition: The series of characters that will  retrieve a particular web page when typed into the address bar in a  Web browser; a domain name or IP address plus other information  that specifies a particular page. ABBRV. URL.

Uniform System of Citation.definition: A guide to legal citation published  by the Harvard Law Review Association and accepted as the stan-  dard guide to citation; synonymous with Blue Book.

Uniform Transfers to Minors Act.definition: An act governing transfers of  property to a trust managed by a custodian for the benefit of named  minors; an act called the Uniform Gifts to Minors Act was the orig-  inal law of this type, but the Uniform Transfers to Minors Act has  replaced it in most states. ABBRV. UTMA.

unilateral.definition: One-sided; performed by one party in a situation  without the participation or agreement of other parties; affecting  only one party or group in a particular situation. See also contract,  unilateral.

uninsured motorist coverage.definition: Insurance that covers the insured  for injury or damage caused by a driver who does not have liability  insurance.

union.definition: (1) A group of states or nations with a central govern-  ment. (2) The act of joining together; a state of togetherness or  harmony. (3) A labor organization that exists to negotiate with  employers on behalf of workers in matters such as wages, hours,  and workplace safety.

United Nations.definition: An international organization founded in 1945 to  help nations cooperate in matters of human rights, economics,  security, and international law. ABBRV. UN.

United Nations Framework Convention on C.definition: A  nonbinding international treaty passed in 1992 that states the desir-  ability of reducing greenhouse gas emissions and slowing climate  change. See also Kyoto Protocol.

United States Attorney.definition: An attorney appointed by the president  to represent the U.S. government in civil lawsuits and other matters  in a judicial district; see also district attorney.

United States Citizenship and Immigratio.definition: A bureau in  the Department of Homeland Security that oversees immigration,  naturalization, visa applications, and petitions for asylum. See also  Immigration and Naturalization Services. ABBRV. USCIS.

United States Code.definition: A compilation of federal statutes, updated  every six years and supplemented in between. ABBRV. USC.

United States Code Annotated.definition: The annotated version of the  USC, supplemented with case notes, cross-references, and histor-  ical references. ABBRV. USCA.

United States Constitution.definition: The principles according to which  the federal government of the United States is organized, enacted  in 1787 and modified by twenty-seven amendments.

United States Court of Federal Claims.definition: The federal court that  hears claims against the U.S. government; this court replaced the  Court of Claims in 1982. Synonymous with Claims Court, U.S.

United States Customs Service.definition: The government service that  collects customs duties, seizes contraband, processes people and  goods coming in and out of the United States, and enforces appli-  cable trade statutes.

United States Department of Treasury.definition: The department of the  federal government that handles financial policy, collects taxes,  issues currency, manages public debt, and oversees several  bureaus including the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and  Explosives, the Secret Service, and the Internal Revenue Service.

United States Department of Veterans Aff.definition: A federal agency  that provides benefits and services to military veterans. ABBRV. VA.

United States Marshal.definition: An officer appointed by the president for  a judicial district who executes all writs and orders issued by  federal courts.

United States Tax Court.definition: An administrative agency that func-  tions as a court to hear disputes over amounts of income, estate,  and gift taxes between taxpayers and the Internal Revenue Service.

unities, four.definition: The four conditions that must exist in order to  create a joint tenancy under common law, including unity of  interest, unity of time, unity of possession, and unity of title.

unit investment trust.definition: A registered trust that purchases fixed-  income securities and holds them to maturity, sold to investors in  units (typically of $1,000 each). ABBRV. UIT.

unity.definition: The state of forming a united whole; agreement or  harmony between people.

unity of interest.definition: Sharing an interest in the same property.

unity of possession.definition: Holding equal rights to use and possess the  same property.

unity of title.definition: Receiving an interest in property through the same  conveyance or instrument.

unjust enrichment.definition: Knowingly receiving and accepting a benefit  without paying for it or a benefit that rightfully belongs to someone  else.

unlawful assembly.definition: The gathering of three or more people with  the intention of disturbing the peace or committing some unlawful  act; definition varies by state.

unlawful detainer.definition: The act of keeping possession of a property  by someone who once had a right to it but no longer does, such as  a tenant whose lease has ended but who refuses to leave.

unlawful entry.definition: Entering property without the owner’s consent  through the use of force or fraud.

unlisted security.definition: A security that is not listed on a stock  exchange and can only be traded over the counter.

unnatural act.definition: Sexual acts that were historically considered  unnatural or wrong, including sodomy or buggery; see also crime  against nature.

unnecessary hardship.definition: A condition found when the physical  characteristics of a piece of property prevent it from being used for  purposes permitted by zoning laws, or when it would be prohibi-  tively expensive to use it in the permitted ways, so zoning laws are  therefore found to impose unreasonable difficulties on the prop-  erty owner and a variance is warranted.

unrealized.definition: Not achieved; not converted into money.

unrealized profit or loss.definition: Profit or loss that has not yet been  converted into actual money, existing only in principle or “on  paper,” such as a rise or loss in stock prices.

unsolicited.definition: Unasked-for; not requested or sought after.

usage.definition: A practice that is usual within a particular business;  the typical way of doing an activity or interpreting a word or  phrase within a profession or kind of business. See also custom,  trade usage.

USA PATRIOT Act.definition: Uniting and Strengthening America by  Providing Appropriate Tools Required to Intercept and Obstruct  Terrorism Act of 2001; a law passed shortly after September 11,  2001, that strengthened U.S. law enforcement agencies for the  purpose of preventing future terrorist attacks.

USCIS.definition: United States Citizenship and Immigration Services.

use.definition: To employ someone or something; to put into action; to  enjoy the benefits of real or personal property. N. The act of using;  the fact of being used; the right to enjoy the benefits of property.

useful.definition: Able to be put to some beneficial use.

usufruct.definition: The civil law right to use property that belongs to  someone else.

usurer.definition: A person who lends money at unreasonably high interest.

usurp.definition: To take over; to supplant; to take over a position of power  by force. N. usurpation.

usurper.definition: A person who usurps a position.

usury.definition: Lending money at excessively high interest rates. ADJ.  usurious.

U.S. v. Microsoft.definition: An antitrust lawsuit brought by the U.S.  Department of Justice against Microsoft Corporation in 1998, in  which the United States alleged that Microsoft had violated antimo-  nopoly laws by bundling its operating system and Web browser  software, thus unfairly preventing rivals from competing for  customers.

utmost.definition: The greatest amount or extent possible. ADJ. utmost.

utmost care.definition: Highest care; the care that a very cautious and  competent person would use.

utmost resistance.definition: The highest degree of resistance a person is  capable of, historically required of a victim of rape in order to  charge the offender with rape.

utter.definition: (1) To say something out loud. N. utterance. (2) To offer  forged money as valid; to introduce forged money into circulation.  N. uttering. ADJ. Complete; total; absolute.

VA.definition: United States Department of Veterans Affairs.

vacate.definition: (1) To leave; to make empty. (2) To make void; to annul.

vacation.definition: A break from work; leave from work to be used for any  purpose, particularly recreation.

vagrancy.definition: The act of living as a vagrant.

vagrant.definition: A person with no regular job or home who wanders  from place to place and supports himself or herself by begging.

vague.definition: Uncertain; unclear; not clearly defined or stated. N.  vagueness.

valid.definition: Properly executed and legally binding; legitimate;  acceptable. N. validity. See also void.

valuable consideration.definition: Something of value given in exchange  for a performance or a promise, which entitles the recipient of the  promise to enforce a claim against a promisor who fails to perform.

value.definition: (1) Worth; cost or price in money; the price a seller would  give a buyer in a bona fide transaction in an open market. (2)  Importance; utility. (3) A moral principle. V. (1) To estimate the  worth of something. (2) To consider important.

vandal.definition: A person who willfully destroys or damages property.

vandalism.definition: The act of willfully destroying or damaging property.  V. vandalize.

vehicle identification number.definition: A unique seventeen-digit number  that identifies any given motor vehicle. ABBRV. VIN.

vehicular.definition: Of or related to motor vehicles, such as automobiles  and motorcycles. See also homicide, vehicular.

venire.definition: (Latin) To come; the writ that summons potential jurors  to court; the group of potential jurors for a case.

venture.definition: A risky undertaking; a business enterprise that involves  some risk. V. To dare to do something; to undertake some enter-  prise that involves risk of loss.

venture capital.definition: Funding for a new business that involves risk  but has potential for great profits. See also joint venture.

venue.definition: The place where something happens; a court that has  jurisdiction over a matter; a court in which cases from a particular  location may be brought; the district in which a federal lawsuit is  brought.

verbal.definition: Expressed in words; oral; spoken rather than written.

verdict.definition: A jury’s or judge’s finding on a question of fact, to be  used by the court in determining its final judgment.

verdict, general.definition: A verdict that announces which party prevails;  the ordinary kind of verdict.

verdict, partial.definition: A verdict that finds a criminal defendant guilty  of one or more charges and innocent of the others.

verdict, special.definition: A jury’s determination on a particular question  of fact alleged in the pleadings. See also directed verdict.

verify.definition: To confirm the truth, accuracy, or authenticity of some-  thing; to attest to the truth of something in an affidavit or other  sworn statement. N. verification.

versus.definition: (Latin) Against; used in the title of a lawsuit to indi-  cate which party is against which. ABBRV. v. or vs.

vest.definition: To accrue; to take effect; to bestow on someone; to give  someone authority, power, or a right; to gain a legal right to some-  thing; to come into possession of something.

vested.definition: Fixed; settled; secure in someone’s possession; having  the right to present or future enjoyment; established or protected  by law or contract.

vested estate.definition: A property interest currently in someone’s posses-  sion or that will certainly come into his or her possession in the  future, and therefore can be given away or sold by him or her.

vested gift.definition: A gift given at present, or a gift that will certainly  take effect in the future and that is not contingent on some  condition.

vested interest.definition: An interest that is recognized as belonging to  someone who has the right to give it away; a personal stake in  some matter.

vested right.definition: In constitutional law, a right that is so firmly  attached to a person that it cannot be taken away by someone else.

vesting.definition: An employee’s acquisition of an unconditional right to  receive retirement benefits after working for an employer for the  required period.

veteran.definition: A person who has served in the armed forces and been  honorably discharged.

veto.definition: The act of rejecting a law or decision that has been passed  by a legislature, usually done by a president or governor who must  sign it in order for it to become valid.

veto, line-item.definition: The power to reject some portions of a law  without rejecting the whole thing. See also pocket veto.

vexatious litigation.definition: A lawsuit brought without probable cause,  out of malice toward the opposing party.

viable.definition: (1) Feasible; able to work. (2) Of a fetus, capable of  living outside its mother’s womb, with or without artificial life  support. N. viability.

vicarious.definition: Experienced or done through another person.

vicarious liability.definition: Liability imposed on one person for the  actions of another based on a relationship between them, such as  that between a principal and an agent or a parent and child.

vice.definition: Immorality; bad behavior; a bad habit. PREFIX. Next in rank;  a deputy for someone else, such as a vice president.

vice crime.definition: A crime involving immorality, such as drug use or  prostitution.

Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties.definition: An international  treaty effective in 1980 that codified and clarified accepted interna-  tional law on the creation of treaties.

violent.definition: Using physical force, especially unwarranted force  used to intimidate or physically defeat someone else, often accom-  panied by anger or strong emotions. N. violence.

violent crime.definition: A crime involving physical force, such as assault  or murder.

violent death.definition: Death caused by a violent external force, such as  another person.

virtual.definition: Almost but not quite as described or intended; effective  for all practical purposes, though not officially acknowledged.

virtual representation.definition: The condition of being represented in  a lawsuit without being named as a party by a party who shares  the same interest, with the nonparty agreeing to be bound by  the judgment.

virus.definition: A computer program that can copy itself and install itself  onto a computer without help from or the knowledge of the  computer’s owner or user.

visa.definition: A document or stamp issued by a country to a foreign  national, giving the holder the right to enter the country for a spec-  ified period and for specified purposes.

visitation rights.definition: Rights granted to divorced parents without  custody to visit their children, and occasionally granted to grand-  parents as well.

vitiate.definition: To ruin something; to void or annul; to impair something’s  effectiveness.

viz.definition: (Latin) Videlicet, meaning obviously, namely, or of  course; in other words; used to explain or rephrase preceding  words in order to clarify them.

voice exemplar.definition: A recording of someone’s voice made during a  criminal investigation, to be used as a basis for comparison against  recorded evidence.

void.definition: Invalid; not legally binding; ineffective; empty. See also  valid. v. To declare something legally invalid.

void ab initio.definition: Never having had legal validity; void from the  beginning.

voidable.definition: Able to be annulled or voided due to some irregu-  larity or problem, but valid until that happens.

voidable contract.definition: A contract that may be legally voided by one  or both parties, but that is valid until voided.

void for vagueness.definition: Describes a statute so obscure, difficult to  understand, or unclear that it is impossible for ordinarily intelligent  people to agree on a meaning, and so it must be void.

void on its face.definition: Describes a statute or instrument that has such obvious errors or imperfections that it must be invalid, especially excessively broad legislation that does not address a particular crime specifically enough and inadvertently impairs many legal rights.

voir dire.definition: (French) To say the truth. (1) The preliminary exami-  nation of potential jurors by the judge and attorneys to decide  whether they are qualified for jury service and whether any  conflicts exist. (2) A hearing done during a trial outside the hearing  of the jury in order to determine some point that the court must  rule on without the jury.

voluntary.definition: Done willingly, without the influence of or pressure  from someone else.

vote.definition: An indication of a choice for candidates in an election; an  individual ballot or other means of indicating such choice  submitted by a voter; the right to vote.

voter.definition: A person who votes; a person who has the right to vote.

Voting Rights Act.definition: A federal law passed in 1965 that guarantees  all citizens the right to vote without discrimination by forbidding  districts to require people to meet conditions such as literacy tests  or educational requirements.

voting trust.definition: A device in which corporate shareholders transfer  their voting rights to a trustee who votes for them in corporate elec-  tions in order to pool their votes and control corporate affairs.

vouch.definition: To verify; to confirm that a person is of good character or  qualified for a position; to assert that something is true, based on  personal experience.

voucher.definition: A small printed document that entitles its holder to  some good or service; a receipt; a document that authorizes the  payment of cash.

voyeurism.definition: The act or condition of being a voyeur.

W-4.definition: A form filled out by an employee informing the employer  how many tax deductions the employee may take and thus how  much the employer should withhold.

wage.definition: Compensation for work performed, particularly pay by  the hour or by production paid weekly or daily to a manual or  skilled worker; often called wages. See also salary. V. To carry on  some enterprise, particularly a war.

wage, living.definition: Compensation sufficient to support a family above  poverty conditions.

wage, minimum.definition: The lowest hourly wage an employer is allowed  to pay workers in most jobs, set by statute.

wager.definition: A bet; an agreement made by two parties that one of them  will pay the other a specific sum if a certain condition occurs. V.  wager.

waiting period.definition: A period of time that must elapse before a  person can take some action or enforce some legal right.

waive.definition: To voluntarily give up a legal right or claim.

waiver.definition: The act of intentionally and voluntarily surrendering a  right or claim; a document that records a person’s surrender of a  right or claim.

wanton.definition: (1) Reckless; malicious; deliberate, unprovoked,  and in disregard of the safety of others. (2) Immodest; sexually  promiscuous.

war crime.definition: During wartime, a violation of the laws of war. See  also Geneva Conventions.

ward of the court.definition: A person who is under the care of the court,  or for whom the court has appointed a guardian.

warehouse.definition: A building used to store goods. V. (1) To store goods  in a warehouse. (2) For a mortgage broker to retain a mortgage  until the mortgage resale market improves. (3) For a corporation to  notify certain investors of a tender offer before it is made public,  allowing those investors to purchase stock at a low price.

warehouse receipt.definition: A document issued by a person who runs a  warehouse, indicating that the goods have been deposited into his  or her care, which serves as a document of title for its holder.

warrant.definition: A written order issued by some authority directing  someone to do a certain act, particularly an order issued by the  state directing a law enforcement officer to arrest someone. V. (1)  To justify; to make necessary. (2) To guarantee or confirm; to affirm  that a title is good; to confirm that something is as it is represented.  See also search warrant.

warrant, death.definition: A warrant issued by a governor or other high  officer ordering a law enforcement officer to execute a convicted  criminal who has been sentenced to death.

warrant, stock.definition: A certificate entitling its holder to buy a certain  number of shares of stock for a specified price.

warrantee.definition: A person who receives a warrant.

warrantless arrest.definition: An arrest made without a warrant, occasion-  ally justifiable if the arresting officer has sufficient probable cause.

warrantor.definition: A person who makes a warranty.

warranty.definition: A promise or assurance that something is true, of a  certain quality, or useful for a particular purpose; an assurance by  one party to a contract that a certain fact is true, given to save the  other party the trouble of confirming it, which is in effect a promise  to cover any losses suffered by the other party if the fact turns out  not to be true.

warranty, express.definition: A promise included with the terms of a sale  agreement, assuring the purchaser that the goods are of a partic-  ular quality and promising to fix or replace them if they are not as  promised.

warranty, implied.definition: A warranty implied by the seller’s actions or  words.

warranty deed.definition: A deed that contains a warranty of good title.

warranty of fitness for a particular pur.definition: A promise by the  seller, expressed or implied, that the purchased item will be suit-  able for the use to which the buyer wishes to put it.

warranty of habitability.definition: An assurance, expressed or implied,  given by a landlord to a new tenant that the premises are suitable  for human habitation.

warranty of merchantability.definition: An implied assurance by a seller  that the goods he or she sells are fit for the general purpose for  which they are sold.

Warsaw Convention.definition: An international convention that regulates  international air transport of people and goods.

wash sale.definition: (1) A sale of stock followed by a purchase of the same stock within a short period of time, usually thirty days; losses on such a sale and repurchase may not be recognized for tax purposes. (2) Simultaneous buying and selling of a stock issue to generate interest in the stock, forbidden by the Securities and Exchange Commission.

wash trade.definition: The sale of a security at a loss and the repurchase of  the same security within thirty days before or after the sale, which  does not result in a capital loss for tax purposes; a wash sale.

waste.definition: (1) The act of using something carelessly for no good  reason. (2) The things left over from producing something,  which have no value and must be disposed of. (3) Damage to or  neglect of property by a life tenant that reduces the property’s  value. V. waste.

wasting asset.definition: A natural resource that is depleted as it is used  and that eventually will cease to be useful, such as a coal mine; also  called wasting property.

watered stock.definition: Stock issued by a corporation whose total worth  is less than its capital investment; stock issued for less than  adequate consideration, as a bonus or as part of compensation,  which means that its true value is actually less than its stated  market value.

watermark.definition: (1) A transparent mark on a piece of paper that can  be seen when the paper is held to a light, used as a sign of genuine-  ness. (2) A mark on the bank of a body of water that indicates the  highest or lowest point to which the water has risen or dropped.

way.definition: A road, path, or passage. See also right of way.

weapon.definition: An object designed or used to hurt or kill, either offen-  sively or defensively.

weapon of mass destruction.definition: A weapon that can kill or injure a very large number of people simultaneously and destroy an enormous range of territory, usually without the aggressor having to come near its area of effectiveness.

webmaster.definition: The individual responsible for administering and  maintaining a website.

website.definition: A collection of documents, images, and other forms of  digital information on one computer that broadcasts them on the  Internet.

Web traffic.definition: The visits a website receives from users.

weight of evidence.definition: The preponderance of the evidence; the  side of the case that has more evidence supporting it.

welfare.definition: (1) Well-being, quality of life. (2) Financial aid paid by  the government to the needy.

Westlaw.definition: A privately owned online legal research system that  provides access to reported decisions, federal and state statutes,  law review articles, and many other materials; see also LexisNexis.

wetland.definition: An area of land on the edge of a body of water, where  aquatic and terrestrial life forms intersect.

when issued.definition: Shortened form of phrase “when, as, and if  issued,” used to describe a security that has not yet been author-  ized for issue, or that has been authorized but does not in fact exist  yet, which can be purchased in a conditional transaction that  becomes complete when the stock is officially issued.

whereas.definition: (1) Considering that something is the case; used in  legal documents to begin an introductory statement. (2) In compar-  ison to; in contrast with.

whistleblower acts.definition: Federal and state laws that prevent  employers from retaliating against employees who report wrong-  doing in the workplace.

wholesale.definition: The selling of goods, usually in large quantities, to  retailers who will in turn sell them to consumers.

wholesaler.definition: A person or company who buys large quantities  of goods from manufacturers or suppliers, and then sells them  to retailers.

widow.definition: A wife who remains alive after her husband dies.

widow’s allowance.definition: Property and money that a widow can claim  from her husband’s estate for her own support.

wiki.definition: A website that can be edited by any user.

wildcat strike.definition: A strike not authorized or organized by a labor  union, usually illegal.

will.definition: (1) Desire; intention; choice. (2) A document in which a  person describes how to distribute his or her property after death.  V. To leave property to someone through a will. See also holograph,  last will and testament, living will, testament.

willful and wanton misconduct.definition: Conduct that displays an inten-  tional disregard of one’s legal duties or of the safety of others.

willful, deliberate, and premeditated.definition: A description of the  mental state necessary for some crimes, such as first-degree  murder. See also wanton.

will, joint.definition: A will disposing of joint property or property that is  owned individually but is treated as common property, signed by  all parties who hold the property.

will, mutual.definition: A will in which two people make mutual provisions  for one another; also called a reciprocal will.

winding up.definition: Concluding the affairs of a corporation or partner-  ship that is being liquidated, including paying off debts and distrib-  uting the remaining assets.

wiretapping.definition: Using an electronic device to eavesdrop on tele-  phone conversations or other communications; see also eavesdrop,  pen register.

withdraw.definition: To retreat; to remove or take away; to take money out  of an account. N. withdrawal.

withhold.definition: To hold something back; to keep something that is  desired by someone else; to deduct income tax from an  employee’s pay and send it to the Internal Revenue Service on the  employee’s behalf. N. withholding.

with prejudice.definition: Describes a claim or action that has been  dismissed and that may not be brought again as a new action.

without prejudice.definition: With no legal rights affected; describes a  claim or action that may be brought as a new action after dismissal.

without recourse.definition: (1) Having no further rights or possible  remedies. (2) Nonrecourse.

Witness Protection Act.definition: A federal statute that provides for the  protection of witnesses in cases involving organized crime.

word of art.definition: A word that has a particular meaning when used in  a particular field, such as law or medicine, and that has a different  meaning or no meaning when used in ordinary speech; also called  term of art.

workers’ compensation.definition: A system of benefits provided by  employers to employees who are injured on the job.

Workers’ Compensation Acts.definition: Federal and state laws that regu-  late the awards given by employers to employees injured on the  job, generally holding that an employer is strictly liable for all  injuries regardless of the employee’s fault, and preventing  employees who receive workers’ compensation from filing civil  lawsuits in an attempt to win damages.

work/life.definition: A person’s combined work and nonwork activities.

work made for hire.definition: A copyrightable work prepared either by an  employee in the course of employment, or by someone commis-  sioned to do the work, on the condition that the person who  prepares the material is paid for the job and does not retain any  rights to it, including copyright. The employer or commissioner of  the work is considered the author for copyright purposes.

work product.definition: Documents, notes, memoranda, and other mate-  rials prepared by an attorney as part of representing a client,  especially in anticipation of litigation on a particular matter.

work product rule.definition: A rule protecting work product from  discovery.

work release program.definition: A program that allows an inmate in a  correctional institution to go out to work during the day and return  to the institution at night.

World Bank.definition: A financial institution that exists to reduce global  poverty by providing loans to help poor nations develop.

World Health Organization.definition: An agency of the United Nations  that works to promote international public health. ABBRV. WHO.

World Intellectual Property Organization.definition: An agency of the  United Nations that promotes creative activity and the protection  of intellectual property. ABBRV. WIPO.

World Trade Organization.definition: An international organization that  regulates trade among nations. ABBRV. WTO.

World Wide Web.definition: The body of interlinked documents on  websites, accessible through the Internet by using a Web browser.  Synonymous with Web.

worth.definition: Monetary value; the quality of something. See also net  worth.

wraparound mortgage.definition: A second mortgage combined with an  older mortgage with a lower interest rate than the current one,  which results in a new interest rate between the old and current  mortgages; the borrower makes payments to the new lender,  who in turn makes payments to the lender that provided the  original mortgage.

writ.definition: A written command issued by a court or other authority  ordering someone to do a particular act.

writ of assistance.definition: An equitable remedy that transfers title to  property that has been determined by the court to belong to the  recipient.

writ of attachment.definition: A court order to seize a debtor’s property;  see also attach.

writ of error.definition: A writ issued by an appellate court to a lower court  ordering it to send the appellate court the record of a case tried in  the lower court so that the appellate court can review it for errors  in application of the law.

writ of execution.definition: A writ ordering a law enforcement officer to  enforce a judgment by the court.

writ of prohibition.definition: An order issued by a higher court to a lower  one preventing it from exceeding its jurisdiction.

written instrument.definition: Something set down in writing; see also  instrument.

wrong.definition: (1) Incorrect; false. (2) Immoral; dishonest; violating  the rights of another. N. An immoral, unjust, or injurious act; a viola-  tion of someone’s legal rights that results in harm; a breach of one’s  legal duty that results in harm to someone else.

wrongdoer.definition: A person who violates someone’s rights or injures  someone else.

wrongful.definition: Causing injury; infringing on someone’s rights; reck-  less; unfair.

wrongful act.definition: An act that infringes on someone’s rights and  injures him or her.

wrongful death.definition: A statutory offense or tort for death caused by  a wrongful act or negligence for which an action can be brought on  behalf of the dead person’s beneficiaries.

wrongful life.definition: A medical malpractice claim that can be brought  on behalf of a child injured at birth, born with birth defects, or  other situations in which the parents allege that the child is the  victim of bad medical practice or advice.

x.definition: A mark used as a signature by someone who cannot write.

year-and-a-day rule.definition: A common law rule, discarded by some  jurisdictions, requiring that death occur within one year and one  day of some wrongful act for that act to be considered murder.

yellow dog contract.definition: An employment contract that threatens to  fire an employee if he or she joins a labor union, prohibited under  most laws and unenforceable in court.

yield.definition: (1) To produce; to deliver. (2) To surrender; to give up. N.  That which is produced by something; the money produced from  an investment.

yield, current.definition: Current return on an investment measured as a  percentage of its current price.

yield to maturity.definition: The return that an investor will receive from a  bond held until it matures.

youthful offender.definition: A criminal who is older than a juvenile but  still young, usually between the ages of 18 and 25, who might  receive special consideration at sentencing to maximize chances  of rehabilitation.

zone.definition: An area with a particular characteristic or assigned use. V.  To divide a town or city into geographical areas with different  building requirements and land use restrictions.

zoning, aesthetic.definition: Zoning designed to encourage the preserva-  tion of beauty or aesthetic features in an area.

zoning, cluster.definition: Zoning that permits small lot sizes and closely  spaced homes along with large areas of public green space such as  parks.

zoning, density.definition: Zoning that emphasizes clustered housing and  aims for a particular population density. See also variance.

abeyance.definition: A state of temporary disuse or suspension; an unsettled  state; the condition of an estate in fee or freehold during a lapse in  succession with no current titleholder or owner.

abuse of discretion.definition: A decision or judgment made by a trial  court that is inappropriate, inconsistent with the facts of the  matter, and not according to precedent or established law.

accommodation loan.definition: A loan given without any consideration or  security out of friendship or the lender’s desire to aid the borrower.

accumulate.definition: To gradually acquire or gather an increasing  amount of something. N. accumulation.

active participant.definition: Someone who engages in some conduct that  is part of the commission of a crime.

administrative law.definition: The body of laws that governs administrative agencies.

adversary proceeding.definition: A hearing or trial with opposing parties,  one seeking relief from the other, and that ends with one party  receiving a favorable outcome at the expense of the other. See  also case.

affirmative action.definition: Deliberate and positive efforts to help victims  of discrimination by remedying effects of past discrimination and  preventing future discrimination.

agent.definition: A person authorized to act for another person, the prin-  cipal, in specific or unlimited ways.

ambulance chaser.definition: A derogatory term for a lawyer who repre-  sents plaintiffs in personal injury cases.

antitrust.definition: Intended to prevent monopolies and trusts and to  promote competition in business. See also Sherman Antitrust Act,  Clayton Act.

appurtenance.definition: Something attached or appended to something  else.

arrest of judgment.definition: A court’s withholding of judgment in a case  when there is some problem with the record that would make the  judgment erroneous.

assets, capital.definition: All property held by a taxpayer.

attainder.definition: Under common law, the forfeiting of all civil and prop-  erty rights after being convicted of treason or a felony. See also bill  of attainder.

automatic weapon.definition: A gun that will continue to fire bullets as  long as the trigger is held down and it still contains ammunition; a  machine gun.

bail.definition: Money or other security given temporarily to the court to  allow a prisoner to be released before trial and to ensure that he or  she will return for trial; if the prisoner does not return for trial, he  or she forfeits the bail. V. To furnish money or property to get  someone released from prison.

banc.definition: (French) Bench; the place where a court sits; the full court  with all judges. See also en banc.

barter.definition: To exchange goods and services for other goods and serv-  ices without using money. N. barter.

benefit of the bargain.definition: A rule that allows a defrauded purchaser  to recover the difference between the real and represented values  of the item purchased.

billable.definition: Able to be billed; used to describe hours worked by  attorneys on behalf of clients who pay their bills by the hour.

blue sky laws.definition: State statutes that regulate the issue and sale of  securities.

boycott.definition: To withdraw from commercial or social dealings with a  person, business, or state as a way of showing displeasure with that  party’s actions. N. boycott.

constructive notice.definition: Notice implied by law, usually because it is  in a public record.

contingent estate.definition: An estate that will only vest if a specific event  occurs.

conversion.definition: The tort of wrongfully taking another person’s property and assuming ownership of it or preventing the owner from  using it. V. convert.

corpus delicti.definition: (Latin) The body of a crime; the person or object  on which the crime is committed, or the prima facie showing that  the person or object was the victim of a crime; a necessary element  for conviction, including the occurrence of some injury and some  criminal act.

count.definition: A distinct statement of a cause of action or a claim in a  plaintiff’s pleading; an allegation of a specific offense or charge in  an indictment.

court of last resort.definition: The highest court to which an appellant can  hope to appeal, such as the Supreme Court for federal cases.

creditor.definition: A person or business to whom a debt is owed. See also  debtor.

cruel.definition: Callously causing pain or suffering. N. cruelty.

custom and usage.definition: A customary practice that has been done so  long that it has acquired the force of law in its locality.

dangerous instrumentality.definition: An object that can endanger people  by either careless or improper use.

defalcate.definition: To embezzle funds that have been entrusted to the  embezzler; to fail to pay over trust funds or other money held in a  fiduciary capacity at the proper time. N. defalcation.

deferred payments.definition: Payments that are spread out over time or  entirely postponed.

delinquent child.definition: A minor below a specified age who tends to  commit crimes or otherwise engages in immoral or disobedient  behavior and therefore needs treatment or supervision. See also  juvenile delinquent.

deposition.definition: A form of discovery before trial in which an attorney  questions a witness under oath and a court reporter makes a tran-  script of the testimony, which can then be used as evidence at trial.

despot.definition: A ruler with absolute power. See also tyrant.

disbar.definition: To expel a lawyer from the bar and rescind his or her  license to practice law. N. disbarment.

dismissal.definition: A judge’s order that terminates a lawsuit, motion, etc.,  without considering the issues involved in the matter.

distributable net income.definition: The maximum amount of money  received from distributions from a trust or estate on which benefi-  ciaries may be taxed. ABBRV. DNI.

divorce.definition: The legal dissolution of a marriage. V. divorce. See also  alimony, annul, separate.

domestic relations.definition: The branch of law that handles family  matters such as divorce, adoption, and custody.

electoral college.definition: The group of representatives chosen from  each state to cast their votes in a presidential election.

employee stock ownership plan.definition: A kind of employee retirement  benefit plan in which the employees own shares in the employer’s  company, often receiving contributions from the employer, and  only recognize income from the shares upon collecting the  proceeds at retirement. ABBRV. ESOP.

enemy combatant.definition: A member of the armed forces of an enemy  state; during war with that state, a member of a force that commits  a belligerent act against a state.

ERISA.definition: Employment Retirement Income Security Act of  1974.

estate tax.definition: A tax on property transferred after the owner’s death  that is levied on the estate.

exception.definition: (1) Something excluded from a category to which it  would ordinarily belong; something that does not follow the  general rule. (2) An objection; an objection to a court’s ruling or an  error. (3) In insurance, a risk that is excluded from a policy. (4) In  property, a piece of land that is not included in a transfer of an  estate or interest. V. except.

execute.definition: (1) To put into effect; to complete; to carry out an  action; to sign a legal document and perform any other tasks  needed to make it legally binding. (2) To put to death. N. execution.

exit interview.definition: An interview conducted between an employee  and employer when the employee leaves his or her job with that  employer, at which the parties discuss the employee’s job satisfac-  tion, performance, and other matters.

extrinsic.definition: Coming from the outside; not an inherent part of  something.

fail.definition: To be unsuccessful at something; to be deficient; to be  unable to meet obligations; to lapse (a legacy). N. failure.

false pretenses.definition: Intentionally using fraud or misrepresentation  to obtain property or money.

federal.definition: Relating to the central government of a union of  states, such as the national government of the United States.

Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure.definition: Procedural rules created  by the Supreme Court in 1945 that govern proceedings in criminal  cases before U.S. district courts and sometimes before U.S.  magistrates.

fetus.definition: An unborn mammal; an unborn human child after the first  eight weeks of gestation. ADJ. fetal.

financial intermediary.definition: A person or company that brings  together borrowers and lenders and specializes in transactions that  change money from one form to another.

fit.definition: Of sufficient quality or ability to meet a specific purpose;  adequate or suitable. N. fitness.

forbear.definition: To resist an impulse to do something; to refrain from  doing something. N. forbearance.

forfeit.definition: To lose the right to something as a punishment; to be  forced to give something up. N. A right or thing lost as a penalty; the  act of forfeiting something. ADJ. Lost or given up as a penalty.

freedom of religion.definition: The right to choose and practice a religion  without interference from the government or from established  churches, guaranteed by the First Amendment of the Constitution.

frivolous claim.definition: A cause of action that has no hope of success,  brought primarily to annoy or embarrass the defendant.

garnishor.definition: A creditor plaintiff who brings a garnishment  proceeding against a debtor.

genocide.definition: The practice of killing a race or ethnic group; system-  atic eradication of an ethnic or national group by murder, forced  birth control, removal of children from the group, and a general  program of persecution.

green card.definition: A registration card that allows a permanent resident  alien to live and work in the United States.

Gun Control Act of 1968.definition: A federal law that regulates interstate  commerce in firearms by prohibiting certain categories of people  from possessing firearms and by limiting trade in firearms to  certain licensed dealers. See also Federal Firearms License, FFL.

harass.definition: (1) To pressure, intimidate, or attack repeatedly; to  annoy; to insult or abuse verbally. (2) To bring a criminal prosecu-  tion against someone without a reasonable expectation of convic-  tion. N. harassment. See also harassment, sexual.

hearing, final.definition: The last stage in a lawsuit, in which the case is  determined on its merits.

hidden defect.definition: A defect in an object or property that is not imme-  diately apparent on inspection and for which a buyer has the right  to revoke an acceptance to purchase.

home port.definition: The port where a ship or vessel is registered, or the  port nearest to the residence of its owner or manager.

in chief, case.definition: The initial case presented at trial by the party  with the burden of proof, i.e., the party bringing the lawsuit,  including testimony and evidence, after which the opposing party  has an opportunity to rebut.

inconsistent.definition: (1) Self-contradictory; not staying the same;  adopting two or more mutually exclusive viewpoints simultane-  ously. (2) Not in keeping with one’s own beliefs. See also prior  inconsistent statement.

indigent defendant.definition: A criminal defendant who lacks the finan-  cial resources to pay for a lawyer to defend him- or herself.

in extremis.definition: (Latin) At the outermost limits; at the point of  death; in extreme difficulty.

informer’s privilege.definition: The government’s right to keep the identity  of an informer secret from the defendant about whom the informer  has provided information.

injury.definition: Harm or damage; damage that comes from the violation  of a legal right.

instruct.definition: To teach; to provide information about something; to  direct or command.

insurgent.definition: A person who rises up against the government or  authorities.

interest rate.definition: The percentage used to calculate interest due on  a loan.

intoxicate.definition: To make drunk; to cause someone to lose control of  his or her physical and mental faculties.

investment banker.definition: A financial institution or broker that buys  newly issued securities and sells them to investors.

issue of law.definition: A question about the law applicable to a case when  the facts of the matter are undisputed.

John/Jane Doe.definition: A fictitious name given to a party to a lawsuit  who wishes to remain anonymous or whose name is unknown;  a fictitious name given to a hypothetical character for purposes  of argument.

Judge Advocate General.definition: A title used by military senior legal  officers. ABBRV. JAG.

juridical.definition: Having to do with legal proceedings, judges, or the  administration of justice.

justice.definition: (1) Fairness; fair and just administration of law; fair  treatment or behavior. (2) A judge; a magistrate; a judge sitting on  the Supreme Court of a state or country.

knowledge.definition: Awareness of or familiarity with something; actual  information; awareness of the positive and negative aspects of  something.

land.definition: (1) Soil or earth, including plants growing on it and  rocks embedded in it. (2) Property, especially in the form of  land; real property.

launder.definition: To move money through different businesses,  accounts, and banks in order to conceal its origins; the federal  crime of obscuring the source of money obtained through illegal  means such as drug dealing, racketeering, and other crimes. N.  laundering.

leading question.definition: A question in which the questioner hints or  suggests to the witness the answer he or she would like to receive;  not allowed during direct examination.

legalese.definition: The formal language used in legal documents; espe-  cially refers derogatorily to unnecessarily complex and opaque  language that is extremely difficult to understand.

let.definition: (1) To rent; to allow someone to use property in exchange for  payment; to grant a license to use property. (2) To award a contract  for a project to one of several applicants who have submitted bids.

liberty.definition: Freedom; freedom from restrictions except for those  imposed by law to prevent people from interfering with the liberty  of others.

limit.definition: To restrict; to set boundaries. N. A boundary or restriction.

lobby.definition: To attempt to influence a politician on a particular issue.  N. A group of people who work to influence politicians on an issue;  an attempt to influence politicians on an issue.

lost property.definition: Property that is no longer in its owner’s possession  due to the owner’s negligence or carelessness and that cannot be  found or recovered.

mail fraud.definition: Using letters sent through the mail to defraud people  out of money.

mandamus.definition: (Latin) We command; a writ issued by a superior  court to a lower court, corporation, or officer, ordering it to do  some act that is a duty required of it by law.

marital rights.definition: The rights that constitute the basis of  marriage and that a husband and wife are entitled to expect  from one another.

master-servant rule.definition: A rule stating that an employer is respon-  sible for conduct by an employee while the employee is working  for him or her.

McCain-Feingold Act.definition: The Bipartisan Campaign Reform Act of  2002, a federal law that sets rules for campaign finance, limiting the  amounts of money that contributors can donate and regulating the  types of advertising that campaigns may use to raise money.

mental cruelty.definition: In marriage, behavior by one spouse that  endangers the mental health of the other or is so humiliating and  unpleasant that it makes the other’s life miserable.

military law.definition: The branch of law that regulates the military and  applies only to members of the armed services.

misuse.definition: To use something in an improper or incorrect way; to  use a product in a way not intended or reasonably foreseen by its  manufacturer. N. misuse.

mutuality of obligation.definition: The principle that both parties to a  contract are either bound to it or not, but one party cannot be  bound if the other is not.

National Rifle Association.definition: A national advocacy organization  that supports gun rights. ABBRV. NRA.

negative evidence.definition: Evidence showing that something is not  the case.

network.definition: A group of computers that are linked to one another  through electronic communications technology. V. To set out to  meet people with the intention of using their acquaintance to  further one’s business or career ambitions.

nonaccess.definition: Lack of opportunity for sexual intercourse, used as  a defense in paternity suits.

novation.definition: The replacement of an old contract with a new one,  usually substituting a new party for one of the original ones.

obligee.definition: The person who is owed the performance of some duty  or the payment of money; a promisee.

of course.definition: Used to describe an action that is a person’s right,  which does not need justification or permission, usually in the  phrase “as a matter of course.”

omnibus clause.definition: A clause in an automobile insurance policy  extending liability coverage to people other than the named  insured; also, a clause in a will that passes on property not known  or mentioned at the time the will was drafted.

operative.definition: Most relevant, meaningful, or significant; describes  the word or words that carry the most important meaning in a  sentence, or that cause a contract or document to take effect.

ordinary care.definition: The degree of care that a person of normal  prudence would exercise in a given situation.

outstanding.definition: Undone; remaining to be done or paid.

parliamentary law.definition: Rules governing procedure in a legislature;  also called parliamentary procedure.

party wall.definition: A wall built on the border between two pieces of  property that is shared equally by both owners.

pawn.definition: To give an object of personal property to another person,  called a pawnbroker, as security for a loan.

pension.definition: A regular payment made to a person after retirement by  his or her former employer or by the government, usually from a  fund to which the person contributed while working.

permissive counterclaim.definition: A counterclaim that a defendant is  allowed to bring regarding a matter that is completely different  from the one brought by the plaintiff in the original complaint. See  also joinder, use, waste.

petition.definition: (1) A formal written request presented to some  authority asking that something be done, often signed by a large  number of people who express their support for the proposed  action; a formal written request to a court asking it to take some  judicial action. (2) In a court of equity, an application to the court  asking for relief on some matter that functions as a complaint does  in a court of law; in most jurisdictions today, the term “complaint”  is used instead of petition. V. To bring a petition.

plaintiff’s attorney.definition: An attorney who specializes in representing  plaintiffs.

prayer.definition: A formal request in a complaint or petition in equity  asking for relief for the plaintiff’s grievance; also called a prayer for  relief or demand for relief. V. pray.

preliminary hearing.definition: An initial hearing on a criminal charge to  give the judge a chance to determine whether the prosecution has  enough evidence to bring the charge to trial.

presumption.definition: The act of presuming; the use of existing facts to  infer other facts that are assumed to be true until they are  rebutted; an assumption that must be made by a court if certain  facts are shown and that will stand until other facts are  presented to rebut it.

prima facie.definition: (Latin) First face; at first sight; based on first  impressions; the initial view of something, accepted as true until  disproven.

prisoner at the bar.definition: A person who is being tried for a crime.

probate court.definition: A court that handles wills, estates, and the  appointment of guardians for orphaned children.

profession.definition: (1) An occupation or job that requires extensive  formal training and usually certification by some body of profes-  sionals within the field, such as with law or medicine. (2) A decla-  ration of belief; a public declaration or announcement. V. profess.

property.definition: That which is or can be owned, possessed, used, and  disposed of; those things owned by someone; possessions, land,  buildings, and all rights and interests that can be owned.

prosecute.definition: (1) To begin legal proceedings against someone, espe-  cially by the state against an accused criminal. (2) To continue to  carry out some action, intending to complete it.

provisional remedy.definition: A temporary remedy given to a plaintiff to  meet a present need while a lawsuit is in progress.

public property.definition: Property owned by the citizenry or the govern-  ment as opposed to a private individual.

putative.definition: Reputed; generally assumed to be; supposed.

radical.definition: Politically extreme, most often extremely liberal;  supporting extreme change or complete reform of a system of  government. N. radical. See also revolution.

realize.definition: To make money from something; to receive profits from  investments. N. realization.

rebuttal evidence.definition: Evidence offered to refute or combat claims  made and evidence presented by an opposing party.

recognizance.definition: A promise or obligation to do an act required by  law, such as to appear in court on a particular date to answer crim-  inal charges.

Red Cross.definition: A group of humanitarian organizations in several  countries that work to help people in need, such as disaster  victims.

refugee.definition: An individual who has been forced to flee his or her  home due to war, fear of persecution, or natural disaster. See  also asylum.

rehabilitate.definition: To restore a person or business to a normal level  of health, freedom, reputation, dignity, finances, or other  capacity; to improve the reliability of a witness whose credibility  has been impeached by opposing counsel; to help a criminal  improve his or her situation so as to abandon crime in the future.  N. rehabilitation.

remainder.definition: Something that is left over; a part of an estate in land  left over after the rest of the estate has been settled; a future  interest in an estate.

some matter.definition: To provide an account of an event, either oral or  written, based on investigation or observation.

respondent.definition: (1) The party against whom an appeal is brought;  see also appellant. (2) The party against whom an equitable  proceeding is brought.

retain.definition: To hold; to keep; to employ or hire; to pay someone, such  as an attorney, an initial fee as a way of securing his or her services.

reversion.definition: A right to property held by its original owner or his or  her heirs to take it back when a current interest ends, such as at the  end of a lease or on the death of the person who currently  possesses it; also called reverter. V. revert.

riparian rights.definition: The rights of those who own property on the  banks of rivers and streams related to use of the water and the land  bordering it.

rule nisi.definition: An ex parte order that will become final unless the  affected party can show a reason why it should not.

sale or exchange.definition: A phrase used in tax to describe a sale or  transfer of property that yields a taxable gain or loss.

search and seizure.definition: A search in which the officers conducting  the search take evidence of a crime if they find it.

Securities and Exchange Commission.definition: A federal agency that  oversees the sale and issuing of corporate stocks and bonds  and corporate takeovers. ABBRV. SEC.

self-employment tax.definition: The 15.3% tax that the self-employed must  pay on their incomes to cover both employee and employer  portions of Federal Insurance Contribution Act taxes.

sentence, noncustodial.definition: A sentence that does not involve  imprisonment, such as a fine.

service by mail.definition: Delivery of a legal document through mail.

several actions.definition: Individual lawsuits brought against multiple  defendants by the same plaintiff out of the same subject matter.

sign.definition: To write one’s name on a document to authenticate or  execute it.

sound body.definition: Healthy, without disease, injury, or physical  impairment.

speech or debate clause.definition: A clause in the Constitution that  provides congresspeople with immunity from prosecution arising  out of any speech or debate conducted during a legislative session,  to ensure that legislators may speak freely without fear of reprisal.

stalk.definition: To follow or watch a person with no legitimate  purpose and in such a way that it alarms the person being  followed. N. stalking.
