status report.definition: A report on the status of a business or endeavor as  of a particular date or time.

stock option.definition: The right to buy stock at a predetermined price,  usually better than market price, during a specified period,  offered as incentive or compensation to employees and managers  of a company.

strikebreaker.definition: A person brought in to do the work of a striking  employee; also called a scab.

subrogor.definition: A person who substitutes another person for himself  or herself.

summons.definition: An order issued by a court informing a defendant that  a lawsuit has been brought against him or her and summoning him  or her to appear in court on a particular day to answer the  complaint.

surveillance.definition: The monitoring or observation of a person or situ-  ation, especially done by police pursuing a suspected criminal or  gathering evidence of a crime.

taking.definition: The act of taking something; a government’s interference  with or regulation of how a property owner can use his or her prop-  erty in such a way that it substantially impairs the owner’s property  right. See also condemn, eminent domain, larceny.

tax return.definition: A document filed by a taxpayer with the Internal  Revenue Service or state tax agency on which the taxpayer  reports a taxable year’s income, deductions, and credits, and  calculates tax liability.

testator.definition: A person who writes and executes a will; a deceased  person whose property is disposed of according to the terms of his  or her will. FEM. testatrix.

tithe.definition: A tenth of one’s income contributed to something such as  a charity or church; in feudal times, the tenth of a farm and other  productions that was required to be donated to the church. V. tithe.

total disability.definition: A person’s inability, due to physical or mental  affliction, to perform any work that he or she is qualified to do and  that is available in the community.

treasury bond.definition: (1) A bond issued by the U.S. Treasury that  matures in a term greater than five years. (2) A bond issued and  then bought back by the same corporation.

tyrant.definition: A single absolute ruler; a ruler who holds absolute power  over the state; a despot.

unavoidable accident.definition: An accident that could not have been  prevented by the parties involved in it, no matter what steps they  took to avoid it, and not caused by their negligence or lack of skill  or care.

unethical.definition: Not ethical; not according to morals or the ethical  standards of a profession.

unionize.definition: To become a member of a labor union or persuade  others to join it. ADJ. unionized.

unity of time.definition: Sharing an interest in property simultaneously.

useful life.definition: For tax purposes, the period during which an asset is  expected to be useful to its owner before wearing out or otherwise  becoming useless.

variance.definition: (1) A discrepancy. (2) A disagreement between the  allegations in a pleading and the evidence offered to prove them.  (3) Permission to use property in a way not allowed under appli-  cable zoning ordinances; see also unnecessary hardship.

vested pension.definition: A pension to which an employee is uncondition-  ally entitled because he or she has completed the required period  of employment, whether or not he or she is working for that partic-  ular employer at the time of retirement.

visitation.definition: A formally organized meeting with someone, espe-  cially the time that a divorced spouse is allowed to spend with the  children from his or her marriage.

voyeur.definition: A person who receives sexual gratification from  watching other people engaged in sex acts or seeing other people  naked, or from watching others in pain.

ward.definition: (1) A territorial division in a town or city. (2) An area of a  hospital or prison. (3) A person, especially a child, under the care  and supervision of a guardian.

whistleblower.definition: An employee who reports wrongdoing by his or  her employer or coworkers.

witness.definition: A person who has seen or experienced some event; a  person who testifies under oath about seeing or experiencing some  event, or about the character of someone involved in a lawsuit. See  also character witness, material witness.

writing.definition: The act of writing; a written document or instrument.

campaign.definition: (1) An organized effort to achieve some goal, usually  election to political office or promotion of a political or social  cause. (2) Military operations in a specific area or done for a partic-  ular reason. V. campaign.

capture.definition: To forcefully take possession of someone or something.  N. capture.

cash method.definition: An accounting method that records only money  that has been paid or received. See also accelerated cost recovery  system.

census.definition: An official count of a population.

champerty.definition: An illegal agreement between a party to a lawsuit  and another person who agrees to pay the costs of pursuing the  lawsuit in exchange for a share of the resulting proceeds.

check, cashier’s.definition: A check written for a bank customer on a  bank’s own account, guaranteeing that the bank will pay the  amount of money recorded on the check.

claim of right.definition: (1) In property, the claim that someone in adverse  possession of land intends to claim the land as his or her own. (2)  In taxation, the requirement that a taxpayer report all income for  the year, even if some of it might have to be repaid in a later year.

clerkship.definition: The period spent working as a law clerk.

coerce.definition: To force someone to act against his or her wishes,  through the use of verbal or physical threats or other forms of  compulsion. N. coercion.

commons.definition: Parks and other land set aside for public use.

complaint.definition: (1) The pleading that begins a civil lawsuit, in which  the plaintiff sets forth his or her causes of action and demands  relief. (2) In criminal law, a charge made before a magistrate that a  particular person has committed an offense, in an effort to begin  the process of prosecution.

conclusion.definition: (1) An ending. (2) A final opinion arrived at through  examination of facts and logical reasoning.

confidential communication.definition: Statements made in private, meant  to be heard only by the person addressed. See also privilege.

consanguinity.definition: Blood relationship; the relationship of people  who share a common ancestor. ADJ. consanguine, consan-  guineous.

Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliati.definition: A  federal law that allows some employees to maintain health  coverage through an employer after leaving employment. ABBRV.  COBRA.

exigence.definition: Urgent need; an emergency; also called exigency. ADJ. exigent.

Uniform Gifts to Minors Act.definition: See Uniform Transfers to Minors Act. ABBRV. UGMA.
